title: Bug Journal 2026-04-18

content:
Daily Report — 2026-04-17 Daily Overview What was done: Developed /checkpoint skill for rapid project switching with context persistence, enhanced MeetingHelper ASR system (multi-language support, Metal GPU via mlx-whisper migration, real-time noise suppression with automatic calibration), and fixed website bugs (build script parameter handling, Hugo sanity checks, CSS class mismatch breaking layout) How it was done: Built skill with save/resume/update modes plus PostToolUse hooks for automation, migrated from CPU-only faster-whisper to Metal-accelerated mlx-whisper, integrated noisereduce library with 0.5s noise fingerprint calibration across 5 ASR backends, conducted parallel code review using exploration agents, fixed parameter handling and CSS selectors through iterative browser testing Impact: Improved developer productivity through persistent task snapshots, enabled GPU-accelerated real-time ASR with flexible language selection and noise suppression in noisy environments, resolved critical website build pipeline issues and restored broken layout caused by theme-template version drift Created task-switching checkpoint skill, enhanced MeetingHelper with multi-language ASR, Metal GPU acceleration, and real-time noise suppression, then conducted comprehensive website code review fixing 11 bugs including critical CSS layout issue
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Website code review and bug fixing — Comprehensive audit of Hugo website directory covering build scripts, config, templates, and CSS. Fixed 11 bugs including update.sh parameter handling, Hugo build sanity check, compress_video.py resource leak, config.yml mainSections, favicon git whitelist, localStorage privacy hardening, parallel compression exit code checks, CSS accessibility support, and critical header.html CSS class mismatch (nav→header-nav) ✅ Create /checkpoint skill for task-switching context management — Built lightweight skill to save/resume/update work progress (plan.md) when switching between coding tasks, with modes: save (capture state), resume (restore context), update (incremental changes), all (cold-start project scan). Added PostToolUse hook for automatic checkpoint after plan mode exits. ✅ Migrate MeetingHelper to mlx-whisper for Metal GPU acceleration — Replaced CPU-only faster-whisper (CTranslate2) with mlx-whisper for Apple Silicon Metal GPU inference, including API migration (WhisperModel → mlx_whisper.transcribe) and warmup logic ✅ Implement real-time noise suppression for MeetingHelper — Added NoiseSuppressor class with automatic noise fingerprint calibration (first 0.5s), integrated denoising into all 5 ASR backends (Sherpa/Qwen/Whisper/Moonshine/Voxtral), made it default-on with –no-denoise opt-out ✅ Implement multi-language ASR support for MeetingHelper — Added –language parameter (zh/en/zh+en) with backend-specific handling: Whisper uses initial_prompt for mixed-language, Qwen3-ASR uses language parameter, bilingual models ignore the flag. Included download-models.sh for batch pre-download of 6 ASR backend models. ✅ Fix MeetingHelper operational issues — Enhanced stop.sh with SIGTERM → 3s wait → SIGKILL escalation plus pgrep cleanup, relocated transcripts/ to project root with gitignore, adjusted silence_threshold (0.008→0.02) and added compression_ratio_threshold/no_speech_threshold to prevent hallucinations Implementation & Fixes ✅ Update MeetingHelper tutorial documentation — Completely rewrote tutorial.md with dual-layer architecture explanation (subtitle overlay + Screenpipe), Python dependency clarification, 6 ASR backend comparison table, BlackHole virtual audio setup, and updated project structure ✅ Website deployment and sync — Synced bugJournal content from staging to website, restored public/.git deployment repository after accidental deletion, executed rclone sync to Google Drive ❌ Fix Claude Code Opus 4.7 model configuration — Fixed ANSI escape code pollution ([1m]) in settings.json and updated model ID from 4.6 to 4.7, but encountered AWS Bedrock permission error preventing Opus 4.7 access Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Website menu layout completely broken (vertical stacking instead of horizontal nav bar) after config.yml modifications Solution: Discovered custom header.html used class=‘nav’ while PaperMod theme CSS (after update) expects class=‘header-nav’ — changed template to match theme
Key Insight: Custom theme overrides must stay synchronized with theme updates — CSS selector mismatches cause silent layout failures without build errors
2. faster-whisper running on CPU only on macOS (no CUDA), causing slow inference Solution: Migrated to mlx-whisper which uses Apple Silicon’s Metal GPU, changing from WhisperModel API to mlx_whisper.transcribe() with warmup step
Key Insight: CTranslate2-based models require NVIDIA CUDA; on Apple Silicon, mlx-based alternatives provide native Metal acceleration
3. Whisper producing repetitive hallucinations (‘Meh Meh Meh…’) when keyboard typing sounds captured Solution: Adjusted silence_threshold from 0.008 to 0.02, added compression_ratio_threshold=2.4 and no_speech_threshold=0.6 to filter non-speech audio
Key Insight: Low silence thresholds cause ASR hallucinations on non-speech sounds; VAD pre-filtering or stricter thresholds essential for noisy environments
4. Single-language ASR parameter insufficient for mixed-language meetings (Chinese+English) Solution: Designed –language with three modes: zh (force Chinese), en (force English), zh+en (Whisper uses initial_prompt for mixed-language, Qwen3-ASR auto-detects)
Key Insight: Mixed-language support requires backend-specific strategies: initial_prompt for Whisper, language=None for Qwen3-ASR, native bilingual for Sherpa
5. MeetingHelper ASR accuracy suffered in noisy environments without pre-processing Solution: Implemented NoiseSuppressor class using noisereduce library with spectral gating (stationary=True, prop_decrease=0.8), automatic noise profile calibration during first 0.5s of capture, integrated into sd.InputStream pipeline before ASR
Key Insight: Real-time audio processing requires denoising between capture and ASR, not after; RMS-based silence detection should happen post-denoising for accurate thresholding
6. Hugo build failures could produce empty or partial public/ directory, but update.sh would still attempt git push deployment Solution: Added if [ ! -f “$PUBLIC_DIR/index.html” ]; then exit 1; fi check after hugo command
Key Insight: Critical build steps need sanity checks before irreversible actions like git push — exit codes alone are insufficient
7. compress_video.py used module-level code that created temp files without cleanup if HandBrakeCLI failed, making it unsafe to import Solution: Refactored into compress_video() function with try/finally for temp file cleanup, moved CLI logic to main()
Key Insight: Python scripts intended for both CLI and import use must separate side effects into functions and use if name == ‘main’ guard
General Issues 8. update.sh run_translation_phase() used shift to discard first argument but call sites passed description strings, creating confusing parameter handling Solution: Removed shift from function and removed description string arguments from all call sites
Key Insight: Shell functions don’t need to explicitly consume description parameters — let callers pass only what the function actually uses
9. stop.sh failed to reliably terminate subtitle window processes, leaving orphaned Python processes Solution: Implemented two-stage termination: send SIGTERM, wait 3 seconds, escalate to SIGKILL if alive, plus pgrep-based cleanup for orphans
Key Insight: PyObjC’s app.run() may not respond to basic signals; robust process management requires timeout + escalation + process scanning
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Performance optimization initiative Role Approach Human User noticed Whisper on CPU, proactively asked why, and suggested switching to mlx-whisper AI AI implemented faster-whisper with device=‘cpu’ without questioning performance limitation until user raised it Difference Analysis: Human demonstrated active performance awareness and domain knowledge (knew mlx-whisper exists), while AI accepted suboptimal default
CSS loading diagnosis Role Approach Human Used browser DevTools and visual inspection (screenshots) to confirm CSS was loaded but layout rules weren’t applying, suspected selector mismatch AI Initially suspected caching issues, port conflicts, and SRI integrity problems — spent multiple attempts clearing caches before examining CSS selectors Difference Analysis: Human used visual debugging (browser render) to narrow hypothesis space early; AI relied on server-side verification that couldn’t detect selector mismatch
Whisper’s condition_on_previous_text parameter Role Approach Human Keep condition_on_previous_text=True as correct default behavior AI Suggested setting condition_on_previous_text=False to prevent repetition loop Difference Analysis: AI misdiagnosed the repetition problem — issue was low silence_threshold capturing keyboard noise, not the continuity parameter. Human’s domain knowledge caught this.
Mixed-language meeting scenario Role Approach Human Immediately asked ‘what if there are two languages?’ when AI proposed single-language –language flag AI Initially designed –language as single-value parameter without considering bilingual meetings Difference Analysis: Human anticipated real-world edge case that AI’s initial design missed; demonstrates deeper understanding of user scenarios
Config.yml menu structure Role Approach Human Noticed website broke after config changes, insisted problem persisted through multiple AI fix attempts, provided visual evidence via screenshots AI Initially restructured menu from top-level to per-language, then reverted after realizing Hugo’s implicit behavior differs across versions Difference Analysis: Human provided ground truth through persistent observation; AI made assumptions about Hugo’s multilingual config behavior without fully understanding version-specific quirks
Cold-start project scan trigger strategy Role Approach Human User insisted expensive project scan should be opt-in via explicit /checkpoint all command, not automatic AI Initially designed cold-start as automatic fallback when plan.md missing, triggered during normal save/resume flow Difference Analysis: Human prioritized predictability and explicit control over convenience for expensive operations; AI prioritized automation without considering cost implications
Default behavior for noise suppression feature Role Approach Human User immediately requested denoising be default-on with –no-denoise opt-out, prioritizing real-world usability AI AI initially implemented opt-in –denoise flag, following conservative pattern of explicit feature activation Difference Analysis: Human recognized practical UX consideration that denoising should be transparent default; AI defaulted to explicit opt-in without considering common use case
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Missed theme version drift issue: custom templates (header.html) were based on old PaperMod version with class=‘nav’, but theme CSS updated to .header-nav selector — no systematic check for theme-template synchronization Accepted CPU-only faster-whisper on macOS without recognizing performance limitation or suggesting Metal GPU alternatives Designed expensive operations (project scan, model downloads) as automatic fallbacks rather than explicit opt-in commands with progress indicators Spent many debugging rounds on CSS loading mechanics (caching, HTTP status, SRI integrity) before checking HTML/CSS selector mismatch — didn’t prioritize front-end debugging pattern (inspect element → check selector) Failed to anticipate that Hugo multilingual menu configuration structure changes would have breaking behavior differences — treated as simple refactoring Initial –language design didn’t account for mixed-language meetings, only considering single-language constraint Misdiagnosed Whisper repetition as condition_on_previous_text issue when root cause was low silence_threshold capturing non-speech sounds Deleted public/.git deployment repository during aggressive cache cleanup without checking if it was a separate git repository General Limitations Did not proactively check for missing dependencies (sounddevice) before attempting to start ASR service, and failed to surface first-time model download (1.6GB) in user-facing progress indicator Learnings Key Learnings Custom Hugo theme overrides must be audited against upstream theme changes — CSS class renames in themes silently break custom templates with no build errors, only runtime layout failures Expensive background operations (large downloads, project scans) should be explicit user actions with progress indicators, not hidden automatic fallbacks Apple Silicon ASR optimization: CTranslate2 (faster-whisper) requires CUDA, use mlx-based alternatives for Metal GPU acceleration on macOS Audio pipeline denoising must occur post-capture, pre-ASR: inserting NoiseSuppressor after data.flatten() but before stream.accept_waveform() maximizes effectiveness. Noise fingerprint calibration using initial silent period (0.5s) enables stationary noise reduction without pre-trained models. Browser-based visual debugging (DevTools inspect → check applied styles → trace selector mismatch) is more effective for CSS layout issues than server-side file verification Mixed-language ASR requires per-backend strategies: Whisper needs initial_prompt guidance, Qwen3-ASR auto-detects with language=None, Sherpa has native bilingual models ASR hallucination (repetitive output) often stems from low silence_threshold capturing non-speech sounds; VAD pre-filtering or stricter thresholds solve this .claude/settings.json is team-shareable (should be committed), while .claude/settings.local.json is personal (should be gitignored) Bash set -e does not propagate exit codes from background jobs — must explicitly capture exit codes with wait $pid || fail_flag=1 pattern for parallel task error handling Claude Code hook system can only execute shell commands, not skills directly; use JSON output to prompt Claude for skill execution after hook triggers Practical Learnings Process termination robustness requires timeout + signal escalation (SIGTERM → wait → SIGKILL) + cleanup scanning (pgrep) ANSI terminal escape codes can pollute configuration files when values are copy-pasted from colorized CLI output, breaking parsers that expect clean strings Python scripts intended for both CLI and import must use if name == ‘main’ guard and wrap side effects in functions — module-level code with resource allocation creates cleanup hazards Hugo’s public/ directory is often a separate git repository for deployment (GitHub Pages) — rm -rf public/ during cache cleanup destroys deployment history Conversation Summaries gadget ✅ Create /checkpoint skill for task-switching context management 14:32:49.684 | claude_code Developed lightweight skill to save/resume/update work-in-progress context (plan.md) for rapid task switching. User refined design through iteration: changed cold-start from auto-trigger to explicit /checkpoint all command, configured PostToolUse hook to auto-run checkpoint after plan mode exits, discussed .claude/settings.json git tracking strategy. Skill created at ~/.claude/skills/checkpoint/SKILL.md with four modes (save/resume/update/all) and hook configuration added to project settings.
MeetingHelper ✅ Multi-language ASR support and Metal GPU acceleration 15:14:58.703 | claude_code Enhanced MeetingHelper ASR system across multiple sessions: fixed stop.sh process termination (SIGTERM → wait → SIGKILL escalation), relocated transcripts to project root, implemented –language parameter (zh/en/zh+en with backend-specific handling), created download-models.sh for batch pre-download, debugged Whisper hallucinations (adjusted silence_threshold/compression_ratio_threshold), and migrated from CPU-only faster-whisper to Metal-accelerated mlx-whisper. User proactively identified performance limitation and suggested mlx-whisper, spotted mixed-language edge case AI’s initial design missed, and corrected AI’s misdiagnosis of condition_on_previous_text parameter. Final system supports flexible language selection with GPU acceleration on Apple Silicon.
✅ Real-time noise suppression implementation and documentation update 15:33:56.474 | claude_code User requested vocal separation research for feeding clean speech to LLM. AI researched Demucs/Spleeter/RNNoise options and proposed two pipelines; user chose real-time preprocessing approach. Implemented NoiseSuppressor class using noisereduce library with 0.5s automatic calibration, integrated across all 5 ASR backends (Sherpa/Qwen/Whisper/Moonshine/Voxtral). User requested denoising be default-on; AI refactored to –no-denoise opt-out pattern. Separately, completely rewrote tutorial.md with dual-layer architecture explanation (local subtitle overlay + Screenpipe backend), Python dependency clarification, 6 ASR backend comparison table, BlackHole virtual audio setup, and updated project structure.
Random Test 🔄 Claude Code Opus 4.7 configuration debugging 01:32:34.253 | claude_code User reported inability to use Opus 4.7 model and requested fix. AI checked Claude Code settings files and discovered two issues: model ID contained ANSI escape code [1m] from terminal formatting, and version was 4.6 not 4.7. AI corrected both settings.json and settings.bedrock.json to use clean us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7-v1 identifier. Subsequent testing revealed AWS Bedrock permission error indicating account lacks access to Opus 4.7 model, requiring AWS Sales contact for approval.
gadget (website) ✅ Comprehensive website code review and bug fixing 15:33:15.251 | claude_code Conducted full-stack audit of Hugo website directory using parallel exploration agents. Discovered 11 bugs across build scripts (update.sh parameter handling, missing Hugo sanity check, compress_video.py resource leak), config (mainSections invalid entry, favicon gitignore, localStorage privacy hardening), and templates (CSS accessibility, parallel compression exit codes). Fixed all issues but introduced menu restructuring bug that broke site layout. After extensive debugging (cache clearing, port checks, HTTP verification), user provided screenshots showing CSS was loaded but layout rules not applying. Root cause discovered: custom header.html template used obsolete CSS class ’nav’ while updated PaperMod theme expects ‘header-nav’ — theme version drift. Final fix restored full site functionality. Also synced bugJournal content via staging script and executed rclone push to Google Drive.

content:
Daily Report — 2026-04-16 Daily Overview What was done: Refactored error recovery demo collection pipeline by eliminating code duplication, changing validation requirements, and installed agent-skills plugin collection globally How it was done: Extracted repeated logic into shared functions, merged duplicate utilities, fixed data corruption bug, changed quota rule from augmentable_only to replay_only, and executed marketplace-based plugin installation workflow Impact: Reduced codebase by 80 lines, fixed obj_orientations bug preventing data loss, made 3 previously rejected demos valid under new validation policy, and enabled access to 20 structured engineering skills for all future projects Refactored error recovery demo collection system with simplified validation codebase and relaxed quota rules, and installed agent-skills plugin globally for Claude Code
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Code simplification in error_recovery project — Eliminated NumpyEncoder duplication across 4 files, merged upsert functions, extracted validation check helpers, and fixed obj_orientations bug ✅ Create revalidate_demos.py batch validation script — Wrote comprehensive script to re-validate all collected demos under new quota rules with dry-run and recount-only modes ✅ Install agent-skills plugin globally — Added addyosmani/agent-skills marketplace and installed the plugin with user scope, providing 20 production engineering skills across 6 development phases ✅ Change default quota rule to replay_only — Modified recovery_collection.yaml and 4 Python files to change validation requirement from replay_and_augmentable to replay_only Implementation & Fixes ✅ Switch Claude Code from Bedrock API to subscription billing — Modified ~/.claude/settings.json to remove Bedrock environment variables and change model identifier Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Bug in revalidate_demos.py:96 where obj_orientations quaternion data was incorrectly written to obj_positions dictionary, causing data loss Solution: Changed line 96 from ‘obj_positions[key[4:-5]]’ to ‘obj_orientations[key[4:-5]]’ to write quaternions to correct dictionary
Key Insight: Copy-paste errors in data loading code can silently corrupt multi-field data structures
2. determine_counts_toward_target returns cached counts_toward_target value when field exists, preventing re-evaluation under new quota rules Solution: Clear counts_toward_target and quota_reason fields before calling determine_counts_toward_target during revalidation
Key Insight: Functions that check for pre-computed values need explicit cache invalidation when re-computing is required
General Issues 3. NumpyEncoder class duplicated in 4 separate files (resegment_recovery_demos.py, 3_mimicgen_recovery_augment.py, 2_collect_recovery_demos.py, revalidate_demos.py) Solution: Moved NumpyEncoder to shared script_utils.py and updated all consumers to import from there
Key Insight: Shared JSON encoding logic should live in common utilities to prevent drift and maintenance burden
4. Initial uncertainty about correct CLI syntax - repository README mentioned /plugin commands but actual CLI uses ‘claude plugin’ structure Solution: Verified actual CLI capabilities using ‘claude –help’ and ‘claude plugin –help’ before executing commands, confirming the correct syntax is ‘claude plugin marketplace add’ and ‘claude plugin install’
Key Insight: External documentation may not match exact local CLI syntax; always verify with local help documentation before executing installation commands
5. tag_npz_validation and save_manifest functions duplicated between revalidate_demos.py and 2_collect_recovery_demos.py Solution: Moved both functions to shared recovery_collection_validation.py module and updated consumers to import from there
Key Insight: Validation and persistence logic should be centralized in the validation framework module
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Validation policy change decision Role Approach Human User recognized that requiring MimicGen augmentation pass was too strict and decided to relax validation to replay-only mode AI AI implemented the technical changes but did not propose the policy change Difference Analysis: Strategic product decision about validation requirements came from human understanding of practical collection constraints; AI executed technical implementation
Implementation Level Plugin installation methodology Role Approach Human Provided repository URL and requested global installation without specifying the technical steps AI Implemented systematic verification workflow: (1) fetch and analyze repository documentation, (2) verify local CLI capabilities, (3) execute installation commands, (4) confirm successful installation with status check Difference Analysis: Human focused on the goal and resource identification; AI provided methodical execution pathway with validation at each step
Bedrock billing configuration Role Approach Human User knew they were using Bedrock API and needed specific configuration changes, had to correct AI’s initial generic advice AI AI initially provided generic Anthropic API key switching instructions, then corrected to Bedrock-specific guidance after user clarification Difference Analysis: User had domain-specific knowledge about their deployment setup that AI initially missed; AI generalized too quickly
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Did not proactively suggest validation policy change; waited for human to identify that augmentable_only requirement was too strict General Limitations Initially provided generic /logout instructions for switching billing modes without considering Bedrock-specific configuration, required user correction to understand Bedrock context Learnings Key Learnings Error recovery demo validation supports four quota rules (success_only, replay_only, augmentable_only, replay_and_augmentable) with different strictness levels; changing rules requires batch revalidation of existing data agent-skills provides 20 production-grade engineering skills organized across six development phases (Define: spec/refine; Plan: architecture/TDD; Build: incremental coding; Verify: testing; Review: code review; Ship: deployment), representing codified senior engineering workflows Systematic code deduplication across multiple files can yield significant line reduction (80 lines in this case) while improving maintainability Functions that cache computed values must explicitly invalidate cache when re-computation is needed; determine_counts_toward_target checks for pre-existing counts_toward_target field Claude Code plugin installation follows marketplace-first pattern: use ‘claude plugin marketplace add ’ to add the plugin source, then ‘claude plugin install @’ to install globally Practical Learnings Injection replay warnings about missing clean trajectories are UX issues, not data quality issues; fallback to direct state load still produces correct post-injection state Conversation Summaries ✅ Validation quota rule change and revalidation tooling 12:07:22.814 | claude_code User requested changing default quota rule from augmentable_only to replay_only so demos only need replay validation, not MimicGen augmentation. Modified config file and 6 Python files to update defaults. Created comprehensive revalidate_demos.py script with dry-run and recount-only modes. Re-validated all tasks; 3 threading demos changed from rejected to accepted under new rules.
✅ Code review skill explanation and simplification 13:05:40.988 | claude_code User asked about /review skill functionality, learned it analyzes GitHub PRs via gh CLI. Then invoked agent-skills:code-simplify which refactored recovery_collection_validation.py and related files: extracted repeated validation checks, merged duplicate upsert functions, centralized NumpyEncoder across 4 files, and fixed obj_orientations data corruption bug. Net reduction of 80 lines.
✅ Global installation of agent-skills plugin for Claude Code 13:03:35.732 | claude_code User requested help installing the agent-skills repository globally. AI fetched the repository documentation to understand it provides 20 engineering skills, verified the correct CLI command structure through local help, and successfully executed the installation sequence: marketplace add → plugin install → status verification. Plugin was installed with user scope and enabled status.
✅ Injection replay warning investigation 04:03:03.865 | claude_code User encountered warning about clean trajectory fallback during demo collection. Analysis revealed it’s a UX issue where operators don’t see injection animation when clean trajectory file is missing, but fallback to direct state load still produces correct post-injection environment. Data quality unaffected.
✅ Claude Code billing mode switch from Bedrock to subscription 18:48:21.122 | claude_code User wanted to switch Claude Code from Bedrock API billing to Claude.ai subscription mode. After initial generic advice, user clarified Bedrock-specific setup. Modified ~/.claude/settings.json to remove CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, AWS_REGION, AWS_PROFILE env vars and changed model from us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1 to claude-opus-4-6. Documented how to switch back.

title: Bug Journal 2026-04-16

content:
Daily Report — 2026-04-15 Daily Overview What was done: Complete environment setup, debugging, and reproducibility implementation for ErrorRecovery robotics project spanning conda environment creation, systematic bug resolution, and dependency tracking How it was done: Conda environment creation with dependency resolution, methodical 5-bug diagnosis and fixes (pip shadowing, missing patches, dependencies, exception handling, assets), upstream comparison and patch generation, automated setup scripting Impact: Transformed non-functional local environment into fully operational and reproducible system deployable on any machine including remote clusters; documented entire troubleshooting process for future reference DesktopLinux What was done: Created and debugged recovery_collect conda environment for Coffee task, resolving interconnected bugs including pip editable install shadowing, missing robosuite patches (z_rotation, get_bounding_box_half_size, total_size parameters), missing robomimic dependency, silenced exception handling, and missing texture assets How it was done: Installed base dependencies (robosuite 1.4.1, mujoco 2.3.2, mimicgen), fixed version conflicts, copied XML assets, monkey-patched missing APIs, then systematically diagnosed root causes using pip show/diff/grep/traceback analysis; applied sequential fixes: removed stale directories, reapplied robosuite_recovery.patch, installed robomimic –no-deps, improved error reporting, copied ceramic.png texture; created requirements.txt baseline via import scanning Impact: Coffee_D0 environment now fully functional and passes all validation checks; TUTORIAL.md section 4 documents complete troubleshooting process with verification scripts; established reproducible dependency baseline MacBook What was done: Resolved environment reproducibility blockers by tracking modified mimicgen library (545 files) and creating patch system for robosuite (13 modified files) How it was done: Compared local copies against upstream repositories (NVlabs/mimicgen, commit c848ca84 for robosuite), filtered CRLF/LF noise using –strip-trailing-cr, generated unified diff patches, updated setup_env.sh to auto-apply patches after upstream installation, committed mimicgen fork directly and pushed to GitHub Impact: Environment now fully reproducible for cluster deployment and collaboration; avoided 587MB robosuite commit via 326-line patch file; any machine can clone and set up working environment Systematically debugged and resolved ErrorRecovery robotics project environment setup, fixing 5 critical bugs blocking Coffee task execution, then established full reproducibility by tracking modified dependencies and creating automated patch system for cluster deployment
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Fix 5 critical environment bugs blocking Coffee task — Resolved pip editable install shadowing, missing robosuite recovery patches (z_rotation, get_bounding_box_half_size, total_size parameters), missing robomimic dependency, silenced exception handling, and missing ceramic.png texture ✅ Create robosuite patch system for reproducibility — Generated unified diff patch (326 lines) for 13 modified robosuite files instead of committing 587MB source tree, updated setup_env.sh to auto-apply patches after upstream installation ✅ Document troubleshooting process in TUTORIAL.md — Added section 4 to TUTORIAL.md with detailed bug summary table, step-by-step diagnosis and fixes, patch inventory, and environment verification script ✅ Track modified mimicgen library in Git repository — Added locally modified mimicgen fork (545 files, 13MB) to shared/mimicgen_workspace/mimicgen/, removed from .gitignore, committed and pushed to GitHub ✅ Create and debug recovery_collect conda environment — Created Python 3.10 conda environment, resolved mujoco version conflict (3.7.0→2.3.2), installed mimicgen and copied XML assets, identified need for patched robosuite from cluster Implementation & Fixes ✅ Analyze modifications against upstream repositories — Compared local mimicgen with NVlabs/mimicgen upstream and robosuite against commit c848ca84, documented compatibility fixes, new tasks, and MimicGen API additions ✅ Create requirements.txt baseline — Scanned 55 project files for imports, created structured requirements.txt with 9 core dependencies and special installation notes, audited actual conda environment packages Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Robosuite missing critical patches (z_rotation parameter, get_bounding_box_half_size, total_size, locations_relative_to_corner) required by MimicGen, causing environment creation failures Solution: Reapplied shared/mimicgen_workspace/robosuite_patches/robosuite_recovery.patch to installed robosuite package using patch -p1; verified all parameters exist in target class signatures
Key Insight: MimicGen requires patched robosuite with custom APIs not present in official releases; patched dependencies must be re-patched after pip install overwrites them; cluster uses local patched copies in shared/mimicgen_workspace/ rather than upstream versions
2. script_utils.py create_env function silently swallowed exceptions with ’except Exception: continue’, showing only generic ‘Could not create env’ error Solution: Modified exception handling to capture last_error and re-raise with original traceback context in RuntimeError, revealing the actual MuJoCo PNG loading error
Key Insight: Exception handling with bare ’except: continue’ or ’except Exception: pass’ makes debugging extremely difficult and should never be used in diagnostic/setup code; always capture the actual exception, log it, and include it in re-raised errors
3. Shared/ directory with critical dependencies was gitignored, making environment non-reproducible for other users or cluster deployment Solution: Tracked modified mimicgen directly in repo (13MB); created patch file system for robosuite to avoid 587MB commit; updated setup_env.sh to auto-apply patches after upstream installation
Key Insight: External dependencies with local modifications should be tracked in-repo (if small) or via patch files (if large) rather than blindly gitignored, especially for reproducible research environments; unified diff patches minimize repository size while preserving reproducibility
4. Pip editable install of mimicgen shadowed by residual site-packages/mimicgen/ directory from previous install, causing import failures Solution: Removed stale site-packages/mimicgen/ directory; pip editable install then correctly mapped to src/mimicgen
Key Insight: Editable pip installs on NTFS cross-filesystem mounts can leave residual site-packages directories that take precedence over editable path mappings; always verify import resolution matches pip show location by checking path and file attributes
5. 776 file differences between installed robosuite and upstream turned out to be mostly CRLF vs LF line ending noise, obscuring 13 real code changes Solution: Used diff –strip-trailing-cr to filter line ending differences, revealing only 13 files with actual code modifications
Key Insight: Cross-platform development creates false positives in file comparison due to line ending differences; always use –strip-trailing-cr or -w flags when comparing across Windows/Linux platforms
General Issues 6. Pip-installed mimicgen missing XML assets (coffee_pod.xml, coffee_body.xml) required by environment definitions Solution: Cloned mimicgen repo and manually copied assets/ directory to site-packages
Key Insight: Setup.py may not package all necessary data files; always verify critical non-Python assets are included in distribution
7. Missing robomimic dependency caused import failures in mimicgen config modules Solution: Installed robomimic with pip install robomimic –no-deps to bypass egl_probe build failure (EGL rendering not needed for SpaceMouse collection)
Key Insight: Use –no-deps to install packages when optional rendering dependencies fail to build but core functionality remains intact
8. Robosuite installation upgraded mujoco to 3.7.0, breaking API compatibility with robosuite 1.4.1 Solution: Force reinstalled mujoco 2.3.2 with –no-deps flag to prevent transitive upgrades
Key Insight: Use –no-deps when reinstalling pinned dependencies to prevent package managers from upgrading transitive dependencies
9. MuJoCo failed to load ceramic.png texture (PNG file load error) because shared/mimicgen_workspace/mimicgen/ lacked assets/textures/ directory present in src/mimicgen upstream Solution: Copied ceramic.png from src/mimicgen/mimicgen/models/robosuite/assets/textures/ to shared/mimicgen_workspace/mimicgen/mimicgen/models/robosuite/assets/textures/
Key Insight: When MuJoCo environments fail with asset loading errors, check both Python import paths and relative asset paths (meshes, textures) referenced in XML model definitions
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Root cause diagnosis for Coffee environment failure Role Approach Human Suspected MuJoCo version mismatch and asked if upgrading to latest MuJoCo 3.x would solve the problem AI Identified it was not a MuJoCo version issue but missing robosuite patches and texture files; explained upgrading to MuJoCo 3.x would break robosuite 1.4.1 API compatibility Difference Analysis: Human hypothesis focused on framework version mismatch; AI traced actual error through diagnostic commands to identify missing patches and assets; upgrading MuJoCo would have been counterproductive
Solving reproducibility problem approach Role Approach Human Immediately recognized shared/ directory not being tracked was a blocker and explicitly requested making it reproducible; pushed for concrete action (‘push to github’) AI Initially suggested three abstract options (add git URL, use submodule, or fork) without checking if local modifications existed Difference Analysis: Human had domain knowledge this was blocking deployment and correctly prioritized action over discussion; AI took academic approach of listing alternatives before diagnosis
Implementation Level Detecting actual vs false file differences Role Approach Human Asked to check if robosuite had ‘any modifications’, implying skepticism about whether real changes existed AI Initially saw 776 file differences and tried to categorize them, eventually discovering most were line-ending artifacts Difference Analysis: User’s question implied suspicion that large diff count might be artifacts; AI initially took diff count at face value before applying proper filtering
Installation strategy and incremental setup Role Approach Human Prioritized conda packages first, skipped system dependencies to minimize complexity and focus on Python environment AI Attempted comprehensive setup including system packages (libhidapi-dev, libglfw3-dev) all at once Difference Analysis: Human’s incremental approach enabled faster iteration and clearer diagnosis of environment issues; AI’s all-at-once strategy would have made debugging more complex
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Spent significant effort monkey-patching missing APIs before identifying that patched robosuite source was the actual requirement; did not recognize shared/mimicgen_workspace/ directory structure as indicator of custom patches until late in debugging Took many diagnostic commands before recognizing editable install was shadowed by residual directory; should have immediately checked site-packages when pip show and traceback paths diverged; did not immediately recognize path conflict as critical indicator Initially proposed three abstract solution options for mimicgen reproducibility without first checking whether local modifications existed or understanding their scope; wasted tool calls checking git history for never-tracked shared/ directory General Limitations Generated initial robosuite patch with absolute paths that needed regeneration with relative paths for portability Attempted upgrading to robosuite 1.5.2 without checking backward compatibility with mimicgen first Learnings Key Learnings MimicGen requires patched robosuite with custom APIs (get_bounding_box_half_size, total_size, locations_relative_to_corner, z_rotation) not present in official releases; version pinning in setup scripts may refer to local patched copies in shared/ directories rather than upstream commits Editable pip installs on NTFS cross-filesystem mounts can leave residual site-packages directories that shadow editable path mappings; always verify import resolution matches pip show location by checking both path and file attributes; patched dependencies must be re-patched after pip install overwrites them Exception handling with bare ’except: continue’ or ’except Exception: pass’ makes debugging extremely difficult and should never be used in diagnostic/setup code; always capture the actual exception, log it, and include it in re-raised errors for proper diagnosis External dependencies with local modifications should be tracked in-repo (if small) or via patch files (if large) rather than blindly gitignored; unified diff patches are the correct tool for tracking modifications to large upstream projects - they minimize repository size, document exact changes, and enable automated application Cross-platform line ending differences (CRLF vs LF) can create massive false positives in file comparisons; always use –strip-trailing-cr or -w flags when comparing across Windows/Linux platforms to reveal actual code changes When robosuite environments fail to load, check three layers: Python import paths (pip show, sys.path), robosuite registration (REGISTERED_ENVS), and MuJoCo asset file paths (textures, meshes, XML relative references) Pip packages may exclude data files (XML assets, configs); always verify critical non-Python files are packaged correctly in distribution; setup.py may miss necessary asset directories Practical Learnings Use –no-deps when reinstalling pinned dependencies or installing packages with optional rendering dependencies that fail to build but aren’t needed for core functionality Presence of .orig and .rej files indicates a patch was attempted, but empty diffs mean the patches may have already been successfully applied (artifacts weren’t cleaned up) Conversation Summaries ErrorRecovery ✅ Coffee environment setup debugging - 5 bug fix chain 04:44:49.213 | claude_code User updated patches and asked if Coffee task could now run. Systematically debugged 5 interconnected bugs blocking environment creation: pip editable install shadowed by residual site-packages directory, robosuite missing recovery patches (z_rotation parameter), missing robomimic dependency, create_env silently swallowing exceptions, and missing ceramic.png texture file. Applied fixes sequentially with verification at each step, added TUTORIAL.md section 4 documenting the entire troubleshooting process with diagnostic commands and verification scripts. Coffee_D0 environment now creates successfully and passes all validation checks.
✅ Fix environment reproducibility by tracking modified dependencies 03:26:06.618 | claude_code User identified that shared/ directory containing modified mimicgen and robosuite wasn’t tracked in Git, blocking cluster deployment. Compared local copies against upstream (NVlabs/mimicgen, robosuite commit c848ca84), filtered CRLF/LF noise, found essential modifications in both libraries (mimicgen: compatibility fixes and new Lift_Config; robosuite: 13 patched files adding MimicGen-required APIs). Solution: committed mimicgen directly (13MB), created 326-line patch file for robosuite (avoiding 587MB commit), and updated setup_env.sh to auto-apply patches. Successfully pushed to GitHub, enabling full environment reproducibility for cluster deployment.
🔄 Initial environment setup and dependency debugging 03:02:12.642 | claude_code Created recovery_collect conda environment and debugged multiple dependency issues. Fixed mujoco version conflict (3.7.0→2.3.2), installed mimicgen and copied missing XML assets, monkey-patched get_bounding_box_half_size into robosuite classes. Discovered root cause: pip-installed robosuite lacks patches from cluster’s shared/mimicgen_workspace/robosuite/. Initially blocked on syncing patched dependencies from cluster.
✅ Create requirements.txt baseline for project dependencies 02:48:44.541 | claude_code User requested requirements.txt update. Scanned all Python imports across 55 non-shared project files using grep-based pattern matching, identified 9 core third-party packages (numpy, scipy, mujoco, h5py, pyyaml, opencv-python, glfw, hidapi, tqdm, imageio, numba, matplotlib), and created structured requirements.txt with sections for core dependencies, rendering tools, utilities, and analysis packages. Ran pip list on recovery_collect conda environment to audit actual installed packages, identifying discrepancies like missing imageio-ffmpeg, pynput, and PyOpenGL in requirements.txt.
Unknown ❌ MacBook authentication failures (90+ sessions) 04:41:54.333 | claude_code Over 90 Claude Code sessions on MacBook showed identical ‘Not logged in · Please run /login’ errors with no actual work performed. These appear to be failed authentication attempts, empty connection tests, or auto-reconnect failures. No user interaction or task completion occurred in any of these sessions.
Token Usage Total: $17.71 — 23.6M tokens
summary: Systematically debugged and resolved ErrorRecovery robotics project environment setup, fixing 5 critical bugs blocking Coffee task execution, then established full reproducibility by tracking modified dependencies and creating automated patch system for cluster deployment
title: Bug Journal 2026-04-15

content:
Daily Report — 2026-04-14 Daily Overview What was done: Executed multi-project technical work across 4 devices: (1) MIHD spatial omics project - implemented 5 modules (HD dataset support, label mixing batch effect analysis, ARI stability testing, patch size experiments, neighborhood query integration), submitted 2 SLURM GPU jobs, launched 3 HD evaluation tasks; (2) Uploaded 18GB robotics learning checkpoint to remote server; (3) Resolved TokenMonitor git branch conflict (54 files, 16 commits divergence on each side); (4) Error recovery benchmark - built diagnostic analysis pipeline, refactored data collection workflow to round-robin mode (1360 demos target), generated 6 task success videos, analyzed error scene training set; (5) Configured independent Claude Code instance on tianhe server; (6) Fixed gadget rclone remote path configuration How it was done: Adopted systematic task decomposition strategies: ccplan skill-driven prioritization + parallel execution (MIHD), background parallel scp + progress monitoring (file upload), git history depth analysis + decision tree comparison (conflict resolution), MuJoCo EGL rendering on GPU node (video generation), CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR environment variable (configuration isolation), config file inspection + path correction (rclone fix). MIHD tasks implemented in 4 waves based on dependencies, prioritizing non-blocking tasks (P1-22/18/19/20), integrating existing infrastructure (P1-4). Git conflict resolved by identifying ‘independent identical refactoring’ nature through commit history comparison Impact: MIHD project unlocked key capabilities: HD dataset (17502 spots, 8μm resolution) RM-IDEAL evaluation pipeline, label mixing diagnostic tool (revealing Layer_4 accuracy only 19.3%), ARI stability quantification framework, prepared data foundation for paper Fig 4/5. TokenMonitor avoided catastrophic merge (54 file conflicts), successfully synced to v0.7.2 (remote superset version). 18GB checkpoint transfer completed robotics learning environment deployment prerequisite. Error recovery benchmark: new collection flow drastically improved UX (24→10 min/task estimate), delivered 6 deliverable videos, comprehensive understanding of error scene generation explaining why 249/1365 scenes appear similar to initial states. Independent Claude Code environment supports multi-device/multi-project isolated configuration. Restored gadget multi-device log synchronization capability DCC What was done: MIHD spatial omics benchmark project: implemented 5 code modules (HD RM-IDEAL data loading, label mixing batch effect analysis, ARI multi-run stability testing, HD intermediate patch size experiments, neighborhood query integration), submitted 2 SLURM GPU jobs, launched 3 HD evaluation tasks, updated plans.md and verification checklist, analyzed current blocking progress (3 GPU jobs running/pending) How it was done: Adopted ccplan skill systematic workflow: (1) mapped 13 meeting tasks to plans.md to identify missing items, (2) created 5 TaskCreate and executed by dependency DAG, (3) each task included new script writing + SLURM job configuration + documentation update. Key technical decisions: P1-22 used Hungarian matching to solve label alignment, P1-18 generated confusion matrix heatmap, P1-19 loaded embeddings from cache to accelerate repeated experiments, P1-20 designed 4 patch size comparison experiments, P1-4 integrated existing neighborhood_aggregate() function. Launched 3 CPU-intensive tasks in parallel (HD RM-IDEAL with different embedding sources) Impact: Unlocked HD dataset (Visium HD 17502 spots) complete evaluation capability: –dataset HD support + STHD annotation loading (100% match) + single section within-sample mode. Label mixing analysis revealed key patterns (Layer_4 worst 19.3%, outer Layer_1/WM best 83-100%, middle layers severely split). ARI stability framework can quantify seed dependency (single test showed 0.363 vs original 0.46, ~0.1 fluctuation). Prepared data pipeline for P1-5 multi-method query comparison and Fig 4/5 paper charts. 2 SLURM jobs (45484777/45484778) submitted awaiting GPU resources MacBook What was done: Completed two independent engineering tasks: (1) uploaded two large tar.gz files (libero_10_gr00t 14GB + qcvla_checkpoints 4GB) to athena.egr.duke.edu server, (2) handled TokenMonitor project git pull conflict (local main and origin/main each had 16 different commits, 54 file conflicts). Also completed error recovery project’s two core functions: analysis pipeline prototype (3 modules), refactored data collection workflow (round-robin + 10 per subtype + three-camera interface) How it was done: File upload: parallel scp two files to athena:~/, monitored progress by repeatedly ssh querying remote file size (qcvla 4GB completed first, libero 14GB from 0→7.6GB→11GB→complete). Git conflict: (1) git status identified divergence, (2) git log compared local/remote commits, (3) git merge –no-commit probed conflict scope, (4) git diff –name-only counted 54 conflicting files, (5) analyzed changes on both sides (both did modular refactoring + cross-platform), (6) judged remote v0.7.2 is superset of local v0.6.0, (7) git reset –hard origin/main to use remote as source of truth.
