content:
Tianhe What was done: Resolved git proxy issues and synchronized 42 files from the gadget repository. Reconstructed the augmentation system architecture for MimicGen. Repackaged the portable collection. How it was done: Diagnosed proxy configuration mismatches using per-command overrides. Created ErrorSceneVariantGenerator to implement reset-based variants. Introduced augmentable-only quota rules. Completed metadata supplementation for MimicGen. Ran pytest unit tests. Impact: Synchronized latest translation support in the cluster development environment. Aligned the augmentation process with the original paper design. Fixed bugs in success_demos.hdf5 (5/5 verification passed). Exposed human demo data quality issues (24 issues, only 3 reproducible, 0 augmentable). tianhe What was done: Completed BetterSSH full-stack project from scratch to production readiness. Standardized workspace permissions configuration. Implemented TokenMonitor using a data archiving system. How it was done: Used the superpowers skill chain (brainstorming needs analysis → ECL-driven implementation → code optimization). Implemented independent modules with 5 subagents in parallel. Simplified permission rules with tool-level wildcards. Implemented time-bound partitioning for deduplication archiving. Impact: Developed a production-ready SSH terminal multiplexer (Node.js+Fastify+React+xterm.js, 195 tests passed). Reduced 13 configuration files from 100 lines to 30 lines. Established an usage-archive layer to prevent data loss. Fixed pricing cache version control bugs. tianhe What was done: Fixed git proxy issues and synchronized 42 files from the gadget repository. Reconstructed the augmentation system architecture for MimicGen. Repackaged the portable collection. How it was done: Diagnosed proxy configuration mismatches using per-command overrides. Created ErrorSceneVariantGenerator to implement reset-based variants. Introduced augmentable-only quota rules. Completed metadata supplementation for MimicGen. Ran pytest unit tests. Impact: Synchronized latest translation support in the cluster development environment. Aligned the augmentation process with the original paper design. Fixed bugs in success_demos.hdf5 (5/5 verification passed). Exposed human demo data quality issues (24 issues, only 3 reproducible, 0 augmentable). tianhe What was done: Fixed git proxy issues and synchronized 42 files from the gadget repository. Reconstructed the augmentation system architecture for MimicGen. Repackaged the portable collection. How it was done: Diagnosed proxy configuration mismatches using per-command overrides. Created ErrorSceneVariantGenerator to implement reset-based variants. Introduced augmentable-only quota rules. Completed metadata supplementation for MimicGen. Ran pytest unit tests. Impact: Synchronized latest translation support in the cluster development environment. Aligned the augmentation process with the original paper design. Fixed bugs in success_demos.hdf5 (5/5 verification passed). Exposed human demo data quality issues (24 issues, only 3 reproducible, 0 augmentable).Solution: The diagnostic proxy configuration is inconsistent: the global Git configuration points to 172.16.31.200:3138, while the environment variable (from proxy_on) uses 127.0.0.1:10087. Using the git -c http.proxy=http://127.0.0.1:10087 -c https.proxy=http://127.0.0.1:10087 command covers each proxy setting, successfully pulling data. Key Insight: Git has two independent proxy configuration layers (global configuration vs environment variable) that may conflict. Using the -c flag for per-command overrides provides an immediate solution without modifying the persistent configuration.
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level MimicGen Augmentation Strategy – Repeated error injection vs Fixed error scene + Reset randomization Role Approach Human Humans identify the fundamental problem with the current logic: re-injecting errors into each target scene for diversity, which does not conform to the original MimicGen design. An error scene should be treated as a fixed base scenario, and variants should be generated through env.reset() with random objects. AI AI initially only examines the code implementation details (_replay_with_mimicgen_targets) and does not question the design from the ‘augmentation strategy’ perspective. It takes humans to point out the problem before AI understands that the original MimicGen’s reset logic should be applied to the error scene. Difference Analysis: Humans consider ‘what constitutes reasonable augmentation’ from a system design perspective, while AI tends to accept the existing implementation and optimize within its framework. This reflects a difference between strategic and tactical levels of thinking.
Whether to Research Existing Solutions Role Approach Human After completing the design presentation, humans actively suggest ‘I need you to search online for existing solutions’, avoiding duplicating efforts. AI AI initially prepares to proceed directly to the implementation phase (writing design documents → assigning writing plans), without suggesting research. Difference Analysis: Humans demonstrate pragmatic engineering thinking (reusing is better than rewriting), while AI tends to fall into the habit of ‘building from scratch’ when not prompted. This is one of AI’s blind spots.
Clarification of NeurIPS Writing Skill Scope Role Approach Human After initial exploration, users clarify: ‘I want to refine the writing methodology—how they complete their work and how they present highlights. I hope to output a nuwa-skill format to help me write papers for the NeurIPS 2026 dataset track.’ They specifically request focus on Oral/Highlight papers (not all 544 papers). AI AI initially interprets the request as refining the paper’s theme/topic. After clarification, it shifts to analyzing the writing structure (chapter organization), narrative strategy (narrative tension), review psychology, and contribution presentation patterns. Difference Analysis: Users have a clear meta-cognitive goal (learning the writing process itself), while AI initially focuses on domain knowledge. The nuwa-skill reference guides the abstraction at the correct level—extracting cognitive frameworks rather than mere facts summaries. This highlights the importance of example-driven specifications for complex creative tasks.
Local Session Support (Not Initially Considered) Role Approach Human During the design process, humans realize the need for a local session in addition to remote SSH. AI AI initially designed only to cover remote SSH sessions, but expanded it to include local sessions after human suggestion. Difference Analysis: Humans discover an implicit requirement during the design process (local session), while AI fails to infer the need for a local scenario due to its reluctance to switch windows. AI needs stronger ability to infer requirements.
Simplification Strategy for Permission Configuration Role Approach Human Users recognize that frequent confirmation is a major friction point in exploratory projects and propose retaining only the confirmation requirement for deletion operations. AI AI provides a technical solution: using tool-level wildcards like “Bash” to replace individual enumeration, changing the deletion command from “deny” to “ask”. Difference Analysis: Users define their requirements and boundaries (exploratory projects vs production projects, deletion operations require confirmation). AI provides technical solutions and optimization suggestions (using tool-level wildcards).
Decision on Archive Scope Role Approach Human Choosing ’local + SSH remote devices’, although SSH already has a remote-cache mechanism, prioritizing additional security with a unified archive layer. AI Recommending ‘only local’ to avoid duplication, as SSH already has a remote-cache mechanism. Difference Analysis: Humans prioritize redundancy and consistency (all data in a single archive system) over technical efficiency (avoiding duplicate mechanisms).
Judgment on Terminal UX Quality Role Approach Human Intuitive evaluation based on intuition: ‘It doesn’t look like a terminal at all, something is wrong’—using real terminals for immediate pattern recognition. AI Technical checklist: identifying missing WebGL renderers, Unicode11, appropriate themes, resize synchronization—the system diagnoses the root cause. Difference Analysis: Humans are good at overall UX evaluation, while AI excels at breaking down technical gaps. Humans identify problems immediately, while AI provides diagnosis and solutions.
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Failure to Propose Technical Research: AI is ready to implement the design but does not suggest researching existing solutions first. Engineers lack the awareness that ‘reusing is better than rewriting’. Inadequate Inferring Implicit Requirements: When users say ‘unwilling to switch windows’, AI only focuses on remote SSH scenarios and fails to infer that local CLI also requires unified management. Stronger scenario completeness reasoning is needed. It took 6 rounds of diagnostics to locate all MimicGen bugs: subtask fragmentation → retract not warp → gripper detects errors → insufficient interpolation steps → parallel path bugs → cache cleanup. AI cannot anticipate all issues at once. First attempt to access the OpenReview API failed, unaware of recent authentication policy changes. All 30+ attempts (API v1/v2, different endpoints, guest login, various headers) returned a 403, until the user provided credentials. The system lacks the latest knowledge of OpenReview authentication requirements for 2026. Failed to detect that SessionManager was never instantiated during the initial architecture scan—it took a lot of time to debug port conflicts and WebSocket issues before the root cause was identified as an empty onMessage handler. AI initially fails to question the rationality of ‘repeated error injection’ design. Humans need to explicitly state ’error scenes should be fixed + reset randomization’ to start refactoring. Incorrect Path Assumption: The Agent initially assumed files were edited under recovery_collection_portable/, but the user had moved to another level. All edits were lost and need to be reapplied in the new path. Not initially recognizing that outdated pricing caching could prevent OpenRouter data loading—it took the litellm.rs cache logic to explicitly trace the version control gap. General Limitations Triggering OpenReview nginx rate limiting, resulting in temporary IP ban (152.3.43.42) after approximately 30 consecutive requests during the diagnostic phase. Exponential backoff or diagnostic authentication requirements should be implemented earlier. Subagent execution process is not transparent: When 5 backend agents run in parallel, users cannot see the intermediate progress (only ‘waiting for completion notification’), lacking real-time visibility. Progress tracking is a UX pain point for large tasks. Learnings Key Learnings The power of the Superpowers skill chain: Brainstorming (requirement analysis) → ccplan (ECL-driven implementation) → optimize (quality improvement) forms a complete software lifecycle. ECL documents serve as cross-session persistent ‘design contracts’, ensuring context consistency across multiple dialogues, agents, and even different models. The value of adversarial review: Among the 9 issues discovered by the Critic agent, 2 are CRITICAL (no authenticated PTY writing, SSH parameter injection) and 4 are HIGH (token brute-force cracking, prototype contamination), exposing them in a production environment would cause a security disaster. Repairs in the requirement phase cost less than in the code phase and production phase. The essence of object-centric warping: EEF relative to object motion patterns is an invariant. This invariant is maintained through coordinate transformation T_eef_new = T_obj_target × T_obj_src⁻¹ × T_eef_source. Two-step understanding: (1) eliminating the absolute position of the source object (2) remapping with the new object’s position. The wisdom behind the original MimicGen design: (1) minimizing the number of subtasks (only 2) (2) having a post-grasp buffer of 10-20 frames to ensure stable capture (3) reducing pose gaps with env.reset() to home position (4) quasi-closed-loop execution (calculating delta for target_pose_to_action step by step). The core design of MimicGen is source demo + env.reset() randomization: Error scenes should be treated as fixed ‘initial states’, and variants should be generated using placement_initializer, rather than injecting errors into each target again. Priority of NeurIPS DB Track reviews (based on 61 reviews): The most valued qualities are ‘comprehensive’ (mentioned 17 times), ‘community’, ’novel’, ‘clear’, and ‘well-designed’ (mentioned 13 times). The most common criticisms are ’lack of comparison’ (insufficient baseline), ‘insufficient detail’ (inadequate methodology), and ’limited scope’ (restricted application). The best paper (Artificial Hivemind) has an average score of 4.75 among Oral papers—AC decides beyond the review scores. The average for Oral (5.00) < Spotlight average (5.16), confirming that scoring is not the only acceptance factor. Parallel agent scheduling mode: Independent modules (Config/Parser/LocalPTY) are assigned to different subagents for parallel implementation, then combined for verification, increasing development efficiency by 3-5 times. The key is identifying nodes in the DAG that are not dependent on each other, maximizing parallelism. Claude Code –output-format stream-json: Through research, it was found that Claude natively supports structured output (JSON separated by line breaks), eliminating the need for regular expression parsing of PTYs. This improves the reliability of the Parser from ‘heuristic’ to ‘deterministic’. Always research before reinventing. Return on Investment for TypeScript strict mode: 195 tests + 0 type errors = production-ready code. The type system is particularly useful during refactoring (after extracting parsed-state-utils, all call points automatically get type checked). Spending 20% more time on typing in the early stages saves 80% of debugging time later. xterm.js is the industry-standard browser terminal (used by VS Code, Replit, Vercel, ttyd). Its quality depends on the complete plugin stack: WebGL for GPU rendering, Unicode11 for CJK, Fit for resizing, SearchAddon, WebLinksAddon. Basic xterm.js alone is not sufficient for production use. MimicGen-ready datasets require specific metadata structure: Each demo_*/datagen_info must contain env_interface_name and env_interface_type, otherwise DataGenerator cannot correctly initialize the environment. Human demonstration data quality verification should be done before collection: Simply achieving teleop success is not enough; replay stability and augmentability must also be verified. Otherwise, a large amount of data will be collected that cannot be used for training. Time boundary partitioning (archive covers completed hours, live source covers current hours) elegantly solves deduplication without explicit level tracking—zero overlap means zero duplicate counts. Claude Code’s permission system supports tool-level wildcards (such as “Bash” matching all Bash commands), allowing minimal configuration instead of dozens of individual rules. Cost of Subtask Fragmentation: Each subtask boundary involves independently checking object poses, re-warp, and 5-step interpolation, with errors accumulating. 6 subtasks = 5 error sources, far more than the original 1. OpenReview API authentication architecture (2026): All data endpoints require login with a token; only the /login endpoint allows anonymous access (returning a verification error). The web frontend uses server-side credentials through Next.js RSC. Rate limits are operated at the nginx layer (based on IP), triggering approximately 30 requests in a short period. The academic paper review API is increasingly locked down, requiring institutional or personal credentials. Terminal resize synchronization requires full-chain coordination: ResizeObserver (frontend) → term.onResize → WebSocket message → SessionManager → PTY.resize(). Mismatches result in lines being broken in the error column. Anti-drag prevents flooding when resizing. Terminal instances should not be destroyed during session switching—they save a rollback buffer (10000 lines). The correct approach is to create once and reuse it through clear() + backend RingBuffer (2MB) across sessions. Cache systems must include explicit version fields when data sources can evolve—just TTL expiration is not enough to handle changes in patterns/sources. The three-layer permission configuration allow/deny/ask can balance security and efficiency: Allow implements automation, ask retains confirmation for dangerous operations, deny is for absolute prohibitions. Claude Code configuration does not inherit automatically (each project requires independent configuration), but can be standardized through automated scripts (such as init-claude.sh) for standardized initialization processes. Documentation for complex robot systems should distinguish between developers and operators: Clearly define the target audience, structure, language, and content retention through structured questions to avoid ambiguity. React closure trap ref pattern: Registers third-party library callbacks (xterm.js onData) in useEffect, which captures expired props, a common bug. Common solution: props → ref, useEffect synchronizes ref.current, and the callback reads ref.current. This is the best practice in the React ecosystem. Three key elements for WebSocket robustness: Redundancy (exponential backoff), backpressure (checking bufferedAmount), and heartbeat (not implemented but should be considered). Distributed system network layers must assume ‘connections may disconnect’ rather than ‘connections should be stable’. Importance of continuous closure detection: Robot grabs may attempt multiple times, and the first gripperClosed > threshold may fail. Continuous closure (≥10 frames) must be detected to confirm a successful grab. Complexity of multi-process debugging: (1) Each worker requires an independent env and augmenter instance (2) Path resolution is more prone to errors (relative paths may miss levels) (3) Absolute paths like demos_dir must be explicitly passed (4) Robosuite WARNINGs need to be suppressed separately in worker functions. Quota counting logic must reflect actual availability: augmentable_only is stricter than replay_only but more reasonable, as non-augmentable demos cannot be expanded even if they can be replayed. State of the art ASR models (2026): True streaming models are rare. Moonshine v2 (73-107ms latency, 34-245M parameters) and Voxtral-Mini-4B-Realtime (480ms, 4B parameters) represent the most advanced. Moonshine uses a callback-based API suitable for real-time transcription; Voxtral requires manual chunking but offers higher accuracy. Both support native acceleration for Apple Silicon (Moonshine uses CoreML, Voxtral uses MLX). Permission requirements differ for exploratory and production projects: Exploratory projects prioritize efficiency (lenient permissions), while production projects prioritize security (strict confirmation). Gradually accumulating configurations can lead to inconsistencies (e.g., 80+ long rules, different project styles), and regularly using templates can improve maintainability. Practical Learnings Git proxy configuration operates at two independent levels: global configuration (git config –global http.proxy) and environment variables (http_proxy, HTTP_PROXY). Inconsistent configurations lead to cryptic TLS errors; using the -c flag for per-command overrides provides an immediate solution while preserving existing configurations.✅ Building NeurIPS 2025 DB Track Paper Writing Skills (nuwa-skill Format)

title: Bug Journal 2026-04-11

content:
Daily Report — 2026-04-10 Daily Overview What was done: Completed strategic planning work (MIHD paper framework, 2246-line blueprint), resolved critical infrastructure issues (Screenpipe version compatibility, LIBERO data conversion bugs, NaN training loss), implemented production features (OpenClaw Sprint 2+3 with 22 code review fixes), and optimized monitoring tools (TokenMonitor pricing unification, OpenRouter API integration) How it was done: Strategic approach: parallel agent orchestration for large-scale documentation and research; systematic debugging for data pipeline issues using root cause tracing; layered code review remediation (CRITICAL→HIGH→MEDIUM→LOW); reverse-engineering Claude Code internals for pricing accuracy; multi-source API integration with fallback hierarchy Impact: MIHD project advanced from concept to executable blueprint with complete reproducibility; MeetingHelper and LifeCopilot production-ready with comprehensive testing; LIBERO and battery training pipelines unblocked; TokenMonitor achieved cost calculation parity with Claude Code while expanding model coverage from 2500 to 3000+ DCC What was done: MIHD benchmark paper strategic planning: framework design (Nature Methods Analysis positioning, 4-task definition, label-free terminology) and complete blueprint documentation (367→2246 lines covering paper plan, architecture, API specs) How it was done: Multi-round strategic dialogue to anchor core thesis (frozen FM + self-supervised fusion paradigm evaluation) → wrote plans.md → used /ccplan skill + 3 parallel agents (Paper Plan, Repository Blueprint, Module Specification) for detailed expansion Impact: Established complete technical and narrative foundation for MIHD paper, resolving positioning ambiguity and creating 1:1 reproducible implementation guide MacBook What was done: MeetingHelper deployment and troubleshooting: fixed Screenpipe 0.2.13 model loading failure (Protobuf error), installed gadget skills, synced error_recovery data from tianhe (196MB), optimized journal club markdown format How it was done: Diagnosed Homebrew Screenpipe deprecated status → uninstalled and switched to npx; SCP download from tianhe; markdown structure cleanup Impact: Resolved Screenpipe compatibility blocker, established reliable meeting recording environment, completed cross-cluster data sync TzJsDesktop What was done: Three major projects: (1) journal club research on 3 spatial transcriptomics papers, (2) OpenClaw Sprint 2+3 implementation with comprehensive code review remediation (22 issues), (3) TokenMonitor optimization (7 bugs), CI fixes, v0.7.2 release, and pricing system unification with OpenRouter API integration How it was done: Parallel agent research for paper analysis; systematic code review→fix workflow with incremental validation layers; git repository initialization for meeting-helper; reverse-engineered Claude Code /cost implementation; multi-source pricing fetch with LiteLLM-priority merge Impact: Journal club material ready with comparative analysis; LifeCopilot reached production quality with 70/70 tests passing and zero technical debt; meeting-helper open-sourced to GitHub; TokenMonitor costs now match Claude Code exactly with global model coverage (Chinese + Western ecosystems) tianhe What was done: Data pipeline debugging and training setup: (1) fixed LIBERO→LeRobot conversion observation.dones type error, (2) verified WHU classification dataset unlabeled pixel handling, (3) installed 113 academic writing skills, (4) prepared battery dataset with uniform temporal sampling, (5) debugged NaN training loss, (6) implemented comprehensive training logging How it was done: Deep analysis of LeRobot 0.4.0 feature mapping mechanism to insert .item() conversion; confirmed ignore_index=-1 correctness across pipeline; configured network proxy for GitHub access; applied linear interpolation for uniform sampling; traced NaN propagation through StandardScaler to identify corrupt data; integrated Python logging module Impact: Unblocked LIBERO data conversion pipeline with correct type handling; validated training logic correctness; deployed complete academic writing toolchain; generated clean 221K-row battery dataset with persistent experiment tracking enabling reproducibility Multi-project development day: completed MIHD benchmark paper blueprint, fixed MeetingHelper Screenpipe compatibility, debugged LIBERO/battery training pipelines, implemented OpenClaw features with zero technical debt, and enhanced TokenMonitor pricing accuracy to match Claude Code
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ MIHD benchmark paper framework design and complete blueprint documentation — Defined paper positioning (Nature Methods Analysis, frozen FM + self-supervised fusion paradigm evaluation), established 4 core tasks, chose label-free terminology, and expanded plans.md from 367 to 2246 lines using parallel agent strategy covering paper plan, code architecture, module API reference, config schemas, data formats, and reproduction guide ✅ LIBERO→LeRobot observation.dones type error fix — Resolved LeRobot 0.4.0 type conflict where shape=(1,) feature mapped to scalar Value but validation required ndarray. Fixed by inserting .item() conversion in episode_buffer before save_episode() call ✅ Battery dataset uniform temporal sampling and NaN loss debugging — Generated uniform-sampled dataset (4 points per cycle via linear interpolation), identified corrupt data row (battery 31, cycle 833 with NaN) causing StandardScaler NaN propagation, removed corrupt row, produced clean 221K-row dataset ✅ OpenClaw Sprint 2+3 implementation with comprehensive code review remediation — Implemented data lifecycle management, session persistence, thought search, dreaming service, and circuit breaker. Fixed 22 code review issues (4 CRITICAL, 7 HIGH, 8 MEDIUM, 3 LOW) including race conditions, deprecated APIs, Pydantic v2 migration. All 70 tests passing ✅ TokenMonitor code optimization and pricing unification — Fixed 7 performance/type safety issues (redundant clones, unnecessary parsing). Unified cache tier pricing to match Claude Code (1.25x rate), added web search tracking ($0.01/request), integrated OpenRouter API for comprehensive model coverage (3000+ models including 92 Chinese), implemented 7-day auto-refresh ✅ MeetingHelper Screenpipe compatibility fix — Diagnosed and resolved Screenpipe 0.2.13 (Homebrew) model loading failure (Protobuf parsing error). Identified deprecated version status, uninstalled Homebrew version, cleaned model cache, switched to npx-based solution, and updated all documentation ✅ Journal club research on 3 spatial transcriptomics papers — Fetched and analyzed STAIG (Nature Commun), QueST (arXiv), and Loki (Nature Methods) using parallel agents. Generated comparative analysis report covering graph contrastive learning progression (node-level → subgraph-level → cross-modal) ✅ Academic writing skills installation (113 skills) — Installed AI-research-SKILLs (94), humanizer (1), and anthropics official skills (18) on tianhe cluster. Configured network proxy to resolve GitHub access issues, created comprehensive usage documentation ✅ meeting-helper GitHub launch — Applied 6 optimizations (MCP tools, Windows health-check script, bash reliability, placeholder URLs, .gitignore, mkdir instruction), initialized git repository, committed 12 files, pushed to TzJ2006/MeetingHelper, created 450-line comprehensive tutorial ✅ TokenMonitor v0.7.2 release — Fixed CI failures (clippy warnings, cargo fmt), merged feature branch, manually bumped version (release script failed on Windows), created tag, pushed to GitHub, updated PR #5 Implementation & Fixes ✅ Battery training logging system implementation — Integrated Python logging module to persistently record experiment parameters, per-epoch metrics (train/test loss, learning rate), early stopping info, and final test results to train.log files in checkpoint directories ✅ WHU classification dataset validation — Verified end-to-end handling of unlabeled pixels (-1) and invalid classes (17, 19): confirmed remap_labels unifies to -1, CrossEntropyLoss correctly ignores, genConfusionMatrix properly masks ✅ error_recovery data cross-cluster sync — Downloaded recovery_collection_portable.tar.gz (196MB) from tianhe to MacBook via SCP, replaced old version, extracted and noted structural changes ✅ gadget skills local installation — Copied 5 skills (ccplan, summarize, optimize, cchypothesis, slurm-gpu) from gadget/skills/ to ~/.claude/skills/ Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. LeRobot 0.4.0 save_episode error: ‘only 0-dimensional arrays can be converted to Python scalars’ on observation.dones field (shape=(1,) np.array) Solution: Root cause: LeRobot’s get_hf_features_from_features() maps shape=(1,) to scalar Value, but validate_frame() requires ndarray. Inserted loop before save_episode() to convert all dones arrays to Python int via .item()
Key Insight: When framework validation and encoding logic conflict, optimal insertion point is the buffer between them (episode_buffer), not modifying feature definitions or bypassing validation
2. Screenpipe 0.2.13 (Homebrew) startup failed with ‘Protobuf parsing failed’ error when loading wespeaker_en_voxceleb_CAM++.onnx model Solution: Identified Homebrew version as deprecated (does not build). Uninstalled via brew, deleted corrupted model cache (~/Library/Caches/screenpipe/models/), switched to npx screenpipe@latest or official desktop app, updated all documentation
Key Insight: Homebrew formula deprecated status is critical signal. When encountering low-level dependency issues, check upstream maintenance status first rather than debugging abandoned versions
3. Battery training loss immediately became NaN from first iteration despite data appearing normal Solution: Systematically traced pipeline: found cycle column had 4 NaN values from corrupt row (battery 31, cycle 833). StandardScaler computed NaN mean/std, causing (data - NaN) / NaN = NaN for all scaled values. Removed single corrupt row
Key Insight: NaN propagation through normalization: Single NaN in training data corrupts StandardScaler statistics (mean/std), causing all subsequent scaled values to become NaN. Always validate data cleanliness before normalization operations
4. TokenMonitor costs don’t match Claude Code /cost output Solution: Discovered TokenMonitor used 2.0x multiplier for 1h cache tokens while Claude Code uses unified 1.25x rate. Changed cache_write_1h pricing in pricing.rs to match Claude Code exactly
Key Insight: Claude Code’s /cost deliberately simplifies pricing (unified rate) despite API providing granular breakdown. UI simplicity can justify data aggregation — not every field needs exposure
5. CircuitBreaker HALF_OPEN race condition: state check outside lock allowed multiple probes to enter simultaneously Solution: Moved state check inside ‘async with self._lock’ block to ensure atomic state check + transition
Key Insight: Concurrent state machines must perform state check and transition atomically within same lock. Any lock-external check introduces race window
6. MIHD paper positioning unclear — initially framed as ‘fusion strategy benchmark’ with vague core contribution Solution: Repositioned as ‘frozen FM + self-supervised fusion paradigm evaluation’ answering ‘can this replace fine-tuned methods’. Changed narrative from method proposal to systematic boundary exploration
Key Insight: Benchmark papers evaluate paradigms, not methods. Core value is systematic boundary exploration, not SOTA claims. Precise problem definition drives correct narrative framing
7. LiteLLM pricing database missing major Chinese AI models (GLM, Qwen, DeepSeek) — systematic gap in Chinese AI ecosystem Solution: Integrated OpenRouter API (92 Chinese model entries) with concurrent fetch using LiteLLM-priority merge: OpenRouter provides base coverage, LiteLLM overwrites where available. Added 7-day TTL auto-refresh
Key Insight: Global AI pricing requires multi-source strategies with geographic diversity. Western databases systematically lack Chinese models; Chinese sources lack Western models. Redundancy is essential not optional
8. Initial uniform sampling used fixed 0-20 second range, producing invalid IR values (16, 15, 14…) instead of expected 0-0.024 range Solution: Changed strategy: interpolate within each cycle’s actual chargetime range (typically 10-13 seconds) rather than forcing into global fixed range. Avoided extrapolation beyond observed data points
Key Insight: Interpolation vs extrapolation: Forcing data into global range beyond observed bounds causes extrapolation with invalid values. Use per-sample adaptive ranges to stay within interpolation regime
9. ‘Zero-shot’ terminology easily misunderstood — STAIG fusion trains 550 epochs, reviewers would challenge if truly ‘zero-shot’ Solution: Switched to ’label-free / annotation-free’ emphasizing no manual annotation needed. Clarified ‘zero-shot’ refers to encoder level (frozen FM), while fusion is self-supervised using spatial coordinates as free signal
Key Insight: Precise terminology prevents reviewer confusion. Layered definition (frozen encoder + self-supervised fusion) avoids ambiguity and highlights practical value (no annotation cost)
10. tianhe cluster GitHub access failed: ‘Failed to connect to 172.16.31.200 port 3138: No route to host’ Solution: Diagnosed outdated proxy configuration (172.16.31.200). User provided ‘source /app/bin/proxy.sh’ command. Updated git config http/https.proxy to new proxy (99.72.0.200:3138), npx openskills install succeeded
Key Insight: HPC cluster network proxies change frequently. When network errors occur, verify proxy effectiveness (env | grep proxy, git config –list) rather than assuming configuration correctness
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level MIHD paper strategic repositioning Role Approach Human User directly stated ‘what I want to do is: now that FMs are so strong, can we use zero-shot FM to do what previously required fine-tuning’ — immediately anchoring core value proposition and 4 concrete tasks AI AI initially followed existing draft structure (‘fusion strategy benchmark’), focusing on technical details rather than strategic positioning. Adjusted after user feedback Difference Analysis: Human excels at high-level narrative and problem definition — user’s direct question revealed paper’s core thesis more clearly. AI initially over-focused on technical execution details rather than strategic framing, showing human advantage in top-level storytelling
Screenpipe issue resolution strategy Role Approach Human User directly asked if problem was lerobot version, then after multiple fix attempts, proactively requested uninstalling Homebrew version AI AI attempted multiple technical fixes (replacing model files, modifying features) before discovering Homebrew version deprecated status in brew info output Difference Analysis: User faster at recognizing problem may lie in upstream version rather than configuration. AI over-focused on technical repair within existing environment. Reflects human sensitivity to ecosystem maintenance status vs AI’s excessive technical detail focus
Code review fix prioritization strategy Role Approach Human Requested ‘fix all issues’ as single batch AI Adopted phased validation: CRITICAL→verify tests→HIGH→verify→MEDIUM→verify→LOW, reducing regression risk Difference Analysis: AI applied defensive layering to avoid cascading failures when fixing 22 issues across 11 files
Parallelization strategy for large document generation Role Approach Human When AI got stuck, user immediately suggested ‘break it into smaller parts and do them in parallel’ AI Attempted single-threaded completion, hesitated when assembling large 2246-line document Difference Analysis: Human faster at recognizing parallelization opportunities. AI knows parallel tools exist but tends toward sequential thinking in practice, requiring explicit user direction to leverage concurrency
Data structure requirement clarification for uniform sampling Role Approach Human User intuitively wanted ‘flatten’ into long sequence, implicitly understanding existing data loader works on simple row-wise sequential data without requiring code changes AI Initially proposed preserving 41-point temporal structure per cycle (3D: battery × cycle × chargetime) which would preserve more information but require modifying data loader architecture Difference Analysis: Human had implicit understanding of system constraints (data loader expects 2D tabular data). AI initially overlooked this constraint and proposed solution requiring extensive code changes. After explicit AskUserQuestion clarification, AI aligned with simpler, more pragmatic approach working with existing infrastructure
Prioritizing cost accuracy over feature completeness Role Approach Human User immediately asked to verify if costs match /cost command exactly, willing to skip UI improvements (chose ‘data pipeline only’ option) to focus on correctness first AI AI initially proposed comprehensive plan including UI display of cache tiers. Only adjusted to data-only scope after user explicitly selected that option Difference Analysis: Human exhibited strong ‘make it right before making it pretty’ instinct, while AI defaulted to feature-complete implementation. User’s judgment prevented scope creep
AI Limitations Critical Limitations LeRobot type problem resolution: conducted 5 different type conversion attempts (int → np.int64 → np.asarray(0-dim) → np.asarray(1-dim) → .item()), failed to directly locate contradiction by reading validate_frame and get_hf_features_from_features source on first analysis Insufficient sensitivity to strategic-level problem definition — initially followed existing MIHD draft structure without questioning ‘what is the core story’, required user’s direct statement ‘what I really want to do is…’ to re-anchor to correct narrative In Screenpipe issue, spent excessive time attempting model file replacement and type conversion fixes, failed to early-identify Homebrew formula deprecated status (despite appearing in brew info output), leading to multiple ineffective attempts Failed to proactively investigate whether 5m/1h cache tokens were both being used in real logs before proposing to expose them. Only checked actual usage patterns when user asked Initially misunderstood battery dataset structure requirement, proposing to keep 41 time points per cycle as high-dimensional input when user wanted simple row-wise flattening. Required explicit AskUserQuestion clarification to understand the constraint When researching pricing sources, didn’t immediately synthesize multi-source merge strategy. Needed explicit user instruction to design LiteLLM-priority fallback architecture General Limitations Lacks proactive parallelization thinking for large-scale tasks — attempted single-threaded approach for 2246-line document generation, only parallelized after explicit user suggestion Cannot discover cluster-specific operational conventions (like /app/bin/proxy.sh) autonomously, must rely on user providing this tacit knowledge Learnings Key Learnings LeRobot 0.4.0 shape=(1,) feature handling design contradiction: validate_frame requires ndarray but get_hf_features_from_features maps to scalar Value. Solution: insert type conversion in episode_buffer layer between validation and encoding StandardScaler NaN propagation mechanism: Single corrupt row with NaN in any feature column causes StandardScaler.fit() to compute NaN statistics (mean/std), propagating to all scaled data through (x - NaN) / NaN = NaN. Always validate data for NaN/Inf before normalization operations Benchmark papers evaluate paradigms, not methods — Nature Methods Analysis papers don’t need SOTA, but systematic answers to ‘how far can this methodology go’. Narrative shifts from ‘our method is best’ to ‘what are the boundaries and bottlenecks of this paradigm’ Homebrew formula deprecated status is critical signal — when encountering low-level dependency issues, check upstream maintenance status first rather than debugging abandoned versions Graph contrastive learning evolution in spatial transcriptomics: STAIG (node-level) → QueST (subgraph-level) → Loki (cross-modal), showing field progression from ‘one method per problem’ to ‘one foundation model for multiple tasks’ Code review layered remediation protocol: CRITICAL (security/races) → HIGH (deprecated APIs/private access) → MEDIUM (types/style) → LOW (linter), with test validation after each layer before proceeding to avoid cascading failures Asyncio state machine correctness invariant: state check + transition must be atomic within same lock; any lock-external check introduces race window Global AI pricing requires multi-source strategies with geographic diversity — Western databases (LiteLLM) systematically lack Chinese models; Chinese sources lack Western models. Redundancy essential not optional Rust performance patterns: avoid unnecessary clone(), use borrowing structs for serialization, prefer line counting over full deserialization when only metadata needed Terminology selection matters: label-free vs zero-shot — ‘zero-shot’ easily misunderstood as ’no training at all’, ’label-free / annotation-free’ accurately conveys core advantage of ’no manual annotation’. Layered definition (encoder zero-shot + fusion self-supervised) avoids ambiguity Parallel agent strategy dramatically accelerates large-scale documentation — split 2246-line document into 3 independent parts (Paper Plan, Repo Structure, Module Specs), each generated by separate agent then assembled. Also accelerates research: 4 agents completed in 14s vs 45-60s sequential Self-supervised learning’s ‘free signal’ framing — spatial transcriptomics coordinates are structured information inherent to Visium chips. Viewing spatial graphs as ‘free supervision signal’ clarifies label-free value and strengthens practical utility (no additional annotation cost) Interpolation vs extrapolation in temporal resampling: Linear interpolation should stay within observed data bounds. Forcing data into fixed global range causes extrapolation producing invalid values. Use per-sample adaptive ranges to avoid extrapolation artifacts Multi-stage data processing pipeline correctness requires end-to-end verification — special values (like -1) handling must be consistent across all stages (preprocessing → Dataset → Loss → evaluation) Claude Code’s /cost deliberately simplifies pricing presentation (unified 1.25x cache rate) despite API providing granular 5m/1h breakdown. UI simplicity can justify data aggregation — not every field needs exposure HPC cluster network proxies change frequently — establish standard check workflow: env | grep proxy + git config –list + test connection, rather than assuming configuration correctness Pydantic v2 migration pattern: dict → model_dump(), but test mocks must synchronously provide same interface via helper functions like _make_settings_mock() JSONL session logs don’t contain all billable attributes — Fast mode tier invisible in logs despite affecting actual costs, limiting what cost-tracking tools can reconstruct from historical data Practical Learnings Weekly pricing refresh (7-day TTL) with background checking strikes optimal balance for LLM cost tracking: frequent enough to catch new models and rate changes, infrequent enough to avoid API quota concerns Conversation Summaries MIHD ✅ Benchmark paper framework design + complete blueprint documentation 14:48:32.241 | claude_code User and AI clarified MIHD paper’s core positioning through multiple rounds: reframed from ‘fusion strategy benchmark’ to ‘frozen FM + self-supervised fusion paradigm evaluation replacing fine-tuned methods’. Key decisions: Nature Methods Analysis style, 4 core tasks (DLPFC/HD clustering, cross-sample query, downstream), ’label-free’ terminology, 3-level self-supervised definition. Subsequently expanded plans.md from 367 to 2246 lines using /ccplan skill with 3 parallel agents (Paper Plan, Repository Blueprint, Module Specification), covering paper strategy, code architecture, API signatures, config schemas, data formats. Full technical specification achieved for 1:1 reproducibility.
MeetingHelper ✅ Fix Screenpipe startup timeout + deployment 04:43:00.851 | claude_code MeetingHelper startup script timed out due to Screenpipe 0.3.278 CLI interface change requiring screenpipe record subcommand. AI modified start.sh, tested successfully. Later discovered Screenpipe v0.2.13 (Homebrew) has speaker diarization model issues (Protobuf parsing failed). Attempted multiple fixes, eventually identified Homebrew version as deprecated. Created npx-based startup script, updated all documentation, uninstalled Homebrew version, cleaned model cache. Committed 5 changes documenting issue and workarounds.
gadget ✅ Journal club research: 3 spatial transcriptomics papers 05:02:21.800 | claude_code User requested analysis of STAIG (Nature Commun), QueST (arXiv), and Loki (Nature Methods) papers. Launched 4 parallel agents to fetch content and explore research tools. Generated comprehensive journal club report covering spatial transcriptomics graph learning progression: node-level → subgraph-level → cross-modal. Minor bash path issue created wrong directory but actual report written correctly to outputs/reports/.
BOSS data conversion ✅ Fix LIBERO→LeRobot observation.dones type error 11:53:38.763 | claude_code User reported TypeError during conversion: ‘only 0-dimensional arrays can be converted to Python scalars’. AI diagnosed root cause: LeRobot 0.4.0 maps shape=(1,) features to scalar Value, but data passed np.array([x]). Tried 5 different type conversions, all failed validate_frame or HF encoding. Final solution: insert loop before save_episode() to convert all dones arrays to Python int via .item(). User expressed impatience during iterations but issue resolved.
chenlu academic writing skills ✅ Install 113 academic writing skills on tianhe 03:37:44.872 | claude_code User provided awesome-ai-research-writing repo URL and requested skills installation. Initial install failed due to proxy issues (172.16.31.200:3138 unreachable). User provided ‘source /app/bin/proxy.sh’ command. AI updated git proxy to 99.72.0.200:3138. Installed 3 sources via npx openskills: zechenzhangAGI/AI-research-SKILLs (94 skills), blader/humanizer (1), anthropics/skills (18). Total 113 skills. Created comprehensive usage guide covering research workflow, core skills (ml-paper-writing, academic-plotting, humanizer, doc-coauthoring), with prompt examples and troubleshooting.
Crossformer Battery IR Prediction ✅ Uniform temporal sampling + NaN loss debugging + logging 02:55:41.738 | claude_code User needed to train Crossformer on uniformly-sampled battery internal resistance data. AI generated uniform temporal sampling dataset using linear interpolation within each cycle’s actual chargetime range. Training immediately produced NaN loss; AI systematically traced root cause to single corrupt row (battery 31, cycle 833) with NaN cycle value poisoning StandardScaler statistics. After data fix, user requested comprehensive logging. AI implemented Python logging module in exp_crossformer.py to record experiment parameters, per-epoch metrics, and test results to persistent train.log files.
LifeCopilot ✅ OpenClaw Sprint 2+3 implementation + code review remediation 00:19:42.904 | claude_code Implemented Sprint 2 (data lifecycle, session persistence) and Sprint 3 (thought search, dreaming service, circuit breaker). Fixed timing-sensitive test failures with before-action-after sandwich pattern. python-reviewer agent identified 22 issues: 4 CRITICAL (CircuitBreaker race, private API, ISO time comparison, silent exception), 7 HIGH (deprecated get_event_loop, private methods, Pydantic v2), 8 MEDIUM (type annotations, fuzzy matching), 3 LOW (ruff). Systematically fixed all using layered approach CRITICAL→HIGH→MEDIUM→LOW with test validation after each. Final result: ruff 0 errors, 70/70 tests pass.
meeting-helper ✅ Optimize + GitHub launch + tutorial writing 00:56:22.836 | claude_code Analyzed project, identified 6 optimizations (MCP speaker tools missing, Windows health-check script, bash $? reliability, placeholder URLs, .gitignore, mkdir). User invoked /ccplan but this was direct fix task. Applied all fixes, initialized git → committed 12 files → pushed to TzJ2006/MeetingHelper successfully. Subsequently generated 450-line comprehensive tutorial covering 15 chapters from installation to advanced usage.
TokenMonitor ✅ Unify cache pricing with Claude Code + integrate OpenRouter API 00:36:12.391 | claude_code User requested research into Claude Code’s /cost implementation. AI discovered it uses local calculation from API usage fields, found TokenMonitor’s 1h cache tier used 2.0x multiplier vs Claude Code’s 1.25x unified rate. Implemented data pipeline to expose cache tier breakdown, unified pricing to 1.25x to match /cost exactly, added web search tracking at $0.01/request. User then asked for authoritative pricing sources noting LiteLLM lacks Chinese models. AI researched and recommended OpenRouter (350 models, 92 Chinese). User directed LiteLLM-priority merge strategy with 7-day auto-refresh. AI implemented openrouter.rs module, modified litellm.rs for concurrent fetch with merge, added background TTL checking. Pricing now covers global AI ecosystem (3000+ models). Created PR #5 with 12-file changeset.
✅ Code optimization + CI fixes + v0.7.2 release 05:22:50.725 | claude_code User triggered /optimize skill. AI identified 7 issues including TypeScript type mismatches (fileCount→entryCount), redundant HashMap clones, unnecessary JSON parsing, missing fallbacks. Fixed all in parallel. Merged feat/cache-tier-web-search-pricing to main (resolved Windows filemode conflicts with core.fileMode=false). Fixed CI clippy warnings (unused variables) and cargo fmt failures. Release script failed on Windows; manually bumped version to 0.7.2, committed, tagged, pushed to GitHub, updated PR #5.
WHU classification dataset ✅ Validate unlabeled pixel handling in training 07:36:33.776 | claude_code User opened process_WHU.py asking if unlabeled regions (-1) would incorrectly participate in training. AI traced full pipeline: (1) process_WHU.py only drops patches entirely consisting of {-1,17,19}; (2) WHUDataset.remap_labels maps -1/17/19 → -1; (3) CrossEntropyLoss(ignore_index=-1) correctly ignores; (4) training OA calculation includes -1 pixels (cosmetic issue only). User asked about testing—AI verified genConfusionMatrix filters -1 via mask, all correct. User declined fix for OA calculation.
Token Usage Total: $103.74 — 106.5M tokens
summary: Multi-project development day: completed MIHD benchmark paper blueprint, fixed MeetingHelper Screenpipe compatibility, debugged LIBERO/battery training pipelines, implemented OpenClaw features with zero technical debt, and enhanced TokenMonitor pricing accuracy to match Claude Code
title: Bug Journal 2026-04-10

content:
Daily Report — 2026-04-09 Daily Overview What was done: Parallel execution across research computing (robot learning data pipelines), infrastructure maintenance (proxy/permissions/multi-instance isolation), content pipeline transformation (gadget bilingual architecture), and two major greenfield projects (LifeCopilot architecture upgrade + meeting-helper creation) plus TokenMonitor enhancements How it was done: tianhe: HDF5 validation, Python script implementation, Node environment variable normalization. TzJsDesktop: Systematic multi-phase refactoring with parallel agent validation, ccplan-driven architecture planning (2 complete Phase 0-11 workflows), TDD approach with comprehensive testing, Win32 API window manipulation, timezone-aware datetime conversion Impact: Established human collection fallback for 626 MimicGen failures; enabled flexible multi-dataset contrastive learning; achieved universal Node CLI proxy solution; transformed gadget to English-first bilingual pipeline; delivered production-grade health monitoring + auto-recovery for LifeCopilot (10 new modules); created deployable meeting assistant with Screenpipe integration; eliminated timezone bugs and UI flicker in TokenMonitor tianhe What was done: Completed MimicGen result analysis, human collection workflow implementation, BOSS multi-dataset refactoring, and Node.js proxy configuration fix How it was done: Direct HDF5 file validation with h5py, wrote three Python teleoperation collection scripts, modified three training pipeline files for multi-source augmentation, normalized shell proxy environment variables (uppercase/lowercase sync + NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY=1) Impact: Delivered complete human collection workflow for 626 failed scenes; enabled single+multiple dual-dataset flexible sampling; established universal solution for all Node CLI tools requiring external API access through corporate proxy TzJsDesktop What was done: All sessions on this device (11 total): gadget English conversion (4-phase with parallel validation), GitHub workspace permission unification, Claude Code multi-instance isolation setup, 2 complete ccplan workflows (LifeCopilot OpenClaw integration + meeting-helper creation), gadget documentation updates, TokenMonitor debugging (3 sessions: Tauri CLI + port + timezone + animations) How it was done: Four-phase refactoring with parallel agent validation discovering 6 residual issue categories; ccplan-driven structured planning (research→adversarial review→feasibility→implementation→testing); extensive use of Agent tool for parallel codebase research; TDD with 70 Rust + 219 frontend tests; Win32 API hide/show workaround for WebView2 async rendering; CSS transition timing analysis Impact: Transformed bilingual workflow (better international reach); eliminated ~200 permission prompts; enabled simultaneous GLM/Claude usage; LifeCopilot gained 10 new modules (health_manager, config_watcher, data_lifecycle, dreaming_service, circuit_breaker, etc.) with 70 passing tests; meeting-helper delivered end-to-end (config, Pipe, MCP, install scripts, docs); TokenMonitor timezone feature eliminates date boundary bugs for cross-timezone SSH servers; animation fixes improve UI polish Cross-device full-stack development day: optimized robot learning pipelines on tianhe (MimicGen analysis + human collection workflow + multi-dataset contrastive learning), resolved infrastructure issues (Node proxy, Claude Code permissions), executed major gadget refactoring (Chinese→English output with Ollama translation), completed two large architecture upgrades (LifeCopilot OpenClaw integration with 70 tests, meeting-helper from scratch via Screenpipe+MCP), and enhanced TokenMonitor with timezone-aware SSH handling plus UI animation fixes
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Refactor gadget summarize module to English output — Converted 4 core files (summarizer.py, formatter.py, weekly_summary.py, monthly_summary.py) from Chinese LLM prompts and Markdown rendering to English. All SUMMARY_PROMPT/CHUNK_MERGE_PROMPT/WEEKLY_SUMMARY_PROMPT/MONTHLY_SUMMARY_PROMPT templates now request English output ✅ Refactor gadget research module to English output — Converted 8 files across scout and profiler subsystems: DEFAULT_LANGUAGE=‘en’, all prompts to English, Markdown rendering labels to English, added label_en property to ResearcherTier enum ✅ LifeCopilot OpenClaw core enhancements integration — Integrated 7 features via ccplan workflow: service health monitoring + auto-restart (health_manager.py with 4-state FSM), CronScheduler concurrency control + replay (per-job lock + global Semaphore), config hot reload (config_watcher.py with watchfiles), JSONL lifecycle management (data_lifecycle.py), session history persistence (SessionManager refactor), thought search + dream consolidation (DreamingService + InsightStore), circuit breaker (closed→open→half-open). Added 10 new module files, modified 15 files, 70 tests passing ✅ meeting-helper AI meeting assistant creation — Created full meeting assistant via ccplan Phase 0-11. Researched existing solutions (Zoom AI, Screenpipe, Meetily), selected Screenpipe+MCP+Claude Code CLI architecture. Implemented 5 features: Screenpipe config (Whisper large-v3-turbo + bilingual + custom vocabulary), custom meeting summary Pipe, MCP setup script, CLAUDE.md Q&A system prompt (27 MCP tools guide), cross-platform install scripts (macOS/Windows). Includes complete ECL planning documentation ✅ Implement human collection workflow scripts — Created three scripts for manual success demo collection: identify_failed_scenes.py (parse logs, output 626 scene IDs), collect_success_from_error.py (SpaceMouse teleoperation with progress tracking), convert_teleop_to_hdf5.py (NPZ→HDF5 conversion and merging) ✅ Refactor PairedDataset for multi-aug-dataset support — Modified build_paired_mapping.py (multi-source input with source tags), paired_dataset.py (aug_datasets list, source-based selection), config.py (aug_repo_ids/aug_sources lists) to support simultaneous sampling from single and multiple OSS augmentation datasets ✅ Fix Node.js proxy for CLI tools — Fixed npx ccusage and all Node CLI tools failing to fetch through corporate proxy. Root cause: Node 24’s –use-env-proxy only reads uppercase HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY. Modified .bashrc and .bashrcy proxy helper functions to synchronize uppercase/lowercase variables and export NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY=1 ✅ Fix residual Chinese in LLM prompts and outputs — Used 3 parallel validation agents to discover 6 issue categories: merge prompt context strings, matplotlib chart labels, DEEP_EVAL comments, overview.md template, JSON repair prompt, config init prompt. Fixed all 6 categories ✅ SSH remote timezone handling — Implemented timezone-aware date filtering for SSH remote servers. Added TZ_OFFSET detection, local timezone conversion for all date operations, timezone persistence and UI display. Fixed 4 instances of incorrect date extraction plus Codex since filter. Added 8 new unit tests. All 307 Rust + 219 frontend tests pass ✅ MimicGen result analysis and verification — Analyzed MimicGen success demo generation results (1282 success / 953 failed), identified 626 failed scenes needing human collection; corrected reporting script bug that misreported pick_place/coffee numbers via direct HDF5 inspection ✅ Float ball collapse flicker fix — Fixed visual flicker when float ball collapses on Windows (direction=‘left’ only). Root cause: WebView2 async rendering during SetWindowPos. Solution: Hide window before resize, show after completion ✅ Float ball collapse movement bug — Fixed ball moving left-then-right during collapse animation. Root cause: CSS .expanded class removal caused justify-content jump during 250ms transition. Solution: Remove .expanded condition from direction-based justify-content rules Implementation & Fixes ✅ Code optimization for gadget summarize/research modules — Implemented 7 optimizations: extracted _render_content_list(), removed unnecessary OrderedDict import, replaced loop+break with next(), extracted _truncate() and _field() helpers, added _load_profile() with error handling, added None guards. Eliminated ~50 lines of duplication ✅ Configure unified Claude Code permissions for GitHub workspace — Created/updated settings.local.json for 6 subdirectories with consistent permissions: auto-allow all built-in tools + common commands, require confirmation only for destructive operations (rm/rmdir/del/rd) ✅ Claude Code multi-instance isolation strategy — Provided 3 solutions for running GLM 5.1 and official Claude Code simultaneously: (1) CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR environment variable (recommended), (2) wrapper script with hardcoded env var, (3) symlink switching. Enables isolated ~/.claude-glm/ and ~/.claude/ directories ✅ Update gadget CLAUDE.md documentation — Updated 5 key sections to reflect Ollama translation architecture migration: Environment Setup (added Ollama installation), Architecture (added common/ollama.py, translation.py, bilingual.py), Cross-Module Dependencies (updated dependency graph), Translation Architecture (new section), Tests (added pytest commands) ✅ meeting-helper project permission configuration — Modified .claude/settings.local.json with three-tier permission strategy: auto-allow 60+ common commands, ask for rm/git push, deny rm -rf/git push –force/git reset –hard/git clean -f ✅ Fix TokenMonitor Tauri CLI failure — Resolved ’tauri’ is not recognized error caused by missing Windows native binding (@tauri-apps/cli-win32-x64-msvc). Deleted node_modules and package-lock.json, reinstalled to trigger correct platform dependency resolution Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. npx ccusage and all Node.js CLI tools couldn’t fetch external data despite working http_proxy/https_proxy environment variables and local proxy running on 127.0.0.1:10087 Solution: Discovered Node 24’s –use-env-proxy feature only reads uppercase HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/NO_PROXY, not lowercase variants. Modified .bashrc and .bashrcy to export both uppercase and lowercase variables in proxy_on/net_on functions, and set NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY=1 to enable Node’s native fetch() proxy support
Key Insight: Node.js 24+ has built-in environment variable proxy support via –use-env-proxy flag/NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY env var, but it strictly requires uppercase variable names. This is a cleaner solution than injecting undici ProxyAgent or wrapping individual CLI tools. Universal fix for all Node CLI tools, not tool-specific workarounds
2. After converting LLM prompts and Markdown rendering to English, validation revealed 6 categories of residual Chinese text that would break the English-first pipeline: LLM context strings, matplotlib chart labels, DEEP_EVAL annotations, project templates, JSON repair instructions, config defaults Solution: Used 3 parallel validation agents (one per module) to systematically grep for Chinese patterns and verify full pipeline consistency. Fixed all 6 issue types across summarize, research, and website modules
Key Insight: LLM prompt translation requires checking not just main PROMPT constants but also: (1) context strings injected during execution, (2) non-text output (charts), (3) code comments appearing in prompts, (4) generated templates, (5) error recovery prompts. Parallel agent validation (split by subsystem) is more thorough than single-pass grep for discovering runtime-injected strings
3. Remote SSH servers in different timezones caused incorrect date filtering - a record timestamped 2024-01-01T23:00:00+00:00 (UTC) was treated as Jan 1 instead of Jan 2 when local timezone is UTC+8 Solution: Added parse_remote_ts_to_local_date() helper that converts FixedOffset timestamps to Local timezone before extracting date. Modified 4 call sites to use local-timezone-aware conversion. Fixed Codex since_iso_filter to include UTC offset for correct cross-timezone string comparison
Key Insight: DateTime::date_naive() extracts the date in the server’s timezone, not local. Must convert to Local timezone first with .with_timezone(&chrono::Local) before calling date_naive(). Critical for any distributed system with cross-timezone data
4. meeting-helper needs Chinese-English mixed language transcription, but Whisper’s language detection is per-chunk (~30s) not per-sentence, causing poor quality for intra-sentence code-switching like “我们需要implement这个feature” Solution: Multi-layer mitigation: (1) added custom vocabulary in screenpipe-settings.json for common bilingual terms, (2) added retranscription workflow in CLAUDE.md guiding users to POST /audio/retranscribe API with bilingual prompt when quality is poor, (3) added Step 4 in pipe to check transcription quality and trigger retranscription for mixed segments, (4) documented limitation explicitly to manage user expectations
Key Insight: Whisper’s architectural limitation (per-chunk language detection) cannot be fully solved by model parameters. More effective strategy: custom vocabulary + secondary transcription + user feedback loop for incremental quality improvement, while honestly disclosing limitations in documentation rather than over-promising
5. LifeCopilot’s BackgroundCoordinator manages 18+ background services but has no health check mechanism. Service crashes only log errors without auto-restart, causing partial system failure without user awareness Solution: Ported health monitoring pattern from OpenClaw: created health_manager.py implementing ServiceHealthManager (4-state FSM: HEALTHY→DEGRADED→FAILED→STOPPED, restart budget 10/hour, exponential backoff 1→2→4→8→60s). Modified BackgroundCoordinator to integrate health management: 5min health checks, auto-restart on failure, mark as degraded when budget exhausted, EventBus notifications. Added GET /api/health endpoint exposing all service status
Key Insight: Porting mature patterns from production-validated open source projects (like OpenClaw’s health monitoring) is more reliable than designing from scratch. Key is understanding original design rationale (why 4-state FSM? why limit restart count? why exponential backoff?) and adapting to target project context
6. Float ball collapse animation on Windows showed visual flicker, but only when direction=‘left’ (ball on right side). Direction=‘right’ (ball on left) did not flicker Solution: Root cause: SetWindowPos simultaneously moves and resizes the window, but WebView2 rendering is async. When ball position jumps (x=96→x=0) during resize, the 1-2 frame gap shows old content clipped incorrectly. Fix: Wrap SetWindowPos with ShowWindow(SW_HIDE) before and ShowWindow(SW_SHOWNOACTIVATE) after, so WebView2 doesn’t render intermediate frames. Applied only to collapse (!expanded) to minimize side effects
Key Insight: Native window geometry changes (SetWindowPos) are synchronous, but WebView2 content rendering is asynchronous. When the window’s left edge moves (direction=‘left’ collapse), the WebView content coordinate system shifts, causing visible flicker during the 1-2 frame rendering lag. Direction=‘right’ doesn’t flicker because the left edge stays at x=0 - only the right edge shrinks
7. Reporting script bug caused pick_place and coffee to read stale full_run_200attempts.log, both showing incorrect 398/260 numbers instead of actual 168/85 Solution: Bypassed log parsing and directly inspected HDF5 files with h5py to get ground truth demo counts
Key Insight: Log-based reporting can drift from actual data artifacts; HDF5 files are the authoritative source for data validation
8. Float ball collapse animation caused ball to move left then right instead of staying in place Solution: Root cause: Setting expanded=false immediately removes .shell.expanded class, causing CSS justify-content to jump from flex-end/flex-start to center. During the 250ms capsule width transition, the ball drifts toward center. Fix: Remove .expanded condition from direction-based justify-content rules so they apply throughout the transition
Key Insight: CSS transitions interact with native window resize timing. Frontend CSS transition (250ms) completes before backend window resize (t=260ms). If CSS rules change immediately based on expanded state, the ball floats during transition. Direction-based positioning must persist throughout transition to anchor the ball
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Search-First strategy (research before implementation) Role Approach Human User emphasized “先搜索现有开源方案再实现” for meeting-helper and interrupted AI when it started requirements analysis: “你能用websearch先研究现有方案吗” AI AI initially wanted to proceed directly to ccplan Phase 0 requirements analysis, preparing to design architecture from scratch. After interruption, AI executed deep research (Screenpipe, Meetily, Natively) and adjusted entire architecture direction (from custom audio capture to Screenpipe+MCP) Difference Analysis: Human knows from experience that “don’t reinvent the wheel” is a golden rule in software engineering, prefers search→evaluate→reuse. AI knows this principle (mentioned in ccplan skill) but in execution tends toward “I can solve this” mindset and skips research phase. Human’s interruption corrected this tendency, teaching AI that mandatory research phase must precede ccplan Phase 0
Node.js CLI proxy fix strategy Role Approach Human User framed problem as ’npx ccusage@latest doesn’t go through proxy’ and asked for ‘one-time-fix solution’, likely thinking about wrapping npx command, modifying ccusage source, or creating custom launch script AI AI investigated full dependency chain: checked ccusage source → confirmed it uses native fetch() without custom dispatcher → checked Node version (24.12.0) → found –use-env-proxy support → discovered uppercase-only variable requirement → verified user only had lowercase variables → proposed shell-level solution by unifying variable case in proxy helper functions Difference Analysis: Human focused on tool-specific workaround; AI found root cause at Node runtime level, enabling universal fix for all Node CLI tools. AI’s solution was simpler (3-line shell function change) vs wrapping/patching individual tools. Key: checking tool dependencies first before proposing solutions
Scope of English conversion for content generation pipeline Role Approach Human User initially requested: ‘modify JSON report → generate_markdown() to only output English version, then translate to Chinese at website level’. This suggested modifying only Markdown rendering layer AI AI recognized this required changing entire LLM prompt chain (not just rendering), proactively checked research module for similar patterns. During validation phase, AI used 3 parallel agents to discover residual Chinese in 6 additional categories (context strings, chart labels, templates, etc.) not obvious from initial requirement Difference Analysis: Human specified desired behavior but didn’t articulate all necessary changes; AI inferred full technical scope (prompts → rendering → charts → templates → error messages). AI’s multi-phase approach (convert → validate → fix residuals → optimize) was more thorough than user’s initial single-step request. Demonstrates AI’s strength in dependency analysis and completeness checking
Realistic limitations of Chinese-English mixed transcription Role Approach Human User asked “你能解决一下关键风险吗?” after meeting-helper v1 completion - explicitly pointing to ECL-documented CRITICAL risk (poor quality for intra-sentence code-switching) AI AI was overly optimistic in Phase 0-9 about Whisper large-v3-turbo’s multilingual capability (DEC-003 confidence=0.75), suggesting it could be “solved”. Only after user explicitly demanded “solve critical risk” did AI adopt more pragmatic mitigation strategy (retranscription API + custom vocabulary + documentation) Difference Analysis: AI tends toward technical optimism (“better model will solve it”), while human knows from practice that some technical limits are architectural (Whisper’s per-chunk language detection can’t be changed by tuning). Human intervention forced AI from “solve problem” mindset to “mitigate risk + manage expectations” mindset, which is more mature for handling inherent technical constraints
Testing hide/show workaround necessity Role Approach Human User asked to test direct SetWindowPos first before adding hide/show complexity, saying ‘ok，可以试试’ - pragmatic approach of validating whether workaround is actually needed AI AI initially proposed hide/show as definitive solution based on theoretical WebView2 async rendering behavior without testing if flicker actually occurs with direct resize Difference Analysis: Human favored empirical testing (’try it first’) over theoretical analysis. This revealed that direction matters - only left direction flickers, making the solution more targeted. AI jumped to fix without establishing baseline. Empirical testing beats theoretical analysis for edge cases
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Initial English conversion (Phase 1-2) missed 6 categories of residual Chinese text: LLM context strings in merge prompts, matplotlib chart labels, prompt annotations, templates, error recovery prompts, and config defaults. Only discovered after user asked ‘check the complete pipeline’. AI’s first-pass conversion focused on obvious locations (PROMPT constants, Markdown labels) but missed execution-time injected strings and non-text outputs AI didn’t anticipate Phase 11 user requests in meeting-helper (retranscription workflow, Q&A logging, speaker management). Though Phase 5 adversarial review found Chinese-English mixed transcription limitation, didn’t propose retranscription as mitigation; though researched Screenpipe’s speaker system, didn’t proactively include in Feature list; designed Q&A interaction but didn’t consider logging requirement. AI’s forward planning still has blind spots - can identify problems but may not propose best solutions, can research features but may not proactively recommend AI understanding of Whisper language detection mechanism was insufficient. In meeting-helper Phase 2-4, marked “Chinese-English mixed language” as confidence=0.75, suggesting Whisper large-v3-turbo + bilingual config could handle well. Actually Whisper’s detection is per-chunk (~30s) not per-sentence, poor for intra-sentence code-switching. This critical limitation wasn’t identified until Phase 5 adversarial review (by code-reviewer subagent). AI tends to rely on “model is powerful” intuition when assessing technical feasibility, overlooking architectural-level inherent constraints Misread stale log file (full_run_200attempts.log) when pick_place and coffee lacked run_200att_*.log files, causing incorrect 398/260 reporting; failed to verify against actual HDF5 output artifacts before summarizing. For data validation tasks, AI should always verify against authoritative source (HDF5 files) rather than relying on potentially stale intermediate logs AI proposed CSS pre-positioning workaround (moving ball left then resizing window) for float ball flicker without fully analyzing IPC timing constraints. Only after user questioned it did AI realize CSS changes and Win32 SetWindowPos cannot synchronize across Tauri IPC boundary - they will always be 1+ frames apart, making approach fundamentally unworkable AI didn’t anticipate second animation bug (left-then-right movement during collapse) that emerged from first fix. Only user testing revealed CSS justify-content issue caused by immediate .expanded class removal during transition General Limitations AI couldn’t complete interactive shell verification on tianhe server: when testing Node fetch() through proxy, got ‘TypeError: fetch failed’ with EPERM. AI correctly identified this as sandbox proxy connection issue rather than code problem, but couldn’t complete real-world validation. Had to rely on theoretical correctness (bash -n syntax check + environment variable verification) instead of end-to-end test Over-reliance on file content caching led to multiple ‘File has been modified since read’ errors. AI should adopt paranoid strategy: always re-read immediately before Edit even if file was just accessed, especially in workflows with linters/formatters or multi-agent concurrency Learnings Key Learnings ccplan’s adversarial review (Phase 5 Adversarial Filtering) is extremely valuable. In meeting-helper, code-reviewer subagent as adversarial critic found 6 critical problems main planner missed, including CRITICAL-level Chinese-English mixed transcription limit (Whisper per-chunk detection causes poor intra-sentence code-switching quality), long meeting context overflow, speaker identification limits, installation complexity, privacy compliance. These weren’t sufficiently identified in Phase 0-4 single-perspective planning. Key insight: complex project planning must introduce independent critical perspective (adversarial review) focusing on “what planner would miss” not “whether plan is correct” MimicGen success rates from error scenes are low (57.4% overall for success demos, 9.57% for stack human demo augmentation); human teleoperation fallback is necessary for comprehensive coverage when automated generation hits success rate plateau Node.js 24+ has built-in environment variable proxy support (–use-env-proxy flag / NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY=1), but it ONLY reads uppercase HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/NO_PROXY, not lowercase variants. Common pitfall when shell proxy setup scripts only export lowercase. Solution: always sync both cases in proxy helper functions. Universal fix for all Node CLI tools When refactoring LLM-driven pipelines from one language to another, validation must cover: (1) static prompt templates, (2) runtime context injection, (3) non-text outputs (charts/images), (4) code comments appearing in prompts, (5) generated templates/scaffolds, (6) error recovery logic. Parallel agent validation pattern (one per subsystem) is more thorough than single-pass grep Porting validated architecture patterns from mature open source projects is more efficient and reliable than designing from scratch. LifeCopilot’s OpenClaw integration demonstrated: health monitoring (4-state FSM + restart budget + exponential backoff), circuit breaker (closed→open→half-open), config hot reload (watchfiles + EventBus), speaker diarization (voice embeddings + calendar assist) all from production-grade implementations. Key: understand original design rationale (why 4 states not 3? why limit restart count? why exponential backoff?) then adapt to target context. “Understand → port → adapt” workflow significantly reduces design risk since source project already discovered pitfalls MCP (Model Context Protocol) as standard interface between AI assistants and external tools greatly simplifies integration complexity. meeting-helper demonstrated MCP’s power: Screenpipe exposes all capabilities through 27 MCP tools (list-meetings, get-meeting, search-content, list-speakers, update-speaker, etc.), Claude Code CLI achieves complete meeting Q&A functionality (real-time query, history search, speaker management) without any custom code. Proves value of standard protocols in AI tool ecosystem - tool providers implement MCP server once, all MCP-supporting AI assistants (Claude, Codex, etc.) can use directly. Compared to REST API or custom integration, each AI assistant would need separate adaptation DateTime timezone handling nuance: date_naive() on DateTime extracts date in source timezone, not local. For remote timestamp filtering, must explicitly convert to Local timezone first with with_timezone(&chrono::Local) before extracting date. Critical for any distributed system with cross-timezone data Multi-dataset contrastive learning requires mapping-level source tagging and Dataset-level list-based architecture; enables flexible sampling from heterogeneous augmentation sources (single vs multiple OSS perturbations) JSONL (append-only log) + in-memory cache hybrid architecture is highly effective for scenarios requiring both persistence and performance. LifeCopilot’s multiple subsystems use this pattern: session history (deque(maxlen=100) + data/sessions/{user_id}.jsonl), audit log, thoughts, mismatches. Key design points: (1) write with atomic operation (write tmp → os.replace) to prevent corruption on crash, (2) lazy load on read (first access reads recent N records) to avoid slow startup, (3) use bounded buffer in memory (like deque(maxlen)) to prevent memory leak, (4) unified timestamp with ISO 8601 UTC for timezone safety, (5) periodic archival (data_lifecycle.py cleans by retention_days) to prevent infinite growth. This architecture is best practice validated at scale in logging systems, event sourcing, audit trails WebView2 rendering is asynchronous relative to native window geometry changes.
