SetWindowPos modifies window size/position synchronously, but Chromium needs 1-2 frames to render new content. For transparent windows with complex layouts, this async gap causes visible flicker. ShowWindow(SW_HIDE) before geometry change and SW_SHOWNOACTIVATE after prevents intermediate frames from being composited by DWM SetWindowPos anchor point behavior: top-left corner is fixed, so resizing smaller means right/bottom edges move inward. When content needs visual anchor to right (like direction=‘left’ float ball), must simultaneously adjust x position by width delta. Fundamental Win32 API constraint affecting all window resize logic CSS transitions interact with React/Svelte state changes non-obviously. Removing class (like .expanded) immediately changes computed styles, but transitions may still be in progress. For multi-step animations involving both CSS and native operations, CSS rules must persist throughout transition period regardless of logical state, or use separate ’transitioning’ state Practical Learnings Character-ratio language detection (5% CJK threshold) is sufficient for technical content classification. English technical docs with occasional Chinese terms typically have <1% CJK ratio, providing wide margin. No need for complex NLP-based language detection for content pipeline classification tasks Code optimization pattern: extract reusable helper for complex parsing, then compose higher-level functions from it. parse_remote_ts → parse_remote_ts_to_local_date pattern eliminates duplication and is easier to maintain than copy-pasting RFC3339 format strings. Performance optimization should reduce redundant work without sacrificing clarity Configuring lenient permissions (auto-allow most operations) for exploratory projects significantly improves development experience while balancing security and efficiency. meeting-helper’s .claude/settings.local.json demonstrated this strategy: add 60+ common commands (Read/Write/Edit/Glob/Grep/WebFetch/git status/npm/python, etc.) to allow list for auto-pass; keep ask confirmation only for destructive operations (rm delete files, git push, git reset –hard); directly deny extremely dangerous operations (rm -rf recursive force delete, git push –force, git clean -f). This three-tier permission strategy (allow/ask/deny) avoids frequent interruptions (exploratory work needs lots of file reads/writes, command runs) while preventing irreversible errors (deletion, remote push need manual confirmation) - best config for exploratory dev environments Conversation Summaries error-recovery-benchmark ✅ MimicGen result analysis and human collection workflow 05:38:51.216 | claude_code Analyzed MimicGen success demo generation (739/1365 scenes succeeded, 626 failed). Identified reporting bug where pick_place/coffee read stale logs. Verified actual counts via HDF5 inspection. Reviewed stack augmentation (9.57% success rate). Implemented three-script workflow for human teleoperation collection: scene identification, SpaceMouse collection with progress tracking, NPZ→HDF5 conversion
BOSS ✅ Multi-dataset contrastive learning refactor 02:10:29.399 | claude_code Refactored PairedDataset and build pipeline to support simultaneous sampling from single and multiple OSS augmentation datasets. Modified build_paired_mapping.py for multi-source input with source tags, paired_dataset.py for aug_datasets list, config.py for aug_repo_ids. Copied 220 BDDL files (5 variants × 44 tasks) and generated mapping JSON. Corrected naming: con→single
System maintenance ✅ Node.js proxy configuration fix for CLI tools 03:44:53.400 | codex + claude_code Fixed npx ccusage and all Node CLI tools failing to fetch through corporate proxy. User reported failure on tianhe server. AI investigated full chain: confirmed ccusage uses native fetch() without custom dispatcher, discovered Node 24 supports –use-env-proxy but only reads uppercase HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY (user only had lowercase), modified .bashrc and .bashrcy proxy helper functions to sync uppercase/lowercase variables and set NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY=1. Validation confirmed syntax correctness and environment variable propagation; real-world test blocked by sandbox proxy restrictions. Final state: code ready for user to source ~/.bashrc and verify. Achieved universal fix for all Node CLI tools
Claude Code multi-instance isolation ✅ Config isolation strategy for GLM 5.1 vs official Claude Code 03:36:36.246 | claude_code User needed to run GLM 5.1 (command: claude) and official Claude Code (command: claude-code) simultaneously without config conflicts. AI explained root cause (shared ~/.claude/ directory) and provided 3 solutions: (1) CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR env var (recommended), (2) wrapper script, (3) symlink switching. User can now use separate config directories for each instance
GitHub Workspace permissions ✅ Unified permission config for GitHub subprojects 05:04:52.273 | claude_code User wanted to skip repetitive confirmations across all GitHub subprojects (gadget, LifeCopilot, OCR, TokenMonitor, everything-claude-code). AI explained per-repo config boundary and batch-created/updated settings.local.json for 6 directories with unified permission schema (auto-allow everything except rm/del/rmdir/rd). Preserved project-specific MCP server configs
gadget ✅ Refactor summarize & research pipelines to English-first output + documentation update 04:15:18.048 | claude_code Major refactoring session spanning two conversations. Session 1: User requested changing article generation from Chinese-first to English-first with Ollama translation at website level. Executed four phases: (1) converted 4 summarize files (LLM prompts + Markdown rendering), (2) converted 8 research files (scout + profiler), (3) used 3 parallel validation agents discovering 6 residual Chinese issue categories (LLM context strings, chart labels, prompt annotations, templates, repair prompts, config defaults) and fixed all, (4) optimized code with 7 improvements (extracted helpers, removed duplication, added error handling). Session 2: Updated CLAUDE.md to reflect Ollama translation architecture migration - modified 5 key sections (Environment Setup, Architecture, Cross-Module Dependencies, Translation Architecture, Tests). Final verification confirmed all changes compile, no remaining Chinese in prompts/output, documentation accurate
LifeCopilot ✅ OpenClaw core + extended enhancements integration (two ccplan rounds) 19:16:30.724 | claude_code Two-round ccplan workflow. Round 1: User requested OpenClaw feature integration. Executed complete workflow: parallel research (2 Explore agents scanning codebases) → user selected “core enhancements” (Health Monitoring + Config Hot Reload + Cron Concurrency Control) with full implementation → generated ECL (openclaw-core-enhancements.yaml with 3 features, 8 Features, 15 configs, 4 EventBus events) → implemented in 3 Phases (CronScheduler concurrency + ConfigWatcher + HealthManager basics → BackgroundCoordinator integration + Cron persistence + auto-restart + CONFIG_RELOADED handling → GET /api/health endpoint) → wrote 3 test files (70 test cases) → verified compilation and tests (70/70 pass). Round 2: User asked “还有其他可以优化的点吗”, triggering second ccplan. AI identified 7 optimization directions → user selected all + full implementation → generated 4-Sprint plan (openclaw-extended-enhancements.yaml) → implemented Sprint 1-3 (data_lifecycle.py general JSONL cleaner, SessionManager JSONL persistence + memory cache, ThoughtStore.search() keyword matching, DreamingService 3-phase AI consolidation, InsightStore creation, CircuitBreaker 3-state FSM). Sprint 4 (DM pairing) marked for future session. Final deliverables: 10 new module files (health_manager, config_watcher, data_lifecycle, dreaming_service, insight_store, circuit_breaker, etc.), modified 15 files, 70 tests passing
meeting-helper ✅ AI meeting assistant creation from scratch + permission optimization 19:21:47.872 | claude_code Complete ccplan Phase 0-11 workflow spanning two sessions. Session 1: User requested meeting assistant creation (recording, transcription, summary, Q&A, screen capture, cross-platform macOS+Win, Claude Code CLI backend). Executed: Phase 0-1 research (parallel agent researching Zoom AI, Teams, Otter.ai commercial solutions, plus Screenpipe/Meetily/Natively open source, identified Screenpipe as best foundation) → user selected Strategy A (build on Screenpipe) and requested cloning repos for deep learning → dispatched 2 Explore agents parallel deep research (Screenpipe’s Pipe mechanism, MCP server, meeting detection; Meetily’s dual-channel audio, VAD, BlockNote editor) → Phase 2 requirement validation → Phase 4 requirement crystallization (8 requirements, 7 Features, 5 Modules) → Phase 5+7 adversarial review + feasibility probe (code-reviewer agent found 6 problems including CRITICAL Chinese-English mixed transcription limit; Explore agent validated 5 key assumptions) → Phase 9 approval → Phase 10 implemented 5 Features (screenpipe-settings.json Whisper config + bilingual, meeting-summary-zh custom Pipe, setup-mcp.sh config script, CLAUDE.md Q&A system prompt, install.sh/ps1 cross-platform install + health-check) → Phase 11 feedback loop (user requested critical risk mitigation + Q&A logging + speaker management, AI added retranscription workflow, custom vocabulary, Q&A logs, speaker MCP tool integration). Final architecture: Screenpipe background (audio + screen capture + Whisper transcription + SQLite FTS5) → screenpipe-mcp (27 tools) → Claude Code CLI (real-time interaction) + meeting-summary-zh Pipe (post-meeting batch summary). Includes complete ECL planning doc (meeting-helper.yaml). Session 2: User complained about frequent Claude Code confirmation prompts. Used update-config skill to modify .claude/settings.local.json with three-tier permission strategy: allow 60+ common commands (auto-pass), ask for rm/git push, deny rm -rf/git push –force/git reset –hard/git clean -f. Config applies to all sessions including subfolders
TokenMonitor ✅ Tauri CLI fix + dev server 404 + SSH timezone handling + float ball animation fixes 2026-04-09 (multiple sessions) | claude_code Four debugging/enhancement sessions across the day. Session 1: npm run tauri dev failed with ’tauri’ is not recognized. Diagnosed missing Windows native binding (@tauri-apps/cli-win32-x64-msvc), deleted node_modules and package-lock.json, reinstalled to trigger correct platform dependency resolution. Session 2: npm run tauri dev failed with Vite “Port 1420 is already in use”. Used netstat to locate PID 42676 (residual node.exe 101MB), suggested taskkill to terminate. Session 3: Implemented timezone-aware date filtering for SSH remote servers. Root issue: remote JSONL timestamps carry server timezone offset (RFC 3339 FixedOffset), but date filtering used date_naive() extracting server date instead of local date, causing off-by-one-day errors. Solution in 4 phases: (1) added parse_remote_ts_to_local_date helper with .with_timezone(&Local) conversion, modified 4 date extraction sites; (2) fixed Codex since_iso_filter to include UTC offset; (3) added TZ_OFFSET detection in extraction script, persistence in .timezone file, UI display in DevicesView; (4) wrote 8 new unit tests. Optimization pass eliminated duplicate parsing. All 307 Rust + 219 frontend tests pass. Session 4: Fixed two related UI bugs in float ball collapse animation. First bug: visual flicker only when direction=‘left’. Root cause: SetWindowPos simultaneously moves window right and shrinks it, but WebView2 rendering is async. Solution: wrap SetWindowPos with ShowWindow(SW_HIDE/SW_SHOWNOACTIVATE) only for collapse. Second bug (discovered during testing): ball moves left-then-right during collapse. Root cause: expanded=false removes .shell.expanded class, jumping justify-content to center during 250ms CSS transition. Solution: remove .expanded condition from direction-based justify-content rules. Also fixed dev server 404 by restoring accidentally deleted index.html via git checkout
Token Usage Total: $9.91 — 9.3M tokens
summary: Cross-device full-stack development day: optimized robot learning pipelines on tianhe (MimicGen analysis + human collection workflow + multi-dataset contrastive learning), resolved infrastructure issues (Node proxy, Claude Code permissions), executed major gadget refactoring (Chinese→English output with Ollama translation), completed two large architecture upgrades (LifeCopilot OpenClaw integration with 70 tests, meeting-helper from scratch via Screenpipe+MCP), and enhanced TokenMonitor with timezone-aware SSH handling plus UI animation fixes
title: Bug Journal 2026-04-09

content:
Daily Report — 2026-04-08 Daily Overview What was done: Large-scale code optimization across two HPC projects, comprehensive ML pipeline debugging (MimicGen/BOSS/Crossformer), translation infrastructure migration from cloud LLMs to local Ollama, robotics augmentation system architectural rewrite, multi-repository Git synchronization, and batch PDF OCR processing How it was done: Systematic code analysis and refactoring using AI-powered optimization tools; architectural diagnosis through code archaeology and execution path tracing; faithful algorithm reimplementation with target-pose waypoint execution; migration to local Ollama translation service with retry mechanisms; cross-platform development environment unification via symlinks Impact: Eliminated 200+ lines duplicated code with 10-50x performance gains; corrected fundamental architecture mismatches in three ML pipelines; established production-ready local translation workflow eliminating cloud API dependencies; unified cross-platform development with single source of truth; deployed complete bilingual website and research documentation DCC What was done: MIHD spatial omics project: code optimization analysis, security review, and paper-style documentation generation How it was done: Systematic analysis using /optimize skill identified 6 priority optimizations including vectorized contrastive loss; security review filtered false positives; generated comprehensive OVERVIEW.md Impact: Identified performance bottleneck with 10-50x speedup potential; validated codebase security posture; produced publication-ready project documentation DesktopLinux What was done: Website translation system completion (74 files), dual-boot time synchronization fix, TokenMonitor upstream merge, and cross-platform folder unification How it was done: Completed batch translation using HY-MT1.5-7B (57min runtime); resolved RTC timezone conflicts; merged 4 upstream commits; replaced separate folders with symlink to Windows partition Impact: Deployed complete bilingual website; eliminated persistent time drift; integrated upstream improvements; achieved single-source-of-truth for cross-platform development TzJsDesktop What was done: Multi-repository Git management, Claude Code permission optimization, dual Ollama translation integration (OCR and gadget projects), and batch PDF OCR processing How it was done: Git operations and configuration via claude_code; large-scale code refactoring and new module development via codex; marker_single for PDF OCR; local Ollama service for translation with retry mechanisms Impact: Synchronized three major repositories; significantly improved Claude Code interaction efficiency; achieved fully local, on-demand translation capability eliminating cloud API dependencies; established robust pipeline for batch document processing tianhe What was done: error_recovery_benchmark comprehensive refactoring, three ML pipeline debugging sessions (MimicGen recovery/BOSS dataset pairing/Crossformer preprocessing), time-uniform battery dataset resampling, Error Recovery architecture deep-dive, and faithful MimicGen augmentation rewrite How it was done: Extracted shared helpers to BaseErrorSkill, fixed HDF5 resource leaks, hardened exception handling; conversion script patching with provenance tracking; scipy linear interpolation for temporal uniformity; extensive code archaeology with line-number references; rewrote augmentation from action-replay to target-pose waypoint execution with data migration Impact: Eliminated 130+ lines duplication and closed all resource leaks in benchmark; corrected BOSS PairedDataset mapping pipeline; delivered standardized time-series dataset; documented full recovery demo lifecycle; replaced incorrect action-replay augmentation with proper MimicGen algorithm Completed cross-platform code optimization and translation automation across HPC/robotics projects, debugged three ML data pipelines, migrated translation infrastructure to local Ollama, and rewrote robotics augmentation system from action replay to target-pose execution
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ error_recovery_benchmark P0+P1 comprehensive refactoring — Implemented 6 FEATs: added 3 BaseErrorSkill helpers, refactored 12 skill files (frame checks, list(keys)[0], normalization), fixed div-by-zero/empty-list safety, closed HDF5 leaks in 4 scripts, narrowed vla_server exceptions; 226/229 tests passing ✅ MIHD code optimization analysis and security review — Scanned MIHD project identifying 6 optimization opportunities (vectorized contrastive loss with 10-50x speedup, duplicate graph-building extraction, simplified naming builders); performed security analysis dismissing all findings as false positives in local HPC context; generated comprehensive OVERVIEW.md with 6 sections ✅ BOSS augmented dataset episode mapping system repair — Modified libero2lerobot conversion scripts to output conversion_index.json with source HDF5 provenance; created build_paired_mapping.py for three-way association (clean index + aug index + episode mapping) generating PairedDataset-compatible mapping supporting 1-to-N OSS variant selection ✅ Crossformer data processing architecture mismatch diagnosis — Compared two implementations with 18x result difference, identified root causes: LIPM patch-based zero-padding breaks DSW_embedding’s segment continuity assumption, lack of StandardScaler normalization, 154k vs hundreds sample difference ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark technical architecture deep-dive — Answered 3 critical questions: (1) why robot moves during teleop (replay_animation enabled), (2) training uses recovery-only segments from post_sim_state, (3) MimicGen augmentation uses object-centric warping. Provided line-numbered code references across 20+ files ✅ Implement faithful MimicGen recovery augmentation — Rewrote recovery augmentation from action-replay to MimicGen-style target-pose waypoint execution: added target_poses recording, backfill migration for 23 stack demos, per-subtask object references, waypoint interpolation, updated configs/tests, verified with unit tests + live runs ✅ gadget project translation architecture Ollama migration — Added common/ollama.py and common/translation.py as shared infrastructure; rewrote three translation entry points (bilingual.py/translate_content.py/translate_site_batch.py); removed transformers dependency; updated all callers; added unit tests ✅ MimicGen experiment status verification and manual collection planning — Debugged incorrect reporting (398 was script bug), verified actual 739/1365 success rate (54.1%), identified 626 failed scenes requiring manual SpaceMouse collection with HDF5 format compatibility ✅ Crossformer battery dataset temporal uniform resampling — Transform irregularly-sampled battery cycle data (chargetime intervals 0-3.17s, std=0.48) into uniformly-sampled trajectories with fixed 1s/5s/10s intervals via scipy linear interpolation; generated 3 resampled datasets with 100% uniform interval distribution ✅ OCR project Ollama translation integration — Implemented translate_ocr_with_ollama.py for bilingual OCR markdown; installed Ollama with HY-MT1.5-1.8B model; implemented on-demand serve startup, Markdown chunking, CJK detection, structure preservation ✅ Batch OCR and translation of Herbs and Roots PDF chapters — Used marker_single with –force_ocr to convert three PDF chapters to markdown; generated bilingual translations using local Ollama (HY-MT1.5-1.8B) with –chunk-chars 2000; added retry mechanism (4 attempts, 5s backoff) to handle service instability Implementation & Fixes ✅ Multi-repository Git synchronization — Synchronized 3 major projects: gadget (commit+push new files), LifeCopilot (commit+push feature branch), everything-claude-code (pull 420 commits with merge conflict resolution) ✅ Ubuntu/Windows cross-platform folder synchronization — Replaced ~/Desktop/algorithms with symlink to Windows partition; unified LifeCopilot, TokenMonitor, gadget contents (Ubuntu as source of truth) ✅ Codex approval workflow optimization for benchmark repository — Configured high-autonomy execution policy via AGENTS.md after discovering .codex path is read-only mount: default approval for non-destructive operations, explicit confirmation only for destructive operations ✅ Website translation completion (74 files) — Batch translated 42 daily bug journals, 9 weekly summaries, 21 research profiles, 2 misc files (Chinese → English) using HY-MT1.5-7B; 74/74 files completed in 57 minutes ✅ Claude Code permission configuration optimization — Configured auto-allow rules for exploratory workspace covering all file operations, agents, common CLI commands (git/python/npm/node), retaining confirmation only for deletions ✅ TokenMonitor upstream merge — Fetched and merged 4 upstream commits: PR #4 integration, v0.7.0 bump, v0.7.1 rate limit fix, GitHub Actions update; no conflicts ✅ Dual-boot time synchronization fix — Diagnosed RTC timezone conflict (Ubuntu local time, Windows UTC); recommended setting both to UTC to eliminate persistent drift Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. BasicContrastive.py contrastive loss bottleneck: per-node Python loop over 3000-4000 spots with tensor cloning each iteration Solution: Vectorized loss computation using batch logsumexp operations eliminating the loop; masked self-similarity with fill_diagonal_(-inf); used boolean masks for positive neighbor filtering
Key Insight: Even mature deep learning code often contains un-vectorized loops from prototyping phase; profiling + vectorization can yield 10-50x speedups with no algorithmic changes
2. Crossformer LIPM version (MSE=9, MAE=1.3) performs 18x worse than master version (MSE≈0.5) despite patch mechanism designed for irregular battery data Solution: Diagnosed fundamental architecture mismatch: Crossformer’s DSW_embedding assumes continuous seg_len time steps with positional encoding, but LIPM patch method creates irregular gaps filled with zeros that break temporal continuity, waste model capacity, and lose time interval information. Recommended: interpolation to uniform grid, time window aggregation, or irregular time-series models (GRU-D, BRITS, NAOMI)
Key Insight: Zero-padding is NOT neutral for sequence models with positional encodings - it actively misleads model to learn ‘zero patterns’ instead of temporal dependencies. Architecture assumptions must match data preprocessing; forcing irregular data into patch+zero format breaks semantic meaning
3. 12 Error Skill files duplicating 150+ lines: frame availability checks (22 occurrences), direction normalization (8 occurrences), list(dict.keys())[0] pattern (14 occurrences) Solution: Extracted 3 helper methods to BaseErrorSkill (check_remaining_frames, normalize_direction, random_direction); replaced list(keys())[0] with next(iter()); added 10 unit tests for new helpers
Key Insight: Code review revealed ‘skill-specific thresholds’ (e.g., e08 using 30 frames vs. standard 20) were actually bugs; duplication hides bugs because fixes aren’t propagated
4. PairedDataset expects {clean_ep_idx: [{aug_ep, overlap}]} but episode_mapping.json uses {task_name: {bddl_idx: [{aug_demo_idx, orig_ep_idx}]}} format; HDF5→LeRobot conversion loses source file/demo index association Solution: Modified conversion scripts to record conversion_index.json = [{lerobot_ep, source_file, source_demo_idx, length}]; created build_paired_mapping.py that parses clean/aug conversion indices, uses episode_mapping’s orig_ep_idx as bridge, aggregates across all bddl_idx, outputs PairedDataset-compatible format with up to 10 aug variants per clean episode
Key Insight: Data provenance must be tracked through every conversion step; three-way association requires explicit bridge keys recorded at generation time; 1-to-N mappings need aggregation across variant indices
5. User needed clarification on recovery demo data boundaries: whether training includes error injection process or only recovery segment Solution: Through code archaeology, confirmed recovery demos record only post-injection teleop actions; actions_list/states_list begin after scene.load() or replay completes; verified via sample NPZ inspection that recovery demo’s first state equals scene’s post_sim_state; training conversion filters for success=true and uses only recovery actions
Key Insight: Error recovery datasets intentionally exclude error-causing actions to prevent models from learning failure modes; only corrective human demonstrations are used for imitation learning
6. Previous conversation concluded 0% augmentation failures were due to ‘warping can’t adapt’, but code inspection revealed some subtypes had dominant NO_WARP segments (position_error_D1: 150/192 steps in correct_position) that aren’t warped at all Solution: Corrected diagnosis: failures aren’t due to warping limitations but due to incomplete warping coverage and fundamental architecture mismatch between action-replay (current) vs target-pose waypoint execution (MimicGen standard)
Key Insight: Attribution errors in root cause analysis persist when conclusions are drawn without inspecting execution paths; repo evidence directly contradicted stated cause: hardcoded NO_WARP labels meant dominant segments were still open-loop
7. Current augmenter used source-action replay with warped targets, while MimicGen actually uses source target-pose extraction + per-subtask waypoint trajectory generation + interpolated execution - fundamental architectural mismatch Solution: Implemented faithful port: added target_poses recording in collection, created DatagenInfo-compatible object pose tracking, per-subtask object-ref specs in config, waypoint interpolation via MimicGen’s WaypointTrajectory, target-pose-to-action execution in augmenter
Key Insight: Surface-level similarity (‘both use object poses’) can mask deep architectural differences; MimicGen’s success depends on waypoint-level closed-loop correction per subtask, not just better warping of open-loop actions
8. 7 HDF5 file handles opened without context managers; exceptions during processing cause resource leaks Solution: Wrapped all h5py.File() calls with ‘with’ statements in 4 script files (_merge_rounds.py, _check_valid_source.py, parallel_mimicgen_generate.py, augment_success_demos.py)
Key Insight: Resource leaks in HPC batch jobs accumulate over long runs causing mysterious OOM kills; Python’s context managers prevent this entire class of bugs
9. Ollama service dropped connection during translation (chunk 10/26) Solution: Added retry mechanism (4 attempts with 5s backoff) to translate_ocr_with_ollama.py
Key Insight: Local Ollama service can have transient failures; automatic retry with backoff is essential for batch processing
10. MimicGen generation report showed incorrect numbers (398 success demos) due to log parsing bug that counted all SUCCESS strings in full_run_200attempts.log regardless of task association Solution: Verified actual results from HDF5 files and generation_report.json, corrected total to 739/1365 (54.1% success), identified 626 specific failed scene_ids via regex parsing, established manual collection plan
Key Insight: Always verify aggregated metrics against ground truth files; log parsing bugs can propagate incorrect statistics when fallback logic reads wrong scope of data
11. AI misunderstood ‘uniform sampling’ as sample distribution balance (per-battery/per-cycle) rather than temporal interval uniformity Solution: User clarified requirement is for equal time gaps between consecutive measurements; AI pivoted to scipy.interpolate-based temporal resampling, analyzing chargetime distribution (35.83% with interval=1, rest irregular) and generating uniform-grid datasets via linear interpolation
Key Insight: Domain-specific terminology requires contextual grounding: ‘uniform sampling’ in time-series contexts typically means temporal regularity, not data distribution balance
12. Existing recovery demos lacked target_poses field required by new faithful augmenter Solution: Created backfill_recovery_target_poses.py that replays each saved demo from recorded initial state and reconstructs controller target poses via env_wrapper.action_to_target_pose(), allowing reuse of existing demo corpus without recollection
Key Insight: Data format migrations can be non-destructive when execution is deterministic: replay from saved state + recorded actions = reconstructed missing fields
13. Bash(git:*) would match all git commands including destructive operations like git clean -f, creating security risk Solution: User chose exploratory workspace’s permissive strategy accepting the risk; AI proposed splitting into specific safe subcommands but user declined
Key Insight: Permission configuration requires tradeoff between convenience and safety; exploratory environments can be more permissive
14. First Ollama large patch failed due to Windows command length limit (CreateProcessAsUserW failed: 206) Solution: Split one large patch into multiple small patches: first land shared modules, then migrate callers individually
Key Insight: Windows command-line parameter length has hard limit; large patches need batch submission
General Issues 15. Website translation workflow needed to handle 74 files scattered across directories; needed to preserve Chinese originals and generate English versions Solution: Used translate_site_batch.py with state tracking to incrementally translate only changed files; swapped language direction (Chinese sources → .zh.md, English outputs → .md) to make English primary language
Key Insight: For bilingual sites where English is primary but content originates in Chinese, inverting filename convention eliminates need to update all internal links
16. everything-claude-code local deletion of 18 skills files created modify/delete conflicts during pull with remote updates Solution: Used git checkout –ours to retain remote version (third-party repository); applied same strategy for package.json/package-lock.json; successfully completed merge
Key Insight: For third-party repositories, prioritize retaining upstream updates over local modifications
17. common/bilingual.py zh_path function name collided with local variable, triggering UnboundLocalError Solution: Renamed imported zh_path to translation_zh_path to avoid naming conflict
Key Insight: Python’s in-function assignment makes variable local even if module-level import exists with same name
18. Conda run parallel execution caused temp file conflicts Solution: Switched to serial execution and later to system Python instead of conda run
Key Insight: Conda run has concurrency limitations with temporary file handling; system Python provides more stability for non-conda-dependent scripts
19. Windows/Ubuntu dual-boot systems diverge in clock time after each reboot; Windows treats RTC as local time, Ubuntu treats it as UTC Solution: Identified Ubuntu configured with RTC in local TZ; recommended reverting Ubuntu to UTC (timedatectl set-local-rtc 0) and configuring Windows to use UTC via registry (RealTimeIsUniversal=1)
Key Insight: The proper fix is making both OSes use UTC for RTC despite Windows defaulting to local time; Linux using local RTC creates daylight saving bugs
20. Attempted to create project-scoped .codex/config.toml but .codex is read-only Lustre filesystem mountpoint blocking mkdir/mv operations Solution: Switched to repository-root AGENTS.md to document execution policy; this approach propagates to all subdirectories via Codex’s project-doc inheritance, achieving directory-tree-wide configuration without filesystem modifications
Key Insight: When system-level constraints (read-only mounts, file locks) block ideal technical solution, hierarchical configuration inheritance provides equivalent functional coverage
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Crossformer architecture compatibility diagnosis Role Approach Human Identified fundamental insight: ‘patch data is divided by time intervals with zero-padding, which is essentially incompatible with Crossformer’s uniform sampling assumption. Does this input still have value for Crossformer?’ - recognized architectural mismatch from first principles AI Initially focused on comparing code differences (data processing, normalization, parameters) without questioning whether patch+zero approach fundamentally violates Crossformer’s design assumptions until user raised core question Difference Analysis: Human reasoned from model architecture fundamentals (DSW_embedding expects continuous segments) to data preprocessing compatibility; AI performed surface-level comparison until prompted to analyze deeper assumptions
Requirement clarification interaction strategy Role Approach Human User patiently answered 5 rounds of clarifying questions in OCR translation task (translation target/output form/target language/format preservation/output location) AI Claude decomposed vague requirements into multiple choice questions through request_user_input tool, progressively narrowing solution space Difference Analysis: Human willing to accept structured requirement clarification process; AI leveraged tool to convert open-ended requirements into choice questions reducing ambiguity
MIHD optimization plan adversarial review findings Role Approach Human After AI’s initial plan, human requested adversarial critic review which identified critical issues: normalize_direction() in-place mutation causing side effects, random_direction() not suitable for direction-difference patterns, select_random_item() masking real bugs AI AI initially proposed generic helper methods without analyzing all usage patterns; critic subagent (when invoked) successfully identified these issues by reading actual implementation contexts Difference Analysis: Human recognized need for adversarial review before implementation; AI in default mode proceeds with first-pass solutions. Critic found issues AI’s main loop missed (in-place mutation semantics, pattern variations across callsites)
MimicGen result verification Role Approach Human Challenged 398 number immediately with skepticism: ‘I only generated 240 error scenes, how can there be 398 demos?’ - focused on sanity check from known input AI Initially accepted aggregated log statistics from combined summary script without verifying against ground truth HDF5 files Difference Analysis: Human used domain constraint (input scene count) to detect reporting bug; AI relied on aggregated metrics without cross-validation until challenged
Interpretation of ‘uniform sampling’ in time-series data preprocessing Role Approach Human Immediately recognized ‘uniform’ refers to equal temporal intervals (delta t consistency) based on domain knowledge of Crossformer requirements AI Initially interpreted as statistical sample distribution uniformity (balancing battery_id/cycle_idx representation), implementing per-category stratified sampling before correction Difference Analysis: Human brought domain context that disambiguated underspecified term; AI relied on general data science intuition without time-series-specific priors
Implementation strategy choice Role Approach Human When AI presented ‘Faithful Port (Recommended)’ vs ‘Minimal Patch’ options, human chose faithful port despite higher implementation cost AI AI recommended faithful port after analyzing that minimal patch wouldn’t address fundamental mismatch (action replay vs waypoint execution), offered explicit choice with trade-off description Difference Analysis: Human prioritized correctness and long-term maintainability over short-term implementation speed; AI provided structured decision framework but deferred to human
Translation service runtime mode Role Approach Human Explicitly requested not to run persistently, open when needed, close when not in use AI Initial design didn’t consider on-demand start/stop scenario, adjusted implementation after user supplemented requirements Difference Analysis: Human reasoned from resource management and actual usage habits; AI easily defaults to always-on service development mode
Handling service instability Role Approach Human No explicit instruction on handling failures AI AI proactively identified Ollama instability pattern and added retry mechanism to prevent future failures Difference Analysis: AI anticipated operational risks and implemented defensive programming without being asked
Security review false positive filtering Role Approach Human Human approved tool permission requests selectively, implicitly filtering out low-risk issues AI AI security agent flagged np.load(allow_pickle=True) as critical but then correctly contextualized as 1/10 confidence in local HPC research environment with no untrusted inputs Difference Analysis: Human’s implicit filtering through permission denial signals reinforced AI’s context-aware risk assessment; both converged on dismissing findings
Root cause diagnosis validation Role Approach Human Asked AI to check whether conclusion from previous conversation was correct, showing awareness that AI conclusions should be verified against code AI Performed detailed code inspection: checked augmentation implementation, recovery demo structure, NO_WARP label handling, per-subtype segment composition to evaluate claim Difference Analysis: Human intuitively questioned conclusion; AI systematically traced execution paths and data to falsify claim with file/line references
Exploratory workspace permission configuration strategy Role Approach Human User explicitly stated: exploratory content except deletions can auto-allow because can always rollback AI Claude initially wanted to inquire detail on each dangerous command and remind risks like git clean Difference Analysis: Human made overall strategic decision based on understanding workspace nature; AI tends to evaluate risks item-by-item seeking confirmation
Batch processing approach Role Approach Human Direct request for batch OCR and translation without specifying technical details AI AI understood requirement and implemented full pipeline: directory setup, OCR execution, file organization, and translation Difference Analysis: Human provided high-level goal; AI filled in complete technical implementation including error handling and verification steps
Implementation Level Folder synchronization strategy Role Approach Human Human specified exact requirements: (1) overlapping files use Ubuntu as source of truth, (2) use Ubuntu’s ‘Life-Copilot’ content to replace Windows ‘LifeCopilot’ but keep name ‘LifeCopilot’, (3) establish bidirectional sync AI AI initially proposed generic rsync/unison-based sync; human clarified simpler symlink approach after seeing filesystem layout Difference Analysis: Human recognized symlink (single source of truth) is architecturally superior to bidirectional sync for co-located filesystems; AI defaulted to sync tools appropriate for remote/disconnected systems
Manual collection strategy decision Role Approach Human Chose direct manual collection of all 626 scenes without first attempting to improve MimicGen success rate - decisive commitment to known-working method AI Offered three options (improve MimicGen, direct collection, analyze distribution first) and asked user to choose - deferred decision Difference Analysis: Human made immediate strategic decision based on time/effort tradeoff; AI presented options but didn’t recommend a path
Configuration strategy when .codex/ path is blocked Role Approach Human Approved AI’s initial plan to use .codex/config.toml, then accepted pivot to AGENTS.md when mount point was discovered AI Proposed canonical .codex/config.toml solution, encountered filesystem constraint, autonomously pivoted to AGENTS.md as functionally equivalent alternative without re-querying user Difference Analysis: AI demonstrated adaptive problem-solving by recognizing configuration-inheritance equivalence and switching strategies without abandoning original goal
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Did not proactively identify Crossformer patch+zero-padding approach as fundamentally incompatible with DSW_embedding’s uniform sampling assumption; only performed architectural analysis after user explicitly asked ‘does this input still have value for Crossformer?’ - surface-level code comparison instead of first-principles reasoning Initial optimization plan missed in-place mutation side effects in normalize_direction() helper; required explicit adversarial critic subagent to identify this issue Reported incorrect MimicGen statistics (398 demos for pick_place/coffee) by citing buggy combined summary script output without cross-validating against ground truth HDF5 files or generation_report JSON; only corrected after user challenged numbers with domain constraint Security review produced false positives: flagged np.load(allow_pickle=True) as critical vulnerability despite all .npz files being internally generated in local HPC pipeline with no network exposure or multi-tenant access Failed to disambiguate ‘uniform sampling’ terminology without explicit clarification, defaulting to statistical distribution interpretation rather than temporal regularity OCR translation script design completely relied on user providing choices through 5 rounds of interaction; AI didn’t proactively analyze ocr_raw.md’s actual characteristics (volume, mixed content) to propose initial solution General Limitations When committing LifeCopilot, would include runtime data like data/, logs/ together; though user said commit all, gitignore explicitly excludes data/*.json, showing insufficient understanding of gitignore rules During gadget translation refactoring, only discovered 4 .zh.md files actually contain English content through –dry-run after code completion; better approach is scanning existing content quality before refactoring Test suite validation did not recognize pre-existing test bugs: 3 taxonomy test failures (stuck_no_progress D1 assertions) were unrelated to refactoring changes but AI did not identify test code itself as buggy until examining implementation AI initially attempted conda commands without activating mimicgen_env, causing import errors; only corrected after seeing ‘No such file or directory (mujoco)’ error by switching to ‘conda run -n mimicgen_env’ Claude initially attempted parallel git fetch execution; single repository network failure caused other parallel commands to cancel, didn’t properly handle single-point failure in parallel tasks Only realized Windows command length limit after first large patch failed; better experience if could predict patch size and proactively batch Attempted parallel conda run execution without anticipating temporary file conflicts, causing process failures Multiple SSH command exit code 255 errors when trying to kill parallel MimicGen processes, required retries and workaround approaches to clean up 384 spawned worker processes Did not anticipate read-only mount point constraint when planning .codex/ configuration strategy, requiring mid-execution pivot Did not initially anticipate Ollama service instability; only added retry after first failure AI had to poll long-running backfill command multiple times with write_stdin checks (5-second intervals) before completion, showing inefficiency in monitoring async operations Learnings Key Learnings Vectorization of tensor operations eliminates Python loop overhead and enables GPU parallelism: BasicContrastive contrastive loss refactor from per-node loop to batch operations yielded 10-50x speedup with identical mathematical behavior Zero-padding is NOT neutral for sequence models with positional encodings where it actively breaks semantic meaning: Crossformer’s DSW_embedding assumes continuous seg_len time steps; zero-padded patches with irregular gaps mislead model to learn ‘zero patterns’ instead of temporal dependencies Code duplication hides bugs because fixes don’t propagate: 12 Error Skill files had inconsistent frame thresholds (e08: 30 vs. standard 20) that code review revealed as unintentional; extracting to shared helpers forces consistency Data provenance tracking must be explicit through every conversion pipeline step: HDF5→LeRobot conversion loses source_file+demo_idx mapping; solution is recording conversion_index.json during conversion to enable bridge-key linkage with generation-time metadata For irregular time-series with non-uniform sampling intervals: (1) interpolation to uniform grid preserves continuity, (2) time window aggregation avoids zero-padding, (3) time-aware models (GRU-D, BRITS, NAOMI) handle gaps natively, (4) adding time delta as feature helps uniform models; never use zero-padding for models with segment continuity assumptions ‘Uniform sampling’ in time-series contexts typically means equal temporal spacing (constant delta t), not balanced sample distribution across categories; critical for sequence models like Transformers that assume regular timesteps Error Recovery Benchmark implements 5-stage pipeline: (1) clean demo context-replay, (2) error skill injection with post-stabilization, (3) human recovery teleop (recording only this stage), (4) trajectory segmentation by RBG heuristics, (5) MimicGen object-centric warping; training data excludes error-injection actions to prevent learning failure modes Faithful algorithm implementation requires understanding full execution model, not just data transformation: MimicGen’s success came from waypoint-level closed-loop correction per subtask, which can’t be replicated by better open-loop warping alone Ollama service can start on-demand and auto-close through process management, suitable for non-persistent use cases; HTTP API connectivity detection can serve as startup signal Multi-round requirement clarification (request_user_input) effectively narrows open-ended tasks to clear technical choices, but each round should focus on key decision points rather than minutiae Local LLM services (Ollama) require robust retry mechanisms for production batch processing due to transient failures Adversarial code review catches issues missed in forward implementation: critic subagent identified in-place mutation semantics, pattern variations, and defensive check locations that main agent overlooked 1-to-N augmented dataset mapping requires aggregation across variant indices: for each clean episode, gather all bddl_idx (0-9 OSS variants) where orig_ep_idx matches, then convert aug_demo_idx to aug_lerobot_ep via conversion_index; some variants fail generation so actual N ≤ 10 Always cross-validate aggregated metrics with ground truth: log parsing bugs can propagate incorrect statistics; verify against direct file counts (HDF5 demo_N groups) and structured reports (generation_report.json) MimicGen-style data augmentation for robot manipulation uses object-centric coordinate transforms: source demo trajectories are warped relative to target object poses, enabling generalization across scene configurations while preserving action semantics When single demo source has only 1 successful trajectory per error subtype, even perfect augmentation will have limited scene coverage; 0% results reflected insufficient source diversity + algorithm mismatch, not just one root cause Data format migrations are safer when execution is deterministic: saved (initial_state, actions) → replay in sim → reconstruct missing fields (target_poses, object_orientations); enables backward-compatible augmentation without recollecting demos Git merge conflict ours/theirs semantics are reversed in different scenarios: git merge theirs is merged branch, git stash pop theirs is stash version, easily confused Claude Code’s settings.local.json supports wildcard permissions (like Bash(git:*)), can configure permissive strategy for specific projects without affecting global settings Python’s transformers library is large and requires GPU dependencies; switching to Ollama local service can reduce website extra from torch+transformers+accelerate to just Pillow Windows sandbox command length has hard limit; apply_patch tool fails on patches exceeding certain size (CreateProcessAsUserW error 206), need to split into multiple small patches Marker_single OCR workflow: –force_ocr for full OCR (ignore embedded text), –disable_multiprocessing and –disable_tqdm for cleaner output in automation Config-driven behavior boundaries (generator_mode: faithful_mimicgen vs legacy_replay) enable architecture changes without abandoning old paths, useful for A/B validation and gradual migration Markdown translation needs to preserve structural elements including frontmatter, code blocks, images, URLs, DOI, page markers; also need to detect source text language to avoid redundant translation For scripts without heavy conda dependencies, system Python is more stable than conda run, especially for concurrent or long-running tasks Practical Learnings Third-party upstream repository local modifications should prioritize retaining remote version during pull; locally deleted files in conflicts use git checkout –ours to restore remote content Filesystem symlinks provide zero-overhead bidirectional sync for dual-boot systems: replacing ~/Desktop/algorithms with symlink to Windows partition eliminates sync lag and guarantees single source of truth Lustre filesystem mountpoints can appear as regular files (size 0, mode 0444) but behave as immutable filesystem boundaries; when direct configuration files are blocked, hierarchical inheritance mechanisms (AGENTS.md, .gitignore-style configs) provide robust alternatives Chunk size tuning is critical for local LLM translation: smaller chunks (2000 vs 4000 chars) improve stability at cost of more API calls Local LLM translation (Ollama HY-MT1.5-7B) is production-viable for technical content: 74-file batch translation completed in 57 minutes with good quality; no API costs or rate limits Conversation Summaries MIHD Spatial Omics Benchmark ✅ Code optimization, documentation, and security review 22:19:05.933 | claude_code Used /optimize skill to scan MIHD project identifying 6 priority optimizations (P0-P3), highest impact being vectorizing BasicContrastive contrastive loss loop for 10-50x speedup. Used /ccplan with adversarial review to design implementation plan; critic identified in-place mutation issues and pattern variations. Generated comprehensive OVERVIEW.md with 6 sections using /summarize skill. Performed security analysis of uncommitted changes checking input validation, unsafe deserialization, code injection; all findings (np.load pickle, subprocess arguments, path construction) dismissed as false positives in local HPC research context. Installed superpowers plugin after initial failed attempts with claude-plugins-official.
error_recovery_benchmark ✅ Comprehensive P0+P1 code refactoring 21:55:31.103 | claude_code Scanned entire error_benchmark/ directory identifying 12 optimization categories. Implemented 6 FEATs: added 3 BaseErrorSkill helpers (check_remaining_frames, normalize_direction, random_direction), wrote 10 unit tests, refactored 12 skill files eliminating 130+ lines duplication, fixed HDF5 resource leaks in 4 scripts (_merge_rounds.py, _check_valid_source.py, parallel_mimicgen_generate.py, augment_success_demos.py), narrowed exception handlers in vla_server, resolved div-by-zero risks. Final validation: 226/229 tests passing (3 pre-existing failures unrelated).
Error Recovery Benchmark ✅ Deep technical Q&A on recovery demo collection and augmentation 18:28:42.026 | codex User asked 3 detailed questions about recovery data pipeline. AI performed extensive code archaeology (20+ files) to provide line-numbered answers: (1) robot movement during teleop is due to default replay_animation showing injection process, can be disabled to load post_sim_state directly; (2) training uses only recovery segments (post-injection teleop actions), not error-causing steps, confirmed via NPZ inspection showing recovery demo’s first state equals scene’s post_sim_state; (3) MimicGen augmentation uses object-centric warping with scene/cross-degree strategies, documented in 3_mimicgen_recovery_augment.py. User confirmed demos with pre-state misalignment were already deleted.
✅ MimicGen demo generation results and manual collection planning 00:09:08.298 | claude_code User followed up on MimicGen experiment status; AI initially reported incorrect 398 success demos due to buggy log aggregation script. After debugging, corrected to 739/1365 (54.1% success, 626 failed). Discussed manual collection strategy for failed scenes. User decided on direct manual collection of all 626 scenes with HDF5 format compatibility. Confirmed existing collection pipeline needs adaptation for 1:1 scene→demo pairing instead of subtype-quota approach. Outlined implementation plan: identify_failed_scenes.py, collect_success_from_error.py (adapted from existing script), convert_teleop_to_hdf5.py for NPZ→HDF5 merge.
✅ Codex approval workflow optimization for exploratory development 21:53:38.303 | codex User requested minimal confirmations for non-destructive operations in benchmark repository tree, preserving only deletion-related approvals. AI planned to create .codex/config.toml with approval_policy=‘never’ and sandbox_mode=‘workspace-write’, but discovered .codex is read-only Lustre mountpoint (confirmed via findmnt). Pivoted to repository-root AGENTS.md, adding ‘Codex Execution Policy’ section defining high-autonomy rules: default approval for read/write/refactor/test, explicit confirmation only for rm/git-clean/reset-hard. Configuration inherits to all subdirectories.
error recovery benchmark ✅ Faithful MimicGen recovery augmentation rewrite 21:57:24.123 | codex User asked AI to validate diagnosis from previous conversation claiming 0% augmentation failures were due to ‘warping can’t adapt’. AI determined conclusion was incorrect by code inspection: some failing subtypes had dominant NO_WARP segments (position_error_D1: 150/192 steps in correct_position). Root cause was incomplete warping coverage and fundamental architecture mismatch between action-replay (current) vs target-pose waypoint execution (MimicGen standard). Implemented faithful MimicGen port: target-pose recording in collection, backfill migration script (backfill_recovery_target_poses.py) for 23 stack demos, waypoint-based augmenter with per-subtask object references, updated configs/tests. Verified with unit tests (40 passed) and live runs: successfully generated augmented NPZs with target_poses for grasp_wrong_pose_D1 and position_error_D1.
gadget website ✅ Batch translation completion (74 files) 00:32:17.563 | claude_code Completed translation of all remaining website content using HY-MT1.5-7B local model: 42 bugJournal/daily, 9 weekly summaries, 21 research profiles, 2 misc files. Total runtime 57 minutes. Spot-checked translation quality across daily journals and research profiles confirming good quality.
✅ Windows/Ubuntu dual-boot time synchronization 03:18:19.239 | claude_code Diagnosed RTC timezone conflict causing persistent time drift between Windows and Ubuntu. Checked timedatectl showing Ubuntu configured with RTC in local TZ. Recommended solution: revert Ubuntu to UTC (timedatectl set-local-rtc 0) and configure Windows registry to use UTC (RealTimeIsUniversal=1).
TokenMonitor ✅ Upstream merge from Michael-OvO/TokenMonitor 21:20:39.779 | claude_code Fetched upstream changes showing 4 new commits: PR #4 merge, v0.7.0 bump, v0.7.1 rate limit fix, GitHub Actions update. Executed git merge upstream/main successfully with no conflicts. Local commit (c0f977f linux floatball bug fix) preserved.
Cross-platform development environment ✅ Ubuntu/Windows folder synchronization via symlink 03:20:27.291 | claude_code User needed to sync ~/Desktop/algorithms (Ubuntu) with /run/media/thomas/Windows/Users/tongt/Documents/GitHub. Solution: copied Ubuntu versions (Life-Copilot, TokenMonitor, gadget) to Windows partition overwriting Windows versions, backed up Ubuntu folder, created symlink. Now both systems share single source of truth.
BOSS ✅ PairedDataset episode mapping repair 03:37:02.563 | claude_code User hit disk quota exceeded (1.9T used, 112GB in augmentation data). Main focus shifted to fixing PairedDataset mapping incompatibility: episode_mapping.json from generation uses {task_name: {bddl_idx: […]}} format but PairedDataset expects {clean_ep_idx: [{aug_ep, overlap}]}. Root cause: HDF5→LeRobot conversion loses source mapping. Solution implemented: (1) modified libero2lerobot_parallel.py and libero2lerobot.py to output conversion_index.json recording source_file+demo_idx→lerobot_ep mapping, (2) created build_paired_mapping.py for three-way association (clean conversion + aug conversion + episode mapping), (3) documented full 5-step pipeline from DA generation to training. Mapping logic handles 1-to-N relationship (1 clean → up to 10 OSS variants, some may fail).
Crossformer ✅ Data processing comparison and architecture mismatch diagnosis 08:24:06.299 | claude_code User compared two Crossformer implementations with dramatically different results (MSE 9/MAE 1.3 vs MSE≈0.5/MAE≈0.5). Through detailed code comparison, identified major differences: (1) LIPM uses patch-based irregular sampling with zero-padding, master uses uniform sampling with StandardScaler; (2) data scales differ (hundreds vs 154k samples); (3) normalization absent in first version; (4) different model architectures (IR head vs standard). User raised critical insight: ‘patch with zero-padding is incompatible with Crossformer’s uniform sampling assumption - does this still have value?’ Diagnosed fundamental architecture mismatch: Crossformer’s DSW_embedding assumes continuous segments, but zero-padding breaks temporal continuity and misleads model. Created recommendations for irregular time-series: interpolation, aggregation, or use specialized models (GRU-D, BRITS). Conclusion: second version better because of architecture match + normalization + larger data.
Crossformer Time-Series Battery Prediction ✅ Temporal uniform resampling of battery cycle data 08:50:32.743 | claude_code User requested time-uniform resampling of LiPM battery dataset (154K rows, 48 batteries). AI initially misunderstood as sample-distribution balancing, then corrected to temporal interpolation after clarification. Analyzed chargetime distribution (mean 0.36s, std 0.48s, only 35.83% with interval=1) and generated 3 uniformly-sampled versions (1s/5s/10s intervals) via scipy linear interpolation. Final dataset: 55,283 rows with perfect temporal uniformity (std=0.0), validated across all batteries.
GitHub Workspace Management ✅ Multi-repo Git sync: check & push LifeCopilot/gadget/ECC 03:48:50.890 | claude_code Checked local vs remote diff across 5 repos. Found LifeCopilot 16 commits ahead on feature branch with uncommitted changes, everything-claude-code 420 commits behind, gadget with minor changes. User requested full sync. Committed and pushed gadget (website scripts), LifeCopilot (all changes including data/logs), pulled and merged everything-claude-code with conflict resolution using git checkout –ours to retain remote version for 18 deleted skills files and package files.
Claude Code Configuration ✅ Optimize permissions for exploratory workspace 04:09:32.747 | claude_code User complained about excessive confirmation prompts in Claude Code. Requested auto-allow for all operations except deletions in this exploratory workspace. Updated settings.local.json with broad allow rules covering all file tools, agents, and common CLI commands (git/python/npm/node/etc). Noted that Bash(git:*) would match destructive operations, offered to split but user accepted tradeoff.
OCR ✅ Generate AGENTS.md and integrate Ollama translation 02:43:39.535 | codex Generated 308-word AGENTS.md documenting OCR workflow (marker-pdf commands, weekly notes structure, Python helper scripts). Noted local .git absence prevented commit convention inference. User requested Ollama-based translation using HY-MT1.5-1.8B. After 5 rounds of clarification (target/format/output/language), implemented translate_ocr_with_ollama.py for bilingual OCR markdown. Installed Ollama via winget, pulled model, implemented on-demand serve startup, Markdown chunking, CJK detection, and structure preservation (frontmatter, code blocks, images, page markers). Verified with syntax check and API smoke test.
✅ Batch OCR and translation of Herbs and Roots chapters 03:28:07.078 | codex User requested OCR and translation for three PDF chapters (FIVE_Oriental_Healers, SIX_Decline, SEVEN_Rediscovery). AI implemented full pipeline: created output directories, ran marker_single OCR with –force_ocr flag (overcoming sandbox network restrictions for Surya model download), added retry mechanism (4 attempts with 5s backoff) to translation script for Ollama instability, switched from conda run to system Python for stability, reduced chunk size to 2000 chars. All three chapters successfully processed generating bilingual translations (85KB, 45KB, 67KB raw + translated versions).
gadget ✅ Migrate all translation paths from transformers to Ollama 02:50:03.816 | codex User requested switching gadget translation from transformers to Ollama with on-demand startup (not persistent). Analyzed three translation entry points: bilingual.py, translate_content.py, translate_site_batch.py. Implemented shared common/ollama.py (HTTP client with autostart, normalize_ollama_host, resolve_translation_model, build_translation_options) and common/translation.py (translate_markdown_document with fragment protection, detect_language, zh_path, clean_translated_document), rewrote all three paths, removed transformers dependency (deleted translate_batch.py), updated all callers (summarize/research), added unit tests. First large patch failed due to Windows command length limit; split into multiple small patches. Fixed zh_path naming conflict in bilingual.py. Verified with py_compile, unittest, and –dry-run (identified 4 .zh.md files with English content as legacy issue). Reduced website extra deps from torch+transformers+accelerate to just Pillow.
Token Usage Total: $16.08 — 28.3M tokens
summary: Completed cross-platform code optimization and translation automation across HPC/robotics projects, debugged three ML data pipelines, migrated translation infrastructure to local Ollama, and rewrote robotics augmentation system from action replay to target-pose execution
title: Bug Journal 2026-04-08

content:
Daily Report — 2026-04-07 Daily Overview What was done: Conducted a comprehensive review of the website/update.sh script, fixed 4 bugs in the script, and helped set up a complete Hugo site deployment environment. This allowed the website update process to recover from continuous failures at exit codes 1/127/128 to a state where all 7 steps were successfully completed. How it was done: Implemented a hierarchical verification strategy: first reviewed the code, then checked dependencies, and finally ran the script. This approach helped identify missing components (Hugo, themes, deployment repository) and logical bugs (duplicate PIDs, missing files, directory protection issues). After manually installing missing components, multiple verifications were performed. Impact: The website’s CI pipeline is now functional. Hugo build and GitHub Pages deployment paths are connected, allowing content to be published without manual intervention. Additionally, silent errors in the video compression branch were identified and fixed, preventing future content loss. Fixed 4 bugs in the website/update.sh script and set up a complete Hugo deployment environment. The website update process has recovered from complete failure to a state where all 7 steps were successfully completed. A full daily/weekly MCP synchronization process has also been initiated.
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Verified the availability of the website/update.sh script — The system checked all dependencies and directory structures of update.sh. Identified several critical and minor issues, including missing Hugo, themes/PaperMod/, public/ repository, and PNG quant/HandBrakeCLI. ✅ Fixed bugs in the update.sh script — Fixed 4 bugs: inconsistent step numbering (1/6→1/7), duplicate video compression PIDs added to the pids array, HandBrakeCLI branch compression did not replace original files, and find commands triggered set -e termination when static/images and static/videos directories were missing. Implementation & Fixes ✅ Set up a complete Hugo deployment environment — This was completed by the user: installed Hugo extended v0.154.5, cloned the PaperMod theme to themes/PaperMod, cloned tzj2006.github.io to public/, and configured GitHub authentication. 🔄 Initiated full daily/weekly/monthly MCP synchronization and merging — Checked the status using the MCP gadget tool. Found that there were sessions for 6 dates (Apr 1-5, Apr 7), but no reports or weekly reports. Started exporting data for Apr 7, and will need to merge data from all dates before generating weekly reports. Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Missing Hugo, causing the script to terminate at Step 5 with “hugo: command not found” (exit code 127) Solution: The user installed Hugo extended v0.154.5 using the system package manager.
Key Insight: set -e will directly terminate the script if any command fails. The lack of Hugo prevented the 7-step process from reaching the critical build stage.
2. Themes/PaperMod/ and public/ directories were missing, preventing Hugo build and deployment Solution: The user cloned the PaperMod theme and tzj2006.github.io deployment repository.
Key Insight: Hugo site deployment depends on three independent components: the Hugo binary, theme repository, and deployment Git repository. Any missing component will cause a silent or terminated failure. These conditions should be clearly stated in the documentation.
General Issues 3. static/videos directory was missing, causing find commands to return errors and set -e to terminate Step 4 unexpectedly Solution: Added a directory existence check before the find command ([ -d “$DIR” ]). If the directory was missing, skip this step.
Key Insight: Operations on optional/empty directories must be protected in the set -e script. Otherwise, normal “directory empty” scenarios may cause unexpected script termination.
4. HandBrakeCLI nested branch PIDs were added to the pids array, and files were not replaced after compression (output was .hb.mp4) Solution: Moved pids+=($!) and echo statements to their respective if/else branches. Added && mv after HandBrakeCLI commands to replace files.
Key Insight: The PID tracking logic for parallel background tasks must be carefully aligned with the if/else control flow. Putting common code outside branches can lead to duplicate execution and silent file replacement errors.
5. git push failed due to insufficient GitHub authentication (could not read Username for ‘[[[HYMTPH_0]]’) Solution: The user configured SSH keys or gh auth and re-run the script. The script passed completely successfully.
Key Insight: HTTPS remotes require pre-configured credentials in non-interactive shells. The deployment repository should use SSH remotes to avoid environmental differences.
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Script verification strategy Role Approach Human The user proposed verification tasks without specifying methods, expecting to know whether the script would work properly. AI AI preferred static analysis: first reviewed the script and CLAUDE.md to understand the architecture, then checked external dependencies, directory structures, and logical correctness. Finally, the script was run for verification. Difference Analysis: AI’s hierarchical verification detected all critical issues before running, avoiding multiple blind runs. However, AI couldn’t handle environment issues at the sudo level, ultimately requiring manual intervention by the user.
Implementation Level Hugo installation method Role Approach Human The user chose to install via the system package manager (ubuntu apt), obtaining v0.154.5. AI AI suggested using snap or downloading pre-compiled binaries from GitHub releases. However, this was not possible due to lack of sudo privileges. Difference Analysis: The user chose the simpler system package method, which was also a newer version than the one suggested by AI. AI’s suggestion focused more on version control and cross-distribution compatibility, which might have been too complex for local development environments.
AI Limitations General Limitations Unable to execute commands that require sudo privileges (installing Hugo, pngquant, etc.). Only able to generate scripts for the user to execute manually. Unable to fully set up the environment. Unable to configure GitHub authentication (SSH keys or gh auth). This prevented the first full deployment from running. The user had to intervene manually. Parallel tool calls failed multiple times during this session due to race conditions. It was necessary to switch to serial calls, which significantly reduced the efficiency of the verification process. Learnings Key Learnings Hugo site deployment requires three independent dependencies to be met simultaneously: the Hugo binary, theme repository, and deployment Git repository. Any missing dependency will cause failures at different stages. These conditions should be clearly listed in the project documentation. Practical Learnings In the bash set -e script, find operations on optional directories must include a directory existence check. Otherwise, normal “directory not created” scenarios may cause unexpected script termination. Code for parallel background tasks (& + pids tracking) must be carefully aligned with the if/else control flow. Putting common code outside branches can lead to duplicate PID tracking and silent file replacement errors. Conversation Summaries Gadget Website ✅ Verified and fixed critical environment issues and logical bugs in the website/update.sh script. Fully established the Hugo deployment process. 04:12:15.435 | claude_code The user requested verification of whether the website update script could run properly. AI used a hierarchical approach to identify three critical issues: missing Hugo, missing themes/PaperMod/, and missing public/ repository. It also identified four logical bugs: inconsistent step numbering, duplicate video PID tracking, HandBrakeCLI branch compression without file replacement, and set -e termination due to missing optional directories. After the user installed the required dependencies and configured GitHub authentication, the script passed all 7 steps successfully. Hugo build and GitHub Pages deployment were also verified.
Gadget Summary 🔄 Initiated full daily/weekly/monthly MCP synchronization and merging process through the MCP gadget tool. 04:25:03.485 | claude_code The user requested simultaneous synchronization and merging of daily/weekly/monthly reports. AI checked the status using the MCP gadget. It was found that there were sessions for 6 dates (Apr 1-5, Apr 7), but no reports or weekly reports. Considering the dependency order from day to week to month, AI decided to export data for Apr 7 first, then merge it in bulk. The sessions ended while the export was ongoing, so the subsequent process was not completed.
