content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-20 Daily Overview What was done: On MacBook, systematically maintain gadget project infrastructure and data management: batch-generate 2026-03-19 daily reports via AI conversation summarization, update 6 README/tutorial files to reflect outputs/ unified directory structure, verify all 10 documentation files line-by-line against actual code, discover and fix rclone sync-all bug (only downloading logs not reports), design and execute personal data removal from git history (452 files, 94MB), and establish centralized rclone sync infrastructure How it was done: Using recursive daily_summary.py export/merge pipeline for report generation; batch Edit tool for documentation updates; 3 parallel Explore agents for exhaustive code-vs-docs verification; implementing _rclone_download_reports() to fix sync-all logic; git rm –cached + git filter-repo for history rewriting; unified rclone configuration with gdrive:gadget remote path Impact: Completed all meta-work for 2026-03-19 reporting; documentation fully consistent with codebase (fixed 7 discrepancies); eliminated root cause of repeated LLM calls in sync-all (finalized check now works correctly); personal data completely removed from git history with GitHub force-pushed (repo 22MB→516KB); established clear data privacy boundary (452 files now synced via rclone instead of git) DCC What was done: No activity today How it was done: N/A Impact: N/A MacBook What was done: Completed gadget repository documentation maintenance, rclone sync architecture fix, personal file removal from git, and 2026-03-19 multi-device report processing How it was done: Updated 6 README/tutorial files with batch Edit (~220 insertions/113 deletions); launched 3 parallel Explore agents for documentation verification; implemented _rclone_download_reports() to fix sync-all; used AskUserQuestion to confirm rclone path (gdrive:gadget), git history rewrite risks, and Search.md attribution; executed 7-step git cleanup plan: updated .gitignore, git rm –cached 435 files, created sync.py wrapper, git filter-repo history purge, migrated rclone remote paths, force-pushed to GitHub, first full rclone data upload; restored .gitkeep files post-filter-repo Impact: Fixed finalized check failure causing repeated LLM consumption in sync-all; documentation matches codebase reality (7 numeric/path discrepancies corrected); designed complete data privacy boundary solution (452 files removed from git + 24 commits history rewritten + rclone unified management); repo size reduced 22MB→516KB; provided clear implementation path for future maintenance TzJsDesktop What was done: No activity today How it was done: N/A Impact: N/A tianhe What was done: No activity today How it was done: N/A Impact: N/A On MacBook with gadget project: batch-generated 2026-03-19 daily reports, comprehensively updated and verified all documentation against codebase (fixed 7 discrepancies), discovered and fixed rclone sync-all bug (missing reports download causing repeated LLM calls), and executed complete git history cleanup removing 452 personal files (94MB) with rclone sync infrastructure establishment (repo size reduced from 22MB to 516KB)
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Design and execute personal data removal from git history + rclone sync infrastructure — Executed comprehensive 8-step plan: (1) Confirmed via AskUserQuestion: use gdrive:gadget unified remote path, keep Search.md in git, accept git history rewrite risks; (2) Updated .gitignore with 17 personal data rules; (3) git rm –cached 435 files (summarize logs/reports, website content, research data, test results) while keeping local copies; (4) Created sync.py with push/pull/status/config commands supporting –dry-run and –category flags for 4 data categories (summarize, website, research, test); (5) git filter-repo removed personal data from all 24 commits (repo 22MB→516KB); (6) Migrated existing rclone data from gdrive:summarize/ to gdrive:gadget/; (7) Force-pushed rewritten history to GitHub; (8) First full rclone push of all personal data. Restored .gitkeep files post-filter-repo. Established clear boundary: git manages public code/config, rclone manages personal data/runtime outputs ✅ Fix rclone sync-all bug: only downloading logs not reports — Discovered that _cmd_merge_sync_all only called _rclone_download_logs() but not reports, causing finalized status checks to fail on local machine when other devices had already generated finalized reports. This led to repeated LLM calls. Implemented _rclone_download_reports() function to download remote reports before checking finalized status, ensuring distributed collaboration state synchronization works correctly ✅ Comprehensive line-by-line documentation verification against codebase — Launched 3 parallel Explore agents to verify every factual claim in 10 documentation files (root, summarize, research, test, website CLAUDE.md/README.md/tutorial files) against actual codebase. Identified 7 discrepancies: test/CLAUDE.md CPU/GPU matrix_size values swapped (CPU single-core should be 2048 not 8192, GPU should be 8192 not 2048), summarize/CLAUDE.md cache paths outdated, research/CLAUDE.md ’two-stage’ vs ’three-stage’ inconsistency + output_dir default wrong, student_discovery.py comment ‘3-6’ vs code ‘3-7’. All fixed via batch Edit tool + grep zero-residue verification. Also scanned for personal file inventory (452 files: summarize logs 133/86MB + reports 80/1.7MB, website content 186/4.9MB, research cache/projects/reports 60/560KB, test results/data 40/852KB, total ~94MB) ✅ Update all README.md and tutorial.md files for outputs/ migration — Updated 6 files (root README/CLAUDE.md, summarize/README+tutorial, test/README+benchmark/README, research/TUTORIAL) to reflect: output paths moved to unified outputs/{logs,reports,cache,data}// tree, new CLI flags (–export-past, –force, –affiliation, –conference, –source, etc.), inter-tool dependencies now via common/ package, sync.py as new top-level tool. ~220 insertions, 113 deletions across all files. Removed outdated warnings about hardcoded user paths after verifying all code uses Path(file).resolve().parent dynamic resolution Implementation & Fixes ✅ Batch generate 2026-03-19 daily reports from multi-device AI conversation logs — Recursively ran daily_summary.py to: (1) parse conversation logs from DCC, MacBook, TzJsDesktop, tianhe covering 5 projects (MIHD, CalendarPro/Life Copilot, Error Recovery Benchmark, robobrain_pi, gadget), (2) generate per-project JSON summaries with tasks/problems/human-vs-AI analysis, (3) merge 4 project summaries into comprehensive daily report with global + per-device overviews. Also processed historical reports for tianhe (Error Recovery Benchmark: SpaceMouse pipeline, MimicGen warping bug, three-pool architecture) and cross-device reports (CalendarPro query routing, robobrain_pi BCE weighting, gadget finalize troubleshooting) Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Personal data files (conversation logs, AI summaries, blog content, research cache, benchmark results) tracked by git and pushed to GitHub, blurring code repository vs data storage boundary with privacy risks Solution: Designed three-layer cleanup: (1) Current working directory: git rm –cached to remove tracking; (2) git filter-repo rewrite all 24 commit history; (3) After force-push, use rclone unified management for personal files (remote path gdrive:gadget). Reduced repo from 22MB to 516KB
Key Insight: Git and rclone responsibility boundary must be clearly defined: git manages public code and reproducible config, rclone manages personal data and runtime outputs. Mixing causes repo bloat (94MB personal data), privacy leaks, and collaboration difficulties. Clear separation establishes data privacy boundary
2. When user ran merge on other devices generating finalized reports, local merge –sync-all still judged all dates as non-finalized, causing repeated LLM calls Solution: AI discovered _rclone_download_logs only downloads logs not reports, causing local finalized checks to fail. Implemented _rclone_download_reports() to pull existing reports from remote before sync-all finalized status checks
Key Insight: In distributed collaboration scenarios, state synchronization must sync both inputs (logs) and outputs (reports), otherwise duplicate-processing logic fails. Complete state sync prevents resource waste (repeated LLM consumption)
3. test/CLAUDE.md documented CPU BLAS matrix_size as 8192 (single-core) and 4096 (all-cores), GPU as 2048 — all wrong Solution: Verified actual code: CpuSingleCoreBLASBenchmark.MATRIX_SIZE=2048, CpuAllCoresBenchmark.MATRIX_SIZE=4096, GpuBenchmark.MATRIX_SIZE=8192. Updated documentation to match reality
Key Insight: Documentation-code drift accumulates silently over time; line-by-line verification with parallel agents catches numeric value errors that are easy to miss in casual reading. Systematic exhaustive verification necessary for correctness
General Issues 4. git filter-repo deleted .gitkeep files along with filtered directories, breaking directory structure preservation Solution: Recreated .gitkeep files (research/cache/, research/projects/, research/reports/) with touch + git add -f, committed as separate ‘restore directory structure’ commit after filter-repo + force-push
Key Insight: git filter-repo’s –invert-paths removes entire directory trees including structural files; .gitkeep restoration must be done as post-filter-repo commit, not pre-filter addition
5. research/CLAUDE.md line 74 said ‘Two-stage evaluation pipeline’ but line 79 title was ‘Three-Stage Evaluation Pipeline’ — internal contradiction Solution: Verified code implements 3 stages (screening → deep analysis → citation impact); updated line 74 to ‘Three-stage’, also batch-replaced all 7 occurrences of ‘两阶段’ → ‘三阶段’ in research_scout.py comments
Key Insight: Documentation self-contradictions (same file, different sections) indicate historical evolution not fully reconciled; grep-based batch correction ensures no residual inconsistency
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Data management architecture: long-term planning vs short-term patching Role Approach Human After AI fixed sync-all bug, user immediately raised higher-level requirement: ‘All files related to me should be synced by rclone, GitHub should not have copies of them’ — fundamentally redefining data boundaries AI AI initially just patch-fixed by adding _rclone_download_reports function, did not proactively examine overall architecture reasonableness. Only conducted comprehensive exploration and solution design after user raised the issue Difference Analysis: User has stronger architectural foresight and data privacy awareness; AI tends to patch problems within current framework rather than questioning the framework itself. Demonstrates human advantage in strategic planning
git filter-repo strategy: incremental cleanup vs full rewrite Role Approach Human User directly specified ‘use git filter-repo + force push’ without considering incremental git rm approach for new commits only AI AI proposed Step 2 (git rm –cached for new commit) + Step 4 (filter-repo for history) as two-phase approach, noting filter-repo’s irreversibility Difference Analysis: Human prioritizes clean slate (entire history purged of personal data, repo 22MB→516KB); AI adds safety layer (git rm first to create checkpoint, then destructive operation). Combined approach provided best of both: clean history + .git backup safety net
rclone sync strategy completeness: input-only vs full state sync Role Approach Human User discovered sync-all only downloads logs, immediately pointed out ‘reports also need to be pulled down’, directly inferring root cause from problem symptom AI AI initially only implemented _rclone_download_logs; after user feedback, realized finalized check depends on reports files and added _rclone_download_reports Difference Analysis: User has more comprehensive understanding of distributed collaboration state sync requirements; AI’s initial implementation only covered input sync (logs) but missed output sync (reports), requiring user correction. Shows human advantage in understanding full state synchronization needs
Documentation verification scope decision: targeted vs exhaustive Role Approach Human User requested ‘check all documentation files and verify every sentence is correct’ — open-ended comprehensive audit without pre-filtering AI AI initially proposed verifying specific sections or using sampling; after user insistence, launched 3 parallel Explore agents to cover all 10 files exhaustively Difference Analysis: Human intuition: systematic exhaustive verification catches accumulation of small drifts that targeted checks miss; AI default: optimize for efficiency by focusing on likely problem areas. Human approach found 7 real issues (CPU/GPU matrix_size values, cache paths, stage count) that would slip through targeted review
AI Limitations Critical Limitations When designing rclone sync logic, AI failed to foresee finalized check’s dependency on reports in sync-all scenario, requiring user to explicitly point out ‘reports also need to be pulled down’. Lacked forward-thinking on distributed collaboration state sync completeness AI did not initially suggest exhaustive line-by-line documentation verification until user explicitly requested ‘check every sentence’; default mode was targeted/sampling-based review which would have missed numeric value errors (matrix_size, PhD year range) General Limitations AI launched 3 parallel Explore agents for codebase scanning (personal file inventory, rclone logic, git history), though comprehensive, efficiency may be suboptimal — this information could be quickly obtained through a few grep/ls/git commands, agent overhead possibly excessive AI did not predict .gitkeep files would be deleted by git filter-repo –invert-paths (directory structure preservation issue); required user observation post-filter-repo to restore them Learnings Key Learnings Git vs rclone responsibility boundary must be clearly defined: git manages reproducible public code and configuration (requirements.txt, scripts, CLAUDE.md), rclone manages personal data and runtime outputs (logs, reports, cache, test results, website content). Mixing causes repo bloat (94MB personal data), privacy risks, and collaboration obstacles. Clear separation establishes proper data privacy boundary Documentation-code drift is inevitable and accumulates silently over time; periodic exhaustive line-by-line verification (using parallel Explore agents for speed) is necessary to catch numeric values, path references, and internal contradictions that casual reading misses. Systematic verification prevents accumulation of small errors Distributed collaboration state synchronization design principle: must sync not only input data (logs), but also intermediate outputs (reports), otherwise logic depending on output state (like finalized checks) will fail across different devices. Complete state sync prevents resource waste (repeated LLM calls) git filter-repo is the correct tool for purging large files/sensitive data from git history (22MB→516KB repo size reduction), but requires full workflow: backup .git, run filter-repo, restore structural files (.gitkeep), force-push, update all clones. Risk management checklist: (1) must backup on all devices, (2) requires force-push, (3) all other clones will break and need re-clone. Should use explicit user confirmation process (AskUserQuestion) to ensure understanding of destructive consequences Practical Learnings When building centralized sync infrastructure (sync.py), supporting –dry-run and –category flags from day one enables safe incremental testing and selective sync without full data upload; rclone remote path migration (gdrive:summarize/ → gdrive:gadget/) is low-risk with atomic move operations. Configuration migration completeness checklist when modifying remote paths: must simultaneously update (1) summarize/daily_summary.py config.json reading, (2) research/research_scout.py configuration, (3) test/ sync logic, (4) website/ Hugo integration, (5) sync.py global config, (6) existing remote data migration script Conversation Summaries 🔄 rclone sync architecture fix and personal file removal from git planning 00:57:00.216 | claude_code User discovered merge –sync-all only downloads logs not reports, causing finalized check failure and repeated LLM calls. AI implemented _rclone_download_reports() fix. User further proposed completely removing all personal files (452 files, 94MB) from git and switching to rclone-exclusive management. AI explored codebase to identify personal file inventory, used AskUserQuestion to confirm rclone path (gdrive:gadget), Search.md attribution, and git history rewrite risks, generated 8-step implementation plan and entered Plan Mode

title: Bug Journal 2026-03-20

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-19 Daily Overview What was done: Advanced four parallel initiatives spanning spatial transcriptomics experiments (Harmony alignment revealing semantic-batch mixing trade-offs), robotic error recovery pipeline (training optimization through portable deployment achieving 139 passing tests), CalendarPro/Life Copilot architectural transformation (multi-intent classifier eliminating single-intent bottleneck, comprehensive codebase audit archiving 2400+ LOC, calendar query fixes, project rename), and knowledge management infrastructure expansion (gadget research tool 5-mode system upgrade with chunked long-text analysis supporting arbitrary-scale deep analysis, comprehensive Yuke Zhu academic trajectory profiling across 2012-2026 covering 18-100 papers through 10+ analysis iterations, and batch consolidation of 30+ days historical daily reports from Feb-Mar 2026) How it was done: Executed controlled experiments (Harmony post-fusion alignment, task completion weighted BCE loss), implemented core infrastructure (multi-intent LLM analyzer, coordinate transformation fixes, chunked long-text analysis with 150K chars/chunk and LLM merge, dynamic timeout formula 300s+120s/50K chars), conducted systematic refactoring (codebase audit archiving 2400+ LOC dead code, Phase 2 cleanup, 5-mode research tool redesign modifying 7 source files), and performed intensive batch processing operations (15-20 researcher profiles including 6 rounds of Yuke Zhu deep dives culminating in deep-wide mode 100-paper full-text analysis with 47-chunk segmentation, 30+ days historical reports consolidation with 12+ JSON repairs, finalize mechanism bug diagnosis) Impact: Discovered fundamental limitations of linear batch correction when embeddings have misaligned dimension semantics; unblocked 10→1000 demo augmentation path through MimicGen warping fix; eliminated CalendarPro’s single-intent architectural constraint and fixed critical bugs; achieved 139/139 passing tests for portable recovery deployment; established complete Yuke Zhu academic trajectory (h-index 56, 24,611 citations, evolution from data infrastructure builder → foundation model pioneer → world model architect); research tool capability transformed from ‘quick overview’ to ‘deep excavation’ supporting arbitrary-scale full-text analysis; consolidated 30+ days work archives establishing unified cross-device work record; identified and documented finalize mechanism deadlock causing infinite LLM API cost loop (3 device-dates with permanent _finalized=False) DCC What was done: MIHD spatial transcriptomics cross-sample alignment experiments, strategic planning for ARI improvement and batch effect reduction, Claude Code demo preparation for lab meeting; historical sessions served as data source for retrospective report generation covering Feb-Mar 2026 milestones (Visium HD adaptation, RM-IDEAL evaluation, scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiments, ~250K LOC dead code cleanup) How it was done: Implemented and evaluated post-fusion Harmony alignment on sections 151508-151673, launched multi-agent exploration for diagnosing clustering ARI gap (0.475 vs SOTA 0.66+) and cross-sample batch effects, designed 30-minute demo covering plan mode, permissions, and AI overconfidence examples using DLPFC data; historical work retrospectively analyzed through gadget summarize tool on TzJsDesktop Impact: Key finding: Harmony improves batch mixing metrics (ASW_batch +36%) but degrades niche-level semantic alignment due to dimension meaning misalignment from per-section training—batch correction and semantic alignment are distinct properties requiring joint training; delivered complete demo materials; decided dual-track approach for ARI and batch effect problems; Feb-Mar historical milestones comprehensively archived MacBook What was done: Error recovery collection environment setup with SpaceMouse integration debugging, remote file transfer, checkpoint upload to athena server; brief environment setup attempt interrupted; historical sessions served as data source for retrospective report generation covering gadget tool development and Desktop Video 4.1 features from Feb-Mar 2026 How it was done: Created recovery_collect conda environment, killed 3Dconnexion driver to release device lock, modified robosuite macros_private.py, fixed EnvWrapper.env attribute bug, downloaded portable package via scp, transferred 14GB checkpoint file; ran setup_env.sh to create recovery_collect conda environment installing mujoco/robosuite/mimicgen dependencies (session interrupted during environment creation); historical work retrospectively analyzed through gadget summarize tool on TzJsDesktop Impact: SpaceMouse successfully operational; discovered only 11/29 error subtypes have generated scenes; successfully transferred large model checkpoint for downstream work; environment setup incomplete; historical MacBook work (gadget summarize model switch to Sonnet, Desktop Video history feature, 4.1 屏保时钟透明毛玻璃修复) archived in structured reports TzJsDesktop What was done: Primary active device executing comprehensive knowledge management and system refactoring: CalendarPro/Life Copilot complete system transformation (multi-intent classifier, calendar query fixes, codebase health overhaul archiving 2400+ LOC, project rename), gadget research tool major upgrade (5-mode system architecture redesign with chunked long-text analysis), intensive academic profiling (Yuke Zhu 10+ analysis iterations culminating in deep-wide mode 100-paper full-text analysis with 47-chunk segmentation and 5-retry optimization), batch historical daily report consolidation (30+ dates spanning Feb-Mar 2026 across DCC/MacBook/TzJsDesktop/tianhe), JSON format repair pipeline (12+ repairs), finalize mechanism bug diagnosis, and tutorial documentation updates How it was done: Designed and implemented Claude CLI-based multi-intent analyzer replacing dual-verify+7-layer keyword hacks, fixed 3-layer calendar query bugs, conducted comprehensive codebase audit and phase-gated refactoring; modified 7 source files (analysis.py adding _MODE_CONFIG and chunking logic, prompts.py adding TRAJECTORY_MERGE_PROMPT, cli.py/research_scout.py/config.py/models.py updating choices) + 3 documentation files; implemented _chunk_papers_by_year greedy algorithm (target 150K chars/chunk), dynamic timeout formula (300s+120s/50K chars), and TRAJECTORY_MERGE_PROMPT for semantic integration; conducted 10+ Yuke Zhu analysis rounds with different paper subsets (from 1 paper to 100 full-text papers auto-segmented into 47 chunks with LLM merge); batch-invoked daily_summary.py export→merge workflow for 30+ historical dates; manually repaired JSON format errors (unescaped quotes, truncated fields, incomplete brackets); diagnosed two-tier finalize mechanism through code review; updated tutorial.md with actual device names Impact: Eliminated CalendarPro’s fundamental single-intent bottleneck (can now handle multi-action messages), restored calendar query functionality, reduced classification code complexity by ~200 lines, removed 2400+ LOC dead code while maintaining full test coverage (373-424 tests passing); research tool capability transformed from ‘quick overview’ to ‘deep excavation’—deep-wide mode supports arbitrary-scale full-text analysis through chunking+merging mechanism breaking single-LLM capacity limit; completed Yuke Zhu comprehensive academic profile revealing evolution from Visual Genome visual understanding → AI2-THOR embodied intelligence → robosuite/RoboTurk ecosystem building → MineDojo/Voyager LLM-driven embodied agents → geometric intermediate representations + GPU-native simulation + VLM alignment (2012-2025, 86-100 papers, 7 breakthrough works); consolidated 30+ days critical historical work records (CalendarPro three major upgrades, ErrorRecoveryBenchmark M5-M14 milestones, MIHD benchmark Visium HD adaptation and code cleanup, Desktop Video 4.1 release); identified 3 device-dates with permanent _finalized=False deadlock (2026-03-03 MacBook, 2026-03-07 MacBook/TzJsDesktop, 2026-03-15 tianhe) causing infinite LLM API cost loop; documented LLM JSON generation systematic format error patterns (special character escaping, long-text truncation, nested object incompleteness) tianhe What was done: Task completion model training optimization, error recovery collection portable deployment preparation, training scene generation with controlled magnitude distribution, MimicGen warping bug fix unblocking 10→1000 augmentation, human demo collection architecture design, git proxy configuration; historical sessions served as data source for retrospective report generation covering Feb-Mar 2026 milestones (Pi0.5 Phoenix training, BC-RNN baseline, ErrorRecoveryBenchmark M5-M14, robobrain_pi training monitoring improvements, LLaVA model conversion, LIBERO evaluation extension) How it was done: Applied positive sample weighting (pos_weight=10) to BCE loss balancing 4% positive samples, created sample_actions_with_completion to reuse VLM prefix features eliminating duplicate forward passes, implemented comprehensive recovery collection refactor fixing SpaceMouse initialization and rendering bugs, created generate_training_scenes.py with ControlledRng for deterministic magnitude sampling (fraction 0/9 to 9/9), fixed _replay_with_warping to actually apply pos_delta transformation, designed 3-tier data pools (Training 10/subtype → Augmentation 200/subtype → Evaluation 100+/subtype), switched git protocol from SSH to HTTPS with proxy (127.0.0.1:10087); historical work retrospectively analyzed through gadget summarize tool on TzJsDesktop Impact: Model training plateau resolved with balanced gradient contributions; created portable deployment package (252MB compressed) with 139 passing unit tests; enables collection of 1320 training scenes with uniform magnitude distribution; unblocked MimicGen augmentation path critical for 10→1000 demo generation (10 seeds × 200 targets × 50% success rate) through pos_delta fix; cleanly pulled 352 files via proxy; Feb-Mar historical tianhe core work comprehensively archived Conducted cross-device multi-project research and development: spatial transcriptomics Harmony alignment experiments revealing semantic-batch mixing trade-offs, robotic error recovery pipeline from training optimization through portable deployment (139 passing tests), CalendarPro/Life Copilot complete architectural refactoring eliminating single-intent bottleneck with multi-intent classifier, and comprehensive knowledge management infrastructure expansion including gadget research tool 5-mode system upgrade with chunked long-text analysis, Yuke Zhu complete academic trajectory profiling (2012-2026, 100+ papers across 10+ analysis iterations), and batch consolidation of 30+ days historical daily reports from Feb-Mar 2026
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Post-fusion Harmony alignment for cross-sample spatial transcriptomics query — Implemented –harmony flag in benchmark_rm_ideal.py, ran experiments on sections 151508-151673, discovered Harmony improves batch metrics but degrades semantic alignment due to non-linear dimension misalignment from per-section independent training ✅ Multi-intent classifier refactor for CalendarPro/Life Copilot — Replaced dual-verify + 7-layer keyword hacks with Claude CLI MultiIntentAnalyzer supporting multi-intent decomposition in single message. Added 30 tests, all 424 tests passing. Eliminates single-intent bottleneck. ✅ Gadget research tool 5-mode system architecture upgrade — Extended from 2 modes (fast/detailed) to 5 modes: fast (3 papers/year, 300-char abstract), normal (10/year, full abstract, new default), deep (10/year + fulltext), wide (unlimited + full abstract), deep-wide (unlimited + fulltext). Modified 7 source files (analysis.py adding _MODE_CONFIG and chunking logic, prompts.py adding TRAJECTORY_MERGE_PROMPT, cli.py/research_scout.py/config.py/models.py updating choices) + 3 documentation files (CLAUDE.md/TUTORIAL.md/parent CLAUDE.md) ✅ Chunked long-text analysis system implementation for超长prompt处理 — Implemented _chunk_papers_by_year function (greedy year-based grouping, per-paper splitting when single year too large, target 150K chars/chunk) and _trajectory_analysis orchestration function (detects prompt length, invokes chunking, invokes TRAJECTORY_MERGE_PROMPT to merge 47 chunk segments); added dynamic timeout formula (300s baseline + 120s per 50K chars); integrated with common/llm.py timeout parameter ✅ Yuke Zhu comprehensive academic trajectory analysis (10+ iterations + deep-wide complete profiling) — Conducted systematic year-stratified analysis of robotics researcher Yuke Zhu (UT Austin RPL + NVIDIA, h-index 56, 24,611 citations, 130 papers) through 10+ analysis rounds with different paper subsets: (1-9) Single papers and year-grouped subsets (Voyager, RelViT, S3O, 2022 three papers, 2021 five papers, 2023 scene reconstruction, 2025 two papers, 2020 three papers, 86-paper complete list 2012-2023); (10) Used newly implemented deep-wide mode for h-index=56 complete data: retrieved 100 papers full-text from ArXiv, auto-segmented into 47 temporal chunks, per-chunk LLM analysis, final merge into complete trajectory report (including 7 breakthroughs, research theme evolution, mentorship relationships). Experienced multiple iteration debugging (chunk size 60K→150K, timeout formula optimization), succeeded after 5 retries. Generated trajectory_summary, breakthroughs with mandatory why_not_before/impact/technical_insight dimensions, research_themes, methodology_evolution, and field_impact for each iteration. 🔄 Strategic planning for MIHD ARI improvement and batch effect reduction — Launched multi-agent exploration to diagnose clustering ARI gap (0.475 vs SOTA 0.66+) and cross-sample batch effect, decided to pursue both problems simultaneously starting with ablation experiments ✅ Fix CalendarPro calendar query three-layer bug — Fixed LLM prompt placement (BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT not INTENT_PROMPT_FRAGMENTS), added keyword fallback detection, fixed get_today_events include_past parameter, added Discord error feedback mechanism via _pending_warnings ✅ Conduct comprehensive CalendarPro codebase audit and Phase 2 refactoring — Mapped 29K LOC, identified 8 orphaned modules (~2400 LOC), archived unused code with README, merged duplicate parse_datetime_with_timezone, deleted broker_models.py and 3 orphaned config settings, fixed broken browser import. All 373 tests pass post-refactoring. ✅ Fix duplicate meal scheduling bug — Root cause: _check_meal_exists used display-format time string instead of ISO datetime; schedule_essentials excluded past events. Fixed both issues and cleaned 53 duplicates. ✅ MimicGen warping bug fix enabling 10→1000 augmentation — Fixed _replay_with_warping in 3_mimicgen_recovery_augment.py: pos_delta was computed but never applied to warped_actions. Now properly transforms trajectories to target object coordinates, enabling 10 seeds × 200 targets × 50% success = 1000 demos. ✅ Refactor recovery collection scripts for portable deployment — Fixed SpaceMouse initialization, added rendering support, implemented dynamic PROJECT_ROOT resolution. Created portable package (252MB compressed) with setup/run scripts. All 139 unit tests pass. ✅ Batch consolidation of 30+ days historical daily reports (Feb-Mar 2026) — Batch-generated approximately 30+ days of historical daily reports using gadget summarize tool chain: Processed multi-device conversation logs covering dates from 2026-02-02 through 2026-03-18 across DCC/MacBook/TzJsDesktop/tianhe devices. Covered MIHD (spatial transcriptomics), error-recovery-benchmark (VLA training), CalendarPro (intent routing), Desktop Video (UI development), gadget (tool chain iteration) projects. Each report includes structured daily_overview (global + per-device), tasks, problems_and_solutions, human_vs_ai, ai_limitations, learnings, and conversation_summaries. Most succeeded in merge, 4-5 dates failed due to API 32000 token output limit. ✅ Gadget summarize finalize mechanism bug diagnosis and documentation — Diagnosed critical two-tier finalize mechanism deadlock: log layer marks _finalized=True only when (target_date != today() AND conversation_count unchanged); report layer requires all_sources_finalized before marking report._finalized=True. Identified 3 historical device-dates with permanent _finalized=False (2026-03-03 MacBook, 2026-03-07 MacBook/TzJsDesktop, 2026-03-15 tianhe) causing infinite loop where sync-all repeatedly invokes LLM merge despite no new conversations. Root cause: initial export on same day (target_date == today()) or conversation count change left False flags synced via rclone, permanently blocking report finalization. Added (finalized) suffix to export output for diagnostic visibility. Solution documented: re-run export on affected device-dates to auto-mark True (date past + count unchanged triggers flag), or manually edit JSON _finalized field and rclone upload. ✅ Optimize task completion model training with weighted BCE loss — Diagnosed class imbalance (4% positive samples), implemented pos_weight=10 in BCE loss, added predict_task_completion method, optimized by reusing VLM prefix features ✅ Training scene generation pipeline with controlled magnitude distribution — Implemented generate_training_scenes.py with ControlledRng for deterministic magnitude sampling (fraction 0/9 to 9/9), enabling 1320 training scenes (132 subtypes × 10 uniform samples) ✅ Claude Code live demo preparation for lab meeting — Designed 30-minute demo covering plan mode, permission system, CLAUDE.md rules, AI overconfidence examples. Prepared demo script and DLPFC spatial transcriptomics data. 🔄 Human demo collection strategy design for error recovery — Architected 3-tier data pools (Training 10/subtype → Augmentation 200/subtype → Evaluation 100+/subtype), decided to collect 5-10 demos per subtype starting with pick_place ✅ Rename CalendarPro to Life Copilot (code layer) — Modified 15 files (~62 lines): Python source, shell scripts, config templates, skill metadata, CLAUDE.md, documentation. All references unified to lifecopilot/Life Copilot. Implementation & Fixes ✅ Optimize CalendarPro test suite to prevent hangs — Added pytest-timeout (global 30s), marked external-service tests with @pytest.mark.external and auto-skip. All 399 tests now pass in 40s instead of hanging. ✅ Implement dev mode auto-restart for CalendarPro — Created dev.py using watchfiles to monitor src/*.py changes and auto-restart bot process. Added –dev flag shortcut. ✅ LLM output JSON format repair pipeline (12+ repair cycles) — Fixed malformed JSON from LLM-generated daily reports and academic analyses exhibiting truncation, unclosed quotes, unescaped Chinese quotation marks (""), missing closing brackets, and nested structure incompleteness. Applied four-stage repair pipeline: direct json.loads → code block extraction → depth brace matching → LLM repair (haiku→sonnet→opus escalation). All failure cases logged to outputs/logs/json_repair_failed/ for manual review. Covered 12+ instances across researcher profiles (RelViT, S3O, VIOLA/Coopernaut/Ditto, robomimic/MAPLE, GraspMolmo/DreamGen, Visual Genome/AI2-THOR, Foundation Models review, CDL/FORGE/Acid, etc.) and daily reports. ✅ Research tool documentation system synchronization — Updated through parallel Agent: research/CLAUDE.md (6 modifications, adding missing citation impact stage, three undocumented CLI parameters –affiliation/–paper/–homepage), research/TUTORIAL.md (4 modifications), parent CLAUDE.md (1 modification), ensuring all mode descriptions, example commands, parameter tables, and dependency descriptions align with code 🔄 Tutorial.md documentation update with actual device names and workflow — Updated summarize/tutorial.md to replace generic device name placeholders (home-server/linux-server/desktop) with actual device names used in multi-device workflow (MacBook/TzJsDesktop/tianhe/DCC), updated rclone remote to actual configuration (gdrive:summarize), and aligned example scenarios with real user workflow. Task planned but implementation interrupted by user. 🔄 Error recovery benchmark environment setup on MacBook — Initiated recovery_collect conda environment creation via setup_env.sh, installing mujoco 2.3.2, robosuite 1.4.1, mimicgen dependencies. Session interrupted during environment creation phase before validation and testing steps. Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Post-fusion Harmony alignment improved batch mixing metrics but degraded cross-sample niche retrieval quality Solution: Analyzed results and concluded Harmony’s linear correction cannot handle non-linear dimension meaning misalignment from per-section independent training. Recommended Joint STAIG multi-section training for next iteration.
Key Insight: Batch correction methods like Harmony assume linear offset but fail when independent training produces embeddings with misaligned dimension semantics—batch mixing and semantic alignment are distinct properties. Linear correction on embeddings trained separately cannot preserve semantic correspondence across samples.
2. CalendarPro could only handle single intent per message, losing subsequent actions like ‘schedule 3PM meeting and move dinner to 7PM’ Solution: Replaced single-intent dual-verify flow with Claude CLI MultiIntentAnalyzer returning list of intents. Updated _process_message_queue to loop through intents and combine responses. Removed 7-layer keyword override system.
Key Insight: Single-intent classification is fundamental architectural limitation, not parsing problem. Keyword hacks (always-general, context-dependent, 7-layer override) were symptoms of broken abstraction. Moving to LLM-native multi-intent removes need for all heuristics.
3. MimicGen augmentation failing: pos_delta computed but never applied to action warping, causing <5% success rate Solution: Fixed _replay_with_warping to apply pos_delta to warped_actions[:, :3] before adding noise, enabling proper object-centric trajectory transformation. Expected success rate: 50%+ for D0 translation errors.
Key Insight: MimicGen augmentation requires actual coordinate transformation, not just noise injection. The 10→1000 path depends on: 10 seeds × 200 targets × 50% success = 1000 demos. Without pos_delta fix, success rate collapses catastrophically.
4. Initial deep-wide analysis of Yuke Zhu with single 5MB prompt (100 papers full-text) far exceeded LLM processing capacity causing failure Solution: Implemented three-layer solution: ①added _MAX_PROMPT_CHARS=150K threshold to auto-trigger segmentation; ②_chunk_papers_by_year greedy algorithm grouping by year + per-paper splitting when single year too large; ③TRAJECTORY_MERGE_PROMPT using LLM to merge 47 chunk analyses into complete report
Key Insight: Ultra-large-scale text analysis key lies in ‘divide-and-conquer + semantic merge’: technically control single LLM load through dynamic segmentation, cognitively guide LLM to extract cross-segment consistency rather than simple concatenation through merge prompt. 150K chars corresponds to approximately 15-20 medium-length papers, empirically optimal point
5. Task completion model training plateaued at loss~0.035 despite grad_norm=0.7-1.0 with 4% positive vs 96% negative samples Solution: Applied positive sample weighting (pos_weight=10) in BCE loss to balance gradient contributions, effectively raising positive sample ratio from 4% to ~30%.
Key Insight: Low loss doesn’t guarantee good positive sample recall in imbalanced datasets. Gradient magnitude alone is misleading when dominated by well-learned negative samples. Class imbalance requires explicit gradient balancing.
6. CalendarPro calendar query returned ’no events today’ for ’this week’ and ’tomorrow’ queries immediately after creating events Solution: Three-layer fix: (1) added Query Intent Classification to BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT (not INTENT_PROMPT_FRAGMENTS which only serves multi_intent.py), (2) added keyword fallback detection in _handle_query() covering LLM failures, (3) fixed get_today_events() to include_past=True showing already-started events, (4) added _pending_warnings for Discord error feedback.
Key Insight: INTENT_PROMPT_FRAGMENTS and BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT serve different code paths—main LLM classification uses BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT. Keyword fallback is reliable LLM safety net. Past events must remain visible to prevent re-creation.
7. Codebase contained 8 fully-implemented modules (~2400 LOC) with comprehensive tests never imported at runtime, creating maintenance burden Solution: Systematic 4-phase refactoring: (1) map all imports and identify orphans, (2) archive unused modules with explanation README, (3) merge duplicates to canonical location, (4) delete genuinely dead code. Verified with 373 passing tests.
Key Insight: Archive > delete for complete implementations. Preserving orphaned but working code in archive/ with context enables future reintegration while cleaning active tree. Audit revealed config drift and broken imports.
8. Duplicate meal events created repeatedly (19 Breakfast, 19 Lunch, 18 Dinner) due to existence check always failing Solution: Changed _check_meal_exists to prioritize start_iso field (ISO datetime) over start_time (display string ‘08:00’). Changed schedule_essentials to get_today_events(include_past=True) so already-started meals remain visible.
Key Insight: Field parsing order matters critically—always prioritize ISO-format fields over display strings to avoid silent parse failures. Past events must remain visible to scheduler to prevent re-creation.
9. Gadget summarize finalize mechanism deadlock: historical log files with _finalized=False permanently exist, preventing report._finalized from ever becoming True, causing sync-all to repeatedly invoke LLM merge wasting API costs Solution: Root cause diagnosed: two-tier finalize (log + report) requires all source logs finalized before marking report finalized. Export sets _finalized=True only when (target_date != today() AND conversation_count unchanged). Historical logs stuck at False because: (a) only exported once when target_date == today(), leaving False; (b) conversation count changed after first export, writing False. _finalized flag embedded in JSON content, synced via rclone. Solution: re-run export on affected devices for these dates—since dates now past and conversation counts stable, will auto-mark True. Alternative: manually edit JSON _finalized field to true and rclone upload.
Key Insight: Two-tier finalize mechanism requires all sources finalized before marking report done; _finalized flag in JSON content synced via rclone ensures cross-device consistency; but historical False entries create permanent deadlock. Core value: save LLM API costs (merge stage), not rclone download time. Fix via ‘data repair’ (re-export to auto-flag) respects existing architecture better than ’logic change’ (removing all_sources_finalized check would break consistency).
10. First segmentation implementation with chunk size set to 60K led to 100 papers being split into 102 chunks (almost 1 paper per chunk), excessive LLM invocations and single papers lacking context Solution: Increased chunk size from 60K to 150K, adjusted segmentation logic to prioritize year integrity, ultimately reduced from 102 chunks to 47 chunks, significantly reducing LLM invocation count while preserving temporal context
Key Insight: Segmentation granularity needs balance between ‘LLM capacity limit’ and ‘semantic coherence’: too fine loses temporal relationships, too coarse triggers timeout; 150K approximately corresponds to 15-20 medium-length papers, empirically optimal point
11. Academic database homonymous researcher conflation: Semantic Scholar merges multiple same-name researchers into single profile causing misleading trajectory analysis (e.g., Yuke Zhu spans medical imaging, computer vision, astrophysics—clearly different people) Solution: AI proactively identifies homonym confusion signals in analysis: excessive domain span, co-author network fragmentation, citation count vs. paper count mismatch. Flags warnings in trajectory_summary opening, recommends manual splitting or paper-level analysis. For Yuke Zhu: distinguished UT Austin/NVIDIA robotics researcher from Mybank Technology researcher, Zhejiang University mathematics researcher, and Ant Group researcher based on institutional affiliation and research domain consistency.
Key Insight: Automated researcher profiling systems must include homonym detection module using multi-dimensional signals: co-author clustering, topic clustering, temporal continuity, institutional affiliation consistency. Cannot rely on database-provided identity resolution.
12. Legacy conversation logs incorrectly skipped during export due to _finalized default value of True Solution: Changed line 1726 in daily_summary.py from data.get(’_finalized’, True) to data.get(’_finalized’, False), added –force flag for explicit re-export control.
Key Insight: Default values in data pipeline logic must align with semantics: export stage should treat missing _finalized as incomplete (False), while merge stage can treat it as stable (True).
13. Multiple analyses failed due to LLM timeout (initial 300s timeout insufficient for 187K merge prompt, 420s still inadequate) Solution: Designed dynamic timeout formula: 300 + max(0, (len-50000) // 50000) * 120, i.e., 300s baseline, additional 120s per 50K chars exceeded. Final merge step succeeded with ~660s timeout
Key Insight: Timeout design should be linearly correlated with data scale rather than fixed value: large model processing speed relatively stable (~200-300 chars/s), estimating time based on prompt length + 50% buffer is reliable strategy
14. LLM-generated researcher profile JSON long-text fields exhibit systematic unescaped quote issues causing JSON parsing failures (12+ errors) Solution: Identified and manually escaped special symbols in trajectory_summary, breakthroughs[].description and other fields (e.g., “让机器人…"→"让机器人…", single quotes in technical terms); for truncated JSON objects, semantically completed missing right brackets and array closing symbols based on context
Key Insight: LLM escaping of embedded quotes unstable when generating long-text JSON fields, especially in academic text with frequent term citations (e.g., “看图理解” to “边看边动”) triggering format errors. Solution requires: (1) explicitly require escaping special characters in prompt; (2) set length limits for ultra-long text fields or adopt multi-round segmented generation; (3) add JSON schema validation step to auto-repair common format errors after generation
15. LLM-generated long JSON outputs systematically fail with format errors: truncation (especially in final fields), unclosed quotes, unescaped Chinese quotation marks (”"), missing closing brackets in nested structures, particularly when containing technical descriptions, Chinese text, and deep nesting (>3 levels) Solution: Implemented four-stage repair pipeline: (1) direct json.loads attempt; (2) extract ```json code blocks; (3) depth-based brace matching to complete unclosed structures; (4) LLM repair with model escalation (haiku→sonnet→opus) for complex errors. Dedicated JSON repair prompt instructs AI to extract and fix JSON, returning only pure JSON without explanations. All failures logged to outputs/logs/json_repair_failed/ for pattern analysis and future optimization.
Key Insight: LLM long JSON generation exhibits systematic failure modes varying across sessions even with identical prompts (string escaping issues vs. truncation vs. nesting errors). Single repair strategy insufficient; requires layered fault tolerance. Chinese quotation marks ("") semantically valid but must be escaped as ASCII double quotes in JSON string values. Production systems need post-generation JSON validation + automatic repair retry.
16. For high-productivity researchers (130+ papers), single-pass analysis with full corpus causes prompt length errors or shallow analysis lacking technical depth. 206-paper analysis for Yuke Zhu failed with ‘Prompt is too long’ error Solution: Adopted year-stratified iterative analysis strategy: segment papers by publication year, analyze 1-3 representative papers per round with deep dive into breakthroughs (description, why_not_before, impact, technical_insight). Maintains analysis depth while controlling single-call complexity. Paper selection: year-based grouping + citation count ranking + conference award identification (Best Paper/Highlight/Spotlight/Oral via LLM recognition), max 10 papers per year.
Key Insight: Year-stratified analysis strategy superior to either full-corpus single-pass (prompt too long/analysis shallow) or random sampling (misses temporal evolution). Balances temporal dimension completeness with paper quality. ‘Few but deep’ iterative mode more effective than ‘many but shallow’ one-shot mode for academic profiling.
17. Completion head inference required duplicate VLM prefix forward passes (once in sample_actions, once in predict_task_completion) Solution: Created sample_actions_with_completion() that captures prefix_out from first VLM forward and reuses it for completion_head, returning (actions, completion_prob) tuple.
Key Insight: Flow matching denoising dominates cost, but unnecessary VLM forwards add measurable overhead. Unified method eliminates redundancy with minimal refactoring.
18. SpaceMouse device open failed despite physical connection due to exclusive driver lock Solution: Killed 3Dconnexion driver processes, modified robosuite macros_private.py to set SPACEMOUSE_PRODUCT_ID=0xc63a for physical device access.
Key Insight: macOS system drivers can lock HID devices exclusively. Use macros_private.py override pattern instead of modifying main config.
19. Daily report merging task: how to avoid verbatim copying of all task items and instead synthesize consolidated view Solution: Specific operations: (1) merge similar tasks into one entry retaining latest status; (2) consolidate same-topic human vs AI analyses; (3) merge multiple sessions from same project into one conversation summary; (4) when deduplicating, retain higher importance value. Requires LLM understanding of task causal relationships (e.g., ‘Implement Feature’ → ‘Fix Bug’ → ‘Verify Fix’ recognized as stages of same work thread) and thematic similarity.
Key Insight: Direct concatenation causes information redundancy. Synthesis requires identifying causal relationships between tasks (sequential stages vs. truly parallel) and thematic similarity (repeated debugging of same module should be one task with phases), not just string matching.
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Spotting subtle default value bug in data pipeline Role Approach Human User analyzed _finalized logic across export/merge stages and identified semantic inconsistency: export should default False (incomplete), merge should default True (stable) AI AI initially focused on adding –force flag without questioning existing default value. Only after user pointed out line 1726 did AI recognize the bug. Difference Analysis: Human applied domain reasoning about pipeline semantics, while AI focused on implementing requested feature without auditing existing logic.
Segmentation strategy trade-off Role Approach Human User provided high-level requirement (‘add chunking and merge’), but did not specify segmentation algorithm details or exact chunk size AI AI chose ‘greedy year-based grouping + per-paper splitting when single year too large’ algorithm, initial chunk size=60K (too small), later adjusted to 150K (empirical value); AI independently decided merge uses LLM rather than simple string concatenation Difference Analysis: User provides high-level needs, AI fills in algorithm selection and parameter tuning details. AI’s first implementation chunk size too conservative leading to 102 chunks, after user interruption (via timeout failure indirect feedback) AI self-adjusted to 150K.
Identifying dimension misalignment in post-fusion batch correction Role Approach Human User accepted Claude’s analysis that Harmony improved batch mixing but degraded semantic alignment, and agreed with recommendation for Joint STAIG training AI AI systematically compared batch metrics vs cross-sample query metrics, identified contradictory improvements/degradations, provided mechanistic explanation (linear correction fails on non-linear dimension misalignment) Difference Analysis: In this case AI provided deeper analysis than human explicitly requested—human trusted interpretation and aligned on next steps. AI’s systematic metric decomposition revealed hidden trade-off.
Multi-intent analyzer architecture decision Role Approach Human User immediately identified single-intent limitation as root cause and proposed Claude CLI solution, skipping incremental improvements to existing dual-verify system AI AI initially explored existing architecture comprehensively before proposing solution. User’s instinct to replace rather than patch was validated by exploration revealing 7 layers of keyword hacks. Difference Analysis: Human made architectural leap directly (replace classification layer). AI took incremental exploration approach. Human’s intuition was correct—dual-verify complexity wasn’t solving fundamental multi-intent problem.
Codebase audit delegation vs direct execution Role Approach Human User rejected AI’s attempt to delegate audit to 3 parallel exploration agents, insisting on direct execution with read/grep/glob tools AI AI initially tried to parallelize audit by spawning 3 agents (src/ audit, tests/ audit, duplication finder). User stopped all three agent calls. Difference Analysis: Human understood over-delegation creates indirection and loses ‘whole picture’ view needed for architectural judgment. Direct tool use kept findings in primary context for coherent report. Lesson in when NOT to use agents.
Training vs test error scene generation strategy Role Approach Human User recognized need to separate training and test scenes into different directories, requested uniform magnitude distribution (not random) for controlled difficulty progression AI AI initially focused on reproducing existing pipeline with random scenes in single directory, had to pivot to stratified sampling with controlled RNG Difference Analysis: Human understood experimental rigor requires train/test split and controlled variables. AI initially missed data contamination and experimental design concerns.
Academic trajectory analysis framework: ‘why_not_before’ dimension as core insight Role Approach Human User designed comprehensive analysis framework requiring trajectory_summary, breakthroughs (with mandatory why_not_before field), research_themes, methodology_evolution, field_impact. Explicitly demanded ‘why now possible’ analysis rather than mere technical description. AI AI initially inclined to describe technical solution itself without deep excavation of historical preconditions. Required user’s schema constraint to enforce structured ‘why_not_before’ analysis. Difference Analysis: User understands core value of academic analysis lies in ’timing judgment of why now feasible’, not pure technical summary. AI defaults to technical overview without historical insight, needs structural prompt guidance.
Finalize bug repair strategy: data fix vs. logic change Role Approach Human User identified problem essence as permanent historical False flags, advocated fixing via re-running export on affected devices, rather than modifying merge finalize logic. Interrupted AI’s attempted logic change. AI AI initially proposed modifying merge-stage finalize judgment logic (removing all_sources_finalized check), attempting root-cause solution. Difference Analysis: User’s approach: ‘fix data’ (re-export to auto-stamp correct flags respecting existing architecture). AI’s approach: ‘change logic’ (modify code so merge no longer depends on source log finalized status). User halted AI’s modification, clearly expressing only need single export run to fix.
Researcher analysis iteration strategy: multi-angle sampling for robustness validation Role Approach Human User chose to analyze same researcher (Yuke Zhu) multiple times with different year subsets of papers (2022/2023/2025/2026), implicitly testing whether tool can infer global research direction from partial information AI AI independently generates trajectory_summary and breakthroughs for each analysis session, lacking cross-session consistency checking. Failed to explicitly articulate inheritance relationship between different period papers. Difference Analysis: Human implicitly tests tool robustness through multiple sampling; AI treats each analysis as independent task without proactively establishing temporal causal chain.
Refactoring scope: keyword logic cleanup Role Approach Human When asked whether to clean up keyword hacks now or later, user chose ‘simplify together’, expanding scope to address accumulated technical debt AI AI offered choice between minimal scope (just add multi-intent) vs expanded scope (also clean keyword hacks), presenting trade-offs. Difference Analysis: Human prioritized thorough cleanup over incremental delivery. User recognized keyword hacks as code smell worth fixing in same effort.
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Long JSON generation exhibits systematic format instability: even with identical prompts, different sessions produce varying failure modes (Chinese quotation mark mixing, truncation, nesting errors). Over 12+ repair cycles required across daily reports and research analyses, particularly when containing extensive Chinese text, technical terminology, and nested structures (>3 levels depth). Lacks self-validation of JSON completeness before output. Launched resource-intensive Explore agent on empty directory without checking if exploration was needed, wasting time until user interrupted Failed to audit _finalized default value logic across export vs merge stages when adding –force flag; required user to spot semantic inconsistency Over-delegated codebase audit to 3 parallel agents despite task requiring holistic architectural judgment. User correctly recognized this would fragment understanding and stopped delegation. Failed to recognize data contamination risk: initially suggested generating scenes without train/test splits until user explicitly requested separate directories AI experienced 5 retries in Yuke Zhu analysis process, each iteration adjusting parameters (chunk size, timeout formula), but failed to predict optimal parameters through single analysis or reasoning, significantly prolonging total time (~2-3 hours) Cannot process researcher profiles with full publication history (200+ papers) due to prompt length constraints. 206-paper Yuke Zhu analysis failed with ‘Prompt is too long’ error. Lacks built-in sampling or hierarchical analysis strategies for large corpora. Initial inability to autonomously detect homonymous researchers from paper metadata alone. Required explicit user flagging to distinguish different researchers with same name. After first correction, improved to proactively flag institutional/domain mismatches. In daily report merging task, initially inclined to enumerate all details rather than proactively synthesize. Required user repeated emphasis of ‘focus on synthesis and distillation, not copying entries verbatim’ to trigger consolidation logic, indicating lack of intrinsic ‘readability’ and ‘information density’ balance awareness. When analyzing same researcher multiple times, failed to identify temporal evolution relationships across different year papers. Each analysis isolated without temporal causal inference dimension. In analyzing academic papers, tends toward ‘what was done’ and ‘what impact’ descriptions, but ‘why not feasible before’ historical dimension excavation requires explicit user prompting to deepen. Defaults to technical summary without historical insight. Initial inability to detect name conflicts in researcher analysis without explicit prompting, generating unified trajectory for papers from different researchers until user flagged domain inconsistency Initial renaming plan only covered display names/code references, missing infrastructure layers (conda env, folder, repo, systemd). Required human correction to expand scope. Rationalized performance inefficiency: accepted duplicate VLM forward passes as ‘acceptable overhead’ without optimization until user questioned redundancy Added Query Intent Classification to INTENT_PROMPT_FRAGMENTS (only used by multi_intent.py) instead of BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT (used by main classification flow), not understanding code path differences In processing ultra-long text fields (e.g., 86-paper complete trajectory_summary), truncation risk exists causing incomplete JSON objects (field_impact and other fields truncated midway). Lacks proactive output length monitoring and warning mechanism. AI chose 60K chunk size initially (too conservative leading to 102 chunks), failed to recognize ‘reasonable chunk count’ implicit constraint in one attempt, needed timeout failure feedback before adjusting to 150K. Lacks prior reasoning about ‘user expects reasonable chunk count (10-50)’. Initially missed relative imports (from ..browser) when searching for module usage with ‘from src.browser’ pattern, falsely concluding browser/ was dead code. Only caught error after checking executor.py directly. Cannot autonomously capture user implicit intent. User’s multi-round analysis of same researcher with different year subsets actually tests tool’s paper selection robustness, but AI unaware of this validation purpose. AI batch-processed gadget/summarize tool causing 4-5 dates to fail due to API 32000 token output limit, but AI did not proactively detect output length limit or propose batch processing solution, passively accepting failure results Cannot proactively identify self-generated JSON format errors, requires human external validation (e.g., JSON parser) to discover problems then provide feedback Lacks global progress tracking for batch tasks—cannot proactively tell user ‘N more days of historical data need processing’ or current completion percentage. User must manually track task progress. Initially designed daily report tool as single-layer end-to-end generation, missing need for hierarchical aggregation (conversation→device summary→daily report) until user corrected with recursive architecture General Limitations Misunderstood domain terminology: ‘by title’ and ‘by abstract’ assumed to potentially mean LLM-based selection logic, while user actual meaning was ‘only summarize title’ vs ‘summarize abstract’. Needs explicit clarification Cannot effectively track processed date lists and pending tasks when handling large volumes of historical daily report data. Requires user-provided explicit task checklist and one-by-one confirmation mechanism. Initially only added terminal ERROR logs for calendar API failures instead of user-visible Discord message output. Needed user clarification to understand product requirement for in-chat error feedback. Learnings Key Learnings Batch correction methods like Harmony provide global distribution mixing but cannot preserve semantic alignment when independent training produces embeddings with misaligned dimension meanings. This requires shared training (Joint STAIG) or foundation models. Batch mixing and semantic alignment are orthogonal properties. MimicGen-style augmentation is fundamentally about coordinate transformation, not noise injection. pos_delta (source→target object offset) must be applied to action trajectories. Without this, 10→1000 augmentation collapses due to <5% success rate. Ultra-large-scale text analysis engineering paradigm: ①automatic segmentation (150K chars ~15-20 papers optimal granularity) ②semantic merge (LLM-driven merge not concatenation) ③dynamic timeout (linearly scaled based on prompt length, 300s baseline+120s/50K chars) ④caching mechanism (avoid retry cost). All four essential for deep-wide mode usability Academic trajectory analysis multi-angle validation method: Through iterative analysis of same researcher’s different paper subsets (from 1 paper to 100 papers), can build stereoscopic cognition from micro (single representative work deep analysis) to macro (complete publication list trajectory characterization). Yuke Zhu case reveals: visual understanding (Visual Genome) → embodied intelligence (AI2-THOR) → robotics learning ecosystem building (robosuite/RoboTurk) → LLM-driven embodied intelligence (MineDojo/Voyager) → geometric intermediate representations + VLM alignment clear evolution trajectory, only fully presentable with complete paper list Yuke Zhu’s research trajectory exhibits clear ‘perception→manipulation→infrastructure→open world’ evolution: from early 2012 visual RL to 2026 DreamDojo world models, consistently centered on ’enabling robots to efficiently learn from data and generalize to real world’ core proposition. Represents ‘data infrastructure builder’ → ‘foundation model pioneer’ → ‘world model architect’ progression. Single-intent classification is architectural constraint that cannot be fixed with keyword hacks. CalendarPro’s 7-layer keyword override system was treating symptoms of broken abstraction. Multi-intent LLM analysis eliminates need for all heuristics. Class imbalance in binary classification requires explicit gradient balancing. Low overall loss can mask poor minority class learning when 96% of samples are one class. pos_weight effectively rebalances gradient contributions. Analysis mode design key dimensions: ①data scale (papers/year) ②analysis depth (abstract vs fulltext) ③computational cost (LLM invocation count). Five-mode system through two boolean dimensions + one numeric dimension combination, covers complete need spectrum from ‘quick overview→deep excavation’ Structured data generation prompt engineering essentials: For fixed schema JSON output, need to explicitly declare in prompt all required fields, their types (string/array/object), length limits, default values (placeholder text when no data), and special character escaping rules. gadget summarize tool’s daily_overview layered format evolution proves: relying only on example inference cannot guarantee output consistency, must use enumeration-style specification to clarify generation logic for each field Robotics learning field research infrastructure building pathway: Yuke Zhu’s academic trajectory reveals important pattern—field breakthroughs often accompanied by infrastructure-type work (AI2-THOR simulation environment, robosuite standard benchmark, RoboTurk crowdsourcing platform, MineDojo internet-scale dataset), these works’ citation counts though not exceeding algorithm innovation papers, but actual impact on overall field advancement may be greater. For any emerging research direction, prioritizing establishment of reproducible evaluation benchmarks and data collection toolchains has more strategic value than publishing single algorithm papers Two-tier finalize mechanism (log + report) requires all source logs finalized before marking report done; _finalized flag in JSON content synced via rclone ensures cross-device consistency; but historical False entries create permanent deadlock. Core value: save LLM API costs (merge stage), not rclone download time. Fix path: re-execute idempotent operation (export) to auto-correct historical metadata, respecting architectural design. Batch iterative workflow (process daily reports date-by-date, academic analysis year-by-year) more reliable than one-shot bulk processing, effectively avoiding JSON truncation and analysis depth insufficiency. Critical for gadget tools to add auto-retry mechanism with JSON validation. Academic database homonym disambiguation problem high-frequency and high-impact data quality issue in automated researcher profiling. Must establish homonym detection module through multi-dimensional signals: co-author clustering, topic clustering, temporal continuity, institutional affiliation consistency. Train/eval data separation is non-negotiable in ML pipelines. Evaluation set must never leak into training. Different data purposes require different generation quotas and strategies. Archive > delete for complete, tested implementations even if orphaned. archive/ pattern with explanation README preserves optionality (future reintegration) while cleaning active tree. Default values in data processing pipelines must match stage semantics: export (missing=incomplete→False) vs merge (missing=stable→True). Subtle inconsistencies cause silent bugs. DateTime field parsing order matters critically—always prioritize ISO-format fields (start_iso) over display-format strings (start_time ‘08:00’) to avoid silent parse failures that break existence checks. Gadget project’s daily report generation tool demonstrates strong information extraction and structuring capability in complex multi-device, multi-project, cross-temporal scenarios. Can accurately distinguish cross-device parallel work, identify task causal relationships, synthesize conversation_summaries, validating production readiness. Research profiler consistently identified Yuke Zhu’s core research directions and methodological evolution from different year paper subsets, proving tool’s academic trajectory extraction capability. Voyager (2023) demonstrated LLM-as-autonomous-agent paradigm: 3.3x more unique items, 15.3x faster tech tree unlocking in Minecraft. Skills stored as executable JS code rather than neural weights, avoiding catastrophic forgetting. Open X-Embodiment (2023): 22 robots, 21 institutions, 160K tasks demonstrated positive transfer across embodiments, establishing ImageNet-moment for robotics data consolidation. Researcher profile information density tiers: Tier 1 = h-index/citation count (quantitative metrics), Tier 2 = representative works list (enumerable facts), Tier 3 = research trajectory narrative (needs LLM to extract implicit evolution logic). Three tiers correspond to fast/normal/deep mode output complexity respectively LLM long-text JSON output fault tolerance requires layered mechanism: direct parsing→code block extraction→brace matching→LLM repair (model escalation). Single strategy cannot cover all failure modes. All failures should be logged for manual review and subsequent optimization. Breakthrough paper characteristic analysis framework (why_not_before + impact + technical_insight) effectively reveals technological innovation’s historical inevitability, domain influence, and core insights, more valuable than simple abstract summaries. Researcher analysis paper selection strategy should balance temporal completeness and quality. Year-based grouping + citation count ranking + conference award identification (via LLM) three-layer mechanism can balance foundational early work and recent frontier breakthroughs at controllable computational cost. LLM-generated JSON systematic error pattern recognition: Through 12+ JSON repair tasks discovered, unescaped quote errors mainly concentrate in: (1) academic term citations (e.g., “看图理解”); (2) Chinese and Western quotation mark mixing; (3) special symbols in technical nouns (e.g., + in C++, mathematical symbols like σ_w²). Solution requires multi-layer protection: prompt explicitly requires escaping rules + output schema validation + regex auto-repair common patterns Year-stratified analysis strategy superior to either full-corpus single-pass (prompt too long/analysis shallow) or random sampling (misses temporal evolution). Balances temporal dimension completeness with paper quality. ‘Few but deep’ iterative mode more effective than ‘many but shallow’ one-shot mode for academic profiling. When implementing recurring event schedulers, must include already-started events in existence checks (include_past=True), otherwise past-time events become invisible and get re-created every cycle. In multi-component systems, understand where prompts are actually used. INTENT_PROMPT_FRAGMENTS and BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT serve different code paths—main LLM classification uses BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT, multi_intent.py uses INTENT_PROMPT_FRAGMENTS. Historical daily report backfill workload substantial but value significant: 30+ days historical records covering multiple projects across 4 devices, involving dozens of technical decisions and hundreds of task entries. Establishing complete work record system foundational for subsequent monthly report generation and project retrospectives. Code refactoring backward compatibility strategy: default value from fast→normal, but from_dict still fallback to fast (protecting old profile data); choices list expansion doesn’t break old config files; re-export shim enables dependent parties to migrate without awareness. Embodies ‘progressive evolution’ superior to ‘destructive rewrite’ Long-running task development experience optimization: ①caching mechanism makes retry cost controllable (47 chunks fully cached, only need to re-run merge) ②progress observability (tqdm+PYTHONUNBUFFERED) ③timeout formula self-adaptive (avoid manual parameter tuning) ④parallel Agent handling independent subtasks (docs update) gadget/research toolkit consists of three independent tools (paper discovery, researcher profiling, citation graph analysis), unified under research_scout.py CLI. Researcher profiling uses citation count + award weighting for paper selection, not LLM scoring When refactoring mode system, preserve existing sorting logic and add new dimensions (quantity, processing depth) as independent parameters. This minimizes risk and keeps behavior predictable Systematic import analysis (grep for patterns + verify init.py exports) essential before declaring modules ‘dead’. Relative imports (..module) require separate search patterns from absolute imports (src.module). Keyword fallback mechanism is reliable LLM safety net—even if LLM fails to return structured data, detecting keywords in original input enables correct routing. Project renaming requires two-layer approach: (1) code/docs (automatable via search-replace), (2) infrastructure (manual: conda, git, hosting, services). Checklist both layers upfront to avoid partial migrations. Daily report synthesis ‘distillation’ requires LLM understanding task causal and containment relationships, recognizing ‘Implement Feature’→‘Fix Bug’→‘Verify Fix’ as stages of same work thread not three independent tasks. This semantic relationship understanding capability key to report readability. pip install -e . (editable mode) is correct way to make shared utility packages importable in multi-tool projects rather than PYTHONPATH manipulation. Hot reload in Python is nearly impossible for stateful systems (Discord bots with event loops, singleton caches, background schedulers). Process-level restart via watchfiles is only practical solution. Practical Learnings robosuite provides official utilities (input2action, input_utils) that handle device→action conversion correctly. Manual parsing of action_dict loses coordinate transforms and controller scaling. Test infrastructure hardening (pytest-timeout, @pytest.mark.external, skip logic) prevents one category of flaky tests from blocking entire suite. Pattern is reusable across projects. Using max_per_year=0 as ‘unlimited papers’ sentinel value clearer than boolean flag or None, because zero never valid use case for paper limit Git rebase workflow (stash → pull –rebase → stash pop) cleanly integrates diverged branches with independent changes, avoiding merge commit clutter. Conversation Summaries MIHD spatial transcriptomics ✅ Post-fusion Harmony alignment experiments, strategic planning for ARI improvement and batch effect reduction, Claude Code demo preparation 02:04:13.480 | claude_code Modified benchmark_rm_ideal.py to add –harmony flag for post-fusion batch correction between embedding loading and cross-sample evaluation. Ran experiments comparing sections 151508-151673 with/without Harmony. Key finding: Harmony improved batch mixing metrics (ASW_batch +36%, graph_connectivity +32%) but degraded semantic alignment (Spearman r 0.189→0.023). Concluded Harmony’s linear correction cannot handle non-linear dimension misalignment from per-section independent training. Recommended Joint STAIG multi-section training as next step. Launched multi-agent exploration to diagnose clustering ARI gap (0.475 vs SOTA 0.66+) and cross-sample batch effects, decided dual-track approach. Prepared comprehensive 30-minute Claude Code demo for Friday lab meeting covering plan mode, permissions, CLAUDE.md rules, AI overconfidence examples using DLPFC spatial data, delivered complete demo materials and speaking script.
Error Recovery (algorithms & benchmark) ✅ Environment setup, task completion model training optimization, comprehensive recovery collection refactor, training scene generation, MimicGen warping bug fix 04:16:46.552 | claude_code Set up recovery_collect conda env with mujoco/robosuite stack. Fixed EnvWrapper.env bug. Resolved SpaceMouse device lock by killing 3Dconnexion driver and configuring product_id=0xc63a. Successfully connected device but discovered only 11/29 error subtypes have generated scenes. Diagnosed training plateau (loss~0.035, 4% positive samples), implemented pos_weight=10 weighted BCE loss to balance gradients, added predict_task_completion inference method, optimized by creating sample_actions_with_completion to reuse VLM prefix features eliminating duplicate forward passes. Major refactor: fixed SpaceMouse initialization (removed env param, used input2action), added rendering support, implemented dynamic PROJECT_ROOT resolution. Created portable deployment package (171MB→252MB compressed) with setup/run scripts and tutorial, all 139 unit tests pass. Implemented generate_training_scenes.py to produce exactly 10 scenes per task×subtype with magnitude uniformly distributed 0.0 to 1.0. Created ControlledRng class making uniform() deterministic (fraction-based interpolation) while keeping randn() random for direction diversity. Deep technical discussion clarifying data collection architecture: collect 5-10 demos/subtype, no failed demos, start with 1-2 tasks. Discovered RecoverySegmenter is for Phoenix MCM/MPM (not MimicGen). Identified critical bug: pos_delta computed but never applied in _replay_with_warping. Fixed warping, enabling 10 seeds × 200 targets × 50% success = 1000 augmented demos. Clarified need for 3 data pools: Training (10), Augmentation (200), Evaluation (100+).
CalendarPro/Life Copilot ✅ Complete system refactoring: multi-intent classifier, calendar query fixes, codebase audit and Phase 2 cleanup, duplicate meal bug fix, project rename, development mode implementation, test suite optimization 00:53:36.473 | claude_code Shipped complete refactor of intent classification system.
