Replaced dual-verify + 7-layer keyword hacks with Claude CLI MultiIntentAnalyzer supporting multi-intent decomposition. Created MultiIntentAnalyzer class with 4-stage JSON parsing, added MultiIntentResult/AnalysisContext models, removed _always_general_keywords and _context_dependent_keywords sets, simplified _process_input from 7 steps to 4. Fixed CLI bug. Added week query support. Wrote 30 new tests, all 424 tests passing. User identified fundamental limitation (dual-verify returns only one intent, complex messages lose subsequent actions). Explored existing architecture revealing 7-layer keyword hacks, then designed 2-tier solution. Created comprehensive implementation plan. Implemented dev.py using watchfiles to monitor src/*.py with multiprocessing.spawn for clean process isolation. Added –dev flag. Configured debounce, grace period, and ignore filters. pytest tests/ hung indefinitely. Added pytest-timeout dependency with 30s global timeout, marked external-service tests with @pytest.mark.external and skipif auto-detection, renamed Typer CLI functions to avoid collection. All 399 tests now pass in 40s. Systematically mapped 29K LOC across 90+ files using grep/glob/read tools (direct execution after user rejected agent delegation). Identified 8 orphaned modules (~2400 LOC), duplicate parse_datetime_with_timezone function, dead broker_models.py, 3 orphaned config settings, broken browser_service import. Generated detailed phase-gated refactoring plan. Implemented all 4 phases: archived 8 src modules + 9 test files + check_skills_status.py with explanation README; merged duplicate parse_datetime_with_timezone; deleted broker_models.py and 3 orphaned config settings; fixed broken browser import. Verified with 373 tests passing. Updated CLAUDE.md. User reported 19 duplicate Breakfast, 19 Lunch, 18 Dinner events. Diagnosed root cause: _check_meal_exists used display-format time string failing fromisoformat(), and schedule_essentials excluded past events. Fixed both bugs, created cleanup script, deleted 53 duplicates, verified with tests. User requested implementing rename plan, then interrupted to expand scope to include conda env, folder, repo, Discord app. Re-explored codebase, executed ~62 line changes across 15 files (code, docs, scripts, configs), provided 6-item manual checklist. User executed /init triggering CLAUDE.md improvement. Supplemented 4 new architecture modules and corrected multiple details. User then reported calendar query bug (this week/tomorrow queries always return ’no events today’). Fixed via three layers: BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT added Query Classification guidance, _handle_query() added keyword fallback, get_today_events() added include_past parameter, added Discord error feedback mechanism. All tests passing, documentation and functionality complete.
gadget (summarize + research tools) ✅ Comprehensive knowledge management infrastructure expansion: 5-mode research tool system upgrade with chunked long-text analysis, Yuke Zhu complete academic trajectory profiling (10+ iterations, 100+ papers), batch historical daily report consolidation (30+ dates), finalize mechanism bug diagnosis, JSON repair pipeline 04:10:40.556 | claude_code User encountered ‘ModuleNotFoundError: common’ when running daily_summary.py export. Diagnosed missing package installation and ran ‘pip install -e .’ to install in editable mode. Successfully exported 23 dates of conversation logs. User requested forced re-export of all dates. Investigation revealed export stage used _finalized default=True (line 1726), causing legacy logs to be incorrectly skipped. Changed default to False for export stage, added –force flag for explicit control. Ran forced export for 17 dates with all successfully uploaded to rclone. Synced local and remote gadget repository using git stash, rebase, and push workflow. Executed intensive batch processing of historical conversation logs using gadget/summarize’s daily_summary.py tool across approximately 30+ dates from February to mid-March 2026. Covered multi-device (DCC/MacBook/TzJsDesktop/tianhe) and multi-project (MIHD, error-recovery-benchmark, CalendarPro, Desktop Video, gadget) work records. Each generated report includes structured daily_overview (global + per-device layers), tasks, problems_and_solutions, human_vs_ai, ai_limitations, learnings, conversation_summaries. Encountered 12+ JSON format errors requiring dedicated repair sessions. Diagnosed critical finalize mechanism deadlock: identified 3 device-dates with permanent _finalized=False blocking report finalization and causing infinite LLM API cost loop. Solution documented: re-run export or manually edit JSON. Added (finalized) suffix to export output for diagnostic visibility. User provided detailed implementation plan requiring extension from 2 modes (fast/detailed) to 5 modes (fast/normal/deep/wide/deep-wide), adding automatic segmentation+merge functionality for wide/deep-wide modes. AI modified 7 source files (analysis.py adding _MODE_CONFIG and chunking logic, prompts.py adding TRAJECTORY_MERGE_PROMPT, cli.py/research_scout.py/config.py/models.py updating choices) + 3 documentation files, implemented dynamic timeout formula (300s+120s/50K chars) and 150K chars/chunk segmentation algorithm. Successfully completed Yuke Zhu (h-index=56, 24611 citations, 130 papers) deep-wide analysis: from ArXiv retrieved 100 papers full-text, auto-segmented into 47 temporal chunks, per-chunk LLM analysis, final merge into complete trajectory report (including 7 breakthroughs, research theme evolution, mentorship relationships). Experienced 5 iteration optimizations (chunk size 60K→150K, timeout formula optimization) ultimately outputting complete academic profile report. Conducted exhaustive academic profiling of Yuke Zhu through 10+ iterative year-stratified analysis rounds analyzing 18-100 representative papers spanning Visual Genome (2016) → robomimic/MAPLE (2021) → CDL/MineDojo/VIMA (2022) → Voyager/S3O/LIBERO (2023) → DexMimicGen/Sirius-Fleet/PRESTO/LongVILA (2024) → DEAS/Cosmos-Predict2.5/QLIP/ASAP/GraspMolmo/DreamDojo (2025-2026). Each analysis round generated comprehensive trajectory_summary, breakthroughs with mandatory why_not_before/impact/technical_insight dimensions, research_themes, methodology_evolution, and field_impact. Identified evolution from ‘data infrastructure builder’ (robosuite/ROBOMIMIC) → ‘foundation model pioneer’ (MineDojo/VIMA/Voyager) → ‘world model architect’ (Cosmos/DreamDojo). Proactively detected and flagged homonymous researchers. Encountered 9+ JSON repair cycles for truncated or malformed outputs. Attempted full 206-paper corpus analysis but failed with ‘Prompt is too long’ error, confirming necessity of year-stratified sampling strategy. User ran /init command verifying research/ CLAUDE.md accuracy. Assistant discovered two small errors and fixed them. User subsequently proposed refactoring researcher profiling from 2 modes to 5 modes with different paper limits and processing depths. After clarifying paper selection should maintain citation+award based approach, assistant created implementation plan covering analysis.py, cli.py and prompts.py changes. Batch-processed approximately 16+ historical dates generating structured daily reports for dates from 2026-02-08 through 2026-03-17. Fixed malformed JSON from LLM-generated outputs exhibiting truncation, unclosed quotes, unescaped Chinese quotation marks, missing closing brackets. Applied four-stage repair pipeline. Updated research/CLAUDE.md (6 modifications), research/TUTORIAL.md (4 modifications), parent CLAUDE.md (1 modification) through parallel Agent ensuring all mode descriptions, example commands, parameter tables align with code.
Token Usage Total: $10.05 — 12.1M tokens
summary: Conducted cross-device multi-project research and development: spatial transcriptomics Harmony alignment experiments revealing semantic-batch mixing trade-offs, robotic error recovery pipeline from training optimization through portable deployment (139 passing tests), CalendarPro/Life Copilot complete architectural refactoring eliminating single-intent bottleneck with multi-intent classifier, and comprehensive knowledge management infrastructure expansion including gadget research tool 5-mode system upgrade with chunked long-text analysis, Yuke Zhu complete academic trajectory profiling (2012-2026, 100+ papers across 10+ analysis iterations), and batch consolidation of 30+ days historical daily reports from Feb-Mar 2026
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-19

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-18 Daily Overview What was done: Extended CalendarPro’s intent classification system with a new BATCH_UPDATE intent to handle multi-task status reports from users How it was done: Implemented semantic routing with 21 utterances, keyword scoring with completion verb detection, LLM prompt fragments, and a calendar-integrated handler across 8 files Impact: Resolved a critical UX gap where users couldn’t efficiently report status on multiple tasks at once, eliminating ‘I don’t understand’ fallback responses for batch updates Added batch update intent to CalendarPro for handling multi-task status reports, with full test coverage and documentation updates
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Implement BATCH_UPDATE intent feature — Added new intent type across 8 files: enum definition, semantic routes (21 utterances), keyword scorer with 2+ completion verb boost, LLM prompt fragment with updates array schema, context assembler mapping, dual verify default reply, discord_bot handler with per-task calendar operations, and improved GENERAL fallback ✅ Diagnose batch update classification failure — Launched 3 parallel exploration agents to understand intent routing system (24 types), task completion handling, and general intent protection. Identified root cause: no BATCH_UPDATE intent exists, semantic router scores ~0.20 vs 0.50 threshold, LLM classifier defaults to GENERAL with clarification type that triggers hardcoded ‘I don’t understand’ template ✅ Create implementation plan for batch update feature — Designed comprehensive 8-step implementation plan covering IntentType enum, semantic routes, keyword patterns, LLM prompts, context assembly, dual verify defaults, discord_bot handler with calendar integration, GENERAL fallback improvements, route JSON regeneration, and test suite Implementation & Fixes ✅ Write comprehensive test suite for BATCH_UPDATE — Created 21 tests covering keyword scoring, enum, handler (complete/cancel/reschedule/note/multi/empty/permission), route registration, context map, prompt fragment, and dual verify default reply. All tests passed with no regressions in 72-test suite ✅ Update CLAUDE.md documentation — Rewrote CLAUDE.md to focus on non-obvious architecture: removed redundant file listings, added ‘Common Multi-File Changes’ section with step-by-step guides for adding AI providers/intents/services/events, documented singleton test isolation, consolidated architecture patterns Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Bot fails to classify multi-task status updates (e.g., ‘A完成了，B也解决了，C需要改时间’), falling back to ‘I don’t understand’ despite substantive user input Solution: Added BATCH_UPDATE intent with semantic route (21 utterances), keyword scorer boost for 2+ completion verbs, LLM prompt teaching updates array schema, and handler that searches calendar, deletes completed tasks, reschedules/notes others, logs completions, and emits EventBus events. Also fixed GENERAL fallback to use substantive LLM replies instead of discarding them
Key Insight: Intent classification failures often stem from three-layer gaps: (1) no enum member, (2) no semantic route examples, (3) no LLM prompt fragment. Fixing all three layers plus keyword scoring creates robust classification. GENERAL fallback should preserve useful LLM responses rather than blindly templating.
2. Semantic router scores batch updates at ~0.20 vs 0.50 threshold, causing LOW_CONFIDENCE classification Solution: Created BATCH_UPDATE_ROUTE with diverse Chinese/English utterances (‘A完成了，B也搞定了’, ’tasks A and B completed, C needs rescheduling’) and added keyword scorer rule: 2+ distinct completion keywords → +0.30 boost, capturing the defining characteristic of batch updates
Key Insight: Embedding-based semantic routing misses multi-entity status patterns. Explicit keyword counting (2+ completion verbs) is a simple but effective heuristic for batch detection that complements embedding similarity.
3. LLM classifier has no batch_update in allowed intent list, defaults to GENERAL with clarification type, which triggers hardcoded ‘I don’t understand’ template that discards useful AI-generated replies Solution: Added batch_update to INTENT_PROMPT_FRAGMENTS with updates array schema (task_name, action, note, new_time) and to allowed intent list in build_prompt(). Improved GENERAL handler to check if clarification-type replies have substantive LLM content (>20 chars, not template phrase) and use it instead of hardcoded template
Key Insight: Fallback templates should be last resort, not default. When LLM generates substantive replies, preserve them even if confidence is low. Template overrides waste the LLM’s reasoning and create worse UX.
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Diagnostic strategy for batch update failure Role Approach Human User showed real Discord conversation failure (‘你好 → bot replies with task summary → user gives batch status → bot says I don’t understand’) and asked ‘how should we solve this?’, providing the concrete symptom without prescribing the diagnostic method AI Claude launched 3 parallel exploration agents to systematically understand: (1) all 24 intent types and routing, (2) task completion handling, (3) general intent protection feature. Then read critical files to verify details, used Plan agent to synthesize findings into implementation plan Difference Analysis: Human provided the ‘what’ (symptom) and ‘why we care’ (UX problem), but not the ‘how to investigate’. AI chose parallel agent-based exploration rather than sequential file reading, showing strategic use of subagents for broad discovery. Human likely would have done more linear ‘read this file, then that file’ approach.
Implementation approach for BATCH_UPDATE feature Role Approach Human User provided a fully structured 8-step implementation plan with exact file paths, line numbers, code snippets, data schemas, and test requirements before asking Claude to execute AI Claude followed the plan precisely, reading files in parallel, making edits sequentially where dependencies existed, regenerating derived data (route JSON), and writing comprehensive tests Difference Analysis: Human did upfront architectural design and specified exact changes; AI executed the plan as a reliable implementer. This shows human taking ‘architect’ role while AI takes ‘builder’ role, which is efficient for complex multi-file changes where the human has already done the systems thinking.
AI Limitations General Limitations In test_batch_update.py, Claude initially tried to import DiscordBot class which caused import errors, then pivoted to testing the handler function in isolation. Could have checked import structure first or used better mocking patterns When first attempting to run full test suite, Claude used pytest tests/ -v --timeout=30 -x which failed because pytest-timeout plugin wasn’t installed. Fell back to running subset of tests instead of installing the plugin or removing the flag initially Learnings Key Learnings Multi-layered intent classification requires coordinated updates across 8+ files: enum (models.py), semantic routes (intent_routes.py), keyword scorer (keyword_scorer.py), LLM prompts (prompts.py), context assembly (context_assembler.py), dual verify defaults (dual_verify.py), handler + routing (discord_bot.py), plus derived data (route JSON). Missing any layer causes classification failure Parallel agent exploration (3 agents for intent routing, task handling, general protection) is more efficient than sequential file reading for complex diagnostics. Each agent provides focused perspective that synthesizes into complete understanding Fallback templates in AI systems should preserve useful LLM-generated content rather than blindly overwriting it. Check if LLM reply is substantive (>20 chars, not template phrase) before replacing with hardcoded fallback Keyword scoring can capture patterns that embedding similarity misses: 2+ distinct completion verbs is a simple heuristic for batch updates that complements semantic routing. Explicit pattern detection is still valuable in neural systems Practical Learnings When implementing features from user-provided plans, reading all affected files in parallel first (rather than one-by-one) enables better understanding of dependencies and allows Claude to batch edits more efficiently Conversation Summaries ✅ Implement BATCH_UPDATE intent for multi-task status reports 23:05:55.704 | claude_code User provided an 8-step implementation plan to add BATCH_UPDATE intent across CalendarPro’s classification system. Claude executed the plan: added enum member, semantic route with 21 utterances, keyword scorer with 2+ completion verb boost, LLM prompt fragment, context assembly map, dual verify default, discord_bot handler with calendar operations, and improved GENERAL fallback to preserve useful LLM replies. Wrote 21 tests covering all components; all passed with no regressions in 72-test suite. Regenerated route JSON with 25 total routes.
✅ Diagnose why bot fails on batch status updates 22:35:46.066 | claude_code User showed real Discord failure: bot couldn’t understand multi-task status update (‘A查看好了，B和C已经解决’), responded with ‘I don’t understand’. Claude set effort to max, launched 3 parallel exploration agents to map intent system (24 types), task handling, and general protection. Read critical files to verify details. Used Plan agent to synthesize findings into root cause: no BATCH_UPDATE intent exists, semantic router scores low (~0.20), LLM defaults to GENERAL clarification that triggers hardcoded template. Created comprehensive 8-step implementation plan saved to .claude/plans/. This plan was later executed in first session.
✅ Update CLAUDE.md documentation for better onboarding 21:55:02.204 | claude_code User ran /init command asking Claude to improve CLAUDE.md. Claude analyzed existing docs, read codebase structure, and rewrote CLAUDE.md to focus on non-obvious architecture: removed redundant file listings, added ‘Common Multi-File Changes’ section with step-by-step guides for adding AI providers/intents/services/events, documented singleton test isolation patterns, and consolidated architecture explanations around multi-file relationships. Final version emphasizes patterns requiring multiple file reads to understand.
Token Usage Total: $4.16 — 5.3M tokens
summary: Added batch update intent to CalendarPro for handling multi-task status reports, with full test coverage and documentation updates
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-18

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-17 Daily Overview What was done: Completed large-scale MIHD refactoring (9 optimizations including O(N²)→O(Nlogk)), achieved comprehensive QCVLA understanding and proposed 11 improvements via cross-project analysis (FLARE + 9 VLA papers), fixed critical VLA inference bugs and separated loss monitoring, generated 2920 error benchmark scenes, optimized data conversion pipelines, configured remote development environment, and enhanced GPU monitoring tool How it was done: Used parallel Agent-based code review for MIHD, conducted iterative Q&A and literature survey for QCVLA, performed systematic debugging and architecture redesign for VLA systems, executed multi-task error injection pipeline, implemented non-blocking I/O for interactive monitoring, automated server setup via SSH Impact: MIHD: eliminated code duplication and latent bugs, prevented HD data OOM. QCVLA: identified P0 quick-wins and proposed perturbation & bridging augmentation to break DAgger saturation. VLA: stabilized inference system, enabled separate action_loss/task_loss tracking. Error benchmark 90% complete (needs D0 sample補充). GPU monitoring usability greatly improved. Remote server ready for distributed development DCC What was done: Conducted comprehensive code review and refactoring of MIHD multi-modal integration codebase, covering 21 modified Python files with focus on eliminating redundancy and performance bottlenecks How it was done: Executed /simplify workflow with three parallel Agent reviews (code reuse, quality, efficiency), applied 9 targeted fixes including centralizing NEEDS_COORDS_FUSIONS constant, hoisting device resolution, replacing O(N²) cdist with cKDTree, removing vestigial wrappers, and reusing DataPreparer instances Impact: Removed 8 duplicate device resolution checks, fixed missing adaln_attention in spatial coordinate fusion list (latent bug), prevented OOM on 17K+ spot HD data via algorithmic optimization, improved code maintainability with centralized constants MacBook What was done: Deep dive into QCVLA latent bridge architecture, cross-project improvement planning via 9-paper survey, FLARE integration analysis, and remote server environment configuration How it was done: Created CLAUDE.md documentation, iterative Q&A to understand 7-step pipeline and covariate shift problem, web search for VLA efficiency/robustness literature (VLA-Cache, AC²-VLA, Spec-VLA, KERV, etc.), extracted transferable techniques from FLARE paper (perturbation augmentation, hard failure mining, phase-aware scheduling, vision-conditional bridging, modular LoRA), automated athena.egr.duke.edu setup via SSH + Node.js + Claude/Codex installation Impact: Achieved complete technical comprehension of bridge mechanism (57.6% compute savings with 4% performance drop). Identified P0 quick wins (enable existing Action-Aware Channel Importance Loss) and high-value P1 improvements (Bridge Confidence Head for adaptive scheduling). Proposed FLARE-inspired perturbation & bridging to address DAgger saturation (+1pp plateau). Server ready for distributed development tianhe What was done: Fixed VLA inference critical bugs, implemented training loss separation, completed error benchmark data generation (2920 scenes), optimized CALVIN dataset conversion, integrated LIBERO custom test suite, fixed GPU monitoring duplicate display issue, diagnosed RoboCasa MimicGen download failure, resolved git proxy and wandb configuration How it was done: Code review identified and fixed 4 inference bugs (None check order, prompt reset, log threshold, state update); modified compute_loss to return (loss_array, aux_dict) tuple with has_aux=True; ran 6-task error injection pipeline (3.5 hours); corrected RLDS-to-LeRobot key mappings and added progress bars; created LIBERO_OBJECT_COM class and BDDL files; implemented ENV+FD verification and parent chain deduplication for gpumon.py; added non-blocking keyboard navigation (arrow keys, command scrolling); traced RoboCasa error through registry to box_links_ds.json; tested proxies and updated git local config; provided wandb API key environment variable solution Impact: VLA inference system stabilized (eliminated crashes and infinite loops), training process now separately monitors action_loss/task_loss in wandb, error benchmark dataset 90% complete (2920 scenes generated, needs D0 sample補充 for some error types), dataset conversion pipeline robust and user-friendly, LIBERO test framework extended, GPU monitoring tool reduced display from 35 to 8 unique processes with nvitop-like interactive features, RoboCasa analysis identified upstream data publishing issue (0 MimicGen Box links in box_links_ds.json), git and wandb configuration issues resolved Cross-device collaboration on MIHD spatial omics refactoring, QCVLA latent bridge deep dive with FLARE-inspired improvements, VLA training/inference infrastructure fixes, error benchmark data generation, and system tool enhancements
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ QCVLA project documentation creation and improvement planning — Created initial CLAUDE.md via /init covering 7-step pipeline, SingleStepDiT architecture, and critical gotchas. Generated improvement_analysis.md with 11 proposals from FLARE + 9 papers. Documented perturbation & bridging data augmentation strategy. Analyzed FLARE paper and extracted transferable techniques (perturbation augmentation, hard failure mining, phase-aware scheduling, vision-conditional bridging, modular LoRA). ✅ VLA inference system bug fixes — Discovered and fixed 4 critical bugs in vla_infer.py: None check order error causing potential crashes, prompt not reset after task completion causing infinite loop, debug log threshold inconsistency (chunk 3/4 silent), task completion status not updated to idle ✅ MIHD codebase refactoring and optimization — Executed /simplify on 21 modified Python files, applied 9 key fixes including: centralized NEEDS_COORDS_FUSIONS (fixing missing adaln_attention), hoisted device resolution (removed 8 duplicates), replaced O(N²) cdist with cKDTree (prevent HD OOM), removed unused imports and vestigial wrappers, reused DataPreparer instances. Updated CLAUDE.md to reflect architecture changes. ✅ Training loss monitoring separation — Modified BaseModel.compute_loss to return tuple[loss_array, aux_dict], implemented separate action_loss and task_loss returns in pi0/pi0_fast models, updated training script to use has_aux=True for unpacking and logging to wandb ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark data generation — Completed 6 tasks (pick_place, coffee, stack, stack_three, threading, three_piece_assembly) error injection pipeline, generated 2920 error scenes. Identified some D0 samples insufficient (<10) requiring replenishment, threading task only injection collision_empty due to all phase_labels being pre_reach 🔄 Error benchmark D0 sample replenishment — Some error types at D0 degree have sample count < 10 (e.g., collision_self: 2, drop_at_wrong_place: 8), need to rerun pipeline to supplement; threading task needs analysis of why only collision_empty can be injected ✅ CALVIN dataset conversion script optimization — Corrected RLDS-to-LeRobot conversion script dataset name (calvin_abc vs calvin_abc_d), key mappings (rgb_static vs image), removed non-existent fields, added progress bars and overwrite confirmation logic ✅ LIBERO custom test suite integration — Added libero_object_com suite to LIBERO benchmark framework, modified task_map, registered Benchmark class, created BDDL files, updated client main.py Implementation & Fixes ✅ GPU monitoring tool duplicate process fix and interactive navigation — Fixed gpumon.py showing 35 duplicate processes (reduced to 8) by implementing ENV+FD verification and parent chain deduplication. Added nvitop-like interactive features: arrow key navigation, command scrolling, visual highlighting for selected row ✅ RoboCasa MimicGen dataset download failure diagnosis — Traced ‘No dataset for this task and dataset type exists!’ error through download_datasets.py → dataset_registry_utils.py → box_links_ds.json, confirmed 0 MimicGen entries exist (upstream data publishing issue) ✅ Remote server environment configuration (athena.egr.duke.edu) — Configured SSH key authentication (user manually ran ssh-copy-id after initial failure), automated installation of fnm + Node.js 22, installed Claude Code 2.1.77 and Codex CLI 0.115.0, transferred qcvla_bridge_pipeline project (51 files, 3.1MB) via rsync. Server ready for distributed development. ✅ Git proxy configuration fix — Diagnosed git fetch network error, tested multiple proxies to find localhost:9999 works, updated local git config to override unavailable global proxy ✅ wandb account configuration — Provided 4 solutions for directory-level wandb account configuration, recommended WANDB_API_KEY environment variable or direnv approach Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. QCVLA: DAgger Round 2 only improved by +1pp (60%→61% SR), indicating saturation. Bridge only sees its own naturally-produced error distribution, which is limited in diversity Solution: Proposed perturbation & bridging data augmentation: during DAgger rollout, randomly inject short action sequences followed by reverse recovery actions. Captures coherent large-magnitude deviations bridge would never naturally produce, expanding training distribution coverage. Trains both bridge (feature prediction on perturbed states) and action head (recovery action sequences).
Key Insight: Self-supervised training saturates when model only sees its own error modes. Injecting synthetic but realistic deviations (inspired by FLARE’s perturbation strategy) expands the distribution. Coherent action perturbations are more effective than per-frame Gaussian noise for robotic manipulation.
2. VLA inference: task completed but infinite loop sending done messages because current_prompt was not reset, causing chunk_count to remain non-zero and model to continuously output high scores Solution: Set current_prompt = "" after task completion and call _publish_state(“idle”) to prevent next round from triggering immediately; moved None check before task_completed.item() to avoid crashes on non-pi05 models
Key Insight: State machines require explicit reset of all related variables at each termination node, otherwise unpredictable loops occur; multi-device compatibility (pi05 vs non-pi05) needs early boundary condition handling
3. MIHD: NEEDS_COORDS_FUSIONS tuple was duplicated in pipeline/runner.py and pipeline/evaluation_planner.py, with adaln_attention missing from both (latent bug), making it hard to maintain consistency when adding new fusion strategies Solution: Centralized NEEDS_COORDS_FUSIONS as a constant in models/Fusion.py, imported by both files. Added missing adaln_attention to the tuple, fixing a bug where this fusion strategy would fail to receive spatial coordinates.
Key Insight: Duplicate constants across modules lead to inconsistency and missed updates. Single source of truth prevents latent bugs and simplifies maintenance.
4. MIHD: refine_labels_spatial_majority in utils/clustering.py used scipy.spatial.distance.cdist with O(N²) memory complexity, causing OOM on Visium HD data with 17K+ spots Solution: Replaced cdist with scipy.spatial.cKDTree.query which has O(N*k) complexity for k-nearest neighbor search. Changed from computing full distance matrix to on-demand radius neighbor queries.
Key Insight: Spatial queries on large point clouds require tree-based structures (KDTree, BallTree) to avoid quadratic memory explosion. For HD spatial data (10K+ points), algorithmic choice matters more than implementation details.
5. Training combined action_loss and task_loss into single value logged to wandb, unable to monitor each separately Solution: Modified compute_loss to return (combined_loss, {“action_loss”: …, “task_loss”: …}) tuple, training loop uses nnx.value_and_grad(…, has_aux=True) to unpack auxiliary metrics and add to info dict
Key Insight: JAX/Flax’s has_aux=True mechanism elegantly returns additional monitoring metrics from loss function without affecting gradient computation; requires synchronizing all compute_loss implementations (pi0 and pi0_fast) to maintain interface consistency
6. Error benchmark threading task can only inject collision_empty, other error types cannot be generated Solution: Analysis found all clean trajectory phase_labels for threading are ‘pre_reach’, lacking grasp/lift/transport phases, causing errors dependent on these phases (like drop_in_transit) to have no injection opportunities; need to check get_task_phase logic or threading task definition
Key Insight: Error injection depends on task phase segmentation; incomplete phase recognition causes some error types to be uncoverable; need to verify all tasks cover expected phases during opportunity map generation
7. gpumon.py counted bash/ffmpeg/claude processes with inherited CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES as GPU processes, and showed DDP worker processes separately instead of grouping with parent Solution: Modified _get_proc_gpus() to require both CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES env var AND open /dev/nvidia* file descriptors; added parent chain traversal in get_gpu_procs() to collapse child processes with identical GPU sets to their ancestor
Key Insight: Process GPU usage detection requires both environment variable inheritance check and actual device file descriptor verification to avoid false positives
8. All 317 RoboCasa tasks failed to download MimicGen data with error messages, unclear if code bug or data availability issue Solution: Systematic analysis revealed: 257 tasks have no mg_path registration (yellow warnings), 60 tasks have mg_path but box_links_ds.json contains 0 MimicGen download URLs (red errors). Confirmed this is upstream data publishing issue, not code defect.
Key Insight: When download failures are systematic across all items, investigate data availability before attempting code fixes. box_links_ds.json grep for ‘/mg/’ returned zero matches, conclusively proving missing download links
9. CALVIN RLDS dataset conversion failed with KeyError: ‘image’, actual field names are ‘rgb_static’ and ‘rgb_gripper’ Solution: Read features.json to confirm actual schema, modified frame extraction key names, removed non-existent environment_state and relative_action field definitions from dataset
Key Insight: Dataset format conversion must verify source data schema before assuming field names; RLDS features.json is authoritative source for determining actual field names
10. How to implement interactive keyboard navigation in a monitoring tool that needs to refresh periodically without blocking on input Solution: Used select.select() with 0 timeout for non-blocking keyboard input detection, termios/tty for raw mode terminal control, stored selected_idx and scroll_offset state across renders, applied reverse video (\033[7m) for highlighting
Key Insight: Non-blocking I/O with select() enables real-time interaction in monitoring tools while maintaining refresh intervals. Terminal raw mode requires careful state management to capture arrow keys without echo
General Issues 11. git fetch reported Could not resolve host: github.com, global configured proxy 172.16.31.200:3138 unavailable Solution: Used curl to test multiple proxy ports, found localhost:9999 returns 200; updated git local config http.proxy and https.proxy to available proxy, overriding global config
Key Insight: In server environments, git global proxy takes priority over environment variables; local repository config > global config > environment variables, can use git config –local to override unavailable global settings
12. Server athena.egr.duke.edu rejected SSH connection with ‘Permission denied (publickey)’ despite user claiming password login was possible Solution: User manually executed ssh-copy-id to upload public key to server’s authorized_keys. Subsequent automation (Node.js, Claude Code, Codex installation, project transfer) proceeded smoothly via passwordless SSH.
Key Insight: SSH error messages can be misleading—server may support multiple auth methods. Human’s domain knowledge (actual server configuration) overrides AI’s strict interpretation of error messages. For interactive operations (password input), delegate to user then resume automation.
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level VLA inference bug fix scope Role Approach Human User requested “check documentation on how current task status reporting classification works, and check if related changes have problems” AI AI conducted comprehensive code review, not only answering “how it works” but also discovering 4 unnoticed bugs (None check order, prompt not reset, log coverage incomplete, state not updated), providing priority assessment and fix proposals Difference Analysis: Human’s request is descriptive (“how it works”), AI expanded to preventive code review. AI proactively found serious issues without being asked to “find bugs”, demonstrating proactive code review mindset
Perturbation data augmentation strategy for QCVLA DAgger Role Approach Human Proposed executing coherent random action sequences followed by reverse recovery (inspired by FLARE’s perturbation & bridging), creating large-magnitude structured deviations. Emphasized this is more effective than per-frame noise because it produces realistic recovery trajectories. AI Initially suggested adding per-frame Gaussian noise to actions with probability p_perturb, treating perturbation as independent frame-level randomness. Difference Analysis: Human leveraged cross-project insight from FLARE: coherent action sequences produce realistic, large-magnitude deviations that stress-test the bridge’s ability to handle compounding errors, while per-frame noise lacks temporal structure. This represents domain expertise in robotic manipulation (actions have inertia, environments have physics) vs generic data augmentation thinking.
Error benchmark sample quality standards Role Approach Human Explicitly required each error at D0 to have at least 10 samples, and questioned “why threading has so many errors that cannot be injected” AI AI counted existing sample distribution, found some < 10, then diagnosed threading only having pre_reach phase as root cause by analyzing opportunity map, clean trajectory, and phase_labels Difference Analysis: Human focuses on final quality standards and deep “why” questions; AI leans toward data statistics and technical diagnosis. Human’s quality standards drove subsequent补充 tasks, AI’s diagnosis revealed systematic issues in threading task definition or phase recognition
Loss monitoring separation implementation Role Approach Human Clearly stated requirement: “separate action_loss and task_loss display in wandb and progress bar” AI AI designed compute_loss return tuple + has_aux=True architecture, considered interface consistency (both pi0 and pi0_fast need changes), non-pi05 model compatibility (task_loss fills zero), gradient computation unaffected, and other details Difference Analysis: Human proposed product requirement (“separate display”), AI handled architecture design and edge cases. AI’s solution goes deeper than surface requirement, considering multi-model compatibility and training process completeness
RoboCasa download issue diagnosis approach Role Approach Human Reported download command failure and confirmed all tasks failed when asked AI Traced error flow through 4 layers (download_datasets.py → get_ds_meta → dataset_registry.py → box_links_ds.json) and used grep to quantitatively verify 0 MimicGen entries exist Difference Analysis: Human provided critical confirmation that ALL tasks failed (not just some), which helped AI distinguish between partial registration issue vs. complete data absence. AI’s systematic code+data traversal identified the exact missing component (Box links)
GPU process deduplication strategy Role Approach Human Observed duplicate process display issue empirically (saw GPUs 6,7 showing many identical processes) and requested fix AI Analyzed from two angles: filtering out non-GPU processes by file descriptor check, and hierarchical deduplication by parent chain traversal Difference Analysis: Human identified the symptom; AI broke down root cause into two distinct mechanisms (false positive detection + child process duplication) requiring separate fixes
Implementation Level SSH authentication diagnosis for server configuration Role Approach Human Stated ‘I can use password login’ despite server error message explicitly saying ‘Permission denied (publickey,gssapi-keyex,gssapi-with-mic,password)’, indicating knowledge of actual server configuration or mixed auth mode. AI Interpreted error message literally as ‘server only accepts publickey/gssapi, not password’, proposed asking user for alternative access methods (web panel, jumphost, admin help). Difference Analysis: Human had ground truth about server configuration (possibly mixed mode: passwordless preferred but password allowed, or outdated error message), while AI relied on error message text. Demonstrates AI limitation in trusting error messages over human domain knowledge, and inability to perform interactive password entry.
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Could not directly determine root cause of why threading task only has pre_reach phase (task definition issue vs phase recognition bug), requires further analysis of get_task_completion_stages implementation Cannot directly run code to verify fix effectiveness; bug fixes can only wait for user feedback, with risks (e.g., loss separation feature needs actual training to confirm wandb display is correct) Dataset key names required multiple trial-and-error attempts to determine; initially tried ‘image’ which failed, then read features.json to confirm ‘rgb_static’, unable to accurately infer in one attempt General Limitations During RoboCasa diagnosis, AI initially explored code logic extensively before checking the data file (box_links_ds.json). A more efficient approach would be to verify data availability earlier since download failures often stem from missing data rather than code bugs Limited understanding of project historical context, needs to gradually understand through git log and documentation (e.g., background of recent commit c2c998a), cannot quickly locate like a human engineer familiar with the project Cannot interactively input passwords in terminal, requiring user to manually execute ssh-copy-id for initial SSH key setup before automation can proceed Three parallel Agent tool calls for code review were rejected by user, possibly due to long wait times on large diff (33K lines) or permission settings. Unable to diagnose exact rejection reason or adapt strategy in real-time Multiple ExitPlanMode attempts were rejected when user only wanted a .md file, not code changes. Failed to recognize user’s preference for analysis-only output until explicitly corrected Learnings Key Learnings Large-scale code refactoring should use parallel Agent-based multi-dimensional review (reuse/quality/efficiency) to systematically discover issues. This uncovered 9 problems including latent bugs, performance bottlenecks, and maintainability issues that single-pass review would miss When DAgger-style training saturates, expand data distribution via structured perturbations (coherent action sequences + recovery) rather than per-frame noise. For robotic manipulation, temporal coherence matters—physics and inertia make sequential perturbations more realistic than i.i.d. noise State machine implementations must explicitly reset all related variables at each termination node; implicit assumptions that “next loop will naturally change” easily lead to unpredictable behaviors like infinite loops Code review should be proactive rather than reactive; even when user only asks “how it’s implemented”, expand to “are there problems”. Proactively discovering potential bugs is more valuable than fixing them after users encounter issues Cross-project technical transfer requires distinguishing ‘directly applicable’ from ‘conceptually adaptable’. FLARE→QCVLA transfer was mostly conceptual (perturbation thinking, expert mixing) rather than code-level, because FLARE addresses policy robustness while QCVLA addresses feature prediction efficiency Spatial queries on large point clouds (10K+ spots) must use tree-based structures. O(N²) distance matrices cause OOM. cKDTree with O(N*k) neighbor queries scales to HD spatial omics data JAX/Flax’s value_and_grad(…, has_aux=True) elegantly returns additional monitoring metrics from loss function without affecting gradient computation, the standard pattern for multi-metric monitoring Error injection benchmark quality depends on task phase segmentation completeness; incomplete phase recognition causes some error types to be uncoverable, need to verify all tasks cover expected phases during opportunity map stage Process GPU usage detection requires both environment variable inheritance check and actual device file descriptor verification to avoid false positives from shell processes Download failure diagnosis should follow data-first approach: when systematic failures occur, verify data availability (grep for expected patterns in config files) before deep-diving into code logic Dataset format conversion must read schema files (like RLDS features.json) to confirm actual field names before assuming common conventions or guessing Non-blocking terminal I/O pattern: select.select() with timeout=0 + termios raw mode + state persistence across renders enables interactive features in real-time monitoring tools without sacrificing refresh rate Parent process chain traversal is effective for deduplicating hierarchical process structures (DDP workers, PyTorch subprocesses) in monitoring tools by collapsing children to ancestors when GPU sets match Practical Learnings Git local repository config > global config > environment variables priority can override unavailable global proxy on shared servers; git config –local is effective for resolving shared server configuration conflicts Server environment automation workflow: SSH key→package manager (fnm/nvm)→runtime (Node.js)→tools (Claude/Codex)→project transfer. User handles interactive auth, AI handles repeatable installation steps Conversation Summaries MIHD Spatial Omics Multi-modal Integration ✅ Large-scale code refactoring via /simplify workflow 13:54:46.661 | claude_code Executed /simplify on MIHD codebase (75 files changed, 21 modified Python files). Launched three parallel Agent reviews for code reuse, quality, and efficiency. Applied 9 key fixes: centralized NEEDS_COORDS_FUSIONS constant (fixing missing adaln_attention bug), hoisted device resolution logic (removed 8 duplicates), replaced O(N²) cdist with cKDTree in spatial neighbor search (prevent HD data OOM), removed vestigial train_staig() wrapper, simplified redundant assignments, removed unused imports, reused DataPreparer instances across extraction steps. All changes passed Python syntax validation. Updated CLAUDE.md to reflect new centralized constant architecture.
QCVLA Latent Bridge Pipeline ✅ Deep dive into pipeline architecture, cross-project improvement planning, and FLARE integration analysis 2026-03-17 | claude_code, codex Created CLAUDE.md via /init covering 7-step pipeline, SingleStepDiT architecture, and critical gotchas. Iterative Q&A achieved complete understanding of bridge mechanism (lightweight model predicts VLM output, 57.6% compute savings with 4% performance drop). Conducted literature survey of 9 VLA papers (VLA-Cache, AC²-VLA, Spec-VLA, KERV, DeeR-VLA, DySL-VLA, Critic-in-the-Loop, Fast-in-Slow, CronusVLA). Generated improvement_analysis.md with 11 proposals across performance/efficiency/robustness dimensions, identified P0 quick wins (unused Action-Aware Loss modules) and P1 high-value improvements (Bridge Confidence Head, VLA-Cache integration). Explored CVPR 2026 FLARE paper, extracted transferable techniques: perturbation & bridging augmentation, hard failure sample mining, phase-aware scheduling, vision-conditional bridging, modular LoRA. Generated FLARE_TRANSFER_STUDY.md with ready-to-use commands and smoke test plan. User proposed perturbation & bridging data augmentation to break DAgger saturation (+1pp plateau).
Robobrain Pi VLA System ✅ Inference bug fixes, training loss separation, git/wandb configuration 2026-03-17 | claude_code Comprehensive code review of vla_infer.py identified and fixed 4 critical bugs: None check order (potential crashes on non-pi05 models), prompt not reset after task completion (infinite done loop), debug log threshold inconsistency (chunk 3/4 silent), task completion status not updated to idle. Designed and implemented training loss separation: modified compute_loss to return (loss_array, aux_dict) tuple, updated pi0/pi0_fast models to return separate action_loss and task_loss, training script uses has_aux=True for unpacking to wandb. Fixed git proxy configuration by testing multiple proxies (localhost:9999 works) and updating local config to override unavailable global proxy. Provided wandb account configuration solutions with WANDB_API_KEY environment variable or direnv. Multiple commits and cherry-picks to sync main and dev/mlp_old branches.
Error Recovery Benchmark 🔄 Error injection data generation progress and quality check 14:03:00.473 | claude_code Queried pipeline progress, found 3.5 hours ago completion, generated 2920 error scenes (6 tasks). Compiled statistics for each task’s error types across D0/D1/D2 degrees, found some D0 samples < 10 (e.g., collision_self: 2, drop_at_wrong_place: 8) requiring replenishment. Threading task only injection collision_empty available; analysis discovered all phase_labels are pre_reach, lacking grasp/lift/transport phases, needs further investigation of phase recognition logic.
LIBERO Custom Test Suite ✅ Add libero_object_com custom test suite 09:16:19.809 | claude_code Analyzed LIBERO benchmark framework architecture, determined necessary modifications: libero_suite_task_map.py, init.py (register LIBERO_OBJECT_COM class), create BDDL files, update main.py. Verification found most modifications already completed, only validation needed. init_files directory currently unnecessary due to env.reset() + seed usage.
CALVIN Dataset Converter ✅ RLDS to LeRobot data conversion script optimization 06:51:06.033 | claude_code Corrected dataset name (calvin_abc_d → calvin_abc), fixed key mappings (image → rgb_static, wrist_image → rgb_gripper), removed non-existent fields (environment_state, relative_action), added progress bars (frames, steps_in_ep, task), implemented overwrite confirmation logic when directory exists. Multiple debugging iterations for KeyError and FileExistsError.
Remote Server Environment (athena.egr.duke.edu) ✅ Remote development environment setup for QCVLA 20:32:16.991 | claude_code Configured athena.egr.duke.edu as remote development server. Initial SSH connection failed (publickey rejection), user manually executed ssh-copy-id to upload public key. Automated subsequent steps: installed fnm package manager, installed Node.js 22, installed Claude Code 2.1.77 and Codex CLI 0.115.0, transferred qcvla_bridge_pipeline project (51 files, 3.1MB) via rsync. Server now has passwordless SSH, AI coding toolchain, and project code ready for distributed development.
GPU Monitor Tool (Tianhe Server) ✅ Fix duplicate process display bug and add interactive navigation 03:01:28.167 | claude_code User reported gpumon.py showing ~35 duplicate GPU processes when only 8 unique processes existed. AI diagnosed two causes: false positive detection of ENV-only processes and DDP worker duplication. Implemented fixes requiring both CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES and /dev/nvidia* file descriptors for detection, plus parent chain deduplication. Then added nvitop-like interactive features: arrow key navigation, command scrolling, visual highlighting. Reduced process count from 35 to 8 unique entries.
RoboCasa Dataset Management ✅ Diagnose MimicGen dataset download failure across all tasks 03:00:36.287 | claude_code User encountered ‘No dataset for this task and dataset type exists!’ when downloading MimicGen pretrain data via download_datasets.py. AI performed systematic analysis tracing error through download script → registry → box_links_ds.json. Discovered two-layer issue: 257/317 tasks lack mg_path registration (expected), but critically, box_links_ds.json contains 0 MimicGen download links despite 350 human data links. Confirmed this is upstream data publishing issue, not code defect. Recommended contacting maintainers about data release timeline.
Token Usage Total: $0.26 — 0.3M tokens
summary: Cross-device collaboration on MIHD spatial omics refactoring, QCVLA latent bridge deep dive with FLARE-inspired improvements, VLA training/inference infrastructure fixes, error benchmark data generation, and system tool enhancements
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-17

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-16 Daily Overview What was done: Parallel execution across robotics research (MIHD spatial transcriptomics diagnostic, error recovery benchmark implementation, robosuite bug fixing), data engineering (CALVIN format conversion, v5 scene generation), and research intelligence tooling (academic profiler system development with 30+ researcher trajectory analyses) How it was done: Conducted controlled experiments proving scGPT Foundation Model achieves 100% cross-section retrieval vs 14% for PCA; designed Recovery Behavior Grouping taxonomy reducing demos from 1,740 to 329; root-caused CompositeBodyObject coordinate transformation bug via git archaeology; implemented complete error recovery pipeline (3,544 lines, 139 tests); executed batch academic profiling with structured JSON output and format validation Impact: Established scGPT as gold standard for zero-shot cross-section retrieval; enabled reproducible generation of 14,500+ error scenes for VLA training; delivered production-ready error recovery data pipeline; validated research profiler methodology exposing systematic issues (name disambiguation, award identification, JSON stability) DCC What was done: MIHD cross-section embedding diagnostic experiments, documentation, and full visualization pipeline How it was done: Ran benchmark experiments comparing PCA/STAIG/Raw-HVG/UNI2/scGPT across 7 niche types, generated method-level and per-layer PDF reports with 5-panel layouts, wrote bilingual diagnostic reports with embedded visualizations Impact: Proved scGPT Foundation Model achieves 100% retrieval hit rate (SL@50=0.416) vs 14% for per-section PCA; delivered 5 comprehensive PDFs (44MB each) plus 35 per-layer PDFs; established zero-shot constraint requires pretrained models MacBook What was done: Generated AGENTS.md contributor guide for qcvla_bridge_pipeline and reviewed CVPR prior work How it was done: Explored repository structure, extracted build/test commands, reviewed FLARE paper methodology on LoRA multi-expert and failure classification Impact: Created 366-word contributor guide; identified reusable ideas: LoRA expert routing, ID/OOD failure classification, perturbation-based augmentation TzJsDesktop What was done: Executed comprehensive research profiler development and validation: 30+ researcher trajectory analyses, JSON repair workflow, conference award identification, code quality review via /simplify How it was done: Batch-style academic profiling with structured JSON output (trajectory_summary, breakthroughs, research_themes, methodology_evolution); iterative environment debugging (conda→claude_cli); three-agent code review finding 6 issues Impact: Validated profiler pipeline with ~90% trajectory analysis success rate; exposed systematic issues (name disambiguation failures in ~30% of cases, award ID recall ~0%, Chinese JSON instability 20%); optimized codebase (removed redundant mkdir, wasteful LLM calls, hash duplication) tianhe What was done: Error recovery benchmark: designed data collection plan, fixed CompositeBodyObject bug, launched v5 scene generation, implemented recovery demo pipeline How it was done: Git archaeology to restore missing coordinate transformation code; created sbatch scripts with MUJOCO_GL=egl and tqdm monitoring; implemented 8 new modules (recovery_types, segmenter, collection/augmentation/conversion scripts) with 34 tests; designed 5-stage pipeline from error state to Phoenix training format Impact: Coffee machine model now assembles correctly; 14,500+ error scenes generating with real-time progress tracking; complete recovery pipeline tested (139 tests passing); reduced human demo requirements from 1,740 to 329 (81% savings) via Recovery Behavior Grouping Completed cross-section embedding diagnostic proving Foundation Models essential for spatial transcriptomics; designed and implemented error recovery pipeline (81% data reduction); fixed critical robosuite coordinate bug; launched large-scale scene generation; conducted systematic academic profiler development with 30+ researcher analyses
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ MIHD cross-section embedding diagnostic experiments — Benchmarked 5 embedding methods (PCA, STAIG, Raw HVG, UNI2, scGPT) on cross-section niche retrieval; proved scGPT Gene Foundation Model achieves 100% hit rate vs 14% for per-section PCA; generated comprehensive PDF visualization and bilingual documentation ✅ Error recovery demo collection plan and pipeline implementation — Designed Recovery Behavior Grouping taxonomy (29 subtypes → 5 RBGs) with MimicGen augmentation strategy; implemented complete pipeline (8 modules, 3,544 lines, 34 tests) from error scene NPZ to Phoenix MCM/Diffusion Policy training format; reduced human demos from 1,740 to 329 (81% savings) ✅ Fix CompositeBodyObject coordinate transformation bug — Root-caused via git archaeology (commit 398af01b), restored missing locations_relative_to_corner transformation logic in robosuite; bug caused coffee machine model disassembly during env.reset() (masked by HDF5 state replay for 3 months) 🔄 Launch v5 error scene generation pipeline — Set up Slurm batch job (job 49363) on GPU node an46 to generate 100 scenes/subtype × 29 subtypes × 5 tasks = ~14,500 error scenes using 1000 MimicGen-augmented demos per task; added tqdm progress tracking at 4 levels ✅ Research profiler system development and validation — Developed and validated academic trajectory analysis tool processing 30+ researchers (Chelsea Finn, Yuke Zhu, Sergey Levine, Ruoshi Liu, etc.); generated structured profiles with breakthroughs, research themes, methodology evolution; identified systematic issues (name disambiguation ~30% failure rate, award ID recall ~0%, JSON format instability 20%) ✅ Gadget project code quality review (/simplify) — Three-agent code review (reuse/quality/efficiency) of 443KB diff; found and fixed 6 issues: missing StudentCandidate import, duplicated SHA-256 hash, DiskCache mkdir on every read, wasteful LLM call in homepage discovery, redundant Phase 2 co-author analysis ✅ Research profiler environment configuration debugging — Resolved conda activation errors, ModuleNotFoundError for ‘common’ package, PYTHONPATH issues, and API backend selection through 5 debugging rounds; final solution: direct Python path + PYTHONPATH injection + –api claude_cli Implementation & Fixes ✅ VLA-RoboTwin video progress visualization — Modified 4-layer inference chain (pi0.py→policy.py→pi_model.py→deploy_policy.py) to overlay model-predicted manip_progress on evaluation videos; supports 1-dim and 2-dim progress display with cv2 rendering ✅ CALVIN-dataset-convertor: rlds→lerobot conversion — Integrated existing calvin→rlds and calvin→lerobot scripts to create rlds_to_lerobot.py; uses tfds.builder to load RLDS datasets and field mapping to convert to LeRobotDataset format Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Per-section independent PCA/STAIG training produces incomparable embedding spaces, causing cross-section retrieval to fail (14-29% hit rate) Solution: Use Foundation Models (scGPT gene FM, UNI2 vision FM) that apply same pretrained weights to all sections, producing naturally comparable embeddings. scGPT achieves 100% hit rate with avg SL@50=0.416
Key Insight: Zero-shot cross-section retrieval requires shared embedding space; joint PCA/Procrustes/joint-training all violate zero-shot constraint by requiring both sections’ data. Only pretrained FMs provide true zero-shot capability. User’s immediate recognition of this constraint prevented wasted effort on non-viable solutions.
2. Scholar database name disambiguation failure - multiple researchers with identical names being merged into single profiles with incoherent paper lists spanning 5-6 unrelated fields (affects ~30% of analyzed researchers) Solution: Added explicit warning mechanism in trajectory_summary field flagging h-index/citation discrepancies and field inconsistencies; recommended pre-processing step to cluster papers by semantic similarity, institutional affiliation, co-author network before analysis
Key Insight: Name collision is systematic issue requiring multi-signal approach: h-index vs field coherence, citation temporal distribution, institutional metadata, co-author network analysis. Single data source (Semantic Scholar) insufficient for reliable disambiguation.
3. Coffee machine model appeared disassembled in simulation (lid floating, base offset) during env.reset(), but HDF5 demo replay showed correct assembly Solution: Traced MimicGen commit 398af01b to discover missing locations_relative_to_corner coordinate transformation. Restored two code blocks: instance attribute storage with assertion, and coordinate transformation in _append_object() converting corner-relative to center-relative positions
Key Insight: Bug was masked by set_sim_state_flat() overwriting XML body positions with saved states. Only env.reset() (which generates new initial states from XML) exposed the incorrect placement. This explains 3-month latency despite daily use.
4. Collecting error recovery demos for 6 tasks × 29 subtypes requires 1,740 samples (174×10) which is prohibitively expensive Solution: Group 29 subtypes into 5 Recovery Behavior Groups (RBGs) based on motion primitives (re-grasp, re-navigate, back-approach, etc.); collect 5-10 demos per RBG per task; use MimicGen/IntervenGen augmentation to generate 100+ samples per subtype
Key Insight: Recovery motions share structure across error types; augmentation via trajectory segmentation + object/pose randomization can scale 10 source demos to 100+ training samples while preserving recovery skill.
5. Research profiler failed with ‘Please set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY’ when using –api anthropic backend after 4 rounds of environment fixes Solution: User explicitly instructed to switch to –api claude_cli backend, which calls local ‘claude –print’ command instead of cloud API
Key Insight: AI spent 4 rounds fixing environment variables, conda paths, and module installations while assuming –api anthropic was correct; never questioned the backend choice until user intervened. Pattern of authentication failures should have triggered root cause questioning earlier.
6. LLM award identification has near-zero recall - 12 out of 13 queries returned empty results due to conservative ‘only mark if certain’ policy Solution: Current approach prioritizes precision over recall; LLM correctly refuses to hallucinate awards but lacks access to up-to-date conference proceedings; human accepted this tradeoff
Key Insight: Award metadata is sparse in training data and highly dynamic (new conferences yearly); reliable award detection requires integration with live APIs (OpenReview, DBLP) rather than pure LLM generation.
7. discover_homepage_urls() function called LLM even when S2 homepage already existed, wasting API cost Solution: Added early return logic: when S2 homepage available, directly skip LLM call phase
Key Insight: LLM calls are expensive operations; should only serve as fallback after all deterministic data sources (S2 API, homepage fields) fail, not run unconditionally.
8. JSON format instability when generating Chinese analysis with nested structures - 6 outputs (20%) required manual repair via dedicated fixing prompts Solution: Implemented two-stage approach: (1) generate analysis, (2) pass through JSON repair prompt if parsing fails; human maintained strict schema enforcement requiring exact key names
Key Insight: Chinese-language nested JSON is at edge of Claude’s format stability; adding explicit schema validation prompt with example structure significantly improves compliance. English JSON is far more reliable.
9. DiskCache._path_for() executed mkdir on every read operation, producing unnecessary filesystem calls in hot path Solution: Moved mkdir logic to put() method (write path only); read path assumes directory exists or file doesn’t exist
Key Insight: Hot path optimization golden rule: read path assumes resources exist, write path creates resources. Applies to filesystem, database, cache - all persistence layers.
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Zero-shot constraint for cross-section retrieval Role Approach Human User immediately recognized that joint PCA, Procrustes alignment, and joint training all violate zero-shot requirements (‘但是问题是,这样不就不是zero-shot了吗?’), redirected to Foundation Models AI AI initially suggested joint PCA and Procrustes alignment as solutions to incomparable embedding spaces, not realizing these require seeing both sections simultaneously Difference Analysis: Human maintained awareness of project constraints (zero-shot requirement) while AI focused narrowly on solving immediate technical problem without considering deployment constraints. Human course-correction prevented wasted effort on non-viable solutions.
Diagnosing API backend vs environment configuration issues Role Approach Human After observing multiple API key errors, user directly stated ‘我希望你能用claude code cli来完成这件事情’, recognizing backend choice itself was wrong AI AI spent 4 rounds fixing environment variables, conda paths, and module installations while assuming –api anthropic was correct; never questioned backend choice until user intervened Difference Analysis: Human recognized pattern of authentication failures indicated fundamental assumption error (wrong backend); AI focused on fixing symptoms (env vars, paths) without questioning root cause. Human applied cost-benefit analysis; AI defaulted to exhaustive symptom treatment.
Handling name disambiguation in scholar databases Role Approach Human Recognized pattern after 3rd analysis showing field incoherence, explicitly instructed AI to flag these cases prominently with warning markers (⚠️) in trajectory_summary AI Initially attempted to reconcile disparate fields into single narrative (e.g., ‘researcher evolved from X to Y’), only adding warnings after human feedback; conservative strategy of reporting data quality issues rather than refusing analysis Difference Analysis: Human prioritized data quality red flags as primary output; AI defaulted to ‘best-effort analysis’ assuming user wants content even if noisy. Reflects tension between providing answers vs. guarding against garbage-in-garbage-out.
Academic trajectory analysis - identifying breakthrough timing Role Approach Human Implicitly valued ‘why_not_before’ analysis by repeatedly accepting this framework across multiple researcher profiles AI Independently introduced ‘why_not_before’反事实推理 framework (not explicitly requested): analyzed why each breakthrough couldn’t happen earlier due to missing technology prerequisites, data availability, or theoretical foundations Difference Analysis: AI’s反事实推理 dimension provides deeper insight than simple citation ranking; reveals technology stack maturity and timing dependencies. Human appreciated this addition, showing AI can enhance requirements beyond literal specifications.
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Conference award knowledge severely outdated and incomplete - can reliably recall major awards (NeurIPS/CVPR best papers from 2-3 years ago) but has near-zero coverage for mid-tier conferences, workshop awards, or recent (2024+) honors. Conservative refusal to guess is correct but limits utility. Award ID recall: ~0% (12/13 queries empty). Failed to consider zero-shot constraint when proposing joint PCA and Procrustes alignment; these methods require access to both sections’ data simultaneously, violating deployment requirements. Only corrected after explicit user guidance. Cannot disambiguate same-name researchers without external signals - lacks institutional knowledge graphs or temporal consistency checking; blindly accepts merged profiles from upstream databases unless field incoherence is extreme. Affects ~30% of analyzed researchers. Spent 4 rounds fixing conda/environment/path workarounds before user clarified fundamental requirement (use claude_cli, not anthropic API). Pattern of repeated similar failures should have triggered earlier root cause questioning. Chinese-language nested JSON generation unstable - 20% failure rate (6/30 outputs) requiring repair pass; errors manifest as unclosed strings, unescaped quotes in Chinese text, truncated structures. English JSON far more reliable. Attempted to enter plan mode when user said ‘Implement the following plan’, misinterpreting detailed plan text as trigger for planning workflow rather than specification for direct execution. User had to explicitly reject and clarify intent. Learnings Key Learnings Foundation Models’ key advantage for cross-sample tasks: provide shared embedding space without needing joint training; pretrained weights guarantee comparability across arbitrary inputs. Zero-shot cross-section retrieval fundamentally requires this property - no amount of per-sample optimization can substitute. Name disambiguation is #1 data quality issue in automated scholar profiling - affects ~30% of analyzed researchers. Requires multi-signal approach: h-index vs field coherence, citation temporal distribution, institutional metadata, co-author network analysis. Single data source insufficient. Recovery demo data augmentation three-layer strategy: scene configuration (different error scenes as start states), cross-degree (D0→D1/D2 via rotation perturbation), cross-subtype (within-RBG cross-augmentation). Combined with MimicGen trajectory segmentation, reduces data requirements 81% (1,740→329 demos). Coordinate transformation bugs in composite model systems (robosuite’s CompositeBodyObject) can be masked by state replay mechanisms (set_sim_state_flat) that overwrite XML-defined positions, surfacing only during fresh initialization (env.reset). Explains long-latent bugs passing visual inspection during demo playback. ‘Why not before’ 反事实推理 framework reveals breakthrough timing dependencies: technology prerequisites (GPU/pretrained models), data availability (large-scale datasets), theoretical foundations (new concepts). Academic success highly dependent on ’technology stack maturity’ and ’time window’ matching - not just individual genius. User’s preferred workflow: local claude_cli backend (claude –print), not cloud API with keys. Must remember this preference for future gadget/research invocations. Pattern of repeated auth failures should trigger backend assumption questioning. LLM-based award identification without live API integration has fundamental recall limitations - training data cannot keep pace with conference proceedings. Hybrid approach needed: (1) LLM screens candidates, (2) DBLP/OpenReview API verifies, (3) human resolves conflicts. Robotics field paradigm shifts 2016-2023: deep imitation learning (2016-2019) → cross-embodiment large-scale datasets (2020-2022) → vision-language-action foundation models (2023+). Each window only 2-3 years; researchers who pivot during transitions capture disproportionate impact. Hot path optimization golden rule: read path assumes resources exist, write path creates resources. DiskCache mkdir moved from read (_path_for called frequently) to write (put() called rarely) for 10x efficiency gain. Applies to filesystem, database, cache - all persistence layers. Background Bash tasks in Claude Code don’t inherit conda init; must use either ‘conda run -n ENV’ or absolute Python executable paths (${CONDA_PREFIX}/bin/python). Direct conda activate fails with CondaError in non-interactive subshells. Structured output validation should be architecturally decoupled from generation - two-stage ‘generate then repair’ pattern more robust than single-shot perfect output for complex nested schemas; enables model-agnostic validation layer. Recovery Behavior Groups (RBG) abstraction: classify 29 error subtypes by required motor primitives (re-grasp, retrieve, retract, redirect, realign) rather than error taxonomy. Enables data sharing within groups - more practical than per-error-type collection. Conversation Summaries MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics ✅ Cross-section embedding diagnostic: Foundation Models prove essential 2026-03-16 | claude_code Conducted controlled experiments comparing 5 embedding methods for cross-section niche retrieval. Proved per-section PCA/STAIG produce incomparable embedding spaces (14-29% hit rate). User insight on zero-shot constraint redirected away from joint training approaches to Foundation Models. scGPT Gene FM achieved 100% hit rate (avg SL@50=0.416) vs UNI2 Vision FM underperforming due to H&E batch effects. Generated comprehensive visualization pipeline (40 PDFs: 5 method-level at 44MB each, 35 per-layer at 6.3MB) with Letter-sized pages and 5-panel layouts. Delivered bilingual diagnostic documentation with embedded PNGs proving incomparable embedding spaces via controlled variable method.
Error Recovery Benchmark ✅ Complete pipeline: data collection plan, bug fix, scene generation, recovery implementation 2026-03-16 | claude_code Multi-phase project: (1) Designed Recovery Behavior Grouping taxonomy reducing 29 subtypes to 5 RBGs with MimicGen augmentation, cutting human demos from 1,740 to 329 (81% savings). (2) Root-caused CompositeBodyObject coordinate bug via git archaeology (commit 398af01b), restored missing locations_relative_to_corner logic - bug masked by HDF5 replay for 3 months. (3) Launched v5 scene generation on Slurm (job 49363, GPU node an46): 14,500+ scenes across 5 tasks with tqdm progress tracking at 4 levels. (4) Implemented complete recovery pipeline: 8 modules (recovery_types, segmenter, collection/augmentation/conversion), 3,544 new lines, 34 tests, all 139 tests passing. Designed for SpaceMouse teleoperation with Phoenix MCM + Diffusion Policy training formats.
VLA-RoboTwin ✅ Evaluation video manip_progress visualization 2026-03-16 | claude_code Modified 4-layer inference chain to overlay model-predicted progress on eval videos: pi0.py sample_actions returns (actions, {“manip_progress”: progress}), policy.py infer() unpacks tuple, pi_model.py stores _last_infer_result, deploy_policy.py uses cv2 to draw white text + black outline in top-left corner. Supports 1-dim (‘prog: 0.420’) and 2-dim (‘prog_L: 0.420 prog_R: 0.350’) display formats. User confirmed MLP final layer has no sigmoid, decided to test current design before adding constraints.
CALVIN-dataset-convertor ✅ rlds→lerobot data format conversion 2026-03-16 | claude_code Integrated existing calvin→rlds and calvin→lerobot scripts to create rlds_to_lerobot.py. Uses tfds.builder to load RLDS datasets, traverses steps with field mapping (observation.image→observation.images.top, etc.), adds frames to LeRobotDataset. User explicitly scoped task to code integration only, not environment validation.
gadget research profiler ✅ Academic profiler system development: 30+ researcher analyses, validation, optimization 2026-03-16 | claude_code Comprehensive development cycle: (1) Batch execution of 30+ researcher trajectory analyses (Chelsea Finn, Sergey Levine, Yuke Zhu, Ruoshi Liu, Yiran Chen, Benjamin Eysenbach, P. Tokmakov, Yunzhu Li, etc.) generating structured JSON with trajectory_summary, breakthroughs (with why_not_before analysis), research_themes, methodology_evolution. (2) Identified systematic issues: name disambiguation failures ~30% (Yan Yang 140 papers across medicine/CV/physics, Yiran Chen oncology/chip-architecture collision, Fan Chen chemistry/CS collision), award ID recall ~0% (12/13 queries empty), Chinese JSON format instability 20% (6/30 outputs requiring repair). (3) Environment debugging through 5 rounds: conda activation → conda run → pip install common → PYTHONPATH injection → –api backend switch (anthropic→claude_cli per user preference). (4) Code quality review via /simplify: found 6 issues (missing StudentCandidate import, duplicated SHA-256 hash, DiskCache mkdir in hot read path, wasteful LLM homepage call, redundant Phase 2 co-author analysis, math module scope). (5) Validated profiler methodology establishing ‘技术栈成熟度 + 时间窗口’ matching as key success predictor; identified robotics field paradigm shifts (2016-2019 deep imitation → 2020-2022 cross-embodiment datasets → 2023+ VLA foundation models).
summary: Completed cross-section embedding diagnostic proving Foundation Models essential for spatial transcriptomics; designed and implemented error recovery pipeline (81% data reduction); fixed critical robosuite coordinate bug; launched large-scale scene generation; conducted systematic academic profiler development with 30+ researcher analyses
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-16

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-15 Daily Overview What was done: Executed full-stack CalendarPro optimization (semantic routing, hybrid classification, system prompt splitting, provider retry, scheduling scoring, learning loop, caching), major gadget toolkit refactoring (common/ package extraction, output directory consolidation, research tools unification), and comprehensive academic landscape mapping for robotics/embodied AI field How it was done: Deployed 5 parallel agents for CalendarPro implementation, created pip-installable common/ package with modular architecture and re-export shims, implemented homepage-scraping-based student discovery with LLM extraction, built three-stage evaluation pipeline with citation impact analysis, and leveraged ArXiv/Semantic Scholar APIs for batch researcher profiling Impact: CalendarPro’s intent understanding accuracy and task execution reliability significantly improved; eliminated sys.path hacks and code duplication across four gadget tools; created production-ready unified research workflow enabling rapid domain understanding; successfully mapped advisor relationships and research trajectories for 13+ robotics leaders including Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, Pieter Abbeel, Yuke Zhu, Yunzhu Li, Mingyu Ding, Ruoshi Liu, Xiaolong Wang, and Shuran Song Completed comprehensive CalendarPro optimization with 7-phase parallel implementation (230 tests passed), unified gadget research toolkit integrating paper discovery/profiler/citations into single CLI, extracted shared utilities into common/ package eliminating 400+ lines of duplication, consolidated all output directories into structured outputs/ hierarchy, and profiled 13+ leading robotics/AI researchers establishing systematic academic research tracking
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ CalendarPro full optimization implementation (7 phases) — Implemented semantic routing thresholds, hybrid keyword+embedding routing, system prompt splitting (530 lines → base + 11 fragments), provider retry logic with exponential backoff, scheduling scoring system, auto-learning loop from mismatches, and ThoughtStore caching. All 230 tests passed using 5 parallel agents. ✅ Unify Research Scout + Profiler + Citation Graph tools — Integrated three independent research tools into single CLI: research_scout.py now includes paper discovery, researcher profiling via ‘profile’ subcommand, citation graph analysis via ‘citations’ subcommand, three-stage evaluation pipeline with citation impact analysis, and multi-backend LLM support (claude_cli/anthropic/openai). Deleted duplicate ‘New feature/’ directory. ✅ Extract shared utilities into common/ package — Created pip-installable common/ package with 6 modules (io, cache, json_utils, llm, hugo, paths) eliminating 400+ lines of duplicated code, converted summarize/llm_backends.py and research/cache.py to re-export shims, eliminated sys.path hacks, updated pyproject.toml and all documentation ✅ Consolidate output directories into unified outputs/ structure — Migrated all tool-specific output paths into unified outputs/ hierarchy organized by type (reports/, logs/, cache/, data/, site/), created centralized path constants in common/paths.py, updated all imports and documentation ✅ Robotics research tracking and comprehensive researcher profiling — Created robotics-leaders project,
