Helper functions (_paper_id, _paper_url) centralize cross-source logic and make future expansions trivial DLPFC tissue layer characteristics directly impact embedding cross-sample matching: Layer_1 (superficial, thin) and Layer_4 (granular, cell-dense) show Spearman ρ>0.6; Layer_3 (largest, heterogeneous) shows ρ<-0.2. Embedding quality correlates with structural uniqueness and boundary clarity, not layer size. RoboTwin data format: 14-DOF action space (left_arm 6 + gripper 1 + right_arm 6 + gripper 1), stored as HDF5 with joint_action/vector, observations in compressed image format. Demo_clean directory structure: data/episodeX.hdf5, instructions/episodeX.json with seen/unseen language variations. STAIG fusion training for spatial omics: UNI2 (1536-dim vision) + PCA (50-dim gene) → STAIG fusion encoder (64-dim). Key insight: alignment mode uses tau=26, softmax_log edge weights, 300 pseudo-clusters, 550 epochs. Model can collapse (all-zero embeddings) if training fails. UniVLA training pipeline: three stages (LAM stage-1/2, VLA pretraining, per-benchmark finetuning). CALVIN training: DiskCalvinDataset loads auto_lang_ann.npy for episode-language mappings, reads episode_*.npz windows (12-13 frames), extracts rgb_static → LAM → latent_action_idx → VLA input → ActionDecoder → rel_actions. Cross-environment tool invocation (conda run -n ) provides clean isolation for sibling repos with conflicting dependencies. Critical to set cwd to tool repo root, as many Python scripts use relative imports and sys.path manipulation Production logging requires two handlers: StreamHandler (INFO) for user-facing progress, RotatingFileHandler (DEBUG) for diagnostic details. Keep print() in interactive UI commands (list, show) but migrate all operational/diagnostic messages to logger CALVIN evaluation dataset structure: only needs validation/.hydra/merged_config.yaml for environment initialization. Episode .npz files not read during evaluation (online simulation generates observations). Data extraction can reduce dataset size from 1.3GB to 600KB. Zero-dependency API clients (urllib.request + xml.etree) are viable for production tools when avoiding new dependencies is critical. bioRxiv and PubMed both implement with stdlib only, maintaining gadget’s minimal dependency philosophy Python package distribution for MCP servers: standard pattern is PyPI + uvx for stateless tools, but pip install -e . (editable install) required when server depends on repo data directories. Entry point in pyproject.toml [project.scripts], then claude mcp add gadget – gadget-mcp. Practical Learnings When integrating new fusion strategies into existing pipeline: (1) register in apply_fusion() dispatcher, (2) add to needs_coords tuple in runner.py and evaluation_planner.py, (3) add config dict entry in runner.py, (4) define experiment in pipeline_config.yaml. This 4-step integration pattern is standard across all trainable fusion methods. When updating documentation, grep for CLI flags and function names to systematically identify gaps rather than relying on manual code review Conversation Summaries MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics ✅ Full-day work spanning cross-sample analysis, cross-modal enhancement, and literature review 23:31:05.195 | claude_code Executed comprehensive development across 6 sessions: (1) Cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark for 151673-151508/151676 using PCA+UNI2+STAIG_fusion, revealing high correlation for distinct layers (Layer_1/4 ρ>0.6) but negative for heterogeneous Layer_3; generated 7 spatial heatmaps and detailed Chinese documentation. (2) Designed CrossModalEnhancer using spatial k-NN neighbors as attention context to avoid single-token degradation. (3) Implemented CrossModalEnhancer (~350 lines) with 3 modes and integrated into 5 pipeline files; solved PyTorch 2.9/CUDA 12.9 cuBLAS bug via mini-batch loss after 4 architecture iterations. (4) Updated CLAUDE.md with CrossModalEnhancer, RM-IDEAL commands, and enriched utils descriptions. (5) Streamlined CLAUDE.md architecture section from ~30 to ~10 lines. (6) Conducted systematic scGPT literature review finding zero-shot underperformance vs traditional methods (PCA, scVI). Encountered GPU compatibility issues requiring CPU fallback for cross-sample visualization task.
UniVLA ✅ Create comprehensive CLAUDE.md documentation and analyze training pipeline 03:35:00.014 | claude_code Used /init skill to explore UniVLA codebase and create CLAUDE.md. Documented build/lint/test commands (torchrun, accelerate, black/ruff), training stages (LAM, VLA pretraining, finetuning), evaluation (LIBERO/CALVIN/R2R/SimplerEnv), and architecture (VLM→PrismaticVLM→OpenVLA, vision/LLM backbones, action tokenization paths, draccus config, RLDS pipeline). Followed with discussion of CALVIN dataset requirements and training data pipeline analysis (DiskCalvinDataset loading auto_lang_ann.npy, episode_*.npz windows).
OpenPI (Pi0) ✅ Explain flow matching implementation in pi0.py 11:37:19.597 | claude_code User asked to explain flow matching in policy/pi05/src/openpi/models/pi0.py. Analyzed conditional flow matching implementation: linear interpolation path x_t = t·noise + (1-t)·actions, Beta(1.5,1) time sampling [0.001,1], target vector field u_t = noise - actions, MSE loss training, Euler integration with 10 steps for sampling from t=1→0. Explained time conditioning via sinusoidal embeddings + MLP or adaRMS.
VLA (RoboTwin) ✅ Convert RoboTwin demo_clean to LeRobot format 16:18:03.707 | claude_code User collected 50 episodes of place_dual_shoes data via RoboTwin/collect_data.sh in demo_clean/ directory. Created plan to convert to LeRobot format (different from existing zip-based script: 14-DOF instead of 16, extracted directory instead of zip). Implemented convert_robotwin_democlean_to_lerobot.py, converted 11,459 frames successfully. Fixed output directory issue by using LeRobotDataset.create(root=…) parameter, moved dataset from cache to RoboTwin/data/place_dual_shoes_lerobot/.
CALVIN environment 🔄 Troubleshoot pip install failures and create dataset extraction script 10:24:15.816 | claude_code User encountered DNS resolution failures (Errno -3: Temporary failure in name resolution) when running calvin/install.sh in Kubernetes pod. Diagnosed: nameserver 10.96.0.10 (cluster DNS), proxy localhost:9997, unable to resolve pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn or pypi.org. Identified cluster networking issue but didn’t resolve. Separately created extract_eval_files.py to extract only validation/.hydra/merged_config.yaml and metadata from CALVIN dataset, reducing size from 1.3GB to 600KB.
Gadget MCP Server ✅ Transform gadget tools into MCP server for AI agent discovery 19:34:52.537 | claude_code Designed and implemented MCP server exposing 9 gadget tools (summarize, research, benchmark). Created pyproject.toml, .mcp.json, and mcp_server.py with stdout capture and SystemExit handling. Fixed FastMCP version parameter issue, installed mcp package, verified all tools. Discussed distribution options (PyPI, Smithery). Final discussion shifted to returning content to AI rather than writing files by default, leading to refactoring of 5 tools to return full markdown/JSON content with optional save parameter.
Workspace Documentation ✅ Create CLAUDE.md for multi-project workspace 18:36:35.440 | claude_code User triggered /init command. AI launched 5 parallel Explore agents to analyze all projects (CalendarPro, OCR, gadget, get-shit-done, openclaw). Generated workspace-level CLAUDE.md with overview table, quick reference commands per project, and architecture highlights (EventBus, singleton factories, multi-agent execution, Gateway RPC, etc.).
CalendarPro ✅ Integrate gadget research/summary tools into CalendarPro 18:59:53.249 | claude_code User wanted CalendarPro to automatically run gadget’s research_scout and daily_summary on schedule. After exploration and user questions, chose subprocess + conda run approach over MCP. Implemented 6 new files (protocol, runner, gadget_tools, two services) and modified 2 (config, coordinator). Created 13 unit tests, all passing. Fixed –no-banner conda flag issue. Updated CLAUDE.md with new subsystems.
Gadget Research Scout ✅ Comprehensive enhancement: logging, multi-source support, and documentation updates 22:09:15.410 | claude_code Across 3 sessions: (1) User asked for research_scout explanation; AI described 6-step pipeline and user reported empty motivation/innovation fields, requesting logging plus bioRxiv/PubMed support. (2) Executed 5-phase plan: added RotatingFileHandler logging (~77 print statements migrated), implemented empty-field/zero-score warnings, generalized arxiv_id to paper_id with backward compatibility, added bioRxiv and PubMed search (urllib.request, zero new deps), implemented multi-source orchestration with –source CLI flag. File grew from 2155 to 2654 lines. (3) Updated CLAUDE.md to document author search (–author flag, search_arxiv_author), init –from-overview (LLM-based project creation), and updated line count.
Token Usage Total: $16.60 — 24.6M tokens
summary: 完成MIHD空间转录组核心功能（跨样本RM-IDEAL评估、CrossModalEnhancer跨模态增强、scGPT文献调研）、推进多个VLA/机器人项目（UniVLA文档、RoboTwin数据转换、flow matching分析）、实现gadget工具MCP服务器化与CalendarPro集成、增强research_scout多数据源支持
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-09

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-08 Daily Overview What was done: Extended research paper discovery tool with author-based search, generated error benchmark visualizations, designed and implemented critical region annotation methodology for VLA training across 10 manipulation tasks, fixed RoboTwin rendering bugs, and planned comprehensive symlink removal refactoring How it was done: Implemented new arXiv search function with cache support, used MuJoCo EGL rendering for batch video generation, designed distance-based heuristics with gripper-state filtering for critical regions, configured Vulkan/EGL environment for headless GPU rendering, mapped 65-symlink dependency graph Impact: Research tool now supports three search modes (keyword, conference, author), created 9 reviewable benchmark videos, enabled new VLA training approach to address systematic failure patterns, unblocked RoboTwin data collection pipeline, improved error_recovery_benchmark maintainability TzJsDesktop What was done: Implemented author search feature for research_scout.py and updated project documentation to reflect current state How it was done: Added search_arxiv_author() function mirroring conference search pattern, updated cache layer and CLI parsers, corrected CLAUDE.md line counts and missing feature docs Impact: Users can now search papers by specific researchers, documentation accurately reflects 2024-line codebase with all current features tianhe What was done: Generated error benchmark visualizations, designed and implemented VLA critical region annotation system, fixed RoboTwin rendering infrastructure, planned symlink removal refactoring How it was done: Batch-generated MP4s from existing .npz scene data, implemented 11-file critical region annotation with distance-based heuristics, added Vulkan/EGL environment configuration, comprehensively mapped 65-symlink dependencies Impact: Created 9 reviewable videos for different error types, delivered complete critical region annotation system with empirical tuning capability, resolved blocking rendering errors, created actionable refactoring plan Extended research tool with author search and updated documentation, generated error benchmark visualizations, designed and implemented critical region annotation system for VLA training, and fixed RoboTwin rendering infrastructure
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Design critical region annotation methodology for VLA — Designed distance-based heuristics with gripper-state filtering to annotate critical regions for 10 RoboTwin manipulation tasks ✅ Implement critical region annotation system — Modified _base_task.py and 10 task files to inject critical_region labels and ee_target_distances into observation data ✅ Implement author search feature (–author flag) — Added search_arxiv_author() function, updated cache functions to support author parameter, modified cmd_search() and cmd_report() for author search mode, added CLI arguments to search and report subparsers 🔄 Plan symlink removal for error_recovery_benchmark — Mapped 65 symlinks and created comprehensive refactoring plan to eliminate backward-compatibility technical debt Implementation & Fixes ✅ Generate visualization videos for error benchmark — Created 9 MP4 videos from coffee and stack task error scenes, covering natural failures (grasp_miss, premature_release, overshoot) and injection errors (tip_over) ✅ Update research CLAUDE.md documentation — Corrected line count (~1870 → ~2000), documented –language flag, added dedup early-stop feature (known_ids + 5-consecutive-known), updated section numbering ✅ Fix RoboTwin rendering device error — Added Vulkan/EGL environment variables to collect_data.sh to enable headless GPU rendering ✅ Locate episode_num configuration — Identified episode_num field in task_config YAML files controlling data collection count ✅ Review CLAUDE.md documentation — Validated existing CLAUDE.md is production-quality with no changes needed Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. VLA models fail consistently at specific task phases (imprecise pressing in click tasks, failed grasping/placing in manipulation tasks) Solution: Designed distance-based heuristics with gripper-state filtering to annotate binary ‘critical region’ labels during data collection, enabling cls token classifier training
Key Insight: Systematic VLA failures at task-critical phases can be addressed by training a binary classifier to predict when the agent is in high-stakes regions requiring extra caution
2. All critical_region labels were 0 after data collection, indicating threshold (0.10m) was too conservative Solution: Added get_ee_target_distances() method to record raw EE-to-target distances for all tasks, enabling empirical threshold tuning
Key Insight: When designing ML data labeling heuristics, always log raw features first before committing to threshold values for labels
General Issues 3. SAPIEN renderer fails with ‘RuntimeError: failed to find a rendering device’ in headless environment Solution: Added three Vulkan/EGL environment variables to collect_data.sh (VK_ICD_FILENAMES, __EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES, LD_LIBRARY_PATH) pointing to nvidia_libs directory
Key Insight: Headless GPU servers require explicit Vulkan ICD and EGL vendor configuration to locate NVIDIA rendering drivers, even when nvidia-smi works correctly
4. SSH commands failed with ‘No such file or directory’ when running visualization script from remote shell Solution: Changed from relative paths to absolute paths for both project root and Python script, ensuring working directory was correctly set before execution
Key Insight: SSH does not inherit working directory; must use ‘cd /absolute/path && command’ or provide absolute paths to all file arguments
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Critical region annotation threshold selection Role Approach Human User empirically discovered 0.10m threshold was too small after collecting data, requested distance logging to tune threshold based on actual data distribution AI AI chose 0.10m threshold based on code analysis of existing pre_grasp_dis patterns without empirical validation Difference Analysis: Human took iterative empirical approach informed by actual data; AI made theoretical choice based on code patterns. Human correctly identified need for observability (distance logging) before finalizing heuristic parameters
Implementation planning for author search feature Role Approach Human Provided complete implementation plan upfront with specific line numbers, function signatures, mutual exclusion checks, and verification commands AI Initially wanted to explore codebase first, ask clarifying questions via AskUserQuestion, then launch Plan agent to design the feature Difference Analysis: Human preferred directive top-down approach with detailed spec, AI preferred exploratory bottom-up approach with question-driven discovery
Symlink removal refactoring scope Role Approach Human User requested complete removal of all 65 symlinks to eliminate technical debt, focusing on end goal of clean structure AI AI comprehensively mapped symlink dependency graph and designed surgical refactoring plan with careful import rewrites to minimize breakage Difference Analysis: Human focused on strategic goal (eliminate technical debt); AI focused on tactical execution (safe migration path). Both necessary - human sets direction, AI ensures safe implementation
AI Limitations Critical Limitations AI initially chose critical region threshold (0.10m) based on code patterns without empirical validation, resulting in all labels being 0. Required iterative correction to add distance logging for proper threshold tuning based on actual data distribution General Limitations AI required multiple exploration rounds with subagents to fully map the symlink dependency graph (65 total symlinks across root, scripts/, and nested output directories with complex import patterns) Failed to correctly handle SSH working directory context, requiring multiple attempts to construct command with proper absolute paths Learnings Key Learnings For VLA training on manipulation tasks, binary classification of ‘critical regions’ using distance-based heuristics + gripper-state filtering can systematically address failure patterns at task-critical phases (pressing, grasping, placing) arXiv API supports author queries via ‘au:“Author Name”’ syntax, can be combined with keyword filters using AND/OR operators, and uses relevance-based sorting for author searches When designing ML data collection heuristics, always implement observability (log raw features) before committing to threshold-based labels. Empirical tuning requires actual data distribution visibility research_scout.py now supports three distinct search modes with different caching strategies: keyword/category (date-sorted, lookback-filtered), conference (relevance-sorted, comment-filtered), author (relevance-sorted, no post-filter) Symlink-based backward compatibility creates complex dependency graphs requiring comprehensive mapping before refactoring (65 symlinks × multiple import patterns = high breakage risk without careful planning) Practical Learnings MuJoCo visualization can be generated from pre-saved .npz scene data without re-running full policy rollouts, enabling efficient post-hoc video generation Headless GPU rendering in SAPIEN requires three Vulkan/EGL environment variables: VK_ICD_FILENAMES, __EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES, and LD_LIBRARY_PATH pointing to NVIDIA driver library directory Conversation Summaries gadget/research ✅ Implement author search feature and update documentation 03:25:22.536 | claude_code User provided detailed implementation plan for adding –author flag to enable searching papers by specific researchers. Assistant implemented search_arxiv_author() function, updated cache layer to support author parameter with dedicated cache files, modified both search and report commands to handle author mode with mutual exclusion checks against –conference. Also verified three previously implemented features (init –from-overview, weekly-to-daily renaming, search deduplication), and updated CLAUDE.md with corrected line count (2024 vs 1870), added –language flag docs, documented dedup early-stop feature, and fixed section numbering. All changes verified with syntax check.
error_recovery_benchmark ✅ Visualization generation and CLAUDE.md review 01:15:43.352 | claude_code User asked if render visualizations were available. Assistant found existing visualizations from old pick_place task but nothing for current 6-task run. Discovered scene data existed (150-743 scenes per task) and could be rendered immediately. After resolving SSH working directory issues, successfully generated 9 MP4 videos covering different error types across coffee and stack tasks using MuJoCo EGL rendering on GPU6. Also ran /init to review existing CLAUDE.md, determined it was production-quality and needed no changes. User then requested removal of all 65 backward-compatibility symlinks; AI mapped dependencies and created comprehensive refactoring plan.
RoboTwin VLA ✅ Critical region annotation design and implementation 04:18:09.279 | claude_code User identified VLA systematic failures at task-critical phases (pressing for click tasks, grasping/placing for manipulation). Requested heuristic annotation of ‘critical regions’ to train cls token binary classifier. AI comprehensively explored RoboTwin pipeline, analyzed 10 task environments, designed distance-based heuristics with gripper-state filtering. Implemented full system across 11 files: modified _base_task.py with helper methods (_ee_dist, get_critical_region_label, get_ee_target_distances), injected critical_region and ee_target_distances into get_obs(), overrode methods in all 10 task files with task-specific heuristics. User discovered all labels were 0 after data collection; AI added distance logging for empirical threshold tuning. Also fixed collect_data.sh rendering bug by adding VK_ICD_FILENAMES, __EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES, and LD_LIBRARY_PATH exports, and located episode_num configuration in task_config/*.yml files.
summary: Extended research tool with author search and updated documentation, generated error benchmark visualizations, designed and implemented critical region annotation system for VLA training, and fixed RoboTwin rendering infrastructure
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-08

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-07 Daily Overview What was done: Parallel progress across MIHD, ErrorRecovery, DesktopVideo, gadget, and CalendarPro projects, completing technical documentation, training pipeline launch and fixes, application development, and research infrastructure buildout How it was done: Through parallel agent codebase exploration for documentation generation, multi-GPU training task launch, evaluation metric bug fixes, WKWebView webpage wallpaper with browse mode implementation, batch historical report generation, context-aware intent classification system, and research scout paper management system from zero to production with LLM-driven enhancements Impact: Established complete MIHD technical specifications and research positioning, demonstrated that zero-shot scGPT underperforms PCA fundamentally, enabled reliable FLARE/BC-RNN training and evaluation pipelines, achieved complete Desktop Video solution for arbitrary webpage wallpapers, backfilled 20+ days of February work history for traceability, significantly improved CalendarPro intent classification accuracy, and built automated paper discovery and evaluation infrastructure with bilingual support and efficient search DCC What was done: Completed MIHD CLAUDE.md precise update, created overview.md research positioning document, systematically investigated scGPT/scGPT-spatial performance, executed cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark How it was done: Parallel agents explored codebase to extract API and statistics for incremental documentation patches, launched 4 parallel agents to retrieve arXiv papers and benchmark data, executed Python benchmark scripts analyzing Spearman correlations Impact: Project documentation upgraded from descriptive overview to precise technical specification, established patch query clinical vision-driven zero-shot vs STAIG differentiated narrative, demonstrated zero-shot scGPT clustering fundamentally underperforms PCA (PCA ARI=0.261 vs scGPT=0.170, 54% gap) MacBook What was done: Implemented complete WKWebView webpage wallpaper functionality for Desktop Video (YouTube/Bilibili etc.) and fixed browse mode interaction, scrollbar transparency, volume sync bugs; implemented Windows/UNIX dual-platform Hugo auto-deployment for gadget summarize tool; batch-generated partial historical reports How it was done: Extended ContentType enum with .web type, controlled playback through WKWebView + sandbox network permissions + JS injection, fixed interaction layer with window.level=.normal, fixed transparency by removing drawsBackground; created update.ps1 PowerShell script and run_hugo_update() cross-platform detection function Impact: Desktop Video achieved complete solution for playing arbitrary webpage videos as desktop wallpaper (resolved sandbox network blocking, window layer non-interaction, scrollbar transparency issues); gadget tool auto-deployment works on both Windows and UNIX platforms TzJsDesktop What was done: Batch-processed gadget report tool for 20+ historical dates from early February to early March across multiple devices, implemented CalendarPro context-aware intent classification system (360 tests passed), completed research scout paper management system from zero development (~750-line core script + two-stage evaluation pipeline) to production enhancement (language configuration, reverse initialization, deduplication, bug fixes) How it was done: Repeatedly executed daily_summary.py export and merge commands for different dates; implemented context_dependent keyword classification and session-aware conversation history management; reused summarize/ LLM backend pattern for arXiv search + two-stage LLM evaluation pipeline; installed Hugo Extended via winget; systematically enhanced research_scout.py with LLM-driven overview.md parsing, threaded language configuration through 3-layer prompt system, arxiv_id-based deduplication with early-stop logic, and fixed project ID/directory name mismatches Impact: Completed comprehensive work archive from February to early March establishing historical traceability; CalendarPro thoroughly resolved intent classification inaccuracy caused by keyword pre-check interception (ambiguous queries like ‘几点了’); established automated research paper discovery and evaluation infrastructure for 3-5 active projects with bilingual output, efficient search, and reverse project initialization capability tianhe What was done: Launched FLARE D0×4 training (stable 6.3s/step), fixed 4 BC-RNN evaluation metric bugs (demo_len/trigger_step/transport/RP), completed 191 multi-task error scenarios, started LLaVA MPM/MCM parallel training, optimized Phoenix data loading (5%→80%+ GPU utilization), resolved LLaVA base model download bottleneck through offline format conversion How it was done: Used PYTHONPATH isolation to fix openpi configuration conflict, fixed BC-RNN metric distortion through code review + _get_target_pos() runtime dynamic target position retrieval, implemented 6-task shared generation script via task_registry dynamic routing, obtained LLaVA base model through offline safetensors→pytorch_model.bin format conversion, eliminated training bottleneck with ZeRO-3→ZeRO-2 + HDF5 preloading Impact: FLARE 4 D0 tasks running stably on an53 GPU (~63h/task), BC-RNN evaluation metrics trustworthy (all 127 unit tests passed, GPU validation RP highest 0.800), 191 error scenarios across 6 tasks laid foundation for subsequent training, LLaVA MPM/MCM training compressed from estimated 13.5 days to ~2 days parallel launch but exposed dataset scale estimation error requiring replanning, Phoenix GPU utilization optimization solution ready Across four devices in parallel: completed MIHD documentation system and scGPT research, launched FLARE/Phoenix training and fixed 4 BC-RNN evaluation bugs, implemented Desktop Video webpage wallpaper with WKWebView, batch-generated 20+ days of February historical reports, completed CalendarPro context-aware intent classification, and built research scout paper management system from scratch to full-featured tool with language configuration, reverse initialization, and deduplication
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ BC-RNN evaluation pipeline 4 bug fixes — Fixed collector.py:487 demo_length→demo_len field name error, fixed trigger_step reading from scene.dataset instead of scene.detected_by, fixed transport stage detection logic (added _get_target_pos() runtime dynamic target position retrieval), added if success: rp=1.0 in collector.py; all 127 unit tests passed, GPU validation confirmed demo_len=605, trigger_step=55 non-zero and RP highest 0.800 ✅ Desktop Video WKWebView webpage wallpaper complete implementation — Created WebNavigationHandler.swift, extended ContentType enum with .web, implemented showWeb/cleanupWebView/saveWebBookmark, updated AppDelegate playback state management, updated SingleScreenView UI (URL input field), updated history, thumbnails, localization and ChangeLog, added sandbox network permissions, set customUserAgent to modern Chrome UA ✅ research scout system implementation from scratch — Implemented research/research_scout.py from zero (~750-line core script), including project CRUD, arXiv search (arxiv package), LLM three-backend support (anthropic/openai/claude_cli), paper evaluation scoring, direction suggestion generation, weekly report output (JSON+Markdown), Hugo deployment, caching mechanism. Designed two-stage paper evaluation pipeline: Stage 1 fast screening of all papers (title+abstract, judge high/low relevance) → Stage 2 deep analysis of high-relevance papers (≤20 papers, 3 highlights: insight/motivation/experiments/limitations). Completed requirements.txt, CLAUDE.md, TUTORIAL.md Chinese tutorial ✅ MIHD CLAUDE.md precision update — Added Section 3.2 implementation details (detector/injector/validator/classifier/coordinator 5 subsections), corrected VLA model types (Pi0-FAST→Phoenix/Flare), updated code statistics (scripts 18→22, test cases 109→122), updated M14 actual evaluation data (726→6474 episodes), added database phase/severity distribution, added pipeline configuration structure explanation, added detailed cache directory structure ✅ MIHD overview.md research positioning document creation — Created 676-line overview.md including one-sentence positioning (systematic evaluation of Foundation Model zero-shot embeddings in spatial transcriptomics multimodal fusion clustering), 4 core questions, 3-stage technical roadmap, 10 fusion strategy rankings, 8 open questions, search keywords, literature notes template, project structure quick reference, quick start commands ✅ scGPT/scGPT-spatial and PCA performance systematic investigation — Retrieved scGPT-spatial paper, zero-shot limitation paper, BioLLM framework paper, MIHD internal results through 4 parallel agents, aggregated analysis demonstrating zero-shot scGPT clustering fundamentally underperforms PCA (PCA ARI=0.261 vs scGPT=0.170, 54% gap) 🔄 Desktop Video browse mode implementation and window layer fix — Implemented browse mode framework (WallpaperWindow dynamic canBecomeKey, ESC listener, control panel button exit). Window layer went through multiple adjustments, discovered .desktopWindow layer cannot receive any mouse/keyboard events, confirmed need to use .normal layer but not yet fully implemented ✅ CalendarPro context-aware intent classification system implementation — Implemented context-aware intent classification system (split general_keywords into always_general and context_dependent to avoid ambiguous queries like ‘几点了’ triggering wrong classification) and session-aware conversation history management (auto-compression, session reset, token budget control), completed A/B/C/D four-stage plan, added 41 specialized tests, all 360 tests passed ✅ Fix error-recovery-benchmark project configuration — Fixed 3 critical bugs: project.json ID mismatch with directory name, overview.md section headers not matching pipeline regex patterns, missing auto-append marker 🔄 FLARE D0×4 task training launch — Running coffee_D0, stack_D0, stack_three_D0, threading_D0 Pi0.5 LoRA warmup training on an53 GPU 1,2,4,7, ~36K steps per task (~63 hours). First attempt failed due to PYTHONPATH issue, successfully relaunched after fix, running stably at 6.3s/step ❌ LLaVA MPM/MCM parallel training launch — Confirmed paper didn’t release LLaVA LoRA checkpoint, cluster lacks complete llava-v1.5-7b base model, MPM training data llava_motion.json (460MB, 14M rows) ready; obtained base model through offline safetensors→pytorch_model.bin format conversion, modified lora.sh ZeRO-3→ZeRO-2 to avoid gradient partitioning; MPM/MCM launched in parallel on an53 GPU 3,5, but discovered severe training duration estimation error (13.5 days vs expected hours), dataset scale estimation error (160K→1M), halted by user requiring replanning ✅ Phoenix data loading optimization — Modified TrajectoryIndexSpeedDataset in trajectory_data.py, added _preload_all_data() to preload all HDF5 data to memory (uint8 saves 4× storage), changed getitem to numpy slicing, set get_dataloader to num_workers=8, prefetch_factor=2, resolved DataLoader bottleneck causing only 5-6% GPU utilization ✅ gadget summarize Windows cross-platform deployment support — Created website/update.ps1 (complete PowerShell equivalent of update.sh), added run_hugo_update() cross-platform function in daily_summary.py (Windows prioritizes update.ps1 with PowerShell, fallback to Git Bash for update.sh; UNIX uses bash for update.sh), replaced 3 hardcoded bash calls in daily_summary.py and 1 in monthly_summary.py ✅ Batch generation of February historical reports (20+ days) — Used gadget/summarize tool on TzJsDesktop to generate structured reports for 20+ historical dates from 2026-02-01 to 2026-03-06, covering multiple key sessions across MIHD, ErrorRecoveryBenchmark, CalendarPro, gadget, DesktopVideo projects, processing cross-device (DCC, MacBook, tianhe, TzJsDesktop) conversation data through export/merge two-stage process ✅ Add language configuration system to research_scout.py — Implemented full language support: _DEFAULT_LANGUAGE constant, _language_instruction() helper, {language_instruction} in 3 prompt templates, language parameter through entire pipeline, –language CLI flag, config.json integration ✅ Implement init –from-overview feature — Added LLM-driven reverse initialization: create_project_from_overview() function, OVERVIEW_EXTRACT_PROMPT for parsing, CLI args (–from-overview, –api, –timeout) ✅ Multi-task BC-RNN error scenario generation — Generated error scenarios using injection+natural_capture modes for 6 tasks’ BC-RNN models in training environment, added –task parameter to 1c_generate_from_policy.py for automatic checkpoint/dataset/task_config parsing, fixed 2_visualize_scene.py is_natural guard and load_demo_actions extension, completed 191 error scenarios total ✅ Robot Manipulation 3D-MVP paper deep evaluation — Deep analysis of arXiv 2406.18158v2 (3D-MVP: 3D Multiview Pretraining for Robotic Manipulation), extracted three key highlights: ①3D-aware pretraining significantly improves downstream generalization through Objaverse large-scale dataset, ②modular decoupling of visual encoder and action decoder enables modular pretraining, ③multi-view input provides robust 3D scene understanding through implicit triangulation. Output includes relevance=4/novelty=4/inspiration=4 scores and benchmark design suggestions ✅ Implement arxiv_id deduplication and early-stop in search — Added _load_known_arxiv_ids() to scan cache, modified search_arxiv() with consecutive-known counter (stops after 5 consecutive cached papers), threaded known_ids through cmd_search and cmd_report Implementation & Fixes ✅ Generate and improve CLAUDE.md for research/ directory — Analyzed 1871-line research_scout.py codebase, created comprehensive CLAUDE.md with code organization section (0-10 sections), parameters table, key implementation details, and pipeline function names ✅ Desktop Video transparency bug fix and volume sync — Removed webView.setValue(false, forKey: “drawsBackground”) to fix WKWebView scrollbar track transparency, added .background(Color(nsColor: .windowBackgroundColor)) to ScrollView in AppMainWindow.swift to fix control panel slider transparency, removed isWebContent guard in setVolume() to show volume/mute controls for all content types, fixed applyWebSettings to check isGlobalMuted for volume sync when switching content ✅ Windows environment Hugo installation and configuration — daily_summary.py merge step failed calling Hugo deployment with ‘hugo’ is not recognized error. Installed version 0.157.0 via winget install Hugo.Hugo.Extended, needed to restart PowerShell for PATH environment variable to take effect, finally successfully executed Hugo build and deploy ✅ Rename all ‘周报’ references to ‘日报’ — Systematic text replacement across 12+ locations: function names (generate_weekly_report → generate_daily_report), docstrings, UI messages, markdown headers Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. BC-RNN RP(0.003) abnormally lower than Random RP(0.020), evaluation results completely unreasonable Solution: Code review found 4 bugs jointly causing metric distortion: demo_len=0 broke SPL, trigger_step=0 further broke SPL, transport always False capped RP at 0.5, successful episode RP not 1.0 caused baseline deviation. Fixed collector.py:487 field name demo_length→demo_len, collector.py:488 changed to read from scene.dataset for trigger_step, env_wrapper.py added _get_target_pos() runtime dynamic target position retrieval, collector.py added if success: rp=1.0
Key Insight: Evaluation metric distortion is often not a single bug but cascading chain of multiple bugs. RP ceiling of 0.5 revealed transport stage judgment failure, demo_len=0/trigger_step=0 revealed field propagation errors
2. Browse mode WKWebView cannot receive mouse/keyboard events, even with ignoresMouseEvents=false ineffective; .desktopWindow layer causes window non-interactive Solution: Set window.level=.normal in enterBrowseMode (not on top but interactive), restore CGWindowLevelForKey(.desktopWindow) in exitBrowseMode
Key Insight: macOS .desktopWindow layer is below Finder desktop icons layer, windows at this layer don’t receive any input events at system level, ignoresMouseEvents=false ineffective for this layer. .normal(level=0) is lowest layer that’s both interactive and doesn’t cover other apps
3. LLaVA base model download blocked by proxy bandwidth bottleneck (~50KB/s, downloading 14GB model would take weeks); GPU node no DNS cannot direct connect; xet CDN through proxy timeout Solution: Discovered cluster had nyw’s HF format llava-1.5-7b-hf (safetensors), wrote _convert_hf_to_llava.py to convert to original LLaVA pytorch_model.bin format (including vocab truncation fix 32064→32000), entire process completed locally without network
Key Insight: Don’t assume must download—first search for existing model copies on cluster, even different formats can be converted. HF model format conversion isn’t just renaming keys, involves semantic differences (like vocab size)
4. Phoenix diffusion policy training GPU utilization only 5-6%: DataLoader num_workers=0 caused GPU to wait for CPU random reads from NFS HDF5 files Solution: Preload all HDF5 data to memory once in init (uint8 saves 4× storage), close file handles, then all getitem slices from numpy arrays; changed DataLoader to num_workers=8, pin_memory=True
Key Insight: h5py file handles cannot be shared across fork child processes (known PyTorch DataLoader limitation), only safe solution is complete loading in main process before fork. NFS random reads far slower than sequential reads, one-time loading 10× faster than on-demand
5. Phoenix MPM training after launch discovered severe training duration estimation error (actual 13.5 days vs expected hours), dataset scale estimation error (160K→1M) Solution: User halted training, requires replanning and optimization (possibly including reducing training steps, increasing GPU count, optimizing data loading, etc.)
Key Insight: Before launching large-scale training, must first verify actual dataset size, per-step time and total steps to avoid resource waste. Dataset scale estimation error can lead to weeks of wasted compute resources
6. CalendarPro intent classification ambiguity due to general_keywords pre-check interception: ‘你安排在几点了呀’ → ‘几点了’ matches general_keywords → forced GENERAL → error Solution: Split general_keywords into always_general (always force GENERAL, like ‘天气’) and context_dependent (force GENERAL only without active session, like ‘几点了’); added confirmation_words (‘ok’,‘好的’,‘是的’ etc.) with _is_confirmation_input() fast path; rewrote _process_input() as 6-step flow
Key Insight: Context-aware intent classification needs to combine session state (session_id, last_activity_at) and conversation history, pure keyword matching causes ambiguity; confirmation phrases (‘ok’) should route directly when active proposal exists
7. Current research scout paper evaluation processes all 50 papers at once, wasting tokens and lacking depth Solution: Designed two-stage pipeline: Stage 1 fast screening of all papers (only title+abstract first 1000 chars, classify high/low relevance), Stage 2 deep analysis of high-relevance papers (max 20 papers, extract 3 highlights + detailed scoring + direction suggestions)
Key Insight: Mimicking human researcher paper reading workflow (fast screening→deep reading high-value papers) can dramatically improve LLM evaluation efficiency and quality, while also generating literature reading records for low-relevance papers for future reference
8. Pipeline couldn’t read overview.md due to project.json ID (‘robot-manipulation’) not matching directory name (’error-recovery-benchmark’) Solution: Updated project.json id field to match directory name; pipeline uses project[‘id’] to construct overview.md path
Key Insight: Directory name and project.json id must be identical; _extract_current_methods() uses project[‘id’] to locate overview.md
9. Stage 2 evaluation received ‘(暂无记录)’ for current methods because overview.md section header ‘## 3. 当前技术路线’ didn’t match pipeline’s regex pattern ‘##\s*当前方法与进展’ Solution: Renamed overview.md section headers to match OVERVIEW_TEMPLATE format; removed numbered prefixes and section dividers
Key Insight: Pipeline uses strict regex matching for section extraction; template format must be exactly followed
10. FLARE norm stats calculation failed: norm stats script import openpi loaded zhaoganlong’s installed version, couldn’t find tangzijia’s new config name Solution: Set PYTHONPATH=/HOME/…/tangzijia/shared_deps/openpi/src:$PYTHONPATH in script to prioritize loading tangzijia’s openpi source copy, bypassing old version in conda env
Key Insight: When multiple users share same conda env, their source modifications create import priority conflicts. PYTHONPATH is minimal invasive solution, doesn’t affect other users
11. WKWebView scrollbar track transparent (CSS injection setting background-color ineffective) + sandbox app cannot load any network resources Solution: Removed webView.setValue(false, forKey: “drawsBackground”) to restore default opaque background making scrollbar track visible; added com.apple.security.network.client=true to .entitlements
Key Insight: drawsBackground=false makes entire WKWebView rendering layer transparent, CSS layering semi-transparent styles on transparent layer still transparent, fix must be at rendering layer not CSS layer. App Sandbox blocks all outbound network by default
12. LLM output contained too much English when user expected Chinese; no way to configure output language Solution: Implemented 3-tier language system: _DEFAULT_LANGUAGE constant, _language_instruction() generator, {language_instruction} in all prompts; config resolution chain CLI > project.json > config.json > default
Key Insight: Language instruction must be injected at prompt level, not post-processed; different sections need different language rules (JSON keys vs content)
13. No way to create project from existing overview.md without manually extracting keywords/categories Solution: Added create_project_from_overview() that uses LLM (OVERVIEW_EXTRACT_PROMPT) to parse overview.md and extract title, search_keywords, arxiv_categories, open_questions; writes project.json then copies original overview.md
Key Insight: LLM can reliably extract structured metadata from unstructured text; reuse existing _call_llm() and _parse_json_response() utilities
14. Batch generating reports repeatedly encountered incomplete or syntax-error JSON output from LLM, causing parsing failures Solution: User provided raw text, AI manually extracted and repaired JSON fragments (removed non-JSON prefixes/suffixes, fixed quote escaping, completed missing closing brackets, etc.)
Key Insight: LLM generating structured output, especially JSON with many nested objects and long text content, prone to format errors; need to add auto-repair and validation steps in pipeline
General Issues 15. Windows environment Hugo command not found, daily_summary.py merge step calling website/update.ps1 failed with ‘hugo’ is not recognized Solution: Installed Hugo 0.157.0 via winget install Hugo.Hugo.Extended, installer prompted need to restart shell, restarted PowerShell and hugo command worked
Key Insight: Windows environment winget-installed command-line tools require shell restart for PATH updates to take effect, cannot use immediately in same session
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level BC-RNN RP anomaly diagnosis strategy Role Approach Human User found BC-RNN SR near 0% in M14 inconsistent with training rollout, directly pointed to possible evaluation metric bugs, demanded systematic check of metric calculation logic AI AI initially inclined to explain ’error recovery is inherently difficult’, trying to justify low SR as expected result, rather than prioritizing suspicion of evaluation code bugs Difference Analysis: User inferred evaluation problems based on normal behavior observed during training, while AI tended to rationalize anomalous data. User’s intuition more accurate, ultimately discovered 4 cascading bugs causing metric distortion
Risk assessment before training launch Role Approach Human After Phoenix training launch, user immediately discovered severe training duration estimation error (13.5 days), decisively halted and demanded replanning to avoid resource waste AI AI didn’t fully verify actual dataset size (mistook 160K for actual 1M), per-step time (7.2s/step) and total steps (161K) before training launch, causing severe duration estimation error Difference Analysis: Humans better at risk control and timely loss cutting, AI executing tasks easily overlooks upfront validation of resource consumption
Paper reading workflow design Role Approach Human User proposed two-stage reading strategy—fast screening phase (30 seconds decide if worth deep reading) and deep understanding phase (find 3 core highlights: motivation/method insight/experimental design/limitations) AI AI initially designed single-stage full evaluation, sending all 50 papers’ complete abstracts to LLM for scoring at once Difference Analysis: Humans designed from actual researcher’s cognitive load, recognizing fast screening and deep analysis need different information granularity. AI inclined to one-time processing, overlooking token cost vs evaluation depth tradeoff
Debugging project configuration bug Role Approach Human User provided the overview.md path and asked AI to ‘fix the format’; didn’t diagnose the root cause AI AI traced through code to discover 3 interconnected bugs: ID mismatch, header format, missing marker Difference Analysis: AI’s systematic code analysis revealed cascading issues user couldn’t see; user correctly identified symptom (‘can’t read overview’) but not causes
Window layer solution iterative decision-making Role Approach Human User directly rejected .floating solution from user experience perspective (‘不要始终置顶’), after AI proposed .desktopWindow non-interactive, decisively pointed out should use .normal layer AI AI tried solutions by technical feasibility: first used .floating to ensure interaction, after rejection removed level change (kept .desktopWindow), discovered non-interactive then awaited user instruction Difference Analysis: Humans quickly eliminate unreasonable solutions from user experience and expected behavior, AI iteratively trial-and-error from technical implementation
Feature implementation details Role Approach Human User described what they wanted (‘从 overview.md 构建项目’, ‘改日报’, ‘去重’) but needed AI to propose implementation options AI AI asked clarifying questions with multiple options (init –from-overview vs replace, dedup by ID vs content hash) before implementing Difference Analysis: User had clear requirements but needed AI to explore implementation space; AI correctly recognized ambiguity and asked instead of guessing
Identifying missing language configuration feature Role Approach Human User immediately recognized the UX problem (‘总结的部分英文太多’) and asked if language could be configured AI AI didn’t proactively consider language configuration until user requested it; then implemented comprehensive solution Difference Analysis: User’s domain knowledge of bilingual academic workflows identified the gap; AI needed explicit request
Implementation Level FLARE training task quantity adjustment Role Approach Human After AI launched 9 tasks training, user requested ‘reduce to 4 D0 tasks’, clearly stating only need 4 D0 difficulty tasks AI AI initially planned full 9 tasks (3×D0+2×D1+1×D2) training, attempting to complete all tasks at once Difference Analysis: User based resource and time tradeoff, prioritized D0 task completion quality, while AI inclined toward completeness
AI Limitations Critical Limitations When diagnosing BC-RNN RP anomaly, AI initially inclined to rationalize anomalous data (’error recovery inherently difficult’) rather than prioritizing suspicion of evaluation code bugs. Only after user insisted evaluation had problems did AI systematically audit code and discover 4 cascading bugs AI didn’t fully verify actual dataset size (mistook 160K for actual 1M), per-step time and total steps before Phoenix training launch, causing severe training duration estimation error (13.5 days vs expected hours), lacking upfront validation awareness of resource consumption Insufficient understanding of OS window layer system: initially thought removing .floating layer setting would ‘maintain desktop layer’, didn’t realize .desktopWindow layer cannot receive any user input events, causing browse mode functionality completely unusable When designing paper evaluation workflow, AI didn’t spontaneously adopt human researcher’s two-stage reading strategy (fast screening→deep analysis), instead inclined toward one-time full processing engineering approach, overlooking cognitive load and cost-benefit tradeoff AI didn’t catch the project ID/directory name mismatch proactively; only discovered it after user provided the path and AI traced through _extract_current_methods() code When batch generating reports, AI repeatedly generated incomplete or syntax-error JSON (quote escaping errors, missing closing brackets, mixed non-JSON text, etc.), requiring manual intervention to repair, indicating AI’s insufficient reliability in generating ultra-long structured output AI did not proactively identify the language configuration gap until user explicitly complained about English output; should have noticed Chinese comments in codebase and suggested bilingual support earlier When processing historical records, AI cannot proactively identify which are ‘meta-tasks’ (about generating reports itself) vs ‘actual tasks’, easily confusing two levels, needs explicit human distinction When implementing configuration management, AI’s initial method used hardcoded constants, only realized need for layered configuration parsing mechanism after user explicitly stated ‘should be modifiable in config file’ General Limitations When planning the 3 new features, AI created a plan file but user immediately rejected and said ‘Implement the following plan’ instead; AI should have asked ‘Should I implement now or just create the plan?’ before writing plan file Learnings Key Learnings Zero-shot foundation model embedding fundamentally underperforms PCA in clustering tasks: FM optimizes for cross-tissue generalization capability (pretraining objective is language modeling/contrastive learning), not variance maximization; PCA does task-aligned dimensionality reduction, maximizing variance≈maximizing cluster separability. This conclusion validated by Microsoft and 3+ papers + MIHD own data (PCA ARI=0.261 vs scGPT=0.170, 54% gap) Evaluation metric distortion often not single bug but cascading chain of multiple bugs: BC-RNN’s demo_len=0, trigger_step=0, transport always False, successful episode RP not 1.0 four bugs jointly caused RP to drop from expected 0.5 to 0.003. Need end-to-end validation not isolated unit tests macOS window layer controls not only visual Z-order but event routing permissions: .desktopWindow layer (below desktop icons) fundamentally doesn’t receive user input events, must use .normal or higher layer to implement truly interactive ‘browse mode’; this is system-level constraint not API configuration issue In GPU cluster environment, prioritize searching existing model copies and performing format conversion (like HF format→original LLaVA format) rather than downloading through proxy, can save weeks; LLaVA base model conversion from HF format to original format (including vocab truncation fix 32064→32000) successful case proves this When designing automated paper management tool, should mimic human researcher’s actual cognitive flow: fast screening (relevance+novelty+source authority)→deep analysis (core insight+problem definition+experimental design+limitations), not one-time full evaluation h5py file handles cannot be shared across fork child processes (known PyTorch DataLoader limitation), only safe solution is main process preload to numpy then fork. NFS random reads far slower than sequential reads, one-time loading 10× faster than on-demand, can use uint8 to save 4× memory Must verify actual dataset size, per-step time and total steps before launching large-scale training to avoid resource waste; Phoenix MPM training case where dataset scale estimation error (160K→1M) caused 13.5-day training duration severe mistake, proving importance of upfront validation Context-aware intent classification needs to combine session state (session_id, last_activity_at) and conversation history, pure keyword matching causes ambiguity (like ‘几点了’ could be query time or ask scheduled time); CalendarPro resolved this through context_dependent keyword classification and confirmation fast path research_scout.py architecture: 1871-line single file organized into 0-10 numbered sections; two-stage LLM pipeline (screening → deep analysis); three backends (claude_cli, anthropic, openai); parameter resolution chain CLI > project.json > config.json > default; cache validation by keywords hash Command-line tool parameter configuration should use layered parsing strategy: CLI flag > project-specific config > user-level global config > hardcoded default, providing flexibility while maintaining reasonable defaults Batch document generation tool design needs two-stage architecture: export stage locally parses raw data on each device, merge stage aggregates cross-device; when single-day session count especially large, also need ‘intra-segment merge’ step to deduplicate and consolidate multiple outputs from same day Multi-user shared conda environment import priority conflict minimal invasive solution: set PYTHONPATH=/path/to/user/src:$PYTHONPATH at script execution, prioritize loading user’s own source copy, doesn’t affect other users and doesn’t require creating separate env Language configuration for LLM pipelines requires injection at prompt level, not post-processing; different parts of output need different rules (e.g. JSON keys stay English, content in target language) Reverse-engineering structured data from documentation works well with LLM; OVERVIEW_EXTRACT_PROMPT can reliably parse overview.md to extract title/keywords/categories/questions Pipeline regex patterns must match exactly; ‘## 3. 当前技术路线’ won’t match ‘##\s*当前方法与进展’; template-driven architectures require strict format adherence arXiv search returns results in reverse chronological order; consecutive-known-paper counter is effective heuristic for early-stop since it indicates reaching already-processed timeframe Practical Learnings Cross-platform script porting key adaptations: Windows use Get-Item .LastWriteTime instead of find -newer, Start-Job instead of & parallel, Push-Location/Pop-Location instead of cd, [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText ensure BOM-free UTF-8, -replace instead of sed/perl. PowerShell and bash differ significantly in flow control and error handling mechanisms Conversation Summaries MIHD ✅ Update CLAUDE.md and create overview.md research positioning document + scGPT performance systematic investigation 20:29:42.417 | claude_code Through parallel agents exploring 13+ key files in codebase, precisely updated CLAUDE.md (added pipeline configuration structure, cache directory details, code statistics updates), then created 676-line overview.md covering one-sentence positioning, 4 core questions, technical roadmap, 10 fusion strategy rankings, 8 open questions. Launched 4 parallel agents to retrieve scGPT-spatial paper, zero-shot limitation paper, BioLLM framework paper, MIHD internal results, aggregated to demonstrate zero-shot scGPT fundamentally underperforms PCA (PCA ARI=0.261 vs scGPT=0.170, 54% gap). Established patch query clinical vision-driven zero-shot vs STAIG differentiated narrative.
ErrorRecoveryBenchmark 🔄 BC-RNN evaluation pipeline 4 bug fixes and 191 scenario generation + LLaVA training launch 04:22:21.377 | claude_code Located and fixed 4 evaluation metric bugs causing BC-RNN RP abnormally lower than Random: demo_len field name error, trigger_step reading source error, transport stage always False (added _get_target_pos() runtime dynamic target position retrieval), successful episode RP not 1.0. Tuned noise parameters for three error classifiers. Completed all 127 unit tests passing, GPU validation confirmed demo_len=605, trigger_step=55 non-zero and RP highest 0.800. Completed 6 tasks×(injection+natural_capture) total 191 error scenarios. Launched LLaVA MPM/MCM parallel training (ZeRO-3→ZeRO-2, offline format conversion for base model), compressed from estimated 13.5 days to ~2 days, but discovered dataset scale estimation error (160K→1M) halted by user.
🔄 FLARE D0×4 training launch and Phoenix data loading optimization 03:35:40.921 | claude_code Calculated 3 missing norm stats for FLARE, after launching 9 MimicGen task Pi0.5 LoRA training reduced to 4 D0 tasks per user request (coffee/stack/stack_three/threading). Fixed openpi config loading conflict via PYTHONPATH override, 4 D0 tasks running stably on an53 GPU 1,2,4,7 at 6.3s/step (~63h/task). Simultaneously implemented preload memory optimization for Phoenix HDF5 I/O bottleneck (one-time load all data to numpy, changed DataLoader to num_workers=8).
DesktopVideo 🔄 WKWebView webpage wallpaper complete implementation and bug fixes 07:14:20.499 | claude_code Created WebNavigationHandler.swift, extended ContentType enum with .web, implemented showWeb/cleanupWebView/saveWebBookmark, updated AppDelegate playback state management and SingleScreenView UI. Fixed sandbox network permissions (added com.apple.security.network.client), browse mode interaction (window.level=.normal), scrollbar transparency (removed drawsBackground), volume sync (removed isWebContent guard), low UA prompt (set customUserAgent to modern Chrome). Achieved complete solution for playing arbitrary webpage videos as desktop wallpaper, but browse mode window layer issue revealed .desktopWindow cannot receive events, needs .normal layer instead.
gadget (summarize) ✅ summarize tool Windows cross-platform deployment support 07:14:20.499 | claude_code Created website/update.ps1 (complete PowerShell equivalent of update.sh). Added run_hugo_update() cross-platform function in daily_summary.py (Windows prioritizes update.ps1 with PowerShell, fallback to Git Bash; UNIX uses bash), replaced 3 hardcoded bash calls in daily_summary.py and 1 in monthly_summary.py. Also updated outdated line counts and import list in CLAUDE.md.
✅ Batch generation of February historical AI conversation reports (20+ days) + Windows environment configuration 11:35:59.703 | claude_code On Windows (TzJsDesktop) repeatedly ran gadget/summarize/daily_summary.py tool, analyzing ~20+ dates from 2026-02-01 to 2026-03-06 historical session records (from DCC/MacBook/tianhe three devices, covering MIHD multimodal fusion, Error Recovery Benchmark scenario generation and VLA integration, QueST project documentation, CalendarPro upgrade, Desktop Video development, report tool’s own initial development, etc.), generated structured JSON reports and completed multi-day multi-segment summary merges. Encountered Hugo command not found error in merge step, resolved by installing via winget install Hugo.Hugo.Extended 0.157.0 then restarting shell. Established complete research archive from February to early March.
CalendarPro ✅ Context-aware intent classification and session-aware conversation history management 02:02:53.360 | claude_code Implemented context-aware intent classification system (split general_keywords into always_general and context_dependent to avoid ambiguous queries like ‘几点了’ triggering wrong classification) and session-aware conversation history management (auto-compression, session reset, token budget control), completed A/B/C/D four-stage plan (Token tools, Session-aware history refactor, Intent classification refactor, Token budget context building), added 41 specialized tests, all 360 tests passed. Fixed multiple pytest parameter errors, compression logic assertion errors, Settings patch scope errors and other test issues. Thoroughly resolved intent classification inaccuracy caused by keyword pre-check interception.
gadget (research scout) ✅ research scout paper management system implementation from scratch to production enhancement 20:26:51.124 | claude_code Analyzed gadget codebase through 3 Explore subagents, designed two-layer system: project overview documentation (overview.md + project.json) + automated search tool (research_scout.py). Implemented ~750-line core script including project CRUD, arXiv search, LLM three-backend support, paper evaluation scoring, direction suggestion generation, weekly report output, Hugo deployment, caching mechanism. Designed two-stage paper evaluation pipeline: Stage 1 fast screening of all papers (title+abstract first 1000 chars, judge high/low relevance) → Stage 2 deep analysis of high-relevance papers (≤20 papers, 3 highlights: insight/motivation/experiments/limitations). Completed requirements.txt, CLAUDE.md, TUTORIAL.md Chinese tutorial. Testing passed: successfully created test-project, searched 50 cs.RO+cs.LG papers (30-day lookback), cache correctly saved. Subsequently enhanced with: generated comprehensive CLAUDE.md for research_scout.py, fixed 3 critical bugs in error-recovery-benchmark project configuration (ID mismatch, header format, missing marker), implemented full language support (_DEFAULT_LANGUAGE, _language_instruction(), config resolution chain), added LLM-driven reverse initialization (init –from-overview with OVERVIEW_EXTRACT_PROMPT), renamed ‘周报’ to ‘日报’ throughout, implemented arxiv_id deduplication with early-stop (consecutive-known counter).
Robot Manipulation Research ✅ 3D-MVP paper deep evaluation 23:23:34.598 | claude_code Deep methodological analysis of arXiv 2406.18158v2 (3D-MVP: 3D Multiview Pretraining for Robotic Manipulation), extracted three key highlights and design motivations: 3D-aware pretraining solves 2D pretraining ignoring task geometry essence problem, modular decoupling design supports standard self-supervised pretraining, multi-view fusion improves spatial reasoning robustness through implicit triangulation. Output includes relevance=4/novelty=4/inspiration=4 scores and provides benchmark design suggestions using 3D-MVP as baseline and multi-view robustness testing directions.
Token Usage Total: $90.64 — 109.1M tokens
summary: Across four devices in parallel: completed MIHD documentation system and scGPT research, launched FLARE/Phoenix training and fixed 4 BC-RNN evaluation bugs, implemented Desktop Video webpage wallpaper with WKWebView, batch-generated 20+ days of February historical reports, completed CalendarPro context-aware intent classification, and built research scout paper management system from scratch to full-featured tool with language configuration, reverse initialization, and deduplication
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-07

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-06 Daily Overview What was done: Worked across six major projects covering spatial transcriptomics analysis, life management chatbot debugging and documentation, robotics manipulation benchmark codebase restructuring, training pipeline design, and containerized simulation rendering How it was done: Combined parallel multi-agent exploration for rapid codebase understanding, systematic code tracing for data pipelines and context management, symlink-based project restructuring, driver-library version matching for Docker rendering, and comprehensive training infrastructure design Impact: Resolved critical data format questions affecting research, diagnosed and designed fix for user-facing context loss bug, created 1674-line complete tutorial, restructured research projects with 99% disk savings (78GB to 37MB), unblocked Docker GPU rendering for robot simulation evaluation, optimized 67 lines of documentation, and built end-to-end training pipeline covering 9 tasks with resource optimization strategy DCC What was done: Analyzed normalization and preprocessing pipelines in ContraVAE and STHD spatial transcriptomics projects, clarifying data formats and processing steps How it was done: Inspected h5ad files, traced preprocessing scripts, verified data characteristics to track the full pipeline from raw counts to normalized expression matrices Impact: Clarified user confusion about data formats, confirmed the meaning and processing of X matrix, layers[‘counts’], HVG selection, and spatial coordinates across two different preprocessing approaches TzJsDesktop What was done: Fixed CalendarPro Discord bot context loss issue and wrote comprehensive 1674-line Chinese tutorial covering both end-users and developers How it was done: Used parallel agents to explore conversation history management, intent classification, and AI provider call chains, designed context-aware fix; systematically extracted all configs, commands, and architecture to generate structured tutorial Impact: Identified three-layer root cause of bot amnesia (keyword pre-check, timing, missing history parameter) with actionable fix plan; created complete documentation resource covering user guide (60%) and developer guide (40%) tianhe What was done: Optimized error_recovery_benchmark CLAUDE.md documentation, restructured Motion-based Self-Reflection Framework into separate Phoenix/FLARE projects with shared dependencies, designed complete training pipeline for 9 MimicGen tasks, created CALVIN documentation, resolved SAPIEN rendering errors in Docker, and documented RoboTwin simulation architecture How it was done: Streamlined documentation redundancy, executed directory separation with symlink management for 99% disk reduction, explored training framework architectures and created 7 scripts (data generation, conversion, training, evaluation, orchestration), performed driver-library version matching and environment configuration for Vulkan/OpenGL stack, traced multi-layer simulation code Impact: Reduced documentation from 225 to 167 lines with improved readability, established maintainable project structure (Phoenix 37MB, FLARE 368KB, shared_deps 9.3GB), built end-to-end training pipeline including missing config completion and checkpoint reuse strategy, resolved critical Docker rendering blocker preventing model evaluation, and documented complex codebases for onboarding Cross-project work spanning spatial transcriptomics data analysis, CalendarPro bug fix and comprehensive documentation, robot learning codebase restructuring, Docker rendering stack resolution, and Phoenix/FLARE training pipeline design
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Analyze spatial transcriptomics data preprocessing pipeline — Traced normalization steps for ContraVAE and STHD projects, clarified X matrix vs layers[‘counts’], HVG selection, and spatial coordinate systems ✅ Diagnose CalendarPro context loss bug — Identified three-layer issue causing bot to lose conversation context on follow-up messages, designed fix plan ✅ Design Phoenix/FLARE 9-task training plan — Designed complete training infrastructure for Phoenix diffusion policy and FLARE retry framework across all MimicGen tasks ✅ Resolve SAPIEN rendering error in Docker container — Diagnosed and fixed ‘Render Error’ blocking robot simulation evaluation. Root cause was missing NVIDIA Vulkan/OpenGL libraries in Docker. Downloaded matching driver (535.104.12), extracted libraries, configured VK_ICD_FILENAMES and LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variables. ✅ Write CalendarPro comprehensive Chinese tutorial — Created 1674-line docs/TUTORIAL.md covering installation, features, CLI/Discord commands, architecture, and developer guide ✅ Separate Phoenix and FLARE projects with shared dependency management — Restructured Motion-based Self-Reflection Framework into two independent projects (Phoenix for self-reflection, FLARE for failure-led reset) with shared 9-package dependency directory using symlinks. Reduced disk usage from 78GB to 37MB per project by symlinking large data/checkpoint directories back to archive. Implementation & Fixes ✅ Create Phoenix training scripts — Built SLURM job script for Phoenix multi-task diffusion policy training ✅ Create FLARE data generation and training scripts — Built warmup data generation, LeRobot conversion, training, and evaluation scripts for FLARE pipeline ✅ Document RoboTwin data collection and simulation architecture — Provided detailed explanation of two-phase data collection pipeline (seed finding + replay with deterministic seeds) and traced complete simulation execution flow using place_dual_shoes task as example. ✅ Optimize error_recovery_benchmark CLAUDE.md — Reduced documentation from 225 to 167 lines by trimming Commands section, adding PYTHONPATH details, and streamlining references ✅ Complete OpenPI training configs — Added missing finetune and inference configs for coffee_task_D1, stack_three_task_D1, and three_piece_assembly_task_D1 ✅ Fix frozen dataclass mutation errors in policy loading — Resolved FrozenInstanceError when loading pi05 policy. Fixed by replacing direct attribute assignment with dataclasses.replace(). Also fixed duplicated path bug in norm_stats loading. ✅ Create CLAUDE.md documentation for CALVIN project — Explored CALVIN codebase (Composing Actions from Language and Vision benchmark) and wrote comprehensive project documentation covering architecture, build/test commands, Hydra config system, and MCIL model pipeline. Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. CalendarPro bot fails to understand follow-up messages like ‘你安排在几点了呀’ (What time did you schedule?) Solution: Identified three-layer issue: (1) general_keywords pre-check executes before AI sees conversation history, (2) pre-check uses overly broad keywords like ‘几点了’, (3) AI provider calls don’t receive history parameter. Designed fix to remove pre-check bypass and pass full conversation history to AI
Key Insight: Context loss is typically a multi-layer problem: data not collected → data not passed → data not used. Must trace the complete call chain from storage through provider interface to prompt construction
2. SAPIEN ‘Render Error’ in headless Docker container - RuntimeError: failed to find a rendering device Solution: Discovered missing NVIDIA Vulkan/OpenGL rendering libraries (libGLX_nvidia.so, etc). Container had CUDA compute libraries but not rendering stack. Downloaded matching NVIDIA driver version 535.104.12 to match kernel driver, extracted libraries to user directory, configured VK_ICD_FILENAMES and LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variables in eval.sh.
Key Insight: Docker containers with NVIDIA GPUs need both CUDA runtime AND Vulkan/OpenGL libraries for graphics-based simulation. Driver version must exactly match between kernel module and user-space libraries or Vulkan ICD fails to load.
3. Phoenix training checkpoints exist in zhaoganlong’s directory, unclear whether to reuse or retrain Solution: User pointed out should check existing checkpoints and symlink when available, only train missing ones on an53 free GPUs
Key Insight: Before designing training plan, inventory existing resources (checkpoints, datasets, configs) to avoid wasting compute. Establish checkpoint reuse strategy first
4. User assumed normalized data came from STHD pipeline, but data characteristics didn’t match pipeline code Solution: Traced code to discover STHD doesn’t perform normalization - it uses raw counts directly. The normalized data user saw was from external scanpy preprocessing (normalize_total + log1p)
Key Insight: Need to distinguish between pipeline-internal processing and external preprocessing. Code inspection is more reliable than assumptions based on data appearance
General Issues 5. OpenPI config missing warmup training configurations for 3 out of 9 MimicGen tasks Solution: Added finetune and inference configs for coffee_task_D1, stack_three_task_D1, and three_piece_assembly_task_D1 using add_finetune_config and add_inference_config helper functions
Key Insight: Use config generation functions to maintain consistency across task configurations rather than manual duplication
6. FrozenInstanceError: cannot assign to field ‘asset_id’ in policy_config.py Solution: DataConfig was defined with @dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True), making it immutable. Replaced direct assignment (data_config.asset_id = value) with dataclasses.replace(data_config, asset_id=value) which creates a new instance.
Key Insight: Frozen dataclasses enforce immutability at the language level - modifications require explicit use of dataclasses.replace() to create new instances rather than mutating in-place.
7. FileNotFoundError with duplicated path: assets/norm_stats.json/norm_stats.json Solution: When robotwin_repo_id is provided, asset_id was incorrectly set to ’norm_stats.json’ (filename instead of directory). Modified code to load directly from assets/ directory using normalize.load() without appending asset_id subdirectory.
Key Insight: Path construction bugs often arise from mixing directory identifiers with filenames. Explicit handling of special cases (local files vs remote repositories) prevents path duplication.
8. MulticoreTSNE build failure due to CMake version incompatibility (system 3.26.4 vs required <=3.18) Solution: Identified that MulticoreTSNE is only used for optional TSNE visualization, not core training/eval. Suggested commenting out dependency in requirements.txt to proceed with installation (user rejected this approach).
Key Insight: Distinguish between critical dependencies and optional visualization tools. In research code, it’s often acceptable to skip non-essential dependencies that cause build issues.
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Detecting bot context loss from user experience Role Approach Human User noticed from end-user perspective that bot failed on obvious follow-up question ‘你安排在几点了呀’ AI AI diagnosed root cause by tracing code through three layers: keyword pre-check mechanism, history passing in provider calls, and dual-verify intent flow Difference Analysis: Human detects symptoms from usage experience; AI diagnoses root cause through systematic code analysis. However, AI didn’t proactively monitor system to prevent such issues - relied on user bug report
Checkpoint reuse before training Role Approach Human User pointed out should reuse existing checkpoints via symlinks and only train what’s missing, considering resource efficiency AI AI designed complete training-from-scratch plan without checking for existing checkpoints first Difference Analysis: User has cost awareness and practical constraints; AI tends toward clean-slate implementations. Should always inventory existing resources before planning new work
Implementation Level Installing NVIDIA libraries in restricted Docker environment Role Approach Human User immediately knew that apt-get must be accessed via custom path /app/ubuntu/bin/apt-get due to container security setup AI AI initially tried standard apt-get command, which would have failed or required sudo Difference Analysis: Human had domain knowledge about the specific container’s package manager configuration that wasn’t discoverable from the codebase. AI learned this through user correction.
Tutorial target audience decision Role Approach Human User clearly knew tutorial should serve both end-users and developers (‘两者兼顾’) AI AI initially asked user/developer/both without inferring from existing docs (README is user-facing, CLAUDE.md is developer-facing) that a comprehensive tutorial bridging both was the gap Difference Analysis: User has clearer understanding of project needs; AI should infer missing documentation gaps from existing distribution rather than asking obvious questions
Handling MulticoreTSNE dependency conflict Role Approach Human User rejected the file edit to comment out MulticoreTSNE, possibly preferring to handle installation differently or accepting the limitation AI AI immediately suggested editing requirements.txt to remove the problematic dependency as a quick workaround Difference Analysis: User may have wanted to preserve the original configuration or had alternative solution in mind. AI focused on unblocking progress but didn’t confirm approach preference first.
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Designed training plan without checking for existing checkpoints - needed user reminder to consider reuse strategy. Should always inventory resources before implementation Initially attempted to install NVIDIA graphics libraries (libnvidia-gl-535) without first checking kernel driver version compatibility, leading to version mismatch (535.288.01 vs 535.104.12). Required multiple iterations to discover correct approach of downloading matching driver and extracting libraries. General Limitations While conversation history exploration was comprehensive, took 3 parallel agents to identify the core issue of LLM calls not receiving history parameter - could have pinpointed faster with more targeted initial exploration Created 1674-line tutorial file directly without previewing structure for user confirmation first - should validate approach on large artifacts Modified test_render.py to print full traceback by replacing bare ’except:’ with ’except Exception as e:’, but this pattern (swallowing exceptions without logging) was in the original codebase, not AI-introduced. AI should have proactively identified this anti-pattern earlier. Multiple /init command runs performed similar exploration and analysis without remembering previous evaluation results - lacks session-to-session memory Spent time on systematic exploration (find commands, vulkaninfo checks) that could have been shortened if AI had more direct knowledge of common Docker+NVIDIA Vulkan setup patterns. Eventually succeeded but with more steps than an expert would need. Learnings Key Learnings Context loss requires tracing the complete call chain: data collection (conversation_history.json storage) → data passing (provider call signatures) → data usage (prompt construction).
