title: Bug Journal 2026-03-05

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-04 Daily Overview What was done: Cross-device multi-project work spanning spatial transcriptomics literature research, documentation improvements, large-scale VLA training infrastructure setup, and comprehensive error recovery benchmark documentation synchronization How it was done: DCC: web-based arxiv paper analysis for evaluation methodology extraction; MacBook: codebase analysis via /init for documentation audit; tianhe: parallel agent exploration for codebase-to-documentation alignment, checkpoint inventory via filesystem traversal, and multi-GPU training orchestration planning Impact: Gained complete understanding of cross-sample spatial niche evaluation framework; improved Desktop Video onboarding documentation; established foundation for multi-week 6-stage VLA training campaign; synchronized error_recovery_benchmark project overview with reality (6,474 episodes, 53 framework files, 22 scripts, 122 tests) DCC What was done: QueST paper evaluation methodology research for spatial transcriptomics How it was done: Fetched and analyzed arxiv HTML version (v1) and abstract page (v3) for evaluation framework details Impact: Clarified evaluation framework using WWL Graph Kernel + Wasserstein distance as ground truth for niche similarity, with Best Match Accuracy and Overall Similarity Accuracy metrics MacBook What was done: Desktop Video CLAUDE.md documentation audit and improvements via /init command How it was done: Analyzed codebase structure (Swift/SwiftUI/AVFoundation), identified gaps in existing documentation, made three targeted edits: fixed AGENTS.md case sensitivity, added localization reminder for Localizable.xcstrings, enhanced ChangeLog format inline documentation Impact: Enhanced documentation clarity and completeness, providing future Claude Code instances with accurate file references and bilingual development workflow guidance tianhe What was done: Full-day session on zhaoganlong framework training setup + error_recovery_benchmark comprehensive documentation update + BC-RNN performance investigation How it was done: Created 7-task implementation plan for 6-stage training pipeline across 9 MimicGen tasks; deployed 3 parallel exploration agents for codebase analysis; performed checkpoint inventory via filesystem traversal; systematic document editing based on actual implementation; started BC-RNN training-vs-evaluation comparison Impact: Established foundation for multi-week 8× A800 GPU training campaign with checkpoint locations verified; documentation now matches reality with accurate statistics, detailed API descriptions, and three-source pipeline architecture; identified critical BC-RNN 0% success rate anomaly requiring debugging Multi-project research and documentation day: investigated QueST paper’s cross-sample query evaluation methodology for spatial transcriptomics, improved Desktop Video CLAUDE.md, created comprehensive zhaoganlong Self-Reflection Framework training guide with checkpoint inventory, and synchronized error_recovery_benchmark documentation to match actual codebase implementation including pipeline architecture redesign.
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ zhaoganlong Self-Reflection Framework training setup and documentation — Created comprehensive 7-task implementation plan for training all 6 stages (Stage 0-5: data prep, Diffusion Policy, LLaVA MPM, MCM feedback collection, LLaVA MCM, Lifelong Learning) across 9 MimicGen tasks on an49 8× A800 GPUs. Documented complete training methodology with checkpoint inventory: Pi0.5 Phoenix (180GB, 21 steps, last modified 2026-02-24), base OpenPI models (~12GB each), diffusion policy snapshots (4 @ 253MB). Began modifying 4 data preparation scripts to enable all tasks. Created docs/zhaoganlong_training_guide.md with inference pipeline modes and complete file path reference. ✅ Update error_recovery_benchmark 项目全景总结.md with actual implementation — Aligned project overview document with codebase reality by deploying parallel agents: added detailed implementation sections for 6 detectors, 4 injectors, 4 validators, 20 error classifiers with API descriptions; updated code statistics (scripts 18→22, tests 109→122, eval runs 7→11); corrected M14 evaluation data (726 ep → 6,474 ep, ~83% complete with per-policy performance); fixed VLA model types (Pi0-FAST → Phoenix/Flare); documented plugin architecture with registry pattern ✅ Redesign error_recovery_benchmark core pipeline architecture — Updated Section 3.1 pipeline to show three parallel error scene sources: (1) Demo Dataset replay + injection, (2) VLA/Policy rollout + injection, (3) Natural failure capture - all feeding into ErrorSceneDatabase. Addressed user’s concern that original diagram only showed demo-based path. Added explanations of each path’s purpose and complementary relationship. ✅ QueST cross-sample query evaluation methodology research — Fetched QueST paper (arxiv 2410.10652) and extracted complete evaluation framework: uses Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman Graph Kernel to construct ground truth from cell type labels, evaluates with Best Niche Match Accuracy (top-1 correctness) and Overall Similarity Accuracy (Pearson correlation with WWL ground truth). Synthetic query experiment design uses 9 queries varying cell type count (1-3) and size (50-200 cells). User corrected to use v3 instead of v1; confirmed v3 exists but no HTML version available. 🔄 Investigate BC-RNN 0% success rate anomaly — User reported BC-RNN shows 0% SR on all normal tasks despite expecting ~60-70% from training rollouts. Started exploring training vs evaluation code differences (observation keys, environment setup, checkpoint loading) but was interrupted. Need to compare training script’s rollout vs benchmark evaluation setup. User mentioned earlier fix for ‘object’ observation key issue - may be related. Implementation & Fixes ❌ Review ErrorSceneDatabase design — User questioned current database design. Explored actual implementation (meta.json index, scene JSON+NPZ pairs, dedup, auto-classification, 4 split strategies). User felt something was ‘strange’ but conversation was abandoned before identifying specific issues ✅ Desktop Video CLAUDE.md improvements — Analyzed Desktop Video codebase via /init command, identified three improvement areas in existing CLAUDE.md, and implemented fixes: corrected agents.md → AGENTS.md case sensitivity, added localization workflow reminder (Localizable.xcstrings), enhanced ChangeLog format documentation with bilingual entry structure and date format notes Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Documentation-code mismatch in error_recovery_benchmark: 项目全景总结.md described outdated pipeline, missing implementation details, and incorrect statistics (726 episodes vs actual 6,474) Solution: Deployed parallel exploration agents to thoroughly read error_framework/, scripts/, configs/, outputs/ directories, then systematically updated document with verified data: detector/injector/validator/classifier implementations, actual M14 evaluation numbers, real code statistics
Key Insight: Documentation drift is inevitable in active projects - need systematic code-to-doc alignment process. Parallel agent exploration pattern effective for large codebases: one agent per major directory, then consolidate findings
2. BC-RNN evaluation shows 0% success rate on all normal tasks (coffee, stack, threading, etc.) despite training rollouts suggesting ~60-70% performance Solution: Started exploration but didn’t complete: need to compare (1) observation dictionary keys between training and eval, (2) environment initialization differences, (3) checkpoint loading correctness, (4) action space consistency. User noted earlier fix for ‘object’ observation key issue - may be related
Key Insight: Training-vs-evaluation mismatch is a common failure mode. The fact that BC-RNN gets 0.28% SR on error recovery (5/1800 successes) but 0% on normal tasks (0/250) suggests environment/observation mismatch rather than policy failure. Need systematic comparison checklist: obs keys, env config, action bounds, camera setup, normalization
3. zhaoganlong data preparation scripts only had coffee_d1 task enabled (8 other tasks commented out), blocking multi-task training pipeline Solution: Systematically uncomment all 9 tasks in TASK_MAPPING dictionaries across 4 scripts, remove pdb.set_trace() debug breakpoints that would halt batch execution
Key Insight: Existing codebase was configured for single-task prototyping; scaling to multi-task production requires batch enabling of task lists across data pipeline, checkpoint paths, and orchestration scripts
4. Pipeline diagram didn’t reflect user’s multi-source vision: only showed demo-based error injection, missing VLA rollout injection and natural failure capture paths Solution: Rewrote Section 3.1 pipeline as three parallel paths (Demo注入, Policy Rollout注入, 自然失败捕获) all converging to ErrorSceneDatabase. Added explanations of each path’s purpose and how they complement each other
Key Insight: Visual diagrams must match user’s mental model, not just code structure. User’s concern about ‘VLA rollout injection during execution’ revealed that code supported feature but documentation omitted it - missing documentation is as bad as wrong documentation
5. PDF version of arxiv paper (v3) returned binary-encoded content, impossible to extract methodology details directly Solution: Switched to HTML version (v1) available at arxiv.org/html/2410.10652v1, which provided full text with all technical details
Key Insight: For arxiv papers, HTML versions are far more machine-readable than PDF versions; always check for HTML availability first
6. zhaoganlong framework checkpoints scattered across multiple locations (~180GB for Pi0.5 Phoenix alone), unclear which are usable Solution: Performed comprehensive filesystem search: found Pi0.5 Phoenix checkpoints (21 steps, 180GB total, last modified 2026-02-24), base OpenPI checkpoints (pi0_base, pi05_base, pi0_fast_base, pi0_libero, each ~12GB), diffusion policy checkpoints (4 snapshots @ 253MB each, dated 2025-08-22). Verified all paths and sizes, marked usability status
Key Insight: Checkpoint inventory is essential for reproducibility. Large checkpoint directories (180GB) indicate many training steps saved - good for analysis but need clear documentation of which step to use for evaluation. Date-based directory structure (2025-08-22, 2026-02-24) helps track training timeline
General Issues 7. User initially asked ‘how this paper do cross sample query’ which was ambiguous - could mean methodology or evaluation Solution: User clarified they meant evaluation methodology, not implementation. Re-fetched paper focusing on evaluation metrics, ground truth construction, and experimental design
Key Insight: ‘How’ questions about papers can mean either (1) technical implementation or (2) validation/evaluation approach - always clarify which aspect the user needs
8. QueST v3 HTML version returned 404 error when accessed directly Solution: V3 only available as PDF (no HTML rendering). Used v1 HTML for detailed content extraction, then checked v3 abstract page for version differences (none mentioned substantively)
Key Insight: Not all arxiv paper versions have HTML rendering; v1 HTML may be sufficient if later versions only contain minor formatting changes
9. Desktop Video CLAUDE.md contained lowercase ‘agents.md’ reference while actual file is ‘AGENTS.md’, causing potential file-not-found issues on case-sensitive filesystems Solution: Fixed reference to use correct uppercase ‘AGENTS.md’ filename
Key Insight: Documentation file references must match exact case to ensure reliability across different filesystem configurations (macOS is case-insensitive by default but can be configured as case-sensitive)
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Pipeline architecture multi-source design Role Approach Human User immediately recognized that the single-source (Demo Dataset) pipeline diagram missed their core vision: wanted VLA rollout injection and natural failure capture shown explicitly as equal error scene sources. They saw the code supported it (1c_generate_from_policy.py --mode injection) but documentation omitted it AI AI initially focused on documenting what was explicitly shown in README/comments, treating demo-based injection as the primary path. Didn’t infer from code capabilities that user wanted multi-source architecture highlighted Difference Analysis: User thinks system-level: ‘what are all the ways errors can be generated?’ AI thinks file-level: ‘what does this script do?’ Human recognized documentation gap from understanding the problem domain (error scene diversity requires multiple sources); AI needed explicit instruction to add parallel paths
BC-RNN 0% success rate interpretation Role Approach Human User immediately flagged this as anomalous: ‘Why is BC-RNN so bad? It’s completely different from training rollouts.’ They knew from domain knowledge that BC-RNN should get ~60-70% on normal PickPlace tasks, so 0% means something is broken, not that the policy is bad AI AI started exploring code structure (training scripts, evaluation scripts, configs) but didn’t immediately prioritize the most likely culprits: observation key mismatch (user mentioned earlier ‘object’ key fix), environment initialization differences, or data normalization issues Difference Analysis: Human used domain knowledge + anomaly detection to infer likely root cause category (environment mismatch). AI’s systematic exploration is thorough but slower - would eventually find issue, but didn’t shortcut to high-probability failure modes first. Shows value of domain expertise in debugging
Distinguishing implementation vs. evaluation when analyzing papers Role Approach Human User immediately clarified ‘No, I mean, how is the evaluation done?’ when AI provided implementation details first AI AI initially interpreted ‘how this paper do cross sample query’ as asking about the technical methodology (graph auto-encoder, contrastive learning, adversarial training) Difference Analysis: Human knew exactly which aspect mattered for their research (evaluation framework), while AI provided comprehensive technical overview by default. Human’s clarification was essential to redirect focus to the relevant evaluation metrics.
Training orchestration granularity Role Approach Human User provided complete implementation plan with 6-stage dependency graph, explicit GPU allocations (Stage 1: GPU 0, Stage 2: GPU 1-4, etc.), parallel execution strategy, and tmux session management requirements AI AI created 7-task TodoList matching plan structure and began systematic file-by-file modifications, starting with reading all files in parallel before making edits Difference Analysis: Human pre-designed full orchestration architecture accounting for hardware constraints (GPU 6-7 already occupied), estimated timelines per stage (Stage 1: 2-4 days, Stage 2: 4-8 hours), and specified failure recovery (tmux sessions persist across disconnects). AI followed instructions but did not proactively suggest monitoring strategies, checkpoint validation, or resource contention handling for the multi-day training campaign.
Documentation-code alignment methodology Role Approach Human User explicitly requested: ‘以文件夹中的内容为准’ (take folder contents as ground truth). They wanted detailed implementation descriptions ’like you just did’ (referring to earlier ErrorSceneDatabase API summary), prioritizing code reality over documentation claims AI AI deployed parallel exploration agents to read all code, then systematically updated documentation. Pattern: explore → verify → document. Used filesystem commands to get actual numbers (wc -l, du -sh, stat), not trusting document claims Difference Analysis: Both agree on ground truth primacy, but human specified output style (detailed API documentation embedded in overview doc). AI would default to shorter updates - human wanted comprehensive treatment to prevent future drift. This shows documentation as ’executable specification’ philosophy
Implementation Level Version specificity for research papers Role Approach Human User explicitly corrected ‘Don’t use v1, there is a v3 version with updates’ showing awareness of paper evolution AI AI defaulted to first available full-text version (v1 HTML) without checking for later versions Difference Analysis: Human tracks version history of papers they’re following; AI tends to use whatever version has best accessibility (HTML over PDF). User’s intervention ensured most current version was consulted.
Documentation improvement scope Role Approach Human User initiated /init command to trigger comprehensive codebase analysis and CLAUDE.md audit, expecting AI to identify gaps and suggest improvements autonomously AI AI analyzed existing CLAUDE.md, found it ‘already excellent and comprehensive’, made three minimal targeted fixes rather than suggesting broader structural improvements Difference Analysis: Human uses /init as a discovery mechanism for potential documentation debt; AI defaulted to conservative ‘it’s already good’ assessment and made only obvious corrections (case sensitivity, missing sections), potentially missing deeper organizational or completeness issues
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Failed to infer multi-source pipeline architecture from code: Even though 1c_generate_from_policy.py clearly supports --mode injection for policy rollout injection and natural capture, AI documented only the demo-based path until user explicitly corrected. Didn’t synthesize system-level architecture from individual script capabilities Agent timeouts on large codebase exploration: Three parallel agents timed out (>1100 seconds each) when exploring zhaoganlong framework, returning 0 tokens and requiring restart. Large directory trees with many files overwhelm agent token budgets or trigger tool call limits Didn’t prioritize high-probability failure modes in BC-RNN debugging: Started broad code exploration instead of immediately checking most likely culprits (observation key mismatch, which user had mentioned fixing earlier). Systematic exploration is thorough but slow - lacks debugging heuristics AI work on tianhe was interrupted mid-Edit operation during create_5hz_dataset_new_motion.py modification, leaving multi-file data pipeline in partially modified state. AI did not proactively checkpoint progress or suggest atomic batch operations (modify all 4 files, then test as a unit) to avoid inconsistent intermediate states Cannot directly parse PDF binary content returned by WebFetch tool. Heavily reliant on HTML versions or pre-processed text for arxiv papers. General Limitations Ambiguous query interpretation: ‘how this paper do X’ could mean either technical implementation or evaluation methodology. Should ask clarifying question when ambiguity exists. Couldn’t identify ErrorSceneDatabase design issues without user specifics: User said design felt ‘strange’ but AI couldn’t infer what specific aspects were problematic (classification logic, source tracking, query API, schema design). Needed explicit problem statement - limited ability to detect design smells from code alone Failed to check for paper version updates before defaulting to v1 HTML. Should always verify latest version when accessing arxiv papers. When conducting /init-triggered CLAUDE.md audit, AI exhibited confirmation bias toward existing documentation quality, concluding ‘already excellent and comprehensive’ after finding well-structured content, rather than probing for latent gaps (e.g., missing troubleshooting sections, unclear dependency version requirements, or incomplete architectural diagrams) Learnings Key Learnings QueST evaluation framework: when no ground truth niche labels exist, construct synthetic ground truth using Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman Graph Kernel over cell type annotations, then validate model’s similarity rankings with Pearson correlation Multi-task ML pipelines built via incremental prototyping (start with 1 task) leave scattered single-task configurations (commented-out task lists, hardcoded paths). Scaling to production requires systematic audit of: data prep scripts, dataset mapping JSONs, checkpoint directories, and evaluation configs. Documentation synchronization pattern: Use parallel exploration agents (one per major directory), verify all numbers from actual files (summary.json, filesystem stats), then systematically update document section by section. Don’t trust document claims - always check code/data ground truth Training-vs-evaluation mismatch debugging checklist: When policy performs well in training but fails in evaluation, systematically check: (1) observation dictionary keys, (2) environment initialization parameters, (3) action space bounds, (4) data normalization, (5) camera rendering setup. BC-RNN 0% SR on normal tasks + 0.28% on error recovery suggests env mismatch, not policy failure Cross-sample niche query evaluation requires: (1) batch-invariant embeddings via adversarial training, (2) ground truth from graph kernels when labels unavailable, (3) dual metrics for top-1 accuracy and overall ranking correlation For spatial transcriptomics, WWL (Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman) kernel can convert discrete cell type labels into continuous niche similarity scores by computing graph-level Wasserstein distance between subgraph representations Large-scale HPC training orchestration requires explicit dependency mapping (Stage 0 → Stage 1+2 parallel → Stage 3 → Stage 4 → Stage 5), per-stage GPU allocation planning, and persistent session management (tmux) to survive multi-day training campaigns and SSH disconnections Multi-source data collection architectures should be explicit in documentation: Error scene generation benefits from diverse sources (demo injection for reproducibility, policy rollout injection for realism, natural failure capture for authenticity). Pipeline diagrams must show all sources as first-class citizens, not buried in script options M14 evaluation results revealed baseline performance: 6,474 episodes across 4 policies on 649 error scenes shows Random (0% SR, 1.9% RP), BC-RNN (0.28% SR, 0.3% RP), Pi0 (0% SR, 0.2% RP), Pi0.5 (0% SR, 0% RP). Near-zero error recovery rates validate benchmark difficulty and establish baseline for future improvement (M15 human recovery data + LoRA finetuning expected to show positive delta) Checkpoint inventory best practices: Record full path, size, last modified date, and usability status for all model checkpoints. Large checkpoint directories (180GB) indicate many saved steps - document which step to use for evaluation. Date-based naming helps track training timeline but needs explicit step-to-date mapping Error framework scale: 53 Python files (~14.7K LOC) with 6 detectors, 4 injectors, 4 validators, 20 error classifiers across 3 families (manipulation, planning, progress). Plugin architecture with DETECTOR_REGISTRY, INJECTOR_REGISTRY, VALIDATOR_REGISTRY, CLASSIFIER_REGISTRY enables extensibility. Per-detector cooldown (20 steps) prevents detection spam Practical Learnings /init command triggers comprehensive codebase analysis + CLAUDE.md generation/improvement workflow, designed for onboarding future Claude Code sessions. Effective for documentation audits even when CLAUDE.md already exists. Conversation Summaries MIHD (Spatial Transcriptomics) ✅ QueST paper cross-sample query evaluation methodology research 22:21:12.826 | claude_code User requested analysis of QueST paper’s evaluation approach (not implementation). Fetched arxiv paper v1 HTML and v3 abstract, extracted complete evaluation framework: WWL Graph Kernel constructs ground truth from cell type labels, evaluated via Best Match Accuracy (top-1) and Overall Similarity Accuracy (Pearson r). Synthetic queries vary cell type count (1-3) and size (50-200). User corrected to use v3, confirmed v3 has no HTML version but substantive content similar.
Desktop Video ✅ CLAUDE.md documentation audit and improvement via /init 20:56:25.688 | claude_code User invoked /init command to trigger codebase analysis and CLAUDE.md generation/improvement. AI analyzed Swift/SwiftUI project structure, read existing CLAUDE.md, determined it was ‘already excellent’, and made three targeted fixes: corrected agents.md → AGENTS.md file reference case sensitivity, added localization reminder to check Localizable.xcstrings when modifying code, and enhanced ChangeLog format documentation. AI confirmed no Cursor/.github copilot rules existed and validated .periphery.yml dead code detection config.
zhaoganlong Motion-based Self-Reflection Framework 🔄 Training infrastructure setup and comprehensive documentation 22:23:48.001 | claude_code User requested implementation of full 6-stage training plan for zhaoganlong Motion-based Self-Reflection Framework across 9 MimicGen tasks on an49 (8× A800). AI created 7-task breakdown, mapped stage dependencies (data prep → Diffusion + LLaVA MPM parallel → MCM feedback → LLaVA MCM → Lifelong Learning), began modifying 4 data preparation scripts to enable all 9 tasks by uncommenting TASK_MAPPING entries and removing pdb breakpoints. Explored framework directory, documented complete training methodology with 6 stages covering HDF5→5Hz→Speed→LLaVA JSON data prep, diffusion policy training with ResNet18+Transformer+UNet, LLaVA MPM/MCM LoRA finetuning, lifelong learning, OpenPI Pi0/Pi0.5 training. Inventoried all checkpoints: Pi0.5 Phoenix (180GB, 21 steps, 2026-02-24), base models (~12GB each), diffusion policy (4 snapshots @ 253MB). Created docs/zhaoganlong_training_guide.md. Workflow interrupted mid-Edit during create_5hz_dataset_new_motion.py modification. Three parallel exploration agents initially timed out (>1100s each) requiring restart.
Error Recovery Benchmark 🔄 Comprehensive documentation synchronization and architecture redesign 03:04:05.254 | claude_code Multi-conversation full-day session on error_recovery_benchmark documentation update. (1) Systematically aligned 项目全景总结.md with actual implementation by deploying parallel agents to explore error_framework/, scripts/, outputs/. Added detailed sections for 6 detectors, 4 injectors, 4 validators, 20 classifiers with API descriptions. Updated code stats (22 scripts, 122 tests, 11 eval runs), M14 evaluation data (6,474 episodes vs documented 726), and database statistics (649 scenes with by_error_type/by_phase/by_severity breakdowns). Fixed VLA model types and version date. (2) User requested pipeline architecture modification to show three-source design: Demo replay + injection, VLA/Policy rollout + injection, Natural failure capture. Updated Section 3.1 pipeline diagram. (3) User questioned ErrorSceneDatabase design, explored actual implementation (meta.json index, scene JSON+NPZ pairs, auto-classification, dedup, 4 split strategies) but conversation abandoned before identifying specific issues. (4) Started BC-RNN 0% success rate investigation - user reported complete failure on all normal tasks despite expecting ~60-70% from training rollouts. Began exploring training vs evaluation code differences but interrupted. Need to check observation key mismatch, environment setup, checkpoint loading. (5) Analyzed Pi0.5 batched eval results: 58.9% overall SR. Compared with BC-RNN baseline (0%) and M14 error recovery results (all near 0%). Found Pi0.5 visualization videos were empty directories - user requested regeneration. (6) Provided scp/sftp/VS Code/WinSCP methods to download document to local machine.
Token Usage Total: $13.83 — 22.3M tokens
summary: Multi-project research and documentation day: investigated QueST paper’s cross-sample query evaluation methodology for spatial transcriptomics, improved Desktop Video CLAUDE.md, created comprehensive zhaoganlong Self-Reflection Framework training guide with checkpoint inventory, and synchronized error_recovery_benchmark documentation to match actual codebase implementation including pipeline architecture redesign.
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-04

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-03 Daily Overview What was done: Four parallel workstreams: DCC implemented complete cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark for MIHD multimodal fusion framework; MacBook batch-processed 13 days of historical conversation logs across 4 devices and 5 projects into structured reports; tianhe built BC-RNN baseline and Pi0.5 LoRA evaluation infrastructure, discovered pi0.5_libero vs pi0.5_base misconfiguration; MacBook also polished screensaver clock visual effects and created Hugo website documentation. How it was done: DCC: explored codebase with 3 Explore Agents, designed independent script loading STAIG fusion embeddings from Phase 2 outputs, computed topology scores with WWL graph kernels vs embedding cosine similarity, generated Spearman/Precision@K metrics and spatial heatmaps. MacBook: used gadget/summarize tool’s two-phase pipeline (export → merge) with Claude Sonnet to extract structured information from JSONL conversation files, manually fixed JSON truncation issues. Tianhe: refactored evaluation scripts for multi-task support, debugged numpy dtype filtering for robomimic compatibility, launched batched GPU evaluation via Slurm, investigated an53 node crash via sacct logs. Screensaver: unified font weights, disabled CALayer implicit animations, replaced SwiftUI material with NSVisualEffectView for .withinWindow blending. Impact: Established reusable cross-sample evaluation toolchain showing fusion embeddings significantly correlate with topological structure (Layer_3 r≈0.44); built structured knowledge base covering robot simulation, spatial transcriptomics, AI assistant projects for retrospective analysis; discovered fundamental training configuration error requiring retraining (LoRA used pi0.5_libero instead of pi0.5_base); delivered production-ready screensaver features eliminating visual glitches; established comprehensive documentation for website maintenance. DCC What was done: Completed RM-IDEAL benchmark from design to implementation to verification to documentation for MIHD project How it was done: Used 3 Agents to explore codebase and design approach, created scripts/benchmark_rm_ideal.py (781 lines), fixed zero-embedding detection and CUDA compatibility, ran 151673↔151676 test on RTX 2080 Ti, generated 6-panel spatial heatmaps with scanpy, wrote docs/CROSS_SAMPLE_QUERY.md (459 lines) Impact: Built reusable cross-sample evaluation tool covering 10 stages from spatial graph construction to evaluation metrics; experimental results show fusion embeddings significantly correlate with topological structure MacBook What was done: Batch-processed 13 days of historical AI conversation records (Feb 7-19) across 4 devices and 5 projects into structured daily reports; polished screensaver clock visual effects (ghost artifact fix, animation smoothing, no-video mode, glass transparency); created Hugo website CLAUDE.md documentation How it was done: Ran daily_summary.py for 4.5 hours continuously with ~20 export operations and 10+ merge operations; used parse_claude_code() to read JSONL session files from ~/.claude/projects/, called AI to extract structured information (tasks/problems/human-AI differences/technical insights), generated Markdown+JSON dual-format reports; manually fixed JSON truncation via repair prompt. Screensaver: unified font weights between highlight and mask layers, wrapped mask updates in CATransaction.setDisableActions(true), replaced SwiftUI .ultraThinMaterial with NSViewRepresentable wrapping NSVisualEffectView with .withinWindow blend mode. Website: analyzed Hugo blog structure and build pipeline (update.sh incremental compression). Impact: Generated 13 complete historical technical logs systematically organizing two weeks of progress across robot simulation, spatial transcriptomics, AI assistant projects; established structured knowledge base for bug retrospection, architecture review, and knowledge transfer. Eliminated screensaver visual glitches in production app; established maintainable documentation standard for future contributors. tianhe What was done: Built BC-RNN baseline and Pi0.5 LoRA evaluation pipeline with multi-task support; debugged training checkpoint formats; initiated 5-task evaluation batch; monitored 8 parallel Pi0.5 training jobs (17k-26k/36k steps); launched 2-GPU evaluation on backup node after an53 crash; discovered critical base model misconfiguration (pi0.5_libero instead of pi0.5_base) How it was done: Refactored evaluation scripts to add –task parameter and per-task checkpoint resolution; created BC-RNN checkpoint symlinks; modified policy_adapter._to_robosuite_obs to filter numpy scalar types (float64) that robomimic TensorUtils cannot handle; created eval_pi05_lora_2gpu.sh for split GPU architecture (GPU 0: VLA server, GPU 1: MuJoCo rollouts); analyzed Slurm logs (sacct) to investigate an53 NODE_FAIL at 08:30; parsed JSONL evaluation results Impact: Discovered coffee task BC-RNN training failure (0% SR); automated evaluation infrastructure for 250 rollouts across 5 tasks; identified critical base model misconfiguration requiring retraining on all tasks; ruled out AI operations as cause of node failure (resource overload 3h after AI activity); successfully deployed evaluation on backup node with estimated 60-70min completion Completed MIHD cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark implementation (design, 781-line script, bug fixes, visualization, 459-line documentation) on DCC; batch-processed 13 days of historical AI conversation logs (Feb 7-19) into structured daily reports on MacBook; built BC-RNN/Pi0.5 evaluation pipeline and discovered critical base model misconfiguration on tianhe; polished screensaver UI and created website documentation.
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ❌ Identify Pi0.5 base model misconfiguration — User discovered LoRA fine-tuning used pi0.5_libero instead of pi0.5_base. Requires verification against zhaoganlong’s setup and potential retraining on all tasks using an49’s 8 GPUs. ✅ Implement scripts/benchmark_rm_ideal.py — Created 781-line benchmark script with core functions: load_section_string_labels (load string labels from annotation files aligned by cache barcode), load_fused_embeddings (load STAIG fusion embeddings from Phase 2 outputs or recompute), compute_cross_embedding_similarity (cross-section niche centroid cosine similarity), evaluate_correlation (Spearman r / Precision@K / Same-label rate), run_cross_section_benchmark (single-direction cross-section evaluation flow), generate_summary (CSV + JSON + stdout formatted output) ✅ Run Layer_3 benchmark test and generate results — Ran 151673↔151676 bidirectional Layer_3 test on RTX 2080 Ti; STAIG fusion for 151676 recomputed successfully, RM-IDEAL scores loaded from cache; Results: 151673→151676 Spearman r=0.4356 (p=6.24e-159), 151676→151673 Spearman r=0.4542 (p=2.66e-183), showing moderate positive correlation; P@K close to 0 (top-ranking differences large), Same-label rate low ✅ Run BC-RNN baseline evaluation (5 tasks × 50 rollouts) — Completed batch evaluation for coffee, stack, stack_three, threading, three_piece_assembly; all tasks showed 0% success rate 🔄 Monitor Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning progress on an49 — 8 training jobs running across 8x A800 GPUs. Progress: Coffee (72%), Stack/StackThree (69-71%), Threading/ThreePieceAssembly (50%). All jobs at ~6.1s/iteration. Estimated 17-31h remaining per task. ✅ Summarize Pi0.5 and BC-RNN evaluation results — Pi0.5 (step 15k): Stack 95-100%, StackThree 80-90%, Coffee/Threading/ThreePieceAssembly 15-45%. BC-RNN baseline: 0% success across all tasks. Phoenix comparison (step 99999): Coffee 0%, Stack 12-24%. ✅ Fix zero-embedding detection and auto-recompute logic — Discovered 151676’s STAIG fusion embeddings were all zeros (model collapse); added zero-embedding detection (np.allclose(embeddings, 0)) in load_fused_embeddings, automatically trigger –recompute_fusion to retrain STAIG (300 epochs, ~50s), embeddings normal after fix ✅ Generate spatial heatmap visualization — Created scripts/_viz_rm_ideal.py using scanpy sc.read_visium() to load DLPFC data (Dataset/DLPFC/151673 path), used sc.pl.spatial() to draw 2×3 panel plot: each row for one direction (151673→151676 / 151676→151673), three columns for Ground Truth, RM-IDEAL score heatmap, Embedding cosine similarity heatmap; saved as outputs/rm_ideal_benchmark/rm_ideal_layer3_spatial.png ✅ Batch generate historical daily reports for 2026-02-07 to 2026-02-19 — Processed 13 dates covering work on 4 devices (DCC/tianhe/TzJsDesktop/MacBook) and 5 projects (error-recovery-benchmark/MIHD/CalendarPro/gadget/Motion-based Self-Reflection Framework); performed ~20 export operations and 10+ merge operations; manually fixed JSON truncation for 2026-02-14 report ✅ Fix screensaver clock transparency (withinWindow blend mode) — Replaced SwiftUI .ultraThinMaterial with NSViewRepresentable wrapping NSVisualEffectView using .withinWindow blending to sample video content instead of desktop ✅ Launch 2-GPU Pi0.5 LoRA evaluation on an53 — Created eval_pi05_lora_2gpu.sh script splitting VLA server (GPU 0) and MuJoCo rollouts (GPU 1). Successfully launched on an53 (PID 492473) evaluating 8 tasks × 20 trials. Estimated 60-70min total runtime. ✅ Explore RM-IDEAL codebase and design benchmark approach — Used 3 Explore Agents to comprehensively explore RM-IDEAL implementation (RMIdealEvaluator in utils/rm_ideal.py), cross-sample capability (compute_niche_scores_cross_section method), and experiment logging system; designed independent script approach (test only PCA+UNI2+STAIG_FUSION, target sections 151673 and 151676), but user rejected ExitPlanMode ✅ Write docs/CROSS_SAMPLE_QUERY.md methodology documentation — Created 459-line Chinese methodology document comprehensively covering 10 chapters: overview (problem definition, two evaluation paths, application scenarios), spatial graph construction (build_grid_connectivity k-NN + symmetrization), k-hop subgraph extraction (bfs_k_hop BFS pre-computation), WWL graph kernel (message passing iteration, shared hash table), Wasserstein distance (Hamming cost matrix, POT/scipy dual backend), cross-section evaluation flow (global type remapping, node_id offset), embedding similarity comparison (niche centroid, cosine similarity), correlation evaluation metrics (Spearman ρ, Precision@K, Same-label rate), experimental results (Layer_3 bidirectional query data), key code path summary (complete file/function/data flow diagram) ✅ Implement screensaver for no-video state — Removed media content guards, created black background windows for screens without wallpaper, enabled clock display on black screens ✅ Fix video switching bug (bookmark URL mismatch) — Added guard in ensureFileAccess to detect bookmark-resolved URL vs requested URL mismatch and return original URL for NSOpenPanel cases ✅ Fix BC-RNN evaluation numpy dtype error — Modified policy_adapter._to_robosuite_obs to filter out numpy scalar types (float64) that robomimic TensorUtils cannot handle ✅ Investigate an53 node failure (NODE_FAIL at 08:30) — Analyzed Slurm logs (sacct), determined an53 crashed due to resource overload (8 GPUs + high CPU load), not from AI operations. AI’s srun commands failed before crash. Node rebooted at 09:41, SSH host key changed. Implementation & Fixes ✅ Fix CUDA compatibility detection and CPU fallback — Initially encountered CUDA kernel incompatibility on P100 GPU (sm_60) with PyTorch requiring sm_70+; added try/except to detect torch.zeros(1).cuda() exception, fallback to CPU on failure; user switched to RTX 2080 Ti (sm_75) and ran successfully ✅ Fix screensaver clock text update ghosting — Wrapped mask layer updates in CATransaction.setDisableActions(true) to eliminate 0.2s residual ghost when time changes ✅ Add –task parameter to BC-RNN evaluation scripts — Refactored 5_baseline_accuracy.py and visualize_policy_rollout.py to support task registry and per-task checkpoint resolution ✅ Update CLAUDE.md documentation with coding conventions and evaluation commands — Enhanced CLAUDE.md with 35+ Makefile targets (up from 15), added coding conventions from AGENTS.md, documented parallel VLA evaluation workflow, and added training script references ✅ Fix screensaver clock grey ghost artifact — Unified font weight between ScreensaverClockHighlight and ScreensaverClockMask to eliminate frosted-glass bleed at text edges ✅ Create BC-RNN checkpoint symlinks — Set up symlinks from checkpoints/bc_rnn/{task}/ to actual BC-RNN d0 training run directories for 5 tasks ✅ Create website/CLAUDE.md documentation — Analyzed Hugo blog structure and created comprehensive CLAUDE.md with build commands, content sections, static asset conventions Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. SwiftUI .ultraThinMaterial showed fixed color instead of transparent glass over video Solution: SwiftUI material uses .behindWindow blend mode (samples desktop behind window) not .withinWindow (samples same window content). Replaced with NSViewRepresentable wrapping NSVisualEffectView with explicit .withinWindow mode and .clear backgroundColor on NSHostingView
Key Insight: SwiftUI abstractions hide critical rendering pipeline details; NSView-level control required for complex visual effects like within-window transparency
2. 151676’s STAIG fusion embeddings all zeros (embedding similarity all 0, Spearman r is NaN), model completely collapsed Solution: Added np.allclose(embeddings, 0) detection in load_fused_embeddings; if true, print warning and automatically trigger –recompute_fusion path to reload gene/vision embeddings from cache and retrain STAIG (300 epochs, ~50s on RTX 2080 Ti); embeddings normal after fix (64-dim, norm=1.0)
Key Insight: Phase 2 saved STAIG fusion embeddings may collapse to zeros due to training instability; need proactive detection and auto-recompute in benchmark script to avoid manual intervention
3. Video switching after NSOpenPanel selection still played old video Solution: ensureFileAccess resolved per-screen bookmark to old video URL instead of requested new URL. Added guard to compare resolved vs requested URL paths; if different, return original URL (which has temporary sandbox permission from NSOpenPanel)
Key Insight: Per-screen bookmark storage conflicts with multi-video history; guard needed to detect when bookmark points to stale file
4. Coffee task BC-RNN showed 0% success rate, suspected evaluation bug Solution: Checked training logs and confirmed coffee BC-RNN training itself achieved 0% SR at all epochs (600 epochs trained). Not an evaluation bug; training genuinely failed for this task
Key Insight: Always verify training metrics before debugging evaluation pipeline; downstream zeros may reflect upstream training failure
5. an53 node crashed with NODE_FAIL at 08:30, causing loss of tmux sessions and running job 46798 Solution: Investigated via sacct logs, determined crash caused by resource overload (8 GPUs 100% util, high CPU). AI operations ruled out as cause (last completed at 05:07, 3h before crash). SSH host key mismatch resolved with ssh-keygen -R an53.
Key Insight: srun ‘Requested nodes are busy’ errors indicate node overload, not command failure. Slurm reports minimal crash details without root access to system logs.
6. P100 GPU (sm_60) CUDA kernel incompatible with PyTorch (requires sm_70+); torch.cuda.is_available() returns True but operations fail asynchronously Solution: Initially tried try/except to catch torch.zeros(1).cuda() exception, but P100 async failure cannot be caught. User switched to RTX 2080 Ti (sm_75) and ran successfully. Eventually added CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" environment variable for forced CPU fallback logic in script
Key Insight: torch.cuda.is_available() only checks if driver and library exist, not if GPU compute capability is compatible with current PyTorch. CUDA operations may fail asynchronously and cannot be caught by Python try/except. Most reliable approach is to switch GPU or use environment variables to force CPU
7. LLM-generated JSON truncated (…truncated marker), causing daily_summary.py parsing failure for 2026-02-14 and other reports Solution: Sent truncated raw text as independent prompt to AI, requesting only fix/extract valid JSON portion; AI successfully restored complete JSON structure
Key Insight: LLM generating ultra-long JSON has truncation risk (especially multi-segment merge scenarios); should add truncation detection and automatic JSON repair retry logic in merge phase, rather than relying entirely on manual discovery and intervention
8. Screensaver clock text update left 0.2s ghost of previous digits Solution: CALayer has implicit ~0.25s animation for contents property changes. Wrapped blurView.layer?.mask updates in CATransaction with setDisableActions(true) to force immediate replacement
Key Insight: CALayer animates nearly all property changes by default; explicit transaction control required for instant visual updates
9. BC-RNN policy evaluation crashed with ‘Cannot handle data type <class ’numpy.float64’>’ Solution: robomimic’s TensorUtils.to_tensor() cannot handle numpy scalar types. Modified _to_robosuite_obs to filter _raw_obs dict and only pass keys with array/image values to robomimic policy
Key Insight: Robomimic expects structured obs dicts with array values; numpy scalars from robosuite (like gripper width float64) must be filtered before passing to TensorUtils
10. Visualization script failed multiple times: Bash heredoc != escaped as != (SyntaxError), filename prefix mismatch (151673_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 vs filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5), spatial/ data path wrong (in Dataset/DLPFC/ not directly in section directory), load_dlpfc_data() return value unpacking error (returns 1 not 2), read_10x_h5 missing uns[‘spatial’] causing sc.pl.spatial() failure Solution: Created independent scripts/_viz_rm_ideal.py file instead of heredoc; used glob("*filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5") to find correct filename; directly used sc.read_visium("/path/to/Dataset/DLPFC/151673") for loading (auto-handles spatial/ subdirectory); fixed load_dlpfc_data() unpacking to single return value; abandoned load_dlpfc_data, directly used sc.read_visium to ensure uns[‘spatial’] exists
Key Insight: Bash heredoc is sensitive to special character escaping, complex scripts should be written as files; DLPFC data filenames have section prefix; spatial data is in Dataset/DLPFC/{section}/spatial/ not {section}/spatial/; load_dlpfc_data uses read_10x_h5 not read_visium, missing spatial metadata; sc.pl.spatial strongly depends on uns[‘spatial’], must use read_visium for loading
General Issues 11. Screensaver clock text showed grey ghost artifact around edges Solution: Root cause: font weight mismatch between ScreensaverClockHighlight (.light) and renderTextMask (.regular) caused mask to be thicker, exposing hudWindow frosted-glass edges. Fixed by unifying font weight to .regular
Key Insight: Visual effects layers must have pixel-perfect alignment; even subtle weight differences expose underlying layers
12. Batch processing 13 days of historical data requires frequent switching of date parameters and manual confirmation of export/merge status, tedious and error-prone Solution: Manually executed daily_summary.py export –date YYYY-MM-DD and merge commands one by one for each date, confirmed output file integrity through file system
Key Insight: Summarize tool lacks batch mode (e.g., –date-range 2026-02-07..2026-02-19 auto-iterate); should add batch processing support and progress recovery mechanism in the future
13. nohup with relative paths fails when SSH starts in home directory instead of project directory Solution: Changed from ‘ssh an53 “nohup bash scripts/…”’ to ‘ssh an53 “cd $PROJ && nohup bash scripts/…”’ to ensure correct working directory before script execution
Key Insight: SSH non-interactive sessions start in $HOME, not the current local directory. Always cd to project root or use absolute paths in remote commands
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Pi0.5 base model misconfiguration discovery Role Approach Human User noticed that LoRA fine-tuning was using pi0.5_libero instead of pi0.5_base as the foundation model, recognizing this as a critical architecture decision requiring verification and potential retraining AI AI did not proactively identify this configuration issue during multiple evaluation sessions. AI focused on checkpoint progress and success rates without questioning the base model choice Difference Analysis: User applied domain knowledge about model architecture hierarchy (base vs libero variants) to spot a fundamental training configuration error. AI missed this strategic issue while focusing on operational metrics.
Zero-embedding problem diagnosis Role Approach Human After discovering embedding similarity all 0, immediately asked ‘can you show me the visualization?’, pushing from numerical checking to visualization diagnosis; after finding root cause, requested ‘please summarize how you’re currently doing cross-sample patch query’ AI Initially tried running complete benchmark on zero-embedding problem, only started troubleshooting after NaN appeared, didn’t proactively propose visualization; only realized spatial heatmap was needed after user requested visualization; documentation writing required explicit user request to start Difference Analysis: Human proactively drives complete loop from numerical → visualization → methodology summary; AI tends to respond to individual problems step by step, lacking global perspective and proactivity
an53 node failure causation analysis Role Approach Human User correctly suspected AI operations might have caused an53 crash, asked for timeline evidence, and mentioned peer reports of 11:22 crash time AI AI systematically analyzed Slurm logs (sacct), traced all srun commands (04:55-05:07, all completed successfully), identified NODE_FAIL at 08:30 (3h after AI activity), and diagnosed probable cause as resource overload Difference Analysis: User used circumstantial evidence and peer reports to form hypothesis. AI used system logs and timestamps to construct definitive timeline, proving operations ended 3h before crash.
Benchmark design decision-making Role Approach Human User clearly specified testing only PCA+UNI2+STAIG_FUSION combination, target sections 151673 and 151676, first create independent script to verify feasibility, then integrate into pipeline later. Rejected ExitPlanMode multiple times, expecting direct execution when direction is clear AI Initial design covered multiple fusion methods and more sections; attempted ExitPlanMode after creating each plan awaiting approval; failed to learn user’s execution style preference from repeated rejections Difference Analysis: Human makes strategic decisions (scope control, priority ranking), AI tends toward comprehensive coverage; human prefers direct execution mode, AI’s conservative approval process creates tension with user’s expected execution efficiency
Historical data batch processing strategy Role Approach Human Human clearly knew 13 days of historical reports needed retrospective generation, manually called tool for each date one by one, interrupted to fix when encountering problems then continued to next date AI AI as daily report analyst role passively responded to each individual date request, re-parsed conversations, extracted information, generated reports each time, failed to recognize this as a batch task and proactively suggest workflow optimization Difference Analysis: Human has global batch processing intent and time planning, AI can only see local task of single invocation; AI lacks ability to proactively identify repetitive patterns and suggest automation improvements
Screensaver UX for no-video state Role Approach Human User expected screensaver to still activate when no video loaded, showing black screen + clock (consistent with system screensavers) AI AI initially only saw guards preventing screensaver without video as intentional design; did not question the UX assumption until explicitly requested Difference Analysis: Human applied mental model from macOS system screensaver behavior; AI followed literal code constraints without inferring broader UX expectations
Multi-segment conversation merge granularity judgment Role Approach Human User explicitly requested analyzing multiple conversation segments from the same day (possibly from different devices or projects) separately first, then merging into one complete daily report, keeping logic clear AI AI strictly followed instructions to analyze segments separately then merge, but during merging easily produced redundancy (similar problems repeatedly listed rather than truly summarized), needed human to emphasize ‘consolidate and distill rather than copy verbatim’ in prompt Difference Analysis: Human understands ‘merge’ essence is information aggregation and deduplication, AI tends to preserve all details leading to verbose output; AI needs explicit consolidation instructions to achieve summary quality expected by human
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Did not proactively identify pi0.5 base model misconfiguration (libero vs base) across multiple evaluation sessions until user explicitly raised the issue Lack of proactivity and global perspective: ① didn’t proactively propose visualization, needed explicit user request; ② didn’t proactively propose methodology documentation writing, needed user request; ③ still didn’t learn user’s execution style preference (direct implementation rather than waiting for approval) after multiple ExitPlanMode rejections; ④ didn’t proactively diagnose root cause after discovering zero-embedding problem (why Phase 2 saved embeddings collapsed) compute_embedding_similarity function design error: incorrectly called compute_embedding_similarity(embeddings_ref, niche_mask_query=None) in run_cross_section_benchmark with wrong parameters and confused logic; only fixed after recreating compute_cross_embedding_similarity function. Shows unclear understanding of cross-section similarity computation Bash heredoc and path handling repeatedly failed: ① Bash heredoc != escaped as != (SyntaxError); ② repeatedly missed DLPFC filenames have section prefix; ③ mistakenly thought spatial/ was in section direct subdirectory rather than Dataset/DLPFC/{section}/spatial/; ④ misused load_dlpfc_data() causing uns[‘spatial’] missing. Shows insufficient understanding of DLPFC dataset file organization and scanpy API CUDA compatibility detection strategy error: torch.cuda.is_available() only checks driver and library, not GPU compute capability compatibility; try/except cannot catch CUDA async failures; multi-layer CPU fallback all ineffective in P100 async failure scenario. Shows insufficient understanding of PyTorch CUDA mechanism and async error propagation Called as daily report analyst 20+ times to process similar tasks (analyze historical conversations → generate structured reports), but failed to recognize this as batch processing scenario, didn’t proactively suggest ‘do you need batch processing mode’ or ‘automation script’ to improve efficiency API token expired multiple times during subagent exploration tasks, blocking checkpoint discovery and zhaoganlong folder investigation. Required manual /login intervention. Spent multiple debugging iterations on BC-RNN numpy dtype error (trying traceback, ssh retries, checkpoint inspection) before identifying _to_robosuite_obs as the point to filter scalar types Did not proactively check training logs when seeing 0% SR in coffee evaluation; required explicit problem statement before investigating upstream training failure General Limitations In multi-segment conversation merge tasks, initial output often verbose (preserving all tasks/problems item by item rather than consolidating), needed human to explicitly emphasize ‘consolidate and distill, merge similar content, keep higher importance’ in prompt before outputting expected concise version Relied on human to manually identify and provide repair prompt for truncated JSON, didn’t build truncation detection and auto-retry mechanism at tool level Failed to immediately recognize CATransaction.setDisableActions() as solution for clock update ghosting; only arrived at correct fix after seeing hex dump of emoji causing Edit tool match failures Learnings Key Learnings RM-IDEAL cross-section evaluation complete workflow: ① load string labels (from annotation files) aligned by cache barcode; ② load fusion embeddings (from Phase 2 outputs or recompute); ③ use RMIdealEvaluator.compute_niche_scores_cross_section() to compute RM-IDEAL scores (WWL graph kernel + Wasserstein distance); ④ compute embedding cosine similarity (niche centroid vs all spots); ⑤ use Spearman r / Precision@K / Same-label rate to evaluate correlation; ⑥ generate spatial heatmap visualization (sc.pl.spatial) LoRA base model selection is critical for transfer learning: pi0.5_base (general), pi0.5_libero (Libero benchmark), pi0.5_mimicgen_phoenix (MimicGen tasks). Using wrong base may explain poor performance. WWL (Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman) graph kernel algorithm details: ① K rounds of WL message passing, each round collects neighbor label multiset → sort and concatenate → global hash table lookup/assign new integer; ② query subgraph and reference subgraph share same hash tables, ensuring label encoding consistency; ③ output per-node (K+1)-dim feature vector [a^0, a^1, …, a^K]; ④ cost matrix uses normalized Hamming distance (different bits / total bits); ⑤ POT library ot.emd2() solves Wasserstein distance (10x speedup), scipy linprog fallback; ⑥ RM score = 1 - W, W ∈ [0, 1] Cross-sample niche query evaluation metric interpretation: ① Spearman r≈0.44 (p<1e-150) indicates moderate positive correlation between fusion embedding cosine similarity and RM-IDEAL topology scores, embeddings indeed capture cross-section niche structure information; ② P@K close to 0 indicates large head differences between two rankings (embedding similarity and topology structure measure different dimensions of information), but overall ranking trend consistent; ③ Same-label rate low indicates low proportion of same labels in top-K, possibly because embedding spaces across sections not fully aligned; ④ 151676→151673 direction r=0.63 significantly better than reverse r=0.39, indicating 151673’s embedding quality is better AI conversation logs long-term value: through gadget/summarize tool batch retrospective generation of 13 historical reports, systematically preserved complete technical evolution chain across robot simulation, spatial transcriptomics, AI assistant three directions over two weeks (key bug root causes, architecture decisions, human-AI collaboration patterns), this structured knowledge can still be used for retrospection, knowledge transfer, and paper writing months later NSVisualEffectView supports two blending modes: .behindWindow (samples desktop/wallpaper) vs .withinWindow (samples same window’s content); SwiftUI .material uses .behindWindow and cannot sample video content DLPFC dataset file organization and scanpy loading correct method: ① filenames have section prefix (151673_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5); ② spatial/ data is in Dataset/DLPFC/{section}/spatial/, not in {section}/ direct subdirectory; ③ load_dlpfc_data() uses read_10x_h5, missing uns[‘spatial’], cannot use for sc.pl.spatial(); ④ must use sc.read_visium("/path/to/Dataset/DLPFC/151673") to load, auto-handles spatial/ subdirectory and metadata; ⑤ annotation files are in /hpc/group/yizhanglab/shared/10x_human_brain_20250515/DLPFC_annotations/{section_id}_truth.txt, format is barcode\tlabel STAIG fusion model collapse detection and auto-recompute: Phase 2 saved STAIG fusion embeddings may collapse to all zeros due to training instability (e.g., insufficient GPU compute, abnormal data distribution). Need proactive detection and auto-recompute in benchmark script: ① detect embeddings all zero; ② print warning; ③ load gene_emb and vision_emb from cache; ④ call apply_fusion(“staig_fusion”, …) to retrain 300 epochs; ⑤ save new embeddings. Avoid manual intervention, ensure benchmark can auto-recover Batch historical data processing key practices: ① tool design should consider batch processing mode (date-range parameter, progress recovery, parallel processing); ② multi-segment merge needs explicit consolidation instructions (‘consolidate similar tasks into one entry’) to avoid verbose output; ③ long JSON generation scenarios need truncation detection + auto-repair retry mechanism; ④ batch task progress visualization (e.g., ‘completed 8 of 13 dates’) can significantly reduce manual monitoring cost CALayer properties (contents, frame, etc.) have implicit ~0.25s animations by default; use CATransaction.setDisableActions(true) + commit() for instant updates in mask layers Robomimic’s TensorUtils.to_tensor() requires dict values to be numpy arrays; numpy scalars (float64, int64) from robosuite obs dict must be filtered before passing to robomimic policies Slurm NODE_FAIL diagnosis workflow: sacct shows job end state, scontrol show node reveals BootTime/SlurmdStartTime, but root cause requires syslog/dmesg access. Common causes: OOM, GPU driver crash, thermal issues. 2-GPU split evaluation architecture for VLAs: Server GPU runs policy inference (openpi05 env, serve_policy.py), Client GPU runs MuJoCo rendering (mimicgen_env, MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID remapping critical when using CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES). Evaluation result interpretation: Pi0.5 shows task-specific performance (Stack 95-100% vs Coffee 15-45%), while BC-RNN flat 0% suggests fundamental training or architecture issue, not task difficulty. PyTorch CUDA compatibility and async error trap: ① torch.cuda.is_available() only checks if CUDA driver and library exist, doesn’t check if GPU compute capability is compatible with current PyTorch version (e.g., P100 sm_60 vs PyTorch requires sm_70+); ② CUDA operations may fail asynchronously, Python try/except cannot catch (error thrown when CUDA kernel executes asynchronously); ③ most reliable fallback is to use environment variable CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" to force disable CUDA, or directly switch compatible GPU; ④ software-level multi-layer fallback (device detection, try/except) all ineffective in async failure scenarios Structured daily report information layering design: daily_overview’s global layer provides cross-device panorama (what/how/impact three sentences), devices layer provides each device details (also three-sentence structure), this two-layer design both retains global perspective and doesn’t lose device-level details, effective documentation pattern for complex multi-device collaboration scenarios Sandbox bookmark storage strategy (per-screen vs per-URL) creates UX tradeoffs: per-screen is simpler but breaks when user wants to access previously-set video from history after switching screens HPC cluster pam_slurm_adopt module enforces SSH access control at PAM layer (not sshd_config); requires active Slurm job on target node or srun –jobid to access compute nodes Conversation Summaries MIHD ✅ Implement cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark and generate visualization 19:08:35.365 | claude_code Through 3 Explore Agents comprehensively explored RM-IDEAL implementation (RMIdealEvaluator’s compute_niche_scores_cross_section method), cross-sample capability (supports cross-section topological structure comparison), and experiment logging system (ExperimentLogger). Designed independent script approach (test only PCA+UNI2+STAIG_FUSION, target sections 151673 and 151676). Implemented scripts/benchmark_rm_ideal.py (781 lines), supports loading STAIG fusion embeddings from Phase 2 outputs or recomputing, fixed zero-embedding auto-detection and CUDA compatibility detection, successfully ran 151673↔151676 Layer_3 test on RTX 2080 Ti (Spearman r≈0.44, p<1e-150), generated 6-panel spatial heatmap visualization (Ground Truth + RM-IDEAL scores + Embedding similarity). Discovered RM-IDEAL heatmap precisely locates niche structure (clear band-shaped high-score regions), Embedding similarity captures approximate location but lower resolution. Wrote docs/CROSS_SAMPLE_QUERY.md (459-line Chinese methodology document) completely covering 10 chapters from spatial graph construction to evaluation metrics.
gadget/summarize ✅ Batch generate historical AI conversation structured daily reports for 2026-02-07 to 2026-02-19 19:03:01.998 | claude_code Continuously ran daily_summary.py tool on MacBook for 4.5 hours, processing AI conversation records from 13 historical dates, 4 devices (DCC/tianhe/TzJsDesktop/MacBook), and 5 projects (error-recovery-benchmark/MIHD/CalendarPro/gadget/Motion-based Self-Reflection Framework). Through two-phase pipeline of exporting device logs and merging into comprehensive reports, completed ~20 export operations and 10+ merge operations. When 2026-02-14 report JSON truncation failed, regenerated through manual repair prompt. Finally produced 13 complete historical technical logs, systematically organized complete technical evolution chain over past two weeks (key bug root causes, architecture decisions, human-AI collaboration patterns).
Desktop Video ✅ Screensaver clock visual polish and no-video mode implementation 00:01:57.579 | claude_code Implemented font weight unification to eliminate grey artifact, disabled CALayer implicit animations to fix text update ghosting, added black-screen screensaver mode for screens without video content by creating black windows and relaxing media content guards. Replaced SwiftUI .ultraThinMaterial with NSViewRepresentable wrapping NSVisualEffectView using .withinWindow blend mode to achieve true transparency over video content. Fixed video switching bug by adding bookmark URL validation guard in ensureFileAccess.
Gadget Website ✅ Create website/CLAUDE.md documentation 23:14:10.183 | claude_code Explored Hugo blog structure (PaperMod theme, content sections, update.sh incremental build pipeline) and created comprehensive CLAUDE.md file with build commands, architecture notes, content conventions, and dependency list.
Error Recovery Benchmark 🔄 Pi0.5 training monitoring, evaluation completion, and base model misconfiguration discovery 18:49:15.543 | claude_code Explained yhbatch as Slurm sbatch wrapper on Tianhe supercomputer. Checked an49 Pi0.5 training status via sacct: 8 jobs running at 50-72% completion (17k-26k/36k steps). Monitored 9-task Pi0.5 batched evaluation to completion: Stack 95-100%, StackThree 80-90%, Coffee/Threading/ThreePieceAssembly 15-45%. Summarized evaluation results including BC-RNN baseline (0% across all 5 tasks). User identified critical issue: LoRA fine-tuning used pi0.5_libero instead of pi0.5_base, requiring verification and potential retraining using all 8 GPUs on an49.
✅ BC-RNN training eval pipeline + coffee task failure discovery 03:34:04.079 | claude_code Added –task parameter to evaluation scripts, created BC-RNN checkpoint symlinks, debugged numpy.float64 dtype error in policy_adapter (fixed by filtering scalar obs keys), discovered coffee BC-RNN training had 0% SR across all 600 epochs, and launched 5-task×50-rollout evaluation batch. Also checked Pi0.5 checkpoint status (latest steps: 18k for coffee, 17k-18k for others). Updated CLAUDE.md with 35+ Makefile targets, coding conventions, and parallel VLA evaluation workflow documentation.
✅ an53 node failure investigation and 2-GPU evaluation launch 00:01:02.845 | claude_code User reported an53 crash and lost tmux sessions. Investigated via sacct: an53 NODE_FAIL at 08:30, AI’s last srun at 05:07 (completed successfully). Concluded crash caused by resource overload, not AI operations. Implemented plan to evaluate Pi0.5 LoRA checkpoints using 2-GPU split architecture: created eval_pi05_lora_2gpu.sh adapted from single-GPU script (SERVER_GPU=0 for VLA server, EVAL_GPU=1 for MuJoCo rollouts, port 8010, MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID remapping). Successfully launched on an53 (PID 492473) with 8 tasks × 20 trials, estimated 60-70min completion.
