title: Bug Journal 2026-03-03

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-02 Daily Overview What was done: Multi-device research and development spanning three major projects: MIHD spatial transcriptomics clustering benchmarks (scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiments, 36 visualizations for weekly report), Desktop Video macOS app critical bug fixes (security-scoped bookmark system, screensaver UI redesign), and Error Recovery Benchmark VLA training (monitored 10 concurrent Pi0.5 LoRA jobs, created external dependencies inventory, diagnosed GPU utilization bottleneck) How it was done: MIHD: systematic pipeline debugging with cache metadata repair and parallel fusion evaluations. Desktop Video: forensic log analysis (4438 lines) revealing root cause, entitlements configuration, SwiftUI material effects. VLA: Slurm cluster management commands (squeue, srun with –jobid to bypass PAM restrictions), systematic config parsing for dependency cataloging, mathematical profiling of action chunking inference patterns Impact: MIHD: discovered fundamental architectural issue where STAIG ignores gene encoder embeddings affecting all published results; completed Monday presentation with 36 visualizations. Desktop Video: eliminated 100% failure rate of video/image restoration after app restart (Code 257 errors). VLA: delivered accurate training progress (an49 38% complete ~37h remaining, an53 27% ~46h remaining), created comprehensive dependency inventory covering 65+ GB across 10 categories, identified root cause of 10% GPU utilization enabling future optimization DCC What was done: MIHD scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiments (11 sections × 5 fusion methods = 55 evaluations), weekly report compilation with 3 statistical charts + 33 per-section clustering visualizations How it was done: Pipeline config yaml + phase2_evaluate.py for parallel experiment execution; matplotlib/seaborn for publication-quality figures; visualize_from_cache.py for per-encoder spatial plots; switched from mclust to kmeans when high-dim embeddings caused hangs Impact: Key scientific finding: STAIG two-stage fusion uses raw HVG matrix instead of gene encoder output → scGPT+UNI2+STAIG = PCA+UNI2+STAIG (ARI 0.546). QFormer achieved best scGPT fusion (ARI 0.370 vs 0.170 gene-only, +118%). Ready for Monday lab meeting with complete evidence MacBook What was done: Desktop Video app: fixed critical security-scoped bookmark bugs (4 bugs: entitlements missing, errorLog concurrency, log spam, double window open) + explored and implemented liquid glass screensaver clock effects How it was done: Forensic log analysis (879 updateClockLabels calls, 671 timer logs) revealed empty entitlements file → bookmark creation always failed → Code 257 on restore. Solution: added com.apple.security.files.bookmarks.app-scope + non-scoped fallback parsing + activeSecurityScopedURLs lifecycle tracking. UI: researched SwiftUI .glassEffect() and .mask() + .ultraThinMaterial for glass text effect Impact: Eliminated 100% failure rate of video/image restoration after app restart (affected all users since v1.0). Reduced log noise by 1550+ spam entries per session. Implemented liquid glass screensaver clock matching macOS native aesthetic, though user later requested fully transparent variant and reported video switching freeze issue tianhe What was done: Monitored 10 concurrent Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning jobs across GPU nodes an49 (6 tasks, 38.3% progress) and an53 (4 tasks, 26.8% progress), created comprehensive external dependencies inventory with 14 categories covering 65+ GB of assets, and diagnosed GPU utilization bottleneck in VLA evaluation pipeline How it was done: Used Slurm commands (squeue, srun –jobid, nvidia-smi) to bypass SSH PAM restrictions, analyzed training logs and checkpoint directories, systematically parsed task_registry.yaml and benchmark configs, created structured markdown inventory with paths/purposes/references, and mathematically profiled action chunking inference pattern (50-step chunks = only 8 GPU calls per 400-step trial) Impact: Delivered accurate training progress reports (an49 tasks ~37h remaining, an53 tasks ~46h remaining), created reusable external_dependencies_inventory.md covering 4 conda envs + 10 datasets + 9 BC-RNN checkpoints + 4 VLA checkpoints + configuration directories + external tools, and identified root cause of 10% GPU utilization (action chunking reduces inference to 8 calls/trial, with GPU work being 20s / 200s total) enabling future batched evaluation optimization Fixed critical MIHD scGPT benchmark bugs discovering STAIG architectural limitation, resolved Desktop Video macOS sandbox permission system with security-scoped bookmarks, monitored large-scale Pi0.5 VLA training across GPU cluster, created comprehensive external dependencies inventory, and diagnosed GPU utilization bottleneck in evaluation pipeline
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Fix Desktop Video security-scoped bookmark system — Root cause: empty entitlements file → bookmark creation failed silently → Code 257 on restore. Fixed by adding bookmark.app-scope permission + non-scoped fallback parsing in restoreFromBookmark/ensureFileAccess + lifecycle tracking via activeSecurityScopedURLs dict ✅ Create external dependencies inventory for error_recovery_benchmark — Built structured markdown inventory (docs/external_dependencies_inventory.md) cataloging 14 categories of external dependencies: configuration directories, AI assistant logs, external binaries, environment variables, Python packages, Google Drive rclone paths, external Git repos, Ollama models, 4 conda envs, 10 HDF5 datasets (~18.4GB), 9 BC-RNN checkpoints, 4 VLA checkpoints (~47GB), bootstrap procedures, and troubleshooting guides. Updated 项目全景总结.md with references ✅ Run scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiments (5 methods × 11 sections) — Evaluated concat, mean, attention, llava_mlp, qformer fusion strategies. QFormer achieved best performance (avg ARI 0.370 exc 151676). Confirmed STAIG fusion ignores gene encoder ✅ Monitor Pi0.5 LoRA training progress on GPU cluster — Checked status of 10 concurrent training jobs across an49 (6 tasks) and an53 (4 tasks) nodes using Slurm commands, analyzed training logs, GPU utilization, and estimated completion times. Delivered comprehensive report showing 38.3% progress on an49 (~37h remaining) and 26.8% on an53 (~46h remaining) 🔄 Diagnose GPU utilization bottleneck in VLA evaluation pipeline — Identified root cause of ~10% GPU utilization: Pi0.5 action chunking (50-step outputs) reduces inference calls to ~8 per 400-step trial (8 × 2.5s = 20s GPU work / 200s total = 10% utilization). User requested optimization plan with web research on Pi0/Pi0.5 inference techniques and batched evaluation implementation, but task was interrupted before plan completion ✅ Fix scGPT cache metadata and re-evaluate baseline — Added cache_version and metadata_json fields to 11 scGPT .npz files, re-ran phase2_evaluate.py for gene-only clustering. Result: ARI improved from 0.114 (buggy) to 0.170 (+49%) 🔄 Debug Desktop Video transparent label and video switching freeze — User reported: 1) wants fully transparent label (not frosted glass), 2) video switching causes freeze. Started investigation via log analysis and SingleScreenView.swift exploration. Session interrupted before resolution Implementation & Fixes ✅ Generate MIHD weekly report visualizations — Created 3 summary charts (method ranking bar, section heatmap, PCA vs scGPT comparison) + 33 per-section clustering plots (PCA/scGPT/UNI2 × 11 sections). Inserted into docs/experiments/weekly_report_20260301.md ✅ Fix Desktop Video errorLog() concurrency and log spam — Rewrote errorLog() to route through LogFile.shared.write() (eliminated concurrent FileHandle writes). Removed 879 updateClockLabels + 671 startScreensaverTimer dlog calls. Fixed double openMainWindow on launch ✅ Research and implement liquid glass screensaver clock — Researched SwiftUI .glassEffect() (macOS 26+) and backward-compatible .mask() approach for macOS 12.4+. Implemented glass text effect using .mask() + .ultraThinMaterial + specular gradient highlights. Time font changed to .thin. Removed background card entirely Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Desktop Video: 100% failure rate restoring videos/images after app restart with Code 257 ‘Operation not permitted’ Solution: 4-part fix: 1) Add com.apple.security.files.bookmarks.app-scope to entitlements (was empty dict). 2) Add fallback in restoreFromBookmark: if .withSecurityScope fails, try options:[] to recover old non-scoped bookmarks, then re-save as scoped. 3) Track active URLs in activeSecurityScopedURLs dict, release in clear(). 4) ensureFileAccess() auto-resolves bookmarks before file I/O
Key Insight: macOS sandbox bookmark system requires BOTH compile-time entitlement declaration AND runtime URL.startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() lifecycle management. Empty entitlements → bookmark creation silently falls back to non-scoped → restore with .withSecurityScope fails → Code 257. The 879-line errorLog() was never writing errors due to concurrent FileHandle race condition, masking the root cause for months
2. MIHD: STAIG two-stage fusion ignores gene encoder embeddings, making scGPT+UNI2+STAIG equivalent to PCA+UNI2+STAIG Solution: Traced through models/Fusion.py and pipeline/runner.py: staig_fusion uses staig_gene_feat (raw HVG matrix from cache) instead of gene_emb parameter. This is architectural, not a bug. Documented in weekly report as key finding that affects interpretation of all STAIG results
Key Insight: Gene encoder (PCA/scGPT) outputs are only utilized by non-STAIG fusion methods. For graph-based fusion like STAIG, the pretrained spatial embeddings don’t enter the GCN node features. This explains why scGPT underperforms: it’s optimized for cross-tissue generalization, not within-dataset variance maximization like PCA
3. VLA Evaluation: Low GPU utilization (~10%) in Pi0.5 evaluation pipeline despite 100% utilization during training, causing slow evaluation throughput Solution: Root cause identified: Action chunking architecture where Pi0.5 outputs 50-step action sequences reduces inference calls to only ~8 per 400-step trial (8 × 2.5s = 20s GPU work / 200s total = 10% utilization). Solution planning interrupted but optimization strategies requested via web research and batched evaluation implementation
Key Insight: VLA evaluation bottleneck is not GPU compute capacity but pipeline serialization: action chunking minimizes GPU calls, and each inference cycle includes CPU preprocessing, WebSocket I/O, and simulation steps that dominate total time. Batched inference with timer-based request collection could improve throughput
4. SSH access denied on GPU nodes an49/an53 due to PAM slurm_adopt rejecting connections with ’no active jobs on this node’ error Solution: Bypassed SSH restriction using Slurm’s srun command with –jobid flag to execute commands directly in allocated job context: srun --jobid=45628 bash -c 'command'
Key Insight: Slurm’s srun with –jobid provides a way to run commands on allocated nodes even when PAM blocks direct SSH, useful for cluster environments with strict access control
General Issues 5. Background task timeouts when using srun on heavily loaded GPU node (an49), with commands taking 60+ seconds to respond Solution: Added –overlap flag and reduced timeout expectations, falling back to simpler nvidia-smi when nvitop unavailable or too slow. Accepted that heavily loaded nodes have higher command latency
Key Insight: When GPU nodes are running 100% utilization workloads, administrative commands via srun can experience significant delays; design monitoring scripts to handle timeouts gracefully and use lightweight fallback commands
6. MIHD: mclust clustering hung indefinitely on scGPT (512d) and UNI2 (1536d) embeddings during visualization Solution: Switched from default mclust to kmeans via –cluster_method flag in visualize_from_cache.py. High-dimensional data causes mclust’s EM algorithm to become computationally intractable
Key Insight: Gaussian mixture model clustering (mclust) scales poorly beyond ~100 dimensions. For foundation model embeddings (512d-1536d), kmeans or spectral methods are necessary for practical runtime
7. Desktop Video log file filled with 1550+ spam entries per session (updateClockLabels × 879, startScreensaverTimer × 671) Solution: Removed dlog from updateClockLabels (called every second during screensaver). Moved dlog in startScreensaverTimer to after all early-exit guards (only logs when actually scheduling timer). Removed dlog from idle timer callback
Key Insight: High-frequency dlog calls in timer callbacks create massive log files that obscure real errors. Logging should be gated on state transitions, not periodic ticks
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level MIHD: Recognizing that scGPT+UNI2+STAIG should equal PCA+UNI2+STAIG Role Approach Human User (Yi Zhang) noticed from pilot results that scGPT spatial embeddings ’look similar to PCA’ and suspected gene encoder might not be used in STAIG fusion AI Assistant initially planned to run all scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiments to get the STAIG ARI, assuming gene encoder would be utilized Difference Analysis: Human intuition from domain expertise (scGPT as dimensionality reducer) led to hypothesis before code verification. Assistant followed literal task (get ARI number) without questioning the fusion architecture. Human-driven investigation revealed the actual implementation detail
VLA: GPU utilization root cause analysis approach Role Approach Human User provided detailed mathematical breakdown: Pi0.5 outputs 50-step chunks, 400-step trials need only ~8 inference calls, 2.5s per call = 20s GPU / 200s total = 10% utilization, with further breakdown of CPU vs GPU work within each 2.5s inference cycle AI AI would have likely started with profiling tools or log analysis to identify the bottleneck empirically before deriving the mathematical model Difference Analysis: User demonstrated systems-level thinking by reasoning from architecture (action chunking design) to derive expected utilization mathematically, rather than relying on measurement-first debugging. This shows deep understanding of the VLA inference pipeline structure
Desktop Video: Diagnosing bookmark bug via log archaeology vs direct code inspection Role Approach Human User provided symptom description and log file location, allowing evidence-based diagnosis AI Assistant read 4438-line log file to discover ‘saved non-scoped bookmark’ pattern (never ‘saved security-scoped’), traced back to empty entitlements file via code search, then verified hypothesis Difference Analysis: Log-driven debugging revealed runtime behavior (bookmark creation always non-scoped) that wouldn’t be obvious from static code analysis alone. The root cause (empty entitlements) was invisible in logs but became clear once non-scoped pattern was identified
VLA: External dependencies documentation format Role Approach Human User wanted a clear, structured file inventory listing exact paths, purposes, and reference locations in codebase for each external dependency AI AI initially created EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES.md with more narrative, documentation-style content explaining concepts and usage patterns Difference Analysis: User recognized need for operational reference (what files exist where) vs explanatory documentation (how to use them). User provided explicit plan specifying table-based structure with paths/purposes/references. This shows user’s understanding that different documentation serves different purposes
Implementation Level Desktop Video: Using existing visualize_from_cache.py instead of writing new script Role Approach Human User interrupted and said ‘don’t rewrite script, use the existing visualize’ after assistant wrote new visualize_weekly_report.py AI Assistant created a custom visualization script (visualize_weekly_report.py with matplotlib/seaborn) to generate 3 summary charts, without checking if existing scripts could be adapted Difference Analysis: Human preferred reusing battle-tested code (visualize_from_cache.py) over creating new scripts. Assistant optimized for exact specification match over codebase consistency. In this case, both approaches succeeded: the new script generated summary charts (method ranking, heatmap, comparison), existing script generated per-section plots
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Failed to question task assumptions in MIHD scGPT experiment. When asked to ‘get scGPT+UNI2+STAIG ARI’, assistant should have verified whether STAIG fusion actually uses gene encoder embeddings before running expensive 25-minute experiments. Human’s casual observation (’looks similar to PCA’) was the key to discovering architectural insight Desktop Video debugging: assistant rewrote errorLog() to fix concurrency but didn’t explain why NO error logs appeared in the 4438-line file. The race condition caused complete log loss, not just corruption. Should have called this out as ‘critical: errorLog has been silently failing, all historical errors are missing’ General Limitations Multiple tool call failures when attempting direct SSH to GPU nodes (an49/an53), not immediately recognizing that PAM slurm_adopt restrictions required srun-based access pattern. Should have identified the access control pattern after first failure instead of retrying SSH multiple times Initial EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES.md was more narrative/explanatory rather than the structured inventory format the user wanted, requiring explicit plan with table-based specification. Should have asked clarifying questions about documentation purpose (operational reference vs conceptual guide) before writing MIHD visualization: assistant created new script (visualize_weekly_report.py) without exploring existing visualization infrastructure first. Should have used Agent tool to survey all visualize_*.py scripts before deciding to write new code. User had to interrupt and redirect Learnings Key Learnings Foundation model embeddings (scGPT 512d, UNI2 1536d) are NOT drop-in replacements for dimensionality reduction (PCA 50d) in clustering tasks. scGPT optimizes for cross-dataset transfer, not within-dataset variance. Architecture choice (gene-only vs multimodal fusion) matters more than embedding quality: QFormer achieved 2× ARI improvement over scGPT-alone by learning gene-vision alignment. STAIG graph-based fusion completely ignores gene encoder outputs, using raw HVG matrix instead Action chunking in VLA models fundamentally changes the GPU utilization profile: Pi0.5 outputs 50-step action sequences, so a 400-step evaluation trial requires only ~8 inference calls (~20s GPU work) vs 200s total time, resulting in ~10% GPU utilization. The bottleneck shifts from GPU compute to pipeline serialization (CPU preprocessing, WebSocket I/O, simulation steps). Batched evaluation with timer-based request collection could improve throughput macOS sandbox security-scoped bookmarks require both compile-time entitlement declaration (in .entitlements file) AND runtime lifecycle management (startAccessingSecurityScopedResource/stop). Empty entitlements → silent fallback to non-scoped bookmarks → 100% restore failure. Bookmark system has no visible error channel when entitlements are missing Log archaeology (reading full log files) can reveal patterns invisible in code: Desktop Video’s ‘saved non-scoped bookmark’ repeated 20× across sessions exposed the missing entitlements. Concurrent write bugs (errorLog FileHandle race) silently destroy diagnostic information. High-frequency timer logs (879 updateClockLabels calls) obscure real errors → need state-based logging, not tick-based Slurm’s srun with –jobid flag can bypass SSH PAM restrictions (like slurm_adopt) by executing commands directly in the allocated job context, essential for cluster environments with strict node access control. When GPU nodes are running 100% utilization workloads, administrative commands via srun can experience significant latency (60+ seconds) → robust monitoring scripts should handle timeouts gracefully and use lightweight fallback commands (nvidia-smi over nvitop) External dependency documentation should serve operational purposes (structured inventory: paths, sizes, purposes, references) distinct from conceptual documentation (explanatory guides). Clear file inventories with exact paths enable reproducibility and dependency auditing. For VLA benchmark project, comprehensive inventory includes 14 categories: config directories, logs, binaries, environment variables, Python packages, rclone paths, Git repos, Ollama models, conda envs, datasets, checkpoints, bootstrap procedures, and troubleshooting guides Practical Learnings SwiftUI .mask() + .ultraThinMaterial enables ‘frosted glass text’ effect on macOS 12.4+ by masking the material blur to the text shape. The material automatically samples content beneath it in the window’s view hierarchy (video player → material → masked text). Dual-layer approach (material mask + gradient overlay) simulates physical glass with refraction + specular highlights Gaussian mixture model clustering (mclust) scales poorly beyond ~100 dimensions. For foundation model embeddings (512d-1536d), kmeans or spectral methods are necessary for practical runtime. mclust’s EM algorithm becomes computationally intractable on high-dimensional data Conversation Summaries MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics ✅ scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiments, weekly report preparation, and visualization generation 04:12:09.065 | claude_code PI requested scGPT+UNI2+STAIG results for Monday presentation. Investigation revealed STAIG two-stage fusion ignores gene encoder embeddings (uses raw HVG matrix instead). Repaired scGPT cache metadata, re-evaluated gene-only baseline (ARI 0.114→0.170), ran 5 fusion methods (concat/mean/attention/llava_mlp/qformer) across 11 sections. QFormer achieved best scGPT fusion (ARI 0.370). Created visualize_weekly_report.py for 3 summary charts (method ranking bar, section heatmap, PCA vs scGPT comparison). User redirected to use existing visualize_from_cache.py for per-section plots. Generated 36 total visualizations (3 summary + 33 per-section for PCA/scGPT/UNI2 encoders) and documented key architectural finding in weekly report
Desktop Video Wallpaper 🔄 Security-scoped bookmark system fix, liquid glass screensaver redesign, and ongoing debugging 22:35:54.328 | claude_code User reported video files failing to load with ‘Operation not permitted’ error after app restart. Root cause analysis via 4438-line log revealed: 1) Empty entitlements file → bookmark creation always non-scoped, 2) errorLog() concurrent writes lost all error logs, 3) 1550+ spam log entries per session, 4) Double window open on launch. Fixed by adding bookmark.app-scope entitlement, implementing non-scoped fallback parsing, rewriting errorLog to use shared LogFile, removing high-frequency dlogs, fixing launch sequence. Redesigned screensaver clock from card-based to Liquid Glass effect using .mask() + .ultraThinMaterial + specular gradient (text itself is frosted glass). User then requested fully transparent label (not frosted) and reported video switching freeze issue. Started investigation via log analysis and SingleScreenView.swift exploration but session was interrupted before resolution
Error Recovery Benchmark 🔄 Pi0.5 VLA training monitoring, external dependencies inventory, and GPU utilization optimization planning 06:44:17.740 | claude_code User requested comprehensive training status across GPU cluster. Successfully gathered data via Slurm commands (srun –jobid to bypass PAM restrictions), analyzed training logs, and delivered detailed report: 6 Pi0.5 LoRA tasks on an49 (38.3% progress, ~37h remaining, 7/8 GPUs at 100%), 4 tasks on an53 (26.8% progress, ~46h remaining, 7/8 GPUs at 100%), plus evaluation status (Stack tasks 95-100% success, Coffee tasks 5-25%). Created comprehensive external dependencies inventory (docs/external_dependencies_inventory.md) with 14 categories cataloging 65+ GB: configuration directories, AI assistant logs, external binaries, environment variables, Python packages, Google Drive rclone paths, external Git repos, Ollama models, 4 conda envs, 10 HDF5 datasets (~18.4GB), 9 BC-RNN checkpoints, 4 VLA checkpoints (~47GB), bootstrap procedures, and troubleshooting guides. Updated 项目全景总结.md with references. User described GPU utilization bottleneck: ~10% utilization due to action chunking (50-step outputs = only 8 inference calls per 400-step trial, 2.5s per call = 20s GPU / 200s total). Requested optimization plan with web research on Pi0/Pi0.5 inference techniques and batched evaluation implementation, but conversation was interrupted before plan delivery
Token Usage Total: $39.97 — 61.9M tokens
summary: Fixed critical MIHD scGPT benchmark bugs discovering STAIG architectural limitation, resolved Desktop Video macOS sandbox permission system with security-scoped bookmarks, monitored large-scale Pi0.5 VLA training across GPU cluster, created comprehensive external dependencies inventory, and diagnosed GPU utilization bottleneck in evaluation pipeline
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-02

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-01 Daily Overview What was done: Parallel execution across three compute environments: spatial transcriptomics visualization and codebase modernization on HPC (DCC), robot learning model training and cluster infrastructure management on supercomputer (tianhe), and development tooling enhancement plus retrospective documentation on local workstation (MacBook) How it was done: DCC leveraged STHD/STAIG pipelines with dataset compatibility fixes and systematic code refactoring via multi-phase plans; Enhanced MIHD CLAUDE.md: added vision enhancement models (STEGO/BYOL/SCAN/SpatialGAT), HD dataset command examples, test scripts, cache directory clarification Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. VisiumHD dataset paths incompatible with DLPFC-oriented pipeline: image file mismatches, spatial coordinate loading failures, mclust not scaling to HD bin counts (17,502 bins vs DLPFC 4,992 spots) Solution: 5-round debugging: fixed path resolution logic, refactored coordinate loading, implemented mclust→KMeans fallback for large datasets, added tqdm + PYTHONUNBUFFERED for visibility. HD crop10large now runs through complete STAIG pipeline with 360x speedup (2 min vs 40+ min per section)
Key Insight: DLPFC and VisiumHD differ by >3x scale; mclust’s EM algorithm doesn’t scale (40 min for 17,502 bins), KMeans provides acceptable quality tradeoff (ARI ~0.17 vs 0.20-0.25) for exploratory spatial transcriptomics
2. Coffee BC-RNN achieved 0% success rate across all 600 epochs while Stack reached 98% Solution: User diagnosed root cause: all 9 task configs from same template only included robot-centric observations (eef_pos, eef_quat, gripper_qpos), missing 57-dim object state (pod/machine/holder positions). Coffee multi-stage precision operations require object info; Stack simple task can rely on vision. Fixed by adding extra_low_dim=[‘object’] to all tasks, regenerating configs
Key Insight: Observation space deficiencies have vastly different impacts across task complexities. Simple tasks can compensate via vision; complex tasks completely fail. Template-based config generation needs per-task observation customization
3. MIHD refactoring Phase 2 dependency order: pipeline/ modules massively import from run_benchmark.py, preventing direct deletion Solution: Executed Phase 2 first (extract shared functions to models/staig_utils.py, models/vision_extractors.py, models/Fusion.py, utils/clustering.py and update all pipeline/ imports), then Phase 3 (delete run_benchmark.py). Verified via grep for residual references and pipeline dry run
Key Insight: Large refactoring must follow gradual dependency decoupling: migrate depended functions first, then delete source files, avoiding intermediate import failures. Topological ordering is critical
4. Desktop Video PowerSave/PowerSave+ modes used per-screen updatePlayState(for:) logic, causing new window creation to only affect current screen while others continued playing Solution: Modified SharedWallpaperWindowManager.updatePlayState(for:) to delegate .powerSave and .powerSavePlus cases to AppDelegate.shared?.updatePlaybackStateForAllScreens(), ensuring global re-evaluation on window events
Key Insight: Per-screen state methods must explicitly delegate to global evaluators when mode semantics require system-wide decisions; local+global duality requires careful routing
5. JAX crashes with ‘No GPU/TPU found’ when CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" set for CPU-only norm_stats Solution: Added JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu environment variable alongside CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" to force JAX CPU backend initialization
Key Insight: JAX requires explicit backend selection via JAX_PLATFORMS, not just CUDA device masking. CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES alone causes backend initialization errors
6. Pathologist annotation coordinates (μm scale on full slide) needed mapping to 8um bin grid for VisiumHD crop10large comparison Solution: Discovered r_big//4 mapping rule through empirical testing, achieved 100% barcode match (17,502/17,502 bins), validated via spatial overlay inspection
Key Insight: VisiumHD’s multi-resolution grid (2um bins with 4×4 aggregation to 8um) requires integer division coordinate transform; Zenodo metadata lacked explicit mapping documentation
7. Desktop Video Automatic mode lacked battery state awareness, always using PowerSave logic even when macOS was in Low Power Mode Solution: Added NSProcessInfoPowerStateDidChange observer in applicationDidFinishLaunching; implemented powerStateDidChange handler; updated shouldPauseAllVideos() to check ProcessInfo.processInfo.isLowPowerModeEnabled: normal power → allOverlaysCompletelyCovered(), low power → anyOverlayCompletelyCovered()
Key Insight: macOS Low Power Mode queryable via ProcessInfo.isLowPowerModeEnabled and observable via NSProcessInfoPowerStateDidChange; adaptive behaviors benefit from both query + observation
8. GPU VRAM exhausted - all 8 GPUs near full, blocking new training jobs Solution: Identified Phoenix serve_policy.py (PID 2413737) consuming ~407GB across 7 GPUs with 0% utilization (idle server). Killed it plus two other idle VLA servers (PIDs 2046779, 2138978, ~61GB each). Freed GPUs 0-6 completely
Key Insight: Idle model serving processes can monopolize GPU memory for days if not monitored. Regular nvidia-smi checks and process audits prevent resource starvation. Multi-GPU models especially wasteful when idle
9. Training hangs indefinitely on wandb.init() ProxyError (no proxy configured on compute node) Solution: Added WANDB_MODE=disabled to training command environment
Key Insight: HPC compute nodes often lack internet access; disable cloud logging services for batch jobs (W&B, MLflow, Comet) or configure offline mode
10. Gadget merge –sync downloaded all logs and processed as single batch with just a warning about multiple dates, requiring manual iteration for backfilling Solution: Modified cmd_merge() to check if args.date is None when args.sync is True, then delegate to _cmd_merge_sync_all() which groups files by date (YYYY-MM-DD_device.json pattern), processes each date in separate subprocess, skips dates with existing reports, batch deploys at end
Key Insight: Unifying –sync behavior to match –sync-all eliminated confusion and made batch backfilling efficient (eliminated 50+ manual commands for February history)
11. Pi0.5 training jobs 46551 and 46553 both failed/cancelled Solution: Analyzed training logs: both jobs manually cancelled via scancel command. Job 46551 only reached ~579 steps of 100k target. Root cause: user intervention, not system/code error
Key Insight: Manual job cancellation is different from training failure - distinguish between system errors and user-initiated stops when diagnosing ‘failed’ runs
12. Norm_stats path mismatch: script checks checkpoints//norm_stats/ but compute_norm_stats.py writes to assets//benchmark/mimicgen_/norm_stats.json Solution: Updated both skip-check (Step 1) and prerequisite-check (Step 2) to use correct assets/ path structure
Key Insight: Tool output paths don’t always match intuitive checkpoint directory structure; verify actual output locations via dry runs
13. Bash tool completely failed: all commands (echo hello, true) returned exit code 1 with no output Solution: User insisted on retries (’try again’); tool eventually self-recovered. Root cause unknown, possibly sandbox binary or NFS filesystem temporary issue
Key Insight: Tool-layer failures sometimes need simple retry rather than deep diagnosis. Read/Write/Glob tools remained functional during Bash failure, serving as fallback for partial tasks
14. Complex multi-project days (Feb 15, 16, 17, 28) exceeded Claude API 32K output token limit during merge operations Solution: No immediate fix; accepted limitation. Most reports stayed under limit due to natural daily workload distribution across devices
Key Insight: Daily reports for high cross-project activity across multiple devices can hit API output limits. Future mitigation: chunked merge, per-device sections, or hierarchical summarization (day→week→month)
General Issues 15. Training script fails with ‘–overwrite/–no-overwrite: ignored explicit argument False’ parse error Solution: Changed –overwrite=False to –no-overwrite (boolean flag syntax, not key=value)
Key Insight: Python CLI boolean flags often use –flag/–no-flag pattern instead of –flag=value syntax
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Problem diagnostic responsibility division Role Approach Human Coffee BC-RNN 0% SR complete diagnostic report provided by user: root cause analysis (missing object key), why Stack unaffected (simple task vision sufficient), dataset object dimension comparison AI Claude responsible for executing fix: modify code to add config field, batch regenerate files, verify fix results, update documentation. Did not participate in initial problem diagnosis Difference Analysis: Human undertook high-level problem diagnosis and root cause analysis; AI handled execution-layer code modification and batch processing. Division reflects respective strengths: human’s domain knowledge integration vs AI’s batch execution capability
MIHD refactoring Phase sequencing Role Approach Human User explicitly stated ‘Phase 2 must precede Phase 3’ in plan, recognizing pipeline/ modules heavily import from run_benchmark.py, requiring function migration before source deletion AI AI initially created 6 parallel tasks without recognizing Phase 2-3 strong dependency, only executing sequentially during implementation Difference Analysis: Human has global architecture vision, can foresee dependency-driven execution order constraints; AI tends to treat tasks as independent units for parallelization, lacking deep module dependency understanding

GPU resource optimization strategy Role Approach Human Remembered JAX’s memory pre-allocation behavior and proactively asked if increasing to 0.95 would accelerate training AI Explained memory-bound vs compute-bound tradeoffs, batch size implications, and learning rate scaling concerns; recommended caution for mid-run changes Difference Analysis: Human focused on resource maximization; AI emphasized configuration stability and training quality preservation
GPU resource anomaly identification Role Approach Human User immediately spotted 8 GPU memory occupation but 0% utilization anomaly in nvidia-smi output, directly questioned ‘why are you using 8 GPUs now but GPU usage is 0?’ AI Claude initially didn’t proactively focus on utilization, only reported memory occupation. Needed explicit user prompt before deep analysis of norm_stats computation not requiring GPU inference Difference Analysis: N/A
Finalize mechanism design decision Role Approach Human User clearly required merge finalize logic ‘same as export’: only mark when date before today and confirmed no new content will arrive, ensuring idempotency AI AI implemented more granular logic: merge _finalized=True requires both target_date < today AND all source logs finalized, embodying two-phase propagation semantics Difference Analysis: Human provided high-level requirement (‘same as export’), AI refined implementation with stricter condition (all sources finalized), reasonable elaboration not deviation
Merge workflow behavior expectation Role Approach Human User expected –sync flag without –date to automatically process logs day-by-day (mirroring –sync-all behavior), wanting single command to handle entire backlog efficiently AI AI initially implemented –sync to download all logs and process as single batch, only warning about multiple dates without splitting processing Difference Analysis: Human prioritized automation and parallel batch processing (one subprocess per day); AI’s original implementation prioritized simplicity (single batch) at cost of manual iteration. User’s approach better suits backfilling scenarios with 10+ days
Pi0.5 finetuning task execution order Role Approach Human User proactively specified ‘start with coffee_d0 first’ demonstrating gradual validation strategy (verify single-task feasibility before scaling to 9 tasks) AI AI inclined toward parallel submission of all 9 tasks or full planning phase, lacking spontaneous ‘start small then scale’ experimental discipline Difference Analysis: User exhibits engineering pragmatism (de-risk via incremental validation); AI defaults to comprehensive parallelization without considering failure mode containment
Tool failure response strategy Role Approach Human When Bash tool failed, user adopted simple direct strategy: ‘please keep trying’, ’try again’. No root cause pursuit, trusted retry might solve problem AI Claude attempted multiple diagnostics: tested different commands (echo/true/:), tried dangerouslyDisableSandbox, tried different timeout settings, used Read/Write as backup. Inclined toward understanding root cause rather than blind retry Difference Analysis: Human’s pragmatism vs AI’s analytical completeness. In black-box failure scenarios, simple retry may be more efficient than deep diagnosis. But AI’s backup solution exploration (using Write to create directory) also provided value
