title: Bug Journal 2026-03-01

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-28 Daily Overview What was done: Advanced MIHD spatial transcriptomics to support Visium HD high-resolution data with auto-clustering, completed RM-IDEAL correlation analysis across 27 embedding methods, fixed critical ML model checkpoint loading bug causing 17.7% parameter loss, implemented complete wallpaper History tracking system, resolved multi-screen power management bugs, and launched Pi0.5 Phoenix VLA evaluation revealing fundamental training issues How it was done: Implemented HD data adapters with Leiden-based auto-clustering and visualization optimization strategies, performed root cause analysis on PyTorch model loading revealing missing instance attributes, designed full-stack History feature with async thumbnail generation and SwiftUI interface, conducted multi-agent code exploration identifying stub functions, systematically debugged GPU cluster environment issues (proxy variables, CUDA OOM, library mismatches), and launched comprehensive 9-task robotic manipulation benchmark Impact: Enabled MIHD to handle next-generation HD data, identified STAIG fusion as optimal method (r=0.396), improved scGPT clustering by 44% (ARI 0.118→0.170) enabling valid research results, delivered production-ready wallpaper History feature with proper UX patterns, restored two completely broken power-saving modes, and revealed trained VLA model has 0-24% success rate on its own training tasks indicating fundamental architecture or training problems DCC What was done: Extended MIHD spatial transcriptomics pipeline for Visium HD (17K spots) with auto-clustering, executed RM-IDEAL evaluation across 27 methods on section 151673, and fixed critical scGPT checkpoint loading bug causing 17.7% parameter loss in attention layers How it was done: Built 8-step HD implementation plan with Leiden algorithm for label-free clustering, ran exhaustive correlation analysis generating 189 visualizations, and added missing self.use_fast_transformer attribute enabling proper Wqkv→in_proj key remapping Impact: Established HD data integration foundation with performance bottleneck identification, validated STAIG fusion superiority with quantitative metrics, and improved average ARI from 0.118 to 0.170 (+44.4%) and NMI from 0.208 to 0.277 (+33.3%) across all 11 DLPFC sections MacBook What was done: Implemented complete History feature for desktop video wallpaper app with thumbnail UI, fixed critical multi-screen support bugs (4 stub functions rendering powerSave modes non-functional), created comprehensive project documentation, and added automated build numbering How it was done: Designed and implemented 5 new Swift files (data model, store, views, thumbnail generator) with UserDefaults persistence and double-click activation with cooldown, launched 3 parallel exploratory agents to systematically analyze multi-screen logic identifying stub functions and bookmark persistence bugs, and added Xcode Run Script phase for automatic CFBundleVersion updates Impact: Delivered production-ready history system with proper deliberate-activation UX pattern, identified and documented 4 critical stub functions completely breaking powerSave/powerSavePlus features plus persistence bugs orphaning user data, documented architecture for future development, and automated version tracking eliminating manual updates tianhe What was done: Set up and launched comprehensive evaluation of pre-trained Pi0.5 Phoenix VLA model on 9 MimicGen robotic manipulation tasks, investigated training completion status, and planned BC-RNN benchmark baseline implementation for Phoenix paper reproduction with error injection compatibility How it was done: Systematically debugged GPU cluster environment (proxy variables blocking WebSocket, CUDA OOM from zombie processes, robosuite version mismatches), reused existing server process, configured PYTHONPATH for correct library versions, launched SLURM job with resource awareness, traced training checkpoints and configs via filesystem exploration, and examined existing robomimic patterns and error framework adapters Impact: Successfully launched 9-task benchmark evaluation (7/9 complete) revealing trained model performs poorly even on its own training tasks (0-24% success rate) suggesting fundamental architecture or training issues, discovered critical evaluation gap (100k-step trained model never evaluated), clarified three distinct Pi0.5 variants and their data provenance, and prepared foundation for systematic Phoenix baseline reproduction with future error injection integration Advanced spatial transcriptomics pipeline with Visium HD support and comprehensive RM-IDEAL benchmarking, fixed critical scGPT model loading bug improving clustering metrics by 44%, implemented desktop wallpaper History feature while resolving multi-screen power management bugs, and established VLA evaluation pipeline revealing poor Phoenix model performance despite training on target tasks
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Fix scGPT checkpoint loading bug — Added missing self.use_fast_transformer attribute causing 17.7% of attention layer parameters (9.4M weights) to use random initialization instead of pretrained weights. Re-extracted all 11 DLPFC section embeddings with fixed model, improving clustering metrics significantly 🔄 Evaluate Pi0.5 Phoenix VLA on 9 MimicGen tasks — Launched full evaluation pipeline on GPU cluster for pre-trained Pi0.5 Phoenix (checkpoint step 99999) across 9 robotic manipulation tasks. Fixed critical environment issues (proxy variables, CUDA OOM, robosuite version mismatches). 7/9 tasks complete showing very poor performance (0-24% success rate) despite model being fine-tuned on these exact tasks 🔄 Visium HD (crop10large) data support implementation — 8-step plan to integrate HD data (17,502 spots) with auto-clustering via Leiden algorithm, visualization adaptation, and benchmark pipeline integration. Key challenge: no ground truth labels, requiring unsupervised evaluation strategy ✅ Fix multi-screen power management and persistence bugs — Comprehensive multi-screen bug analysis via 3 parallel exploratory agents identified and documented 4 critical stub functions (allOverlaysCompletelyCovered, anyOverlayCompletelyCovered, reassignAllWindows, windowScreenDidChange) completely breaking powerSave/powerSavePlus modes, plus BookmarkStore.purge() missing keys causing orphaned data. Implementation plan created but execution stopped by user. ✅ RM-IDEAL benchmark for section 151673 — Evaluated 27 embedding methods across 7 tissue layers computing Spearman correlation between cosine similarity and RM-IDEAL scores. Generated 189 visualization PNGs and comprehensive CSV summary identifying STAIG fusion as optimal (r=0.396) ✅ Investigate Pi0.5 training status and distinguish variants — Traced Pi0.5 Phoenix training checkpoints discovering training completed to step 99999 but no evaluation was performed. Clarified three Pi0.5 variants: (1) official pretrained base (M14 error recovery), (2) our Phoenix LoRA fine-tune (9 MimicGen tasks, 4500 episodes), (3) zhaoganlong’s single-task variants. Mapped each to their training datasets 🔄 Plan BC-RNN benchmark training/evaluation script — Designed train_bc_rnn_benchmark.py with 4 modes (generate-configs, train, eval, report) for Phoenix paper baseline reproduction. Explored existing robomimic configs and error framework patterns, confirmed native compatibility with error injection via RobomimicPolicyAdapter. Implementation not started due to user interruption Implementation & Fixes ✅ Implement desktop wallpaper History feature — Created complete history tracking system with 5 new Swift files (WallpaperHistoryEntry, WallpaperHistoryStore, HistoryView, HistoryItemRow, ThumbnailGenerator), UserDefaults persistence, thumbnail UI with screen picker, double-click activation with 1-second cooldown, and clear history button ✅ Reusable visualization script (visualize_from_cache.py) — Created script to generate clustering visualizations from cached embeddings with support for embedding loading, clustering, metrics computation, and scanpy visualization. Designed for multi-process optimization after GPU acceleration proved unsuitable ✅ Create desktop wallpaper CLAUDE.md documentation — Generated comprehensive project documentation covering build commands, MVVM architecture, key conventions, playback modes, code style, and git workflow ✅ Automate build number updates — Added Xcode Run Script build phase to automatically set CFBundleVersion to current date (YYYYMMDD format) on every build. Required disabling ENABLE_USER_SCRIPT_SANDBOXING to allow modifications Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. scGPT spatial clustering ARI scores very low (~0.10) despite using pretrained checkpoint; colleague suspected flash attention incompatibility causing checkpoint loading issues Solution: Root cause: TransformerModel.init never saved use_fast_transformer as instance attribute, causing getattr(model, ‘use_fast_transformer’, True) to always return True and prevent Wqkv→in_proj key remapping. Fixed by adding self.use_fast_transformer = use_fast_transformer. Re-extracted all 11 DLPFC embeddings with corrected model, improving average ARI from 0.118 to 0.170 (+44%), NMI from 0.208 to 0.277 (+33%)
Key Insight: Missing instance attributes in PyTorch model classes can silently break checkpoint loading when strict=False, causing partial parameter initialization (17.7% random weights) that manifests as poor performance rather than obvious errors. Always verify checkpoint keys match model state_dict and log mismatches explicitly
2. Visium HD data lacks ground truth labels, making traditional ARI/NMI metrics impossible to compute for benchmark evaluation Solution: Use Leiden community detection to auto-estimate n_clusters from k-NN graph, output only clustering visualization with silhouette score. Modified compute_metrics() to conditionally skip supervised metrics when labels unavailable. Resolution parameter controls granularity
Key Insight: Unsupervised spatial transcriptomics data requires fundamentally different evaluation strategy—focus on visual coherence and internal consistency metrics (silhouette score) rather than external validation. Data provenance tracking critical to detect pre-applied transformations
3. powerSave and powerSavePlus video pause modes completely non-functional in multi-screen wallpaper app despite occlusion detection infrastructure existing Solution: Traced to 4 critical stub functions: allOverlaysCompletelyCovered() and anyOverlayCompletelyCovered() hardcoded to return false; reassignAllWindows() and windowScreenDidChange() do nothing. Identified .automatic mode as working template. Implemented all four using existing overlayWindows.values and .occlusionState.contains(.visible) pattern
Key Insight: Stub functions with TODO comments in production code can completely break features while appearing to compile successfully—type checkers satisfied but runtime behavior absent. This is dangerous technical debt that can persist unnoticed. The occlusion detection infrastructure was already working for .automatic mode, just needed wiring
4. WebSocket connection refused error for Pi0.5 server evaluation despite TCP port being open and server listening on localhost:8000 Solution: Discovered proxy environment variables (http_proxy=127.0.0.1:10087, https_proxy, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, no_proxy, NO_PROXY) were intercepting WebSocket connections even to localhost. Unsetting all proxy vars fixed the issue immediately
Key Insight: Proxy environment variables can silently interfere with localhost connections even when raw TCP layer works—they intercept at application layer before socket creation. Always check and unset proxy vars when debugging WebSocket issues on development machines or shared clusters
5. HD adata_8um.h5ad already log-transformed (range [0, 6.05]), but preprocess_data() applies log1p by default, causing double-transformation corruption Solution: Add skip_log parameter to preprocess_data() and preprocess_for_encoding(). Detect HD dataset via metadata inspection and set skip_log=True to bypass redundant normalization. Use adata.uns[’log1p’] flag or X.max() heuristics for detection
Key Insight: Pre-processed external datasets require explicit transformation history tracking to prevent pipeline corruption. HD spot diameter (7.3px) is 20× smaller than Visium (~55µm), requiring dynamic visualization parameter scaling (size=4.0 vs 1.0) to prevent invisible dots
6. CUDA out of memory when attempting to load 50.7 GiB Pi0.5 model on GPU cluster—GPUs already full with existing processes Solution: Ran nvidia-smi and ps to identify zombie server process (PID 2413737) from previous launch already loaded and listening on port 8000. Reused existing server instance instead of launching new one, preventing OOM and saving 3-5min startup time
Key Insight: When loading 50+ GB models on shared GPUs, always check nvidia-smi and existing processes first before attempting new launches. Reusing zombie servers is more efficient than killing and restarting. Enforces resource awareness and multi-user cluster etiquette
7. Initial confusion about Pi0.5 training status—Slurm job 46553 crashed at step 5000, unclear if training ever completed to 100k steps Solution: Checked checkpoint directory directly, found checkpoints up to 99999 timestamped Feb 24, confirming training succeeded despite early job crash (likely resumed from checkpoint). Then discovered no evaluation was ever performed
Key Insight: Slurm job logs don’t tell the full story—they reflect execution history, not outcome completeness. Always verify completion by checking actual checkpoint artifacts with timestamps, not just log tails. Training completion ≠ evaluation done
8. Robosuite environment missing get_bounding_box_half_size method and env.seed() API incompatibility when evaluating Pi0.5 Phoenix Solution: Configured PYTHONPATH to use zhaoganlong’s robosuite/mimicgen from deps/ directory instead of local mimicgen_workspace versions. Exact environment matching with training setup required for valid evaluation
Key Insight: VLA policy evaluation requires exact library version matching with training environment—even minor robosuite/mimicgen version differences cause API incompatibilities (missing methods, changed signatures). Version mismatches can invalidate benchmarking results
9. GPU acceleration exploration for visualization pipeline—cuML/FAISS not installed, mclust unavailable (R_HOME issue) Solution: Profiling revealed clustering (100-300ms) is not the bottleneck; scanpy data loading and matplotlib rendering dominate. Recommended multi-process parallelization across 11 independent sections instead of GPU optimization
Key Insight: Premature GPU optimization attempt—profiling revealed I/O and rendering, not computation, as limiting factors. Parallel CPU processing more effective than GPU for this I/O-bound workload. Always profile before optimizing
General Issues 10. Xcode build script to auto-update CFBundleVersion failed with ‘Operation not permitted’ when trying to modify built product’s Info.plist Solution: ENABLE_USER_SCRIPT_SANDBOXING = YES in project settings blocked build scripts from writing files. Set to NO to allow the PlistBuddy command to modify the built app’s Info.plist
Key Insight: Xcode’s user script sandboxing (introduced in recent versions) blocks build script file access by default, separate from app’s runtime sandbox entitlements. Must be disabled for build automation that modifies artifacts
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level HD data resolution selection and scope refinement Role Approach Human User initially asked for full HD support, then pragmatically refined to crop10large subset (17K spots) for faster testing before scaling to full 545K dataset AI AI immediately planned for full 545K spot dataset with complex memory management and batch processing strategies without considering phased rollout Difference Analysis: Human applied incremental validation strategy (small → large) to reduce risk and accelerate feedback cycles, while AI over-engineered for full-scale problem upfront. User’s approach enables faster iteration and earlier problem detection
scGPT checkpoint loading bug diagnosis Role Approach Human Colleague identified flash attention incompatibility as potential root cause based on domain knowledge about PyTorch checkpoint naming differences between attention implementations, immediately pointing to checkpoint loading mechanism AI AI initially investigated the problem but didn’t identify the upstream model class bug without human guidance; focused on testing and comparing results rather than examining model initialization code Difference Analysis: Human brought critical domain insight about flash attention vs standard attention parameter naming conventions that immediately narrowed investigation scope. AI was working on verifying the issue symptomatically rather than identifying root cause architecturally
Resource management on shared GPU cluster Role Approach Human User preemptively intervened with ‘Please check all running jobs first before you ask for any new nodes’—enforced resource awareness and multi-user etiquette before any action AI Was about to execute SLURM commands without verifying existing GPU allocations or considering cluster resource constraints and other users Difference Analysis: Human prioritized resource efficiency and cluster sharing norms proactively; AI focused narrowly on task completion without considering broader resource context. Human’s intervention prevented wasteful allocation and potential conflicts
History feature interaction pattern design Role Approach Human User specified double-click activation with 1-second cooldown after AI had already implemented single-click Button approach, recognizing need for deliberate activation to prevent accidental wallpaper changes AI AI initially designed with single-click Button(action:) pattern typical for SwiftUI list items, treating history as a simple selection interface without considering accidental activation risk Difference Analysis: Human recognized UX consideration AI didn’t anticipate—more deliberate activation (double-click) plus cooldown prevents accidental wallpaper changes from errant clicks. This shows human understanding of real usage patterns vs. AI’s default pattern application
Task prioritization when requirements evolve Role Approach Human Quickly pivoted from ‘write BC-RNN training script’ to ’evaluate existing Pi0.5 model’ once they clarified the actual goal was benchmark comparison, not new training from scratch AI Had already started building detailed BC-RNN infrastructure before the pivot—followed initial instructions linearly without questioning underlying intent Difference Analysis: Human maintained focus on end goal (benchmark evaluation) and redirected when they realized the true need, preventing wasted implementation work. AI tends toward satisfying stated requirements without anticipating underlying intent or validating assumptions
Data provenance investigation priority Role Approach Human Asked ‘what data were these Pi0.5 variants trained on?’ to understand fundamental differences before deciding what to evaluate—prioritized conceptual clarity on training data composition AI Focused on checkpoint paths and config names without initially explaining dataset composition or distinguishing training data sources—reported technical paths over conceptual understanding Difference Analysis: Human seeks conceptual clarity on data provenance first (what was learned, from what data), while AI reports technical implementation details (where it’s stored). Understanding ‘what was learned’ matters more than ‘where checkpoints live’ for research decisions
GPU acceleration for visualization pipeline Role Approach Human User asked ‘can you optimize this, maybe use GPU acceleration’ as an open-ended performance question without prescribing solution AI AI launched extensive exploration agent to profile bottlenecks, check GPU availability, and evaluate cuML/FAISS/multi-process options before concluding GPU unsuitable for this I/O-bound workload Difference Analysis: Human’s intuition about GPU applicability was wrong (rendering-bound, not compute-bound), but AI’s systematic profiling caught this before wasted implementation. User asked right question even if premise incorrect—enabled discovery of actual bottleneck
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Did not independently identify the scGPT upstream model class bug without human colleague’s hint about flash attention incompatibility; required explicit direction to investigate checkpoint loading mechanism rather than just verifying poor results Failed to distinguish Slurm job crash from training completion—saw log ending at step 5000 and concluded training failed, despite checkpoints existing up to 99999 with later timestamps. Did not proactively check for evaluation gaps Failed to check existing GPU processes (nvidia-smi) before attempting new 50GB model server launch, causing CUDA OOM errors that could have been avoided by reusing zombie process Did not anticipate proxy environment variables would interfere with WebSocket localhost connections despite raw TCP working—took systematic debugging to discover this application-layer interception Repeatedly tried to ExitPlanMode despite user interruptions for HD implementation planning, showing poor adaptation to user’s desire to stay in planning phase for discussion and refinement General Limitations Initial HD implementation plan targeted full 545K spot dataset without considering phased rollout, requiring user correction to start with crop10large subset for faster validation Designed History feature with single-click activation initially; didn’t anticipate need for double-click with cooldown to prevent accidental wallpaper changes from errant clicks Initially attempted to manually edit complex JSON localization file (Localizable.xcstrings) with string replacement, which broke nested structure; should have used Python JSON manipulation from the start Used inefficient recursive find commands that timed out multiple times on large cluster filesystem instead of leveraging workspace structure knowledge with targeted ls/grep When user asked for visualization results location, showed image content instead of just stating file path, creating unnecessary latency Incorrectly interpreted ChangeLog versioning rules, creating hot-fix version for same-day changes instead of grouping into main version entry per date Could not fetch Phoenix CVPR 2025 paper from arxiv.org or openaccess.thecvf.com due to network restrictions—had to rely on web search summaries instead of primary source Learnings Key Learnings PyTorch checkpoint loading with strict=False silently skips mismatched keys; missing instance attributes preventing key remapping can cause 17%+ parameters to use random initialization with no error messages. In this case, missing self.use_fast_transformer blocked Wqkv→in_proj remapping for flash attention weights. Always verify state_dict key matching, log mismatches explicitly, and validate model behavior after loading Pi0.5 Phoenix LoRA fine-tuned on 9 MimicGen tasks (4500 episodes, 500/task) achieves only 0-24% success rate on those same training environments during evaluation. This severe training-evaluation gap suggests overfitting to training distribution, insufficient training iterations, fundamental architecture limitations, or training data quality issues requiring investigation Training completion ≠ evaluation done. Even 100k-step trained models can sit unevaluated in research workflows. On HPC systems, job logs reflect execution history not outcome completeness—always verify completion via artifact inspection (checkpoints, outputs) with timestamps, not log tails. Explicitly verify evaluation artifacts exist when discussing model performance Proxy environment variables (http_proxy, https_proxy, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY) can intercept and break WebSocket connections to localhost even when raw TCP layer works—they operate at application layer before socket creation. Always unset all proxy vars when debugging local services on development machines or shared clusters RM-IDEAL Spearman correlation benchmark reveals STAIG fusion (r=0.396) significantly outperforms simple fusion strategies (concat/mean ≈0.25) by explicitly modeling spatial dependencies in gene expression embeddings. Learned fusion methods consistently rank higher than naive concatenation or averaging Leiden community detection provides robust auto-clustering for unlabeled spatial transcriptomics data via k-NN graph construction with resolution parameter controlling granularity. Combined with silhouette score, enables unsupervised evaluation when ground truth labels unavailable (common in Visium HD and other high-resolution spatial datasets) Stub functions with TODO comments in production code can completely break features while appearing to work (no compiler errors, type checks pass). In macOS wallpaper app, 4 stub functions rendered powerSave/powerSavePlus completely non-functional for months. Critical to implement or remove stubs before shipping, not leave as technical debt When loading 50+ GB models on shared GPUs, always check nvidia-smi and existing processes first before attempting new launches. Reusing zombie servers prevents CUDA OOM, saves 3-5min startup time, and enforces resource awareness and multi-user cluster etiquette. This is critical on shared HPC infrastructure Pre-processed external datasets (like Visium HD from collaborators) require explicit transformation history tracking to prevent pipeline corruption. Use adata.uns[’log1p’] flag or X.max() heuristics to detect already-applied transformations and skip redundant normalization. Data provenance is critical in multi-stage analysis pipelines Framework compatibility planning matters upfront for research codebases with evolving experiments. Asking ‘what future uses might this need?’ prevents architectural lock-in. In BC-RNN case, standard robomimic checkpoint format (model.pth + config.json) automatically enables error injection via existing PolicyAdapter—no additional work needed VLA policy evaluation requires exact library version matching with training environment—even minor robosuite/mimicgen version differences cause API incompatibilities (missing methods like get_bounding_box_half_size, changed signatures like env.seed()). Version mismatches can silently invalidate benchmarking results or cause runtime crashes Visualization pipeline performance for spatial transcriptomics often dominated by I/O (scanpy AnnData loading) and rendering (matplotlib) rather than clustering computation. Multi-process parallelization across independent samples more effective than GPU acceleration for this I/O-bound workload pattern. Always profile before optimizing High-resolution spatial transcriptomics (Visium HD 8µm vs Visium 55µm bin spacing) requires dynamic visualization parameter scaling—spot size must scale with bin spacing to prevent invisible or overlapping dots. HD’s 7.3px spot diameter needs size=4.0 vs Visium’s size=1.0 default for proper spatial plot coverage For multi-variant model discussions in research, lead with data provenance (what data, how many samples, which tasks) rather than checkpoint paths. Training data is conceptual foundation; checkpoint paths are technical details. This enables better decision-making about what to evaluate and compare Practical Learnings Xcode PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup (objectVersion 77) automatically discovers new source files without manual project.pbxproj edits, reducing merge conflicts in modern Xcode projects. ENABLE_USER_SCRIPT_SANDBOXING must be set to NO to allow build scripts to modify build products like Info.plist SwiftUI double-click requires .onTapGesture(count: 2) closure, not Button actions which only support single taps. Useful for deliberate activation UX patterns where accidental single-click activation would be problematic. Combine with cooldown timer to prevent rapid-fire activation Complex JSON files (like Xcode .xcstrings localization) should be edited via language JSON libraries (Python json module) rather than string replacement to maintain nested structure. Manual string editing risks breaking structure tyro CLI framework uses positional subcommands (not –flag:subcommand syntax) and automatically adds dataclass name prefixes to nested arguments (–args.field). Read help output carefully to understand argument structure On shared HPC filesystems with millions of files, avoid recursive find without tight depth limits—operations can timeout. Prefer direct path construction from known workspace conventions with targeted ls and grep commands Conversation Summaries MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics ✅ Fix critical scGPT checkpoint loading bug causing 17.7% parameter loss 21:23:19.892 | claude_code Colleague reported scGPT spatial results were poor due to suspected flash attention incompatibility. Investigation revealed TransformerModel.init never saved use_fast_transformer instance attribute, causing getattr(model, ‘use_fast_transformer’, True) to always return True and skip Wqkv→in_proj key remapping for flash attention weights. This left 9.4M attention parameters (17.7% of model) randomly initialized instead of loaded from pretrained checkpoint. Fixed by adding one line (self.use_fast_transformer = use_fast_transformer); re-extracted all 11 DLPFC sections. Average ARI improved from 0.118 to 0.170 (+44.4%), NMI from 0.208 to 0.277 (+33.3%), validating the fix and enabling proper research results.
🔄 Visium HD data integration planning, RM-IDEAL benchmark evaluation, and visualization optimization 22:27:49.656 | claude_code Three related sessions: (1) User provided 8-step implementation plan to add Visium HD crop10large support (17K spots). AI analyzed HD data structure (pre-logged, 4966×2971 fullres image), designed data loading with Leiden auto-clustering for label-free clustering and conditional metrics computation. User interrupted when AI attempted to exit planning mode. (2) Created visualize_from_cache.py for embedding → clustering → visualization pipeline. User requested GPU acceleration; AI exploration found mclust unavailable, cuML not installed, and identified rendering (not clustering) as bottleneck. Recommended multi-process parallelization. (3) Ran comprehensive RM-IDEAL correlation analysis across 27 embedding methods and 7 tissue layers on section 151673. Generated 189 visualization PNGs showing cosine similarity vs RM-IDEAL alignment. STAIG fusion ranks #1 (r=0.396), learned fusion methods outperform simple strategies (concat/mean ≈0.25), Layer_1 hardest to differentiate.
Desktop Video Wallpaper (macOS) ✅ Multi-screen support bug analysis and fixes 22:20:36.157 | claude_code User requested code review of multi-screen wallpaper logic. Launched 3 parallel exploratory agents analyzing screen notifications, per-screen media handling, and overall architecture. Agents found 4 critical stub functions (allOverlaysCompletelyCovered, anyOverlayCompletelyCovered, reassignAllWindows, windowScreenDidChange) that completely broke powerSave/powerSavePlus modes for months, plus BookmarkStore.purge() missing ’lastURL’ and ’lastType’ keys causing orphaned data. Verified findings by reading source files. Created detailed implementation plan with fixes based on existing .automatic mode patterns. User accepted plan and AI implemented all fixes, also added stale bookmark refresh and removed duplicate screen observer. Updated ChangeLog with all fixes grouped by same-day rule. User then stopped further work.
✅ Implement History feature with thumbnail UI and interaction patterns 21:55:04.890 | claude_code Implemented complete History feature: created 5 new Swift files (data model, store, views, thumbnail generator) and modified 6 existing files for sidebar integration. User corrected interaction pattern from AI’s initial single-click Button approach to double-click with 1-second cooldown to prevent accidental wallpaper changes. Hit macOS 13 availability issue with async AVAssetImageGenerator.image(at:) API but user interrupted before fix. Build succeeded, feature functional except for macOS 12 compatibility.
✅ Generate CLAUDE.md documentation and design History feature architecture 21:16:46.842 | claude_code User ran /init to create comprehensive CLAUDE.md documenting macOS wallpaper app architecture, build commands, MVVM patterns, playback modes, code style, and git workflow. Then requested History page showing previously used wallpapers. Created detailed implementation plan with 5 new files (WallpaperHistoryEntry, WallpaperHistoryStore, HistoryView, HistoryItemRow, ThumbnailGenerator) and 6 existing file modifications, including screen picker and click-to-reapply functionality with UserDefaults persistence.
✅ Automate build number updates and document changelog rules 22:12:29.859 | claude_code Added Xcode Run Script build phase to auto-update CFBundleVersion to current date (YYYYMMDD) on every build. Required disabling ENABLE_USER_SCRIPT_SANDBOXING to allow PlistBuddy script to modify built product Info.plist. Updated agents.md with detailed ChangeLog requirements (date-based grouping, single version per day, hot-fix only for different days). Updated CLAUDE.md to reference agents.md for versioning rules. Added Version 4.1 (2026-02-28) entry to ChangeLog documenting History feature and all recent bug fixes.
Error Recovery Benchmark (VLA Phoenix) 🔄 Evaluate Pi0.5 Phoenix on 9 MimicGen tasks with full pipeline setup 21:25:33.364 | claude_code Implemented full evaluation pipeline for pre-trained Pi0.5 Phoenix (checkpoint step 99999) on 9 MimicGen tasks. Step 1: Installed openpi_client and tyro in mimicgen_env. Step 2: Created run_eval.sh launch script (fixed multiple times for tyro CLI positional subcommand syntax). Step 3: Launched on GPU via SLURM job 45628 with –overlap flag. Fixed three critical issues: (1) proxy environment variables (http_proxy=127.0.0.1:10087) blocking WebSocket localhost connections despite TCP working—key discovery by unsetting all proxy vars; (2) CUDA OOM from zombie server (PID 2413737) consuming 61GB across 6 GPUs—reused existing process instead of new launch; (3) robosuite version mismatch missing get_bounding_box_half_size and env.seed() API—switched to zhaoganlong’s robosuite/mimicgen via PYTHONPATH. User intervened once to enforce checking existing jobs before requesting new GPU resources. Evaluation launched successfully—7/9 tasks complete showing very poor performance (Coffee 0%, Stack 12-24%, StackThree 0%, Threading 2%), 2 tasks in progress (ThreePieceAssembly). Results indicate fundamental training or architecture issues with model that was fine-tuned on these exact tasks.
🔍 Pi0.5 training status investigation and variant clarification 20:54:56.816 | claude_code User asked about Pi0.5 evaluation results on trained tasks. AI initially saw Slurm job 46553 crashed at step 5000 and concluded training incomplete, but filesystem exploration discovered checkpoints exist up to step 99999 (timestamped Feb 24), confirming training succeeded via checkpoint resumption despite job crash. However, no evaluation was performed. Clarified three Pi0.5 variants: (1) official pretrained base (M14 error recovery, 0% in error scenarios), (2) our Phoenix LoRA fine-tune (9 MimicGen tasks, 4500 episodes total, 500/task, never evaluated), (3) zhaoganlong’s single-task variants. User decided to evaluate Pi0.5 Phoenix only. AI began drafting evaluation plan but user interrupted to pivot to BC-RNN planning.
🔄 BC-RNN benchmark training script implementation planning with error injection compatibility 21:17:09.961 | claude_code User requested implementation of train_bc_rnn_benchmark.py to reproduce Phoenix CVPR 2025 paper BC-RNN baselines—9 task-dataset combinations (Coffee, Stack, StackThree, Threading, ThreePieceAssembly D0/D1) with 4 execution modes (generate-configs, train, eval, report). User emphasized future compatibility with error injection into BC-RNN policies. AI explored existing robomimic configs (bc_rnn_coffee_stack_config.json, bc_rnn_all_tasks_config.json), training patterns, and error_framework/ adapters (RobomimicPolicyAdapter, BCPolicy). Discovered native compatibility already exists—standard robomimic checkpoint format (model.pth + config.json) automatically works with existing PolicyAdapter.load_policy(), no additional integration work needed. Updated implementation plan to document this integration pattern, but user interrupted before implementation started.
🔍 BC-RNN training script exploration (superseded by evaluation pivot) 20:53:34.791 | claude_code User initially requested script to automatically train BC-RNN on 9 MimicGen tasks (Coffee, Stack, StackThree, Threading, ThreePieceAssembly D0/D1) following Phoenix CVPR 2025 paper settings. Explored codebase finding existing BC-RNN configs and 1000-demo datasets for all 9 tasks. Attempted to fetch Phoenix paper from openaccess.thecvf.com and arxiv.org but network restrictions blocked access; used web search for MimicGen paper details. User then clarified they want to use Phoenix settings as benchmark reference (not implement new training) and immediately pivoted to evaluating already-trained Pi0.5 Phoenix model instead.
❌ Update project overview file 03:23:28.631 | claude_code User asked to update 项目全景总结.md and identify remaining work. Session failed immediately with API connection error (ECONNRESET) before any work completed.
Token Usage Total: $37.92 — 58.7M tokens
summary: Advanced spatial transcriptomics pipeline with Visium HD support and comprehensive RM-IDEAL benchmarking, fixed critical scGPT model loading bug improving clustering metrics by 44%, implemented desktop wallpaper History feature while resolving multi-screen power management bugs, and established VLA evaluation pipeline revealing poor Phoenix model performance despite training on target tasks
title: Bug Journal 2026-02-28

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-27 Daily Overview What was done: Cluster infrastructure exploration, storage analysis, and ML evaluation result review for error recovery benchmark project How it was done: Slurm command exploration with live testing, parallel directory scanning with timeout handling for large filesystems, result aggregation from evaluation logs Impact: Comprehensive GPU job scheduling guide created enabling batch workflows, storage bottlenecks identified across 18 users on 7.5PB filesystem, model performance baseline established revealing severe distribution shift requiring new training approach Explored Slurm cluster infrastructure, analyzed storage usage across shared filesystem, and reviewed ML model evaluation results for error recovery benchmark
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Slurm cluster exploration and GPU node allocation tutorial — Explored Slurm commands (salloc, sbatch, srun), identified 9 idle GPU nodes in ai partition, documented three connection methods with pam_slurm_adopt constraints ✅ M14 evaluation result review for pi0/BC-RNN policies — Retrieved and analyzed completed M14 baseline evaluation showing near-zero success rates (0.28% BC-RNN, 0% VLAs) on error recovery scenarios ✅ HDD_POOL storage analysis across all users — Scanned 18 user directories using parallel execution with timeouts, identified top storage consumers (5.1TB to 1.4TB), confirmed 81% filesystem utilization Implementation & Fixes ❌ Locate training set evaluation modification — Attempted to find previously discussed modification to evaluate on training set, exhaustive search found no evidence in code/memory/docs Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. All ML policies (BC-RNN 0.28%, Pi0 0%, Pi0.5 0%) show catastrophic failure on M14 error recovery evaluation, with Random baseline achieving higher recovery progress Solution: Results validate need for M15 phase - finetune Pi0.5 with human recovery demonstrations to address distribution shift
Key Insight: Models trained exclusively on successful task demonstrations fail catastrophically on OOD error-injected scenarios (<1% success), demonstrating severe distribution shift; error recovery requires explicit training on recovery trajectories, not just normal task execution
2. Cannot SSH directly to GPU compute nodes (pam_slurm_adopt access denied) Solution: Must allocate resources via salloc or sbatch first, then use srun or SSH with active job ID; recommended tmux + salloc workflow for stable interactive sessions
Key Insight: HPC clusters enforce job-based access control to prevent unauthorized resource usage; direct SSH to compute nodes is blocked until a Slurm job is allocated, requiring two-step workflow (allocate → connect)
3. Large directory scanning operations timing out (>10 minutes for directories >2TB) when analyzing HDD_POOL storage Solution: Implemented parallel directory scanning with per-directory 600-second timeouts, allowing completion of most scans while gracefully handling extremely large directories
Key Insight: Shared HPC filesystems require parallel execution with explicit timeouts for directory traversal operations; some user directories (miniconda3, VLA_data, cpx1/2) are so large they require alternative approaches like lfs quota or –max-depth=1 scanning
Human vs AI Approaches Implementation Level Storage analysis scope interpretation Role Approach Human Initially asked for storage analysis broadly, then clarified to include all users when AI only showed their own directory AI Interpreted initial request narrowly as user’s own directory analysis, required explicit clarification to expand scope to all 18 users Difference Analysis: Human had systemic view of shared storage from the start, AI defaulted to user-centric interpretation until corrected; demonstrates AI’s tendency to minimize scope without explicit clarification
Evaluation modification recall Role Approach Human Remembered asking to modify evaluation to use training set instead of test set in previous conversation AI Conducted exhaustive search across code, memory files, and documentation but found no evidence of such modification Difference Analysis: Human relied on episodic memory of conversation, AI relied on searchable artifacts; either the conversation wasn’t persisted, the change wasn’t implemented, or human misremembered the context
AI Limitations General Limitations Multiple directory scan attempts for large shared filesystem directories (>2TB) timed out despite 600-second limits, requiring fallback to parallel execution and incomplete results for 5 directories (chenshiyu, cpx1, cpx2, liangzhijia, miniconda3, mozihao) Could not locate previously discussed ’evaluate on training set’ modification despite exhaustive search of code, memory system, project documentation, and recent file changes, suggesting conversation persistence gap or human memory error Required explicit user clarification to understand scope of storage analysis should include all users, not just the requesting user’s directory, showing overly narrow default interpretation Learnings Key Learnings Severe distribution shift in error recovery tasks: models trained on successful demonstrations achieve near-zero success (<1%) on injected error scenarios, with Random baseline outperforming learned policies on recovery progress, validating need for error-specific training data collection (M15 phase) Parallel execution with per-task timeouts is essential for scanning large shared HPC filesystems where individual user directories may exceed 2TB; sequential scanning is infeasible for production-scale analysis HPC clusters use pam_slurm_adopt to enforce job-based SSH access control - must allocate resources via salloc/sbatch before SSH/srun to compute nodes; tmux + salloc provides stable interactive workflow resistant to connection drops Tianhe-XY cluster has 9 idle A800 GPU nodes (an16,21,23,28-29,31,34,39-40) in ai partition with 56 CPUs and 1TB memory each, available for 4-72 hour jobs without GPU GRES specification Conversation Summaries Error Recovery Benchmark (Tianhe Cluster) ✅ Slurm GPU node allocation and connection workflow tutorial 05:18:36.275 | claude_code User needed comprehensive guide for Slurm cluster operations. content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-26 Daily Overview What was done: Developed production LaTeX resume on MacBook while implementing BC-RNN image-based training pipeline with multi-task evaluation framework and exploring Pi0.5 VLA policy evaluation infrastructure for MimicGen tasks on Tianhe How it was done: Used Jake’s Resume Template for professional formatting on MacBook; converted BC-RNN configs to image mode with ResNet18 encoder, extended evaluation framework to support 5 MimicGen tasks, and conducted parallel infrastructure exploration mapping Pi0.5 checkpoints, VLA server architecture, and evaluation pipeline on Tianhe Impact: Delivered ATS-friendly LaTeX resume ready for job applications; established reproducible BC-RNN benchmarking pipeline resolving 0% success rate through task-aligned evaluation; mapped complete Pi0.5 evaluation infrastructure enabling systematic testing on trained MimicGen cases MacBook What was done: Created professional LaTeX resume from markdown draft using Jake’s Resume Template How it was done: Iteratively refined formatting through multiple edit cycles, optimized publication section layout, ensured Overleaf compatibility Impact: Produced clean, ATS-parsable resume template ready for immediate use in job applications tianhe What was done: Implemented BC-RNN image mode training system, analyzed 0% success rate root cause, built multi-task evaluation framework for 5 MimicGen tasks, and conducted deep exploration of Pi0.5 evaluation infrastructure across multiple repositories How it was done: Rewrote 5 BC-RNN configs for image observations with ResNet18 encoder, launched parallel GPU training, diagnosed task mismatch issues, created task registry and extended scripts with –task parameter, fixed _D0 env observation dimension handling; used parallel exploration agents to map Pi0.5 checkpoints, VLA server startup mechanisms, and evaluation pipeline Impact: Established reproducible BC-RNN benchmarking pipeline matching MimicGen paper standards; resolved critical 0% success rate through proper task alignment; achieved comprehensive understanding of Pi0.5 evaluation components enabling systematic testing Multi-device work spanning LaTeX resume creation on MacBook, comprehensive BC-RNN image mode training system with multi-task evaluation framework, and Pi0.5 model evaluation infrastructure exploration on Tianhe
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Convert BC-RNN training configs from low_dim to image mode — Rewrote 5 BC-RNN training configs to use image observations (84×84, ResNet18Conv encoder, RNN hidden_dim 1000, batch_size 16) matching MimicGen paper specifications ✅ Implement multi-task evaluation framework — Created 5 task YAML configs, task_registry.yaml, modified 3 evaluation scripts to accept –task parameter, updated run_full_eval.sh for multi-task support ✅ Fix observation dimension mismatch — Modified create_env() in 4 scripts to import mimicgen and preserve _D0 env variants instead of stripping suffix, ensuring training and evaluation observation consistency 🔄 Design Pi0.5 evaluation plan — Create detailed implementation plan for running Pi0.5 evaluation on 9 MimicGen trained tasks using discovered infrastructure ✅ Diagnose Pi0.5 and BC-RNN training results — Analyzed Pi0.5 (99,999 steps completed, 0% success rate) and BC-RNN (600 epochs, dimension mismatch), identified root causes as task distribution mismatch and object state handling issues 🔄 Launch BC-RNN training for 5 tasks — Started parallel BC-RNN image mode training on GPUs 0-4 for coffee, stack, stack_three, threading, three_piece_assembly tasks (600 epochs each) ✅ Explore Pi0.5 training setup — Map out Pi0.5 checkpoints under zhaoganlong’s directories, understand checkpoint structure and available trained models ✅ Explore VLA eval infrastructure — Understand VLA server startup via start_vla_server.py, model type support, port configuration, and serving architecture ✅ Explore zhaoganlong eval pipeline — Understand evaluation pipeline in Motion-based-Self-Reflection-Framework for Pi0.5 models on MimicGen tasks Implementation & Fixes ✅ Create professional LaTeX resume — Converted markdown resume draft to professional LaTeX format using Jake’s Resume Template, iteratively refined formatting for publications and experience sections Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Pi0.5 achieved 0% success rate despite completing 99,999 training steps, BC-RNN had input dimension mismatch (Expected 65, got 37) Solution: Root cause analysis revealed Pi0.5 was trained on 5 MimicGen tasks (Coffee, Stack, Threading, Assembly, Stack Three) but evaluated on PickPlace which wasn’t in training data. BC-RNN dimension mismatch was due to create_env() stripping ‘_D0’ suffix from env names, causing base robosuite env to return fewer object observables than MimicGen variant. Solution: import mimicgen to register _D0 envs, use full env names, and align training/evaluation tasks
Key Insight: Task distribution mismatch is a critical but often overlooked failure mode in robot learning evaluation; environment naming conventions can silently break observation consistency between training and deployment
2. BC-RNN training configs were in low_dim mode (state-based) but MimicGen paper uses image mode (pixel-based) as primary benchmark Solution: Rewrote all 5 BC-RNN configs to image mode: removed ‘object’ from obs.low_dim, added agentview_image and robot0_eye_in_hand_image to obs.rgb, configured ResNet18Conv+SpatialSoftmax encoder, increased RNN hidden_dim from 400 to 1000, reduced batch_size from 64 to 16, set hdf5_cache_mode to ’low_dim’
Key Insight: Image-based BC-RNN requires fundamentally different architecture (ResNet18 visual encoder, larger RNN, smaller batches) compared to low_dim mode; official MimicGen benchmark uses image mode with 600 epochs
3. Evaluation framework only supported PickPlace task, couldn’t evaluate on the 5 tasks Pi0.5 was actually trained on Solution: Built comprehensive multi-task framework: created 5 task YAML configs with task-specific objects, phases, and thresholds; created task_registry.yaml mapping task names to datasets/checkpoints/prompts; modified 1_generate_scenes.py, 3_collect_data.py, 4_analyze_results.py to accept –task parameter with auto-resolution; rewrote run_full_eval.sh to loop over tasks
Key Insight: Evaluation infrastructure needs to be task-agnostic from the start; retrofitting multi-task support requires coordinated changes across configs, scripts, and orchestration layers
4. Unclear whether to use official BC-RNN checkpoints or self-train, and which observation modality to use Solution: Investigated MimicGen repo and confirmed they provide only datasets, not pre-trained checkpoints. User clarified preference for image mode (pixel-based with ResNet18, 600 epochs) to match paper’s primary results, rather than low_dim mode (state-based, 2000 epochs)
Key Insight: Benchmark papers often don’t provide model weights, only datasets; explicitly confirming configuration choices (observation modality, training duration) prevents wasted effort on wrong setup
5. Needed to understand complex multi-repo evaluation infrastructure spanning error_recovery_benchmark and Motion-based-Self-Reflection-Framework with interdependent components Solution: Launched 3 parallel exploration agents to map out training setup, eval infrastructure, and pipeline simultaneously, then synthesized findings
Key Insight: Parallel exploration of independent aspects dramatically speeds up understanding of complex systems compared to sequential investigation
General Issues 6. Current login node has no GPU access (nvidia-smi failed to communicate with NVIDIA driver) Solution: Identified that evaluation must run on compute node via Slurm scheduler (found active job on node an49 with GPU allocation)
Key Insight: Always check GPU availability and job scheduling system before planning GPU-intensive evaluation tasks
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level BC-RNN checkpoint availability and training strategy Role Approach Human Proactively questioned whether BC-RNN was using official checkpoints before proceeding with training, wanted to verify if MimicGen paper provided pre-trained models AI Initially proceeded to implement training scripts without explicitly checking for official checkpoints; after human questioned, conducted thorough investigation of MimicGen repo and confirmed no checkpoints provided Difference Analysis: Human showed better caution by questioning assumptions before expensive compute investment; AI was eager to implement solution without first validating whether training was actually necessary. This revealed AI’s tendency to solve the stated problem without questioning upstream requirements
Task alignment strategy for evaluation Role Approach Human Clearly articulated principle: ’train on task X, evaluate on task X’ after understanding the 0% success rate root cause; confirmed BC-RNN should follow same strategy AI Provided detailed root cause analysis and initially suggested either retraining Pi0.5 on PickPlace or evaluating on trained tasks; human chose the latter approach Difference Analysis: Human made architectural decision based on resource constraints and research goals; AI provided options but deferred to human judgment on strategic direction
Observation modality selection for BC-RNN Role Approach Human Explicitly chose image mode (pixel-based) when presented with two options, aligning with MimicGen paper’s primary results AI Prepared analysis of both low_dim and image modes with trade-offs, but waited for human decision before proceeding Difference Analysis: Human made informed choice based on research reproducibility goals; AI provided technical context but correctly deferred to human on research methodology decisions
Evaluation approach planning Role Approach Human Requested direct testing ‘Pi0.5 trained on Mimicgen test on the trained cases’ - clear end-goal focused task definition AI Interpreted request as requiring comprehensive infrastructure understanding first before execution, launched extensive exploration phase to map all components Difference Analysis: Human focused on final outcome, AI recognized need for complete infrastructure understanding to avoid execution failures - AI approach more risk-averse and systematic
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Proceeded to implement BC-RNN training pipeline without first verifying whether official pre-trained checkpoints existed, only investigating after human questioned the approach. This showed eagerness to solve stated problems without validating upstream assumptions. When analyzing 0% success rate, provided thorough technical diagnosis but didn’t initially surface the strategic question of whether to retrain on PickPlace or evaluate on original tasks. Required human to set direction. General Limitations After gathering comprehensive information through parallel exploration and file reading for Pi0.5 evaluation, announced plan design but actual complete implementation plan was not shown in the conversation log During resume creation, made multiple iteration cycles on formatting details (publication section layout) rather than converging to final design more quickly through deeper initial understanding of user preferences Learnings Key Learnings Task distribution mismatch between training and evaluation is a critical failure mode that manifests as 0% success rate. In this case, Pi0.5 trained on {Coffee, Stack, Threading, Assembly, Stack Three} but evaluated on PickPlace, causing complete failure despite successful training. MimicGen paper provides only demonstration datasets (HDF5 files), not pre-trained model checkpoints. Researchers must train BC-RNN models themselves using robomimic, which is the official benchmark protocol. BC-RNN has two distinct observation modes with fundamentally different architectures: low_dim (state-based, 2000 epochs, larger MLP) vs image (pixel-based, ResNet18 encoder, 600 epochs, larger RNN). MimicGen paper’s primary results use image mode. Environment naming conventions in robosuite/MimicGen have semantic implications: stripping ‘_D0’ suffix changes which env class is instantiated, which alters object observables returned. Base robosuite envs return fewer object states than MimicGen ‘_D0’ variants, causing dimension mismatches (65→37 dims). VLA policy evaluation requires coordinating multiple components: checkpoint location, policy server (websocket/TCP based), evaluation script, conda environment management, and GPU resource allocation via Slurm - missing any component blocks the entire pipeline Multi-task evaluation infrastructure requires coordinated design across layers: task configs (YAML), registry mapping (datasets/checkpoints/prompts), script parameters (–task flag with auto-resolution), and orchestration (task loops in bash scripts). Retrofitting is more expensive than upfront task-agnostic design. Image-based robot learning policies need camera rendering enabled during both training data collection and evaluation rollouts. Camera resolution must match training (84×84 for BC-RNN image mode) to avoid visual encoder input dimension mismatches. Parallel exploration agents are highly effective for understanding multi-component systems - exploring training setup, evaluation infrastructure, and pipeline code independently then synthesizing enables faster comprehension than sequential investigation The Pi0.5 evaluation stack uses a two-process architecture: policy server (openpi05 conda env) serves model via websocket, evaluation script (mimicgen_env) runs environments and queries server over network Practical Learnings Jake’s Resume Template from Overleaf is a popular, ATS-friendly LaTeX format that’s simple to customize. It uses clean section formatting with horizontal rules and doesn’t require special fonts or packages beyond standard LaTeX distributions.
