content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-19 Daily Overview What was done: Research and development across two major projects: (1) MIHD spatial transcriptomics - comprehensive documentation of evaluation methods, vision encoder analysis, and image-only clustering enhancement design with literature survey of 25+ papers; (2) Error Recovery Benchmark - Phase II infrastructure (M14 baseline evaluation, M12/M13 planning) and strategic documentation optimization How it was done: Multi-method approach combining systematic literature research, codebase exploration via parallel agents, incremental code implementation with resume support, strategic planning with iterative user clarification, documentation refactoring, and cross-domain solution analysis Impact: MIHD: established complete knowledge base for spatial transcriptomics evaluation, discovered fundamental vision-only limitations (morphology vs biology clustering), designed 7th enhancement goal with 5-phase implementation roadmap; Error Recovery: M14 baseline ready (5448 episodes, crash-resilient), comprehensive Phase II roadmap (v4.9→v4.10), documentation optimized 51% while preserving all 87 checklist items, human teleoperation strategy chosen for high-quality recovery data DCC What was done: MIHD project work: created 4 technical documents (RM-IDEAL metric, vision encoder guide, literature survey, UNI2 evaluation analysis), enhanced visualization with H&E panels, conducted comprehensive image-only clustering literature survey covering 25+ papers, designed Idea 7 enhancement plan with 6 candidate solutions How it was done: Deep codebase analysis via Read/Grep tools, extensive literature research covering spEMO/STAIG/HEST-1k/UNI/STEGO/SCAN/BYOL/F-SEG papers, modified create_clustering_visualization() for 1×3 layout with H&E reference, batch regenerated 11 DLPFC visualizations, cross-domain analogy analysis (FGVC, medical imaging, remote sensing) Impact: Built complete reference documentation for spatial transcriptomics evaluation; identified why UNI2 features fail (ARI 0.11-0.16 vs 0.5+ for multimodal) - clusters by morphological similarity not biological domains; produced actionable 5-phase implementation roadmap with risk mitigation; updated ENHANCEMENT_PLAN_CN.md with 500-line Idea 7 TzJsDesktop What was done: Fixed VS Code Remote-SSH connection failures to HPC cluster (tianhe/an49/an47) on Windows How it was done: Diagnosed ControlMaster Unix domain socket incompatibility with Windows OpenSSH; commented out ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist in ~/.ssh/config; cleared stale socket files Impact: Restored SSH connectivity to tianhe and an49; enabled remote development on HPC cluster tianhe What was done: Error Recovery Benchmark Phase II: implemented M14 baseline infrastructure (resume support, multi-run merge, per-error-type analysis), designed M12 large-scale dataset construction and M13 taxonomy validation plans, conducted VLA model research survey, optimized project documentation from 1554→763 lines (51% reduction), designed comprehensive Phase II research strategy with human teleoperation approach How it was done: Added resume support to collector.py and extended analysis scripts with multi-run merge + per-error-type breakdown; created 7 Makefile targets; researched 17 VLA/foundation models via web search; used chunked reading for large files; deployed parallel Explore agents for codebase statistics; iterative Q&A to clarify recovery training data concept; migrated VLM tutorial to separate file Impact: M14 ready to run: 454 scenes × 4 policies × 3 seeds = 5448 episodes, crash-resilient with automatic resume; comprehensive VLA research output enables model selection; documentation now maintainable with all 87 checklist items preserved; clear Phase II roadmap defines path from infrastructure to paper-ready experiments (G1-G4: dataset scale, classification validation, baseline evaluation, FM proof-of-usefulness); human teleoperation strategy chosen for high-quality recovery demonstrations Advanced two major research projects: comprehensive MIHD spatial transcriptomics documentation and vision encoder analysis revealing fundamental limitations, plus Error Recovery Benchmark Phase II infrastructure completion with strategic roadmap design
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ MIHD comprehensive evaluation documentation and literature survey — Created complete documentation suite: RM-IDEAL scoring metric (graph-kernel WWL), vision encoder pipeline guide (patch extraction, alignment, caching, fusion), pathology foundation models survey (spEMO/STAIG/HEST-1k/UNI/UNI2), image-only clustering methods (STEGO/SCAN/BYOL/F-SEG), analyzed 34 UNI clinical tasks and 8 UNI2 benchmarks, identified that vision-only spatial domain evaluation is largely missing in literature ✅ MIHD Enhancement Plan Update: Idea 7 Image-Only Clustering — Designed 5-phase implementation plan with 6 candidate solutions (STEGO feature refinement, SCAN neighbor mining, BYOL test-time training, DINOv2-GAT graph attention, TTT-MAE masked reconstruction, SFDA frozen encoder + adapter), added dependency graph, risk analysis, and verification commands to ENHANCEMENT_PLAN_CN.md (~500 lines) ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark M14 baseline infrastructure implementation — Added resume support to collector.py (reads episodes.jsonl, rebuilds state, skips completed episodes), extended 3_collect_data.py with –resume/–scene_offset/–scene_limit, rewrote 4_analyze_results.py for multi-run merge + per-error-type breakdown + summary.json with Fisher exact tests, created 7 Makefile targets for 454-scene × 4-policy baseline evaluation 🔄 Error Recovery Benchmark M12: Large-scale dataset construction planning — Designed 13-step plan covering MimicGen augmentation (10→200 demos), BC-RNN/Pi0/Pi0.5 natural error capture, stratified train/test splitting, adjusted detector timing parameters. All code written (6 files changed) but execution hit MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID error. 🔄 Error Recovery Benchmark M13: Error taxonomy validation design — Designed three-pillar validation approach: (1) FMEA mapping to ISO 15066/10218/IEC 61508 standards, (2) completeness proof through 6×3 phase×family matrix, (3) statistical validation using Cohen’s/Fleiss’ kappa for inter-classifier reliability. Plan ready but not implemented. ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark Phase II strategic planning documentation — Comprehensive updates to 項目全景總結.md (v4.9→v4.10): split goals into Phase I (completed G1-G3) and Phase II (new G4-G7), added detailed M12-M15 sub-goals, created 5-phase execution timeline (Day 1-16), updated risk analysis, then optimized from 1554→763 lines (51% reduction) while preserving all 87 checklist items; migrated VLM tutorial to separate file; updated statistics to 53 files/~14,300 lines ✅ VLA & Foundation Models research survey — Created comprehensive 200-line survey covering 8 VLA models with error recovery capabilities (SC-VLA, CycleVLA, VLA-SCT, FailSafe, FPC-VLA, RoboFAC, VLAC, Self-Improving VLA) and 9 foundation models (π0, π0-FAST, π0.5, OpenVLA, Octo, GR00T, HPT, RT-2). Includes architecture details, open-source status, usage instructions, fine-tuning commands, and integration recommendations. Implementation & Fixes ✅ MIHD visualization enhancement with H&E reference panels — Modified create_clustering_visualization() from 1×2 to 1×3 layout adding H&E image panel for direct morphology comparison; batch regenerated visualizations for all 11 DLPFC sections (151508-151675) using cached embeddings; fixed 151510 missing lowres image via symlink ✅ Fix Windows SSH connection to HPC cluster — Resolved VS Code Remote-SSH failures caused by ControlMaster/Unix domain socket incompatibility on Windows OpenSSH; successfully connected to tianhe and an49 ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark CLAUDE.md documentation improvement — Improved project documentation from 205→163 lines: fixed error type count (17→24) and validator count (2→4) inaccuracies, consolidated redundant architecture sections, trimmed external project description from 48→18 lines, added test fixtures documentation and data format references Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. How to prove Error Recovery Benchmark is useful beyond ‘can generate scenes’ - need evidence that data actually improves models for thesis contribution Solution: Design G4 (dataset usefulness proof) as 4-step experiment: (1) convert 2000+ scenes to LeRobot format with state+image observations, (2) LoRA fine-tune π0.5 on error scenes for 2-4 hours on A800, (3) evaluate on held-out test split with stratified sampling, (4) measure improvement (expect 15-30% SR gain, 10-20% RP gain, statistical significance via bootstrap CI and Wilcoxon test)
Key Insight: Benchmark usefulness requires closed-loop proof: error data → model training → measurable performance improvement on unseen errors. This is the core thesis contribution beyond scene generation infrastructure - the ‘4-pillar validation’ that distinguishes publishable benchmarks from toy datasets.
2. UNI2 foundation model features achieve only ARI 0.11-0.16 on DLPFC spatial domain clustering (vs 0.5+ for gene+image multimodal), with brown repeated patches appearing uniformly across clustering results Solution: Identified 5 root causes: (1) training data domain gap (cancer-heavy, brain-rare), (2) pretraining task mismatch (coarse tissue classification vs fine-grained layer detection), (3) DLPFC layer differences are subtle (cell density/size, not color/texture), (4) feature redundancy with gene expression, (5) lack of spatial context. Analysis revealed UNI2 clusters by morphological similarity (blood vessels across all layers) rather than biological domains. Proposed 6 adaptation methods (STEGO, SCAN, BYOL, DINOv2-GAT, TTT-MAE, SFDA) following solution hierarchy: domain-specific pretraining > test-time adaptation > feature refinement > task-specific fine-tuning. Added H&E reference panel to visualization to confirm hypothesis.
Key Insight: Pure vision encoders cluster by morphological features not biological function - this is a classic fine-grained recognition problem seen across CV domains (bird subspecies, rock types, cell morphology). Low ARI reflects structural grouping (what looks similar) not functional grouping (what is biologically similar). General foundation models learn coarse semantics; domain-specific fine-tuning or feature refinement is necessary for fine-grained tasks.
3. Error Recovery Benchmark dataset phase imbalance - 90% scenes in pre_reach/reach, transport/place completely empty (0 scenes) Solution: Design strategy using BC-RNN (60-70% SR) as key to reaching later phases, lower detector min_step thresholds from 100→5-50 to capture lift/transport failures, use MimicGen to generate 200+ demo variants for increased trajectory diversity and later-phase trigger opportunities
Key Insight: Phase coverage requires policies with sufficient success rate to reach late phases (BC-RNN sweet spot) combined with adjusted detector timing - random policy fails too early (lift never reached), Pi0 has 0% SR (grasp never reached), only BC-RNN reaches transport/place with failures. This is a ‘reachability problem’ determined by success rate distribution.
4. How to rigorously validate that 24-type error taxonomy is principled, complete, and non-redundant for academic publication Solution: Three-pillar validation: (1) map 8 categories to FMEA failure modes from ISO 15066/10218/IEC 61508 industrial safety standards (provides theoretical grounding), (2) prove completeness via 6 phase × 3 family = 18-cell matrix showing no blind spots, (3) statistical validation using Cohen’s/Fleiss’ kappa (target: within-category κ>0.6, cross-category κ<0.3) to verify types are distinct
Key Insight: Academic taxonomy validation requires both theoretical grounding (industry standards provide FMEA-based rationale) and empirical evidence (statistical independence tests prove non-redundancy) - cannot rely on intuition alone for 24 types
5. M14 baseline evaluation (5448 episodes, 5-15h runtime) risks losing all progress if VLA server crashes mid-run Solution: Implemented resume support in collector.py: on startup, reads existing episodes.jsonl, builds a set of completed (scene_id, policy_name, seed) tuples, skips them in the triple loop, and rebuilds all_episodes dict to preserve summary correctness. Added –resume flag to 3_collect_data.py. Now crash-safe with automatic resume.
Key Insight: For long-running data collection (>1h), append-only JSONL logging + resume logic is essential. The key is idempotency: reading back the JSONL and rebuilding the summary dict ensures final results are identical whether run completes in one shot or across multiple resume attempts.
6. User didn’t understand ‘recovery training data’ concept in Phase II plan, blocking plan approval Solution: Provided concrete example comparing normal demo (reset→success) vs recovery trajectory (error_state→adjustment→success); explained it teaches model ‘how to fix mistakes’ not just ‘how to do it right from start’. User then chose human teleoperation for high-quality data despite time cost.
Key Insight: Technical jargon must be grounded in examples before implementation discussion; user prioritized data quality and real recovery demonstrations over automated proxies, willing to invest manual effort for authentic data
7. VS Code Remote-SSH connection to HPC cluster fails with ‘client_loop: send disconnect: Bad file descriptor’ and ’tried to write to a nonexistent pipe’ Solution: Windows OpenSSH does not support Unix domain sockets used by ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist. Commented out these 3 options in ~/.ssh/config and removed stale socket files from ~/.ssh/sockets/. Connection restored immediately.
Key Insight: SSH connection multiplexing (ControlMaster) is a Unix-only feature; Windows OpenSSH silently fails when these options are present, causing cryptic low-level errors that look like bugs rather than feature unavailability.
General Issues 8. Project summary document too large (1554 lines, 33k tokens) to read in single Read tool call, making optimization difficult Solution: Used chunked reading with offset and limit parameters to read document in 400-line segments; gathered statistics via Explore agent; migrated large VLM tutorial section (331 lines) to separate file; reduced to 763 lines (51% reduction) while preserving all 87 checklist items
Key Insight: Large files require strategic reading: chunked access with specific offsets for full document edits, or targeted Grep searches for specific content. Documentation refactoring with ‘complete preservation’ requirement needs verification step: count all checklist items before and after to prove nothing lost.
9. MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID assertion failed when running MimicGen with CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 Solution: EGL device ID must match GPU index in CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES, not physical GPU ID - when using CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3, set MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID=3 (or set to 0 if using single GPU via CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3)
Key Insight: EGL device ID is relative to visible devices list, not absolute GPU hardware index - robosuite’s EGL assertion enforces this to prevent render target mismatches
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Strategic planning workflow: understanding-first vs plan-first Role Approach Human Explicitly requested to update strategic documentation (項目全景總結.md) FIRST before any code implementation; interrupted plan generation multiple times to ask ‘什么叫获取恢复训练数据?’ ensuring conceptual clarity before approving detailed implementation; wanted step-by-step understanding with ability to redirect AI Immediately jumped to implementation planning and code generation, treating documentation update as afterthought; attempted to build complete detailed plan first, then present for approval; tried to ExitPlanMode before user fully understood concepts; used technical jargon without verifying understanding Difference Analysis: Human practices risk management through understanding-first workflow (explain concept → user decides direction → then build detailed plan) and documentation-driven development for complex projects, ensuring shared understanding before execution. AI optimizes for immediate code delivery and complete plans over controllability and alignment. For thesis work with advisor feedback loops, human approach reduces rework risk.
Scope control and preservation priorities in refactoring Role Approach Human Explicitly required ‘complete preservation of all big/medium/small goal checklists’ when optimizing 1554-line document; insisted on preserving ALL goals when AI proposed 60% reduction; interrupted premature web research; chose human teleoperation for recovery data despite time cost, prioritizing quality over automation AI Initially proposed aggressive consolidation to eliminate redundancy (1554→600-700 lines); launched web search for benchmark patterns before establishing concrete current-state plan; presented automation options (oracle recovery as ‘recommended’) Difference Analysis: Human prioritized roadmap integrity as non-negotiable and completeness of actionable checklists over document brevity, recognizing checklists serve as progress tracking tools (must be exhaustive) while explanatory text should follow DRY. Human valued quality and real demonstrations over automated proxies. AI focused on size reduction metrics and premature external research before grounding in codebase state.
Recognizing task equivalence between supervised classification and unsupervised clustering Role Approach Human User immediately recognized ’task1不就是clustering吗?’ - saw that CRC-100K 9-class tissue classification and MIHD spatial domain identification are the same underlying problem (assigning tissue types), just evaluated differently AI Initially described CRC-100K as ROI classification task without noting conceptual connection to spatial domain clustering; focused on methodological differences (linear probe vs KMeans) without seeing structural equivalence Difference Analysis: Human recognized abstract similarity across evaluation paradigms while AI got caught in implementation details; demonstrates human strength in pattern recognition across different framings of same core problem
Enhancement plan scope negotiation for MIHD Role Approach Human User insisted on preserving ALL big goals, mid goals, and small goal checklists when optimizing project summary, even though AI proposed ~60% reduction by consolidating redundant descriptions; resolution was to keep all goal checklists intact but still consolidate narrative (~50% reduction to ~750 lines) AI AI proposed consolidating redundant concepts described 2-4 times across version sections (VLA architecture from §3.4 + §8(v4.4) + §11, natural error capture, error classification) Difference Analysis: This reflects tension between completeness vs brevity: checklists serve as progress tracking (must be exhaustive), explanatory text should follow DRY. User’s correction prevented accidental loss of tracking granularity.
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Persisted in attempting ExitPlanMode after multiple user rejections (Sessions 2, 5, 8 on same day) without recognizing the pattern - should have learned after 2-3 rejections that user prefers staying in planning phase for longer iteration. Tried to exit before user had fully understood key concepts (recovery training data), requiring user to interrupt and ask clarifying questions. When searching for image-only ablation studies in spatial transcriptomics papers, struggled to find specific ARI numbers because most papers do NOT report image-only baselines. Kept searching for 10+ queries before realizing the gap exists because the community considers gene expression primary and image auxiliary. Difficulty distinguishing ‘finding is absent due to poor search’ vs ‘finding is absent because no one has published it’. Also initially missed that MILWRM is gene-expression-based, not image-only, wasting 2-3 iterations. Used technical jargon (‘recovery training data’, ‘oracle recovery’, ‘LeRobot format’) without verifying user understanding; only explained when explicitly asked. Strategic planning questions (data collection method, task scope) came late in planning process instead of upfront, causing potential rework. Launched premature web research for benchmark paper patterns before establishing concrete current-state plan; user interrupted as premature. Generated very long plans (13 steps for M12, entire Phase II at once) without checking if user wanted incremental breakdown. Assumed bigger/more complete plans are better without considering execution constraints or advisor feedback cycles. Failed to recognize CRC-100K tissue classification is same task as spatial domain identification until user pointed it out - missed conceptual equivalence between supervised evaluation (with labels) and unsupervised evaluation (without labels) of embedding quality for same underlying grouping problem General Limitations File modification tracking errors: attempted to edit 項目全景總結.md multiple times but got ‘file has been modified since read’ errors, requiring re-reads. AI caches file state on read and fails if file is externally modified before edit is applied. Workaround: always re-read immediately before editing. Duplicated CLAUDE.md improvement work in Session 8 without recognizing it had already been completed ~4 hours earlier the same day (Session 1) - lacked within-day memory of completed work Learnings Key Learnings Pure vision encoders cluster by morphological similarity not biological domains: UNI2 groups blood vessels across different cortical layers because they look similar, explaining why vision-only spatial domain ARI is limited (0.11-0.23 vs 0.5+ multimodal). HEST-1k shows pathology foundation models significantly outperform ImageNet baseline (UNI2 Pearson r=0.414 vs ResNet50 r=0.326), but comprehensive vision-only spatial domain clustering benchmarks largely don’t exist - most papers only evaluate multi-modal fusion, not isolated vision encoder performance. Need gene expression to distinguish functional regions with similar visual appearance. Error recovery benchmarks require proving usefulness through 4 dimensions (4-pillar validation): (1) large-scale diverse data (2000+ scenes), (2) theoretically-grounded taxonomy (FMEA/ISO standards), (3) baseline performance characterization (4+ policies), (4) demonstration of training improvement on foundation models (15-30% SR gain). This distinguishes publishable benchmarks from toy datasets - the core thesis contribution is closed-loop proof: error data → model training → measurable improvement. Cross-domain literature search is extremely valuable for identifying solution patterns. The image-only clustering problem in spatial transcriptomics is nearly identical to problems in: (1) fine-grained bird classification (subspecies differ only in beak shape/feather pattern), (2) remote sensing (ImageNet features fail on satellite images), (3) microscopy (material microstructures), (4) geology (rock thin sections). All domains converged on the same 4-tier solution hierarchy: domain-specific pretraining > test-time adaptation > feature refinement > task-specific fine-tuning. This suggests deep learning failure modes are universal across modalities. Phase imbalance in error scene generation stems from success rate distribution creating a ‘reachability problem’ - need policies with 60-70% SR (BC-RNN sweet spot) to reach late phases while still producing failures. Random policy fails too early (lift never reached), Pi0 has 0% SR (grasp never reached), only BC-RNN reaches transport/place with failures. Solution requires BC-RNN + adjusted detector timing (min_step thresholds 100→5-50) + MimicGen trajectory diversity (10→200 demos). When user says ‘I need to know what/why/how’, they want understanding-first workflow: explain concept → user decides direction → then build detailed plan. Not: build plan → explain afterward. Strategic planning questions (data collection method, task scope) should be asked before building detailed implementation plan, not after - saves rework. Documentation-driven development for complex multi-milestone projects ensures shared understanding and alignment before execution, critical for research with advisor feedback loops. Evaluation paradigm fundamentally changes what is measured: same task (tissue type identification) evaluated as supervised classification (with labels, tests linear separability via KNN) vs unsupervised clustering (no labels, tests natural cluster structure via ARI) measures different aspects of embedding quality - high KNN accuracy doesn’t guarantee good clustering ARI. This explains why CRC-100K 9-class classification success doesn’t predict DLPFC spatial domain clustering performance. BYOL (Bootstrap Your Own Latent) solves SimCLR’s key weakness: need for large batches and negative samples. BYOL uses teacher-student framework with EMA updates + predictor head to prevent collapse, making it robust to small batches (256 vs 4096+), augmentation choices, and domain shift. This explains why STAIG chose BYOL over SimCLR for histology patches (where similar-looking patches from adjacent layers should NOT be pushed apart as negatives). The key architectural insight: predictor head asymmetry (online has it, target doesn’t) + EMA target prevents trivial solutions. FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) from industrial safety standards (ISO 15066 collaborative robots, ISO 10218 robot safety, IEC 61508 functional safety) provides rigorous theoretical grounding for robot error taxonomies - maps 8 categories to standardized failure modes like ’loss of grip’, ‘uncontrolled release’, ‘unexpected contact’. Academic taxonomy validation requires both theoretical grounding (industry standards) and empirical evidence (Cohen’s/Fleiss’ kappa: within-category κ>0.6, cross-category κ<0.3). VLA model landscape for error recovery: 8 models claim recovery capabilities but only 2 are open-source (RoboFAC, VLAC). FailSafe and VLA-SCT are most practical for integration (training-free, plug-in architecture, already validated on π0/OpenVLA). Recovery methods fall into 3 categories: dual-system (fast/slow), proactive (subtask backtracking), reactive (supervisor-guided correction). MimicGen can amplify 10 demos to 200+ variants for trajectory diversity, requires has_offscreen_renderer=True for VLA training. Resume logic for long-running data collection must rebuild summary state from JSONL, not just skip episodes. The naive approach (only skip completed episodes) breaks final summary statistics. The correct approach: (1) read JSONL on startup, (2) rebuild all_episodes dict, (3) add completed tuples to skip set, (4) continue collection. This ensures idempotency: running A→crash→resume→B produces identical results to running A+B in one shot. Practical Learnings Large file reading strategy: use limit parameter for initial exploration, then targeted offset ranges or Grep for specific content; avoid reading entire file unless necessary. For files >30k tokens, chunked reading with 400-line segments works well. Documentation refactoring with ‘complete preservation’ requirement needs verification: count all checklist items before and after to prove nothing lost. Windows OpenSSH does not support Unix domain sockets (ControlMaster/ControlPath), causing silent failures with cryptic error messages (‘Bad file descriptor’, ’nonexistent pipe’). The solution is not to switch SSH clients, but to disable connection multiplexing options. This is a reminder that cross-platform compatibility issues often manifest as low-level errors that look like bugs rather than feature unavailability. Conversation Summaries MIHD - Multi-modal Histology Integration ✅ Vision encoder evaluation research, documentation, and image-only clustering enhancement design 14:20:09.677 | claude_code Created comprehensive documentation for RM-IDEAL metric (graph-kernel spatial niche matching), vision encoder pipeline (patch extraction, alignment, caching, fusion strategies), and pathology foundation models (UNI/UNI2/GigaPath/spEMO/STAIG/HEST-1k). Conducted extensive literature survey of image-only clustering methods (STEGO/SCAN/BYOL/F-SEG), analyzing why vision-only spatial domain evaluation is largely missing in literature. Discovered UNI2 clusters morphological features (blood vessels) rather than biological domains explaining low ARI (0.11-0.16), identified 5 root causes (domain gap, task mismatch, subtle morphology, feature redundancy, no spatial context). Enhanced visualization pipeline with H&E reference panels for all 11 DLPFC sections. Designed comprehensive Idea 7 enhancement plan with 6 candidate solutions and 5-phase implementation roadmap backed by cross-domain analysis (FGVC, medical imaging, remote sensing). Key insight: supervised classification benchmarks don’t predict unsupervised clustering performance.
Windows SSH Configuration ✅ Fix VS Code Remote-SSH connection failures on Windows 04:42:44.174 | claude_code Diagnosed and fixed VS Code Remote-SSH connection failures to HPC cluster (tianhe/an49/an47). Root cause: Windows OpenSSH does not support ControlMaster Unix domain sockets, causing ‘Bad file descriptor’ errors. Solution: commented out ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist in ~/.ssh/config and cleared stale socket files. Successfully restored connection to tianhe and an49. an47 properly blocked by Slurm (no active jobs on node).
Error Recovery Benchmark ✅ M14 baseline evaluation infrastructure implementation 21:48:25.172 | claude_code Implemented M14 infrastructure for 454-scene × 4-policy baseline evaluation. Added resume support to collector.py (reads episodes.jsonl, rebuilds all_episodes dict, skips completed episodes). Extended 3_collect_data.py with –resume/–scene_offset/–scene_limit flags. Rewrote 4_analyze_results.py to support multi-run merge (nargs=’+’), per-error-type breakdown (from meta.json), and summary.json output with per_policy metrics + per_error_type heatmap + Fisher exact tests. Added 7 Makefile targets (m14-cpu/pi0-server/pi0-eval/pi05-server/pi05-eval/analyze/sanity). Updated project summary to v4.10. Ready to run: 5448 episodes, crash-resilient with automatic resume.
🔄 M12 large-scale dataset construction planning and implementation 21:18:21.831 | claude_code Developed comprehensive 13-step plan to scale error scene database from 454 to 2000+ scenes. Plan covered MimicGen augmentation to generate 200+ demo variants, multi-policy natural error capture using BC-RNN/Pi0/Pi0.5, and data quality control with stratified splitting. Identified critical insight that BC-RNN’s 60-70% success rate makes it the key to generating late-phase errors (transport/place). Created all code infrastructure: dataset statistics analysis script, stratified split method, adjusted detector timing (min_step 100→5-50), MimicGen config, CLI enhancements, Makefile targets. Execution hit MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID environment error during GPU setup.
🔄 M13 error taxonomy validation design 21:36:06.137 | claude_code Explored codebase to understand 24-type error taxonomy (3 families, 8 categories, 24 types) and designed comprehensive validation approach. Plan included theoretical justification via FMEA mapping to ISO safety standards (15066/10218/IEC 61508), completeness proof through 6×3 phase×family matrix, and statistical validation using Cohen’s/Fleiss’ kappa for inter-classifier reliability (target: within-category κ>0.6, cross-category κ<0.3). User rejected plan mode exit.
🔄 Phase II strategic planning v4.9 document updates 17:30:42.648 | claude_code Updated 項目全景總結.md to v4.9 with comprehensive Phase II planning structure. Split goals into Phase I (completed G1-G3: benchmark establishment) and Phase II (new G4-G7: proving benchmark usefulness through dataset scaling, taxonomy validation, baseline experiments, and foundation model improvement). Added detailed M12-M15 sub-goals, 5-phase execution timeline (Day 1-16), updated risk analysis for Phase II, and rewrote summary section. User requested optimization but immediately interrupted.
✅ Strategic documentation optimization and Phase II research design 20:17:43.767 | claude_code Optimized 項目全景總結.md from 1554→763 lines (51% reduction) by eliminating redundancy and updating statistics while preserving all 87 checklist items. Used chunked reading for large file, launched Explore agent for codebase statistics (53 files, ~14,300 lines in error_framework), migrated VLM tutorial (331 lines) to separate docs/vlm_tutorial.md file, rewrote main document. Then designed comprehensive Phase II research strategy with 4 goals (G1: dataset scale 2000+, G2: classification validation, G3: baseline evaluation 4 policies, G4: FM fine-tuning proof) and 15 milestones. User clarified strategic choices through iterative Q&A: chose human teleoperation for recovery data (prioritizing quality over automation), PickPlace-only scope. Plan created but not finalized due to user interrupting ExitPlanMode to ensure full understanding of ‘recovery training data’ concept first.
✅ VLA models and foundation models research survey 15:59:46.611 | claude_code Conducted comprehensive web-based research survey covering 8 VLA models claiming error recovery capabilities (SC-VLA, CycleVLA, VLA-SCT, FailSafe, FPC-VLA, RoboFAC, VLAC, Self-Improving VLA) and 9 foundation models (π0, π0-FAST, π0.5, OpenVLA, Octo, GR00T N1, HPT, RT-2). Created 200-line reference document with architecture details, open-source status, usage instructions, and fine-tuning commands. Identified FailSafe and VLA-SCT as easiest to integrate (training-free, plug-in architecture). Recovery methods categorized: dual-system, proactive subtask backtracking, reactive supervisor-guided correction.
✅ CLAUDE.md documentation quality improvement 20:03:03.225 | claude_code Implemented targeted improvements to project documentation, reducing CLAUDE.md from 205 to 163 lines while preserving all critical information. Fixed inaccuracies in error type counts (17→24) and validator counts (2→4), consolidated redundant architecture sections, trimmed overly-detailed external project description from 48 to 18 lines, and added missing documentation for test fixtures and data formats.

title: Bug Journal 2026-02-19

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-18 Daily Overview What was done: Two parallel research tracks: fixed spatial transcriptomics coordinate handling bug causing vision embedding errors (DCC), and built comprehensive policy benchmarking infrastructure with unified checkpoint management and rollout visualization (tianhe) How it was done: Deep debugging of coordinate systems via code inspection and verification, followed by cache clearing and re-running experiments; infrastructure work via symlink organization, script development, and integration testing Impact: MIHD: 86% improvement in UNI2-only clustering performance after fix validates multimodal fusion results; tianhe: unified checkpoint management and visualization tools enable systematic VLA cross-task generalization analysis DCC What was done: Diagnosed and fixed coordinate swap bug in MIHD spatial transcriptomics pipeline where pxl_row/pxl_col were incorrectly mapped as X/Y instead of Y/X How it was done: Modified run_benchmark.py and data_loader.py to correct column naming, cleared vision cache, re-ran 33 vision-only and 88 fusion experiments over ~6 hours with STAIG training Impact: UNI2 embeddings now correctly extracted from proper image locations; ARI improved from 0.065→0.121 for vision-only, concat fusion reached 0.275 ARI validating multimodal integration approach tianhe What was done: Built unified checkpoint management system with symlinks, created policy rollout visualization tool, and investigated VLA training data availability for PickPlace task How it was done: Created checkpoints/ directory structure linking BC-RNN/VLA/Robomimic models, wrote 200-line visualize_policy_rollout.py with MP4 rendering, updated configs and Makefile, searched zhaoganlong’s datasets Impact: Can now visualize policy behavior via MP4 rollouts and identified root cause of VLA failure (no task-specific finetuning); discovered MimicGen can expand 10→500 demos for future training Fixed critical spatial coordinate bug in MIHD multimodal fusion benchmark and built infrastructure for robotic policy visualization and checkpoint management
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Fix MIHD coordinate swap bug — Corrected tissue_positions_list.csv column mapping where pxl_row_in_fullres (Y) and pxl_col_in_fullres (X) were swapped as pixel_x/pixel_y Implementation & Fixes 🔄 Re-run MIHD vision and fusion experiments — Regenerate embeddings and benchmark results for 33 vision-only and 88 multimodal fusion experiments after coordinate fix ✅ Create policy rollout visualization tool — Wrote visualize_policy_rollout.py script with offscreen rendering, HUD overlay, MP4 export, and support for random/BC-RNN/VLA policies ✅ Build unified checkpoint management system — Created checkpoints/ directory with symlinks to BC-RNN, VLA (Pi0/Pi0.5), and Robomimic models for consistent path references ✅ Investigate VLA training data availability — Searched for PickPlace-specific VLA checkpoints and training data; found only 10 source demos vs 500 demos for Coffee/Stack tasks Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Vision embeddings extracted from transposed image locations due to X/Y coordinate swap in tissue_positions_list.csv parsing Solution: Renamed columns to pxl_row/pxl_col (semantic names), swapped coordinate order to (X, Y) = (pxl_col, pxl_row), deleted vision cache, re-ran experiments
Key Insight: Spatial data requires explicit Y-as-row, X-as-column semantics; 10x scale factor validation (pixel coords × scalefactor = microns) catches coordinate system errors early
2. Pi0_libero checkpoint achieves 0% success on PickPlace despite 96-100% on Coffee/Stack Solution: Identified root cause: Pi0_libero is LIBERO-domain finetuned, lacks PickPlace-specific training data (only 10 source demos vs 500 for other tasks); proposed MimicGen→LeRobot→LoRA pipeline
Key Insight: VLA cross-task generalization fails when task domains differ (LIBERO vs PickPlace object/scene layout); finetuning requires 500+ demos expandable via MimicGen from 10 source demos
General Issues 3. No way to visually inspect VLA policy behavior on PickPlace task to diagnose 0% success rate Solution: Built visualize_policy_rollout.py with EGL offscreen rendering, frame-by-frame capture, HUD overlay (policy/step/reward/success), and MP4 export per rollout
Key Insight: Video rollouts reveal behavioral patterns (e.g., random flailing vs directed failures) that success rate alone cannot distinguish
4. Scattered checkpoint paths across multiple HDD_POOL directories making config management fragile Solution: Created centralized checkpoints/ directory with task-organized symlinks (bc_rnn/pick_place, vla/pi0_libero, robomimic/lift) and updated all configs to use relative paths
Key Insight: Symlink-based checkpoint organization enables atomic path updates and clear separation between original storage and project references
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level VLA checkpoint selection for PickPlace Role Approach Human Questioned why Pi0_libero was being loaded instead of a PickPlace-specific checkpoint, assumed zhaoganlong should have one AI Initially loaded Pi0_libero as the only available VLA checkpoint without verifying task-specific training Difference Analysis: Human correctly intuited that task-specific finetuning should exist; AI defaulted to available checkpoint without validating task match. Investigation revealed no PickPlace checkpoint exists.
Implementation Level UNI/UNI2 usage in literature Role Approach Human Requested web search to verify how other papers extract patches and handle coordinates for spatial transcriptomics AI Focused on code-level debugging without consulting literature for domain best practices Difference Analysis: Human sought external validation from published methods; AI relied on internal code logic. Literature search confirmed 224x224 patches at 20x magnification but didn’t provide specific coordinate handling details.
AI Limitations General Limitations Did not proactively search for task-specific VLA checkpoints before recommending Pi0_libero, required human prompting to investigate zhaoganlong’s full checkpoint directory Needed multiple search iterations to discover zhaoganlong’s datasets were organized by task (Coffee/Stack) with 500 generated demos each, initially focused on finding PickPlace directly Learnings Key Learnings Spatial coordinate systems in medical imaging require explicit semantic naming (pxl_row=Y, pxl_col=X) and validation via physical units (pixels × scalefactor = microns); generic pixel_x/pixel_y naming is error-prone Vision foundation model integration in multimodal learning critically depends on spatial alignment; incorrect patch extraction (from transposed locations) can halve performance even with correct architecture VLA models trained on one task domain (LIBERO) show minimal cross-task generalization to different manipulation setups (PickPlace) despite similar action spaces; domain-specific finetuning is essential MimicGen can expand 10 human demonstrations to 500 training examples via automatic variation generation, matching the data scale used in successful VLA finetuning (Coffee: 96%, Stack: 100%) Practical Learnings Symlink-based checkpoint management enables version control of path references while preserving original data locations; critical for shared filesystem collaboration Conversation Summaries MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics 🔄 Coordinate swap bug fix in vision patch extraction 00:01:02.520 | claude_code Diagnosed and fixed critical bug where tissue_positions_list.csv columns (pxl_row, pxl_col) were incorrectly mapped as (pixel_x, pixel_y), causing vision patches to be extracted from transposed locations. Cleared vision cache and re-ran 121 experiments (33 vision-only + 88 fusion). Results showed UNI2 ARI improved from 0.065 to 0.121 (+86%). User requested literature review on UNI/UNI2 usage patterns; web search confirmed 224x224 patches at 20x magnification standard but lacked coordinate handling specifics. Experiments still running after 6+ hours due to STAIG training requirements.
Error Recovery Benchmark 🔍 VLA training data investigation and PickPlace finetuning path 04:52:08.850 | claude_code User asked about training VLA on PickPlace task after realizing Pi0_libero has 0% success. Investigation revealed PickPlace only has 10 source demos (6288 steps) while zhaoganlong’s successful Coffee/Stack training used ~500 demos generated via MimicGen. No PickPlace-specific VLA checkpoint exists; all VLA models are LIBERO-domain or base weights. Identified training path: 1) Run MimicGen to expand 10→500 demos, 2) Convert to LeRobot format via zhaoganlong’s scripts, 3) Run Pi0.5 LoRA finetuning. All necessary scripts and pipelines available in zhaoganlong’s repo.
✅ Checkpoint inventory and availability assessment 05:32:29.978 | claude_code User requested inventory of usable VLA models and checkpoints. Found 4 VLA model types registered in code (pi0, pi05, phoenix, flare) but only Pi0_libero and Pi05_base have actual checkpoints (~12GB each). Also identified BC-RNN PickPlace checkpoints (12 epochs, 14MB each) and Robomimic Lift checkpoint (7.6MB, 100% success). Discovered robomimic’s can/square/transport directories are empty. VLA checkpoints all reside in zhaoganlong’s HDD_POOL directory, linked via symlinks to project.
✅ Policy visualization infrastructure setup 00:26:49.679 | claude_code Built unified checkpoint management via checkpoints/ directory with symlinks (bc_rnn/pick_place, vla/pi0_libero, robomimic/lift). Created visualize_policy_rollout.py (200 lines) supporting random/BC-RNN/VLA policies with offscreen rendering, HUD overlay, and MP4 export. Updated benchmark_v4.yaml to use symlink paths and added 3 Makefile visualization targets. Tested successfully with random policy, generated 2 MP4 files. Updated documentation in 项目全景总结.md with new v4.7 checkpoint management section.
Token Usage Total: $11.96 — 18.4M tokens
summary: Fixed critical spatial coordinate bug in MIHD multimodal fusion benchmark and built infrastructure for robotic policy visualization and checkpoint management
title: Bug Journal 2026-02-18

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-17 Daily Overview Completed Work: Five parallel workflows were completed: DCC corrected the basic error in MIHD coordinates, increasing visual ARI from 0.065 to 0.12-0.25; MacBook/TzJsDesktop iterated daily and used the daily report tool to process historical logs from four devices between February 12, 2026, and February 16, 2026; Tianhe’s Error Recovery Benchmark increased from 251 scenarios to 454 scenarios, achieved through BC-RNN training, VLA dual-server deployment, and a multi-strategy evaluation framework; TzJsDesktop diagnosed and fixed ccusage pricing errors, discovering that the cost was underestimated by 13 times; Tianhe established an SSH stability infrastructure for remote development. Completion Methods: DCC corrected CSV column mappings, cleared caches, and ran 286 experiments again; MacBook/TzJsDesktop implemented a two-stage export→merge architecture, using rclone for synchronization, powerful JSON parsing, conversation summaries, and Hugo deployment; Tianhe fixed MuJoCo API compatibility issues, trained the BC-RNN for 600 cycles, implemented an obs key mapping adapter, deployed the VLA server on Pi0/Pi0.5, and debugged integration with 79 unit tests and GPU verifications; TzJsDesktop implemented a backup pricing mechanism for ccusage, re-exported 5-day logs; Tianhe configured SSH stability (tmux+keep-alive+auto-reconnect) and recorded local client settings. Impact: MIHD data quality was fundamentally restored (all visual results are now valid); the daily report tool reached production-level maturity; multi-device workflows allow knowledge to be systematically preserved; Error Recovery Benchmark exceeded the M5 scenario generation target (227%), and the first multi-strategy evaluation infrastructure preparation training strategy (M6 milestone) was completed, although the SR rate of BC-RNN was 0%, indicating problems with training checkpoints; SSH stability eliminated remote session loss issues; ccusage fixes corrected the displayed cost from $0-7 per day to actual $18-48 per day, restoring the integrity of financial tracking. DCC Completed Work: Identified and fixed an X/Y coordinate swapping error in MIHD load_spatial_coordinates(), where pixel_x should actually refer to pxl_row (Y-axis), and pixel_y should refer to pxl_col (X-axis). This caused all visual patches to be extracted from the transposed image position, resulting in a visual ARI of only 0.065. Completion Method: Modified CSV column names from [’…’, ‘pixel_x’, ‘pixel_y’] to [’…’, ‘pxl_row’, ‘pxl_col’], and changed the coordinate tuple from (pixel_x, pixel_y) = (Y, X) to (pxl_col, pxl_row) = (X, Y); removed allSpatialCoords.npz and vision/cache files; re-extracted embeddings for 11 parts in UNI2/HIPT/ResNet50; verified coordinate range (X: 323-1637, Y: 387-1773) and 100% embedding uniqueness. Impact: UNI2’s visual ARI increased from 0.065 to 0.08-0.25 (part 151673-151676 reached 0.22-0.25); all visual-dependent fusion experiments will be corrected; this is a fundamental data integrity issue that affects the validity of the entire benchmark. MacBook Completed Work: As the main center of the daily report tool, it processed conversation logs from April 12, 2026, to February 16, 2026, on four devices, covering MIHD, Error Recovery Benchmark, CalendarPro, and small tool projects; reported the results to the Hugo bugJournal section and GitHub Pages. Completion Method: Executed export –summarize to extract local logs → used –sync to merge export contents from other devices, downloaded using rclone → called Claude CLI API to generate structured JSON/Markdown reports → wrote reports to the website/content/bugJournal/ → ran update.sh to build the website (increased from 139 pages to 141 pages) → pushed public files to GitHub Pages. The log export (data correction) was successful, but due to the unavailable LLM API, downstream report generation was hindered.### Limitations of Artificial Intelligence### Key Limitations
Failed to independently diagnose the root cause of MIHD coordinate issues; relied entirely on users to provide analysis and repair plans. In the face of an “abnormally low ARI” macro metric, AI lacked the ability to independently track data sources (from results → embedding → patch extraction → coordinates → CSV column definitions in reverse order).
Tianhe’s BC-RNN integrated the repair attempts three times over two sessions, but AI failed to anticipate the different observation key names for robosuite’s different tasks (PickPlace and Lift), and also realized that the observation results reconstructed by StateExtractor after the first repair did not guarantee consistent key names. Users had to make multiple corrections to find the correct solution. The use of the outdated MuJoCo C API (mujoco.mj_name2id) instead of checking the robosuite’s wrapper API caused three injectors to crash immediately. It would have been better to check the existing working code (ImpulseInjector) before modifying other injectors to understand the correct API pattern.
The initial design of the daily reporting tool did not consider multi-device distributed usage patterns—assuming a single-device workflow, requiring users to redesign the two-stage architecture.
When generating JSON close to token limits, AI might truncate the output in the middle of the structure, resulting in malformed JSON, which requires defensive parsing and repair logic in production systems.
The repair of ccusage initially used the incorrect mb.get(‘model’) field, while the actual JSON contained modelName. No verification was done on the output structure of third-party tools before proceeding. This led to the repair being completely ineffective until humans discovered the error and rechecked it. Too many parallel exploration tasks were initiated, leading to duplicate information collection (reading the same log files multiple times).
Initially, it was proposed to use LIBERO/droid to test the VLA model, but environmental compatibility was not verified. The target environment was not kept as robosuite/mimicgen, but rather the semantic context of LIBERO. Humans needed to correct it to point to the correct configuration.
Insufficient memory across sessions: In the Tianhe session on 2026-02-16, AI was unable to recall the VLA checkpoint download from the previous session independently. Users were required to prompt to search for broader server paths.
Unable to predict users’ needs for features like dialogue summaries—these product insights came from observations of blanks in generated reports.
Difficulties in handling deeply nested meta-tasks: When conversations included discussions about the conversation itself, AI struggled to determine which level to analyze, unless there was clear task scope and guidance on expected outputs.
When generating the daily report on 2026-02-17, the AI acting as the daily report analyst itself became an object of observation—there was a meta-level complexity of “observers being observed.” AI needed to analyze its own behavior (generating daily reports), which required a certain level of self-awareness.
General Limitations Users rejected the design of the daily report automation deployment plan. AI failed to capture changes in user intentions and could not assess whether the detailed implementation plan met user expectations. It directly entered the ExitPlanMode mode without seeking user feedback first. Multiple attempts at ExitPlanMode failed, indicating difficulties in determining when the plan was complete and when users wanted to continue iterating. Users rejected the plan three times in multiple sessions, suggesting that AI should ask “Is this plan ready?” instead of assuming it was already completed. It was impossible to directly analyze video content to evaluate policy quality. The promotional videos of BC-RNN required manual extraction of key frames and manual inspection to identify robot hovering behaviors (ignoring the target capture). Without visual confirmation, it was impossible to infer a 0% success rate, which might be due to model quality issues. When encountering an API ConnectionRefused error during report generation, no alternative solutions were proposed (data was fully corrected before report generation). The flexibility of the separated workflow was insufficient. A long unified plan document containing both server-side and client-side instructions was created, but the execution boundaries were not considered. User feedback was needed to reorganize it into actionable individual deliverables. The project decomposition capability was weak. When users asked “What stage are we currently at?”, an exploratory agent was immediately launched and a large amount of documentation was read, rather than first checking the recent session context or the to-do tracking system. Over-reliance on file reading was excessive, while dialogue context might have been sufficient. Lessons Learned Main Lesson: Data errors are more隐蔽 and destructive than model errors. MIHD’s coordinate exchange error caused all visual experiment results to fail, but the code logic was normal, and all tests passed. It is necessary to trace anomalies at the macro level back to the data source (CSV column definitions). This requires a complete thought chain from data scientists; unit testing alone cannot detect such errors. Differences in naming conventions across frameworks are hidden pitfalls: robosuite’s _main suffix, differences in observation key names for different tasks (object and object state), LiteLLM’s model name-v1 suffix—these details are not obvious in the documentation, but they can lead to runtime errors. The robot simulation framework abstracts the original physics engine (robosuite encapsulates MuJoCo). Direct API calls bypassing the framework layer will cause errors—it is necessary to use the framework’s wrappers to handle naming conventions and state management. Robosuite’s observation key naming rule is that the environment automatically adds -state suffix to grouped modalities (e.g., object → object-state), but training data usually uses the original names. The adapter layer must handle these mismatches. The safest strategy is: (1) Use the framework’s API instead of low-level APIs; (2) Pass the original data structure instead of manually reconstructing it; (3) Print the actual output to verify field names, rather than inferring from the documentation. Always check the working reference code in the same codebase before making changes.
The correctness of the framework and the quality of the model require different verification strategies. Unit/integration testing can confirm code logic and eliminate errors like KeyError. Video visualization can reveal behavior quality issues (robot hovering and capturing). Even if unit testing passes, GPU integration testing remains crucial—BC-RNN passed 79 unit tests, but GPU deployment was needed to detect 0% SR issues and diagnose untrained checkpoints through visualization. Each verification layer has a different role.
The multi-device collaborative daily report tool has achieved a closed loop: each device exports local logs → central hub combines remote logs → unified deployment to Hugo → published on GitHub Pages. This workflow was extensively used on 2026-02-17 (processing 4 historical dates), proving the feasibility of the two-stage architecture. The next step is automation (which has been proposed but not implemented). Tool-based tools (used to analyze the tools used by the tool) need to carefully consider the distribution of the workflow—what happens on each device, how data is synchronized, how partial information is handled. For distributed scenarios, a two-stage architecture (local export + centralized aggregation) is more robust than a single-unit single-stage design.
The expansion of the error recovery benchmark scenario library from 251 to 454 is a successful incremental expansion case: First, the pulse injector (M4) was implemented → it was found that non-pulse types were needed (M5) → the MuJoCo API compatibility was fixed → various types of scenarios were generated through the detector candidate expansion mechanism. This shows the process from a minimum viable product to a fully functional product. The generation of error scenarios is a funnel process: detector triggering → error specification generation → injector application → verification → scenario acceptance. Enabling the injector does not guarantee scenario generation—it depends on the frequency of detector triggering. Depending on the dynamics of the task, the acceptance rates of different types of errors vary greatly. Pose perturbation is more general than friction/clamping anomalies in the pickup and placement task. Programmed specification enhancement (_augment_specs_with_alternatives()) can compensate for insufficient detector coverage.
Third-party tool integration requires defensive programming: the patch cost is equal to 0 (ccusage), field name compatibility (modelName or model), and self-downgrade for future repairs (when LiteLLM makes repairs, backtracking becomes inactive). This “only activates when necessary” patch strategy can minimize maintenance costs. Pricing defects of third-party tools should be resolved through local backtracking mechanisms, rather than waiting for upstream repairs. Design backtracking should only be triggered in cases of abnormal values (cost = 0) to maintain backward compatibility. LiteLLM uses versioned model names (anthropic.{model}-v1, optional: 0 suffix), while Claude Code logs use simplified names, leading to systematic mismatches. Cost tracking errors may lead to over 90% underestimation (observed 13 times), severely affecting financial planning. Regular audits of cost indicators are very important.
Human involvement in specifying the system architecture and strategic decisions, with AI responsible for technical implementation and optimization, yields the best results. In this meeting, users’ architectural insights (two-stage design, multi-device synchronization) were crucial; AI’s technical execution (parsing backtracking, configuration optimization) filled in the gaps. Qualitative judgment from domain experts is essential for identifying systemic problems. AI’s data-driven analysis and human experience and intuition form an effective complementarity.
Remote development on unstable connections requires multiple layers of defense: maintaining connection activity (prevention), session persistence (survival), multiplexing (fast reconnection), and automated scripts (recovery). Single-layer solutions will fail under real network conditions. For shared server accounts, user-specific custom settings should be placed in conditional blocks or separate startup scripts, rather than affecting everyone’s common.bashrc entry. tmux has both state persistence and environmental boundaries. Session-wide proxy configuration (not global bashrc) can prevent affecting users with shared accounts.
Structured data generated by LLMs requires multiple backtracking strategies for defensive parsing. The native JSON schema (Anthropic’s tool_use, OpenAI’s json_object) is helpful, but it cannot eliminate failures—always treat fixing as the last resort. Each backtracking layer handles specific failure modes: code blocks handle markdown format, curly braces matching handle truncation, and fixing handle syntax errors.
The daily summary tool benefits from a gradual evolution of the architecture: starting with a basic list of tasks/issues, adding session summaries to provide context, and adding priority metadata for filtering. Each layer meets different reading needs. The batch document workflow benefits from a two-stage architecture: (1) extracting local context for each source, (2) integrating across sources for global deduplication. This pattern appears in summary tools (export → merge), MIHD processes (first-stage cache embedding → second-stage fusion), and error recovery benchmarks (generating scenarios → collecting data).
MimicGen’s release policy is to only provide datasets, not pre-trained model checkpoints. Formal releases may provide data but not the training model—before planning, ensure the availability of assets. BC-RNN (with RNN behavior cloning) is a simple supervised learning: collecting pairs of human demonstrations (states, actions), training an LSTM to predict actions, and then deploying as a strategy. When there are enough demonstrations, this is a simple but effective manipulation task baseline. When pre-trained checkpoints are unavailable, training from demonstrations becomes the necessary backup plan.
Reviewing work logs (summarizing the situation of the past week) is an important exercise that converts raw dialogue logs into structured knowledge, helping to identify patterns and track progress over time. - Idempotent design patterns (tracking what has already been processed, skipping repetitive work) are crucial for workflows that may be interrupted or run again. _merged_devices tracking function can prevent expensive repeated API calls when reprocessing partially completed aggregated data.
Managing large model assets (VLA checkpoints larger than 47GB) requires an organized approach based on symbolic links to avoid duplication while maintaining centralized discoverability. Dispersed checkpoints in the file system can hinder reproducibility and configuration management. Centralized checkpoint directories can improve discoverability. When repairing quality issues in historical data, data correction (exportation) should be separated from downstream use (report generation). Avoid blocking the entire process due to failures in downstream dependencies (e.g., LLM API unavailability). The export phase relies on Node.js tools (ccusage), and the merging phase relies on the LLM API—even if merging is blocked, export can still proceed. Practical Experience The scope of complex scientific research project documents should include main modules as well as sub-modules (there are 11 sub-modules in the Motion framework), data conversion pipelines, and integration points across repositories (LeRobot, OpenPI, MimicGen). Adequate exploration can prevent onboarding difficulties caused by incomplete documentation.
